# Vivicy — exhaustive test matrix

Reconciled fingerprint: `579fb97edb08f8afc0af69208eaef64bfdb2170ce59ecafb4903d3c0720051d4` @ commit `71313e676d5afc3ca69cac1c4f7b366b67b761ff`


This file is the exhaustive, always-current inventory of every test case for Vivicy — every behavior the system has, whether it is covered by a test today or is a known GAP. It is **committed and machine-guarded**: the `Reconciled fingerprint` line above hashes the behavior-bearing source tree and records the HEAD commit at reconciliation time, and `scripts/test-matrix.test.ts` fails the vitest suite when code changes without this file being reconciled and re-stamped (`npm run matrix:stamp`). `git log test/TEST-MATRIX.md` is the audit trail of reconciliations. It is the single source of truth for "what should be tested" across the app (`app/`, `components/`, `lib/`) and the factory (`factory/`). It was assembled from a full per-area audit pass plus three adversarial cross-matrices (user journeys, parallel/merge chaos, process/crash chaos).

## How ids work

- Every case has a stable id: `[<area>.<n>]` for area matrices (e.g. `[extraction-gates.42]`) and `[cross.<name>.<n>]` for cross-matrices (e.g. `[cross.chaos-process.20]`). A few non-numeric suffixes (`SKILLS-GAP`, `SCOPE-NOTE`, `X4`) mark scope flags and adversarial additions.
- Ids are **stable and never renumbered**. New cases append at the next free number in their area. When a behavior is removed from the code, delete its case line — the id is retired, never reused.
- Line format: `- [id] scenario | expected outcome | kind (unit / integration / e2e-ui / e2e-process / boundary) | covered-today (test file + test name) or GAP`. A case whose line contains `GAP` anywhere is counted as a gap (including partial gaps where one half of the scenario is covered and the other is not).
- Deduplication: a full cross-file near-duplicate analysis was run at assembly time. Zero true duplicates were found — every near-identical pair (e.g. the many per-CLI "no VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT → exit 2" cases, or `retry-stage dev` via CLI vs via `POST /api/control/retry-stage`, or the factory's `computeDoneIds` vs the control plane's independent done-rule reimplementation) is a **distinct entry point or a deliberately independent reimplementation** and is kept as its own case. When a future edit makes two cases genuinely the same scenario, merge them into one line and keep **both ids** on it.

# Area matrices

## Area: app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit

### app/page.tsx

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.1] Initial mount calls `GET /api/map` with `cache: "no-store"` and shows the loading spinner + "loading" text until it resolves. | Loading state renders `Loader2` spin icon and `t("loading")`. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.2] `/api/map` responds non-ok (e.g. 500) on the initial (foreground) load. | State becomes `error`; message resolves from `body.detail` ?? `body.error` ?? generic "request failed" fallback, translated via `errorText(tErrors, "route.<code>")` when `body.code` is present. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.3] `/api/map` throws/network-fails on the initial load. | Catch branch sets `error` state with `err.message` or the generic-failure fallback. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.4] `/api/map` returns `{ empty: true, reason: "no_target" }`. | State becomes `empty` with reason `no_target`; renders `OnboardingEmptyState` (the calm no-target onboarding state — the Vivi panel stays closed until user action; no `PipelineWidget`, no `MapEmptyState`, no full-screen chooser). | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.5] `/api/map` returns `{ empty: true, reason: <anything but "no_target"> }` (e.g. `"empty_canonical"`). | Renders `MapEmptyState`; `PipelineWidget` is mounted alongside it but is trigger-less/dormant (renders nothing until a host drives `open`). | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.6] `/api/map` returns a full `ArchitectureMapData` payload (no `empty` flag, or `empty !== true`). | State becomes `ready`; renders `ArchitectureMap` + `VivicySidebar`, and `PanelToggle` appears; `PipelineWidget` is mounted as a trigger-less/dormant overlay (renders nothing until a host drives `open`). | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.7] Component unmounts while `loadMap` is in flight (fetch not yet resolved). | `mountedRef.current` guards every subsequent `setState` call; no "update on unmounted component" warning/crash. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.8] A background (`foreground=false`) `loadMap` call fails (e.g. periodic refresh via `onMapRefresh`). | The failure is silently swallowed — state does NOT flip to `error` (only `foreground` loads surface an error banner). | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.9] `runExtract` succeeds (`res.ok && body.ok !== false`). | `extracting` toggles true→false; success toast with `body.summary`; `loadMap(true)` re-runs; `extractError` stays/becomes null. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.10] `runExtract` returns `{ ok:false, code:"empty_canonical" }`. | No toast; sets `extractError` inline (message resolved via `errors.control.empty_canonical` or `body.error` fallback) so `MapEmptyState`'s Import CTA can guide the user — this is treated as an onboarding signal, not a failure. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.11] `runExtract` returns `{ ok:false, blocked:true, summary }`. | `toast.error` with the blocked title and `body.summary` (or default blocked description) — a human-attention case distinct from a transient failure. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.12] `runExtract` returns `{ ok:false }` with neither `code:"empty_canonical"` nor `blocked` (a genuine transient failure). | `toast.error` failed title; description resolves via `errors.control.<code>` when `body.code` present, else `body.summary`/`body.error`/HTTP-status fallback. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.13] `runExtract` throws (network error). | `toast.error` failed title with `err.message` or the network-failure fallback; `extracting` resets to false in `finally`. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.14] `runExtract` while unmounted resolves after unmount. | `finally` block guards `setExtracting(false)` with `mountedRef.current` — no state update after unmount. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.15] `onImported` callback fires (via `MapEmptyState`'s child import flow). | Clears `extractError` and reloads the map (`loadMap(true)`). | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.16] `onAgentsWarning` fires twice with the identical message string in a row. | Second call is deduped via `lastAgentsWarningRef` — only one `toast.warning` fires. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.17] `onAgentsWarning` fires with a different message than the last one. | A new `toast.warning` fires and `lastAgentsWarningRef` updates. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.18] Selecting a node then switching `state.kind` away from `ready` and back (e.g. map goes empty then ready again with a different node set). | `selected` memo recomputes; if the previously selected node id no longer exists in `state.data.nodes`, `selected` becomes `null` rather than throwing. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.19] Selecting an edge via `onSelect({type:"edge", id})` where `edgeIndexFromId` yields an out-of-bounds index (data raced/stale). | `state.data.edges[index]` is `undefined` → `selected` resolves to `null`, no crash. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.20] Clicking `PanelToggle`'s cycle button. | `panel.cycle()` invoked (delegates to `usePanelState`), `SidebarProvider`'s `open`/`style["--sidebar-width"]` update accordingly. | e2e-ui | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.21] `onProjectChanged` fires (from the panel's onboarding acquisition, Vivi activity, or the trigger-less `OpenProjectDialog` the setup bar keeps mounted). | `projectSignal` increments, `loadMap(true)` re-runs, AND the lifted `/api/project` state re-fetches (the projectSignal effect) — one refresh path for every source, so a project change always re-surfaces loading/error/empty states honestly. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.22] `isEmptyPayload` type guard receives non-object, `null`, or an object with `empty` not strictly `=== true` (e.g. `empty: "true"`, `empty: 1`). | Guard returns `false` in every case — only a strict boolean `true` is treated as the onboarding payload. | unit | GAP

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.339] Mount fetches `GET /api/agents/health?fresh=1` once — the `?fresh=1` re-probe on every page load means installing a CLI and reloading clears the gate honestly instead of serving a stale per-process memo; while it is in flight (`agentsHealth === undefined`) the page renders the existing loading skeleton — the gate NEVER flashes before the probe resolves. | Loading spinner until the health fetch settles; the boot probe always bypasses the memo. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.355] First-boot loading state (`agentsHealth === undefined`, health probe in flight) — the copy of the boot loading skeleton. | Renders the generic "Loading…" (`app.loading`), asserted synchronously on the initial render before the probe resolves; no feature-specific wording. | unit | app/page.test.tsx ("renders the generic loading copy while the health probe is in flight")
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.340] Health resolves with either (or both) CLI binary missing (`agentsGateBlocked` true). | ONLY `AgentsGate` renders — no SetupBar, no map, no onboarding, no sidebar; and the Vivi launcher (`ViviPanel`) is NOT mounted — no "Open Vivi" bubble in the DOM (absent, not merely disabled): the launcher exists only once the CLI gate is passed. | unit | app/page.test.tsx ("both CLIs missing: the install gate shows and the launcher bubble is absent from the DOM" + "one CLI missing: still gated — the launcher bubble is absent from the DOM")
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.353] Both CLIs present and health settled (`agentsGateBlocked` false). | The normal app renders and the Vivi launcher (`ViviPanel`) mounts, gated by `agentsHealth !== undefined && !gateBlocked` (present exactly when the normal app is on screen — settled probe, gate not blocking); the `AgentsAuthBanner` mounts under the stricter `agentsHealth && !gateBlocked` since it consumes the health data. Both derive from the same `gateBlocked` source of truth as the install gate — no second probe, no duplicated predicate. | unit | app/page.test.tsx ("both CLIs installed: the launcher bubble mounts, no install gate")
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.341] The gate's "Check again" hands a fresh snapshot up via `onHealth` and both CLIs are now present. | `gateBlocked` recomputes false and the normal app branches render without a reload. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.342] The health fetch itself fails/throws (server unreachable, malformed body). | `agentsHealth` becomes `null` — FAIL OPEN: no gate, no auth banner, the normal app renders (the app worked before the gate existed); the Vivi launcher renders as part of that normal app (`agentsHealth !== undefined && !gateBlocked` holds for `null`), so a transient probe failure never strands the user without Vivi. | unit | app/page.test.tsx ("health probe fails (no agents in the body): the app fails open — no gate, launcher still renders")
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.343] Health resolves with both present but an agent verifiably signed out. | `AgentsAuthBanner` renders over the normal app (never while `gateBlocked`); dismissible, non-blocking. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.344] The current project is LIFTED to the page (`/api/project` fetched on mount and on every `projectSignal` bump): `SetupBar` is fully controlled by the `project` prop, and `ViviPanel` receives `projectRoot` with three-valued semantics (`undefined` in flight, `null` none, string root). | One source of truth; a transient project-fetch failure keeps the last known value. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.345] `hasTarget` derivation for the panel: `empty`+`no_target` → false; `ready` or any other `empty` reason → true; `loading`/`error` → undefined (unknown). | The panel's onboarding view AND the launcher-bubble visibility key off exactly this value — `hasTarget === false` (the no_target screen) hides the bubble so the empty-state CTA is the sole opener; the same `hasTarget` prop is the single source for both (upload-vivi-chat.410). | unit | GAP

### app/layout.tsx

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.23] `generateMetadata` renders the `<title>`/`<meta name="description">` using `t("appTitle", {brandName, brandTagline})` / `t("appDescription")` and `BRAND` constants. | Title/description strings are correctly interpolated from `BRAND.name` / `BRAND.tagline`. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.24] Root layout renders with self-hosted Geist/Geist Mono fonts (no network request to Google Fonts). | `<html>` carries `fontMono.variable`, `font-sans`, `geist.variable`, and `antialiased`; no external font fetch happens at build/runtime. | integration | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.25] A browser extension (e.g. Grammarly) injects `data-lt-installed`/`data-gr-ext-installed` attributes onto `<html>`/`<body>` before hydration. | `suppressHydrationWarning` on both elements prevents a hydration-mismatch warning/error; the rest of the tree is still fully hydration-checked. | e2e-ui | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.26] `NextIntlClientProvider` receives `messages` from `getMessages()` and renders `TooltipProvider` + children + `Toaster`. | Children render with i18n context available; `Toaster` mounts once at the root (not duplicated per page). | integration | GAP

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.27] Mount fetches `GET /api/settings` and populates `settings` state from `body.settings` when present. | `QuotaFooter` and any settings-derived label reflect the fetched settings once resolved. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.28] `GET /api/settings` fails/throws on mount. | Catch swallows the error; `settings` stays at `DEFAULT_SETTINGS`; UI never blocks on this fetch. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.29] Component unmounts before the settings fetch resolves. | `cancelled` flag prevents `setSettings` after unmount. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.30] `SettingsDialog`'s `onSaved` callback fires with a new settings document. | `setSettings` updates the shared state that also feeds `QuotaFooter` (single source of truth — no duplicated settings state). | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.31] Default render — all six Accordion sections (Information, Filters, Details, Tasks, Pipeline, Skills) render as `AccordionItem`s, and only "tasks" is expanded by default (`defaultValue={["tasks"]}`). | Tasks content visible on first render; other five sections collapsed. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.32] Sidebar carries `side="right"`, `collapsible="offcanvas"`, `role="complementary"`, and `aria-label` interpolating `BRAND.name`. | Accessibility landmark + label present and correct on both desktop-docked and mobile-Sheet renders (shared per `Sidebar` primitive's role-hoisting logic). | e2e-ui | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.33] `SectionLegend` receives `view` + `data.nodes` + `data.statusLegend` and renders in the `SidebarFooter` above `QuotaFooter`, each separated visually. | Legend and quota footer both render in the footer region, legend first. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.SKILLS-GAP] `sidebar.tsx` imports `SectionSkills` from `@/components/sidebar/section-skills`, which is NOT among the files provided for this area's review. | The Skills accordion section's full behavior (fetch, states, gating) is untested by this pass — needs its own file read/case list. | n/a | GAP (out of declared scope — flag only)

### components/sidebar/panel-toggle.tsx

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.34] Desktop viewport (`isMobile=false`), `next="peek"`. | Button label "Open panel", icon `PanelRightOpen`, `data-open` reflects the `open` prop passed in (not `openMobile`). | unit | panel-toggle.test.tsx
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.35] Desktop viewport, `next="wide"`. | Button label "Widen panel", icon `ChevronsLeft`; click fires `onCycle` exactly once and does not touch the mobile Sheet. | unit | panel-toggle.test.tsx
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.36] Desktop viewport, `next="closed"`. | Button label "Close panel", icon `PanelRightClose`. | unit | GAP (label untested for this specific next value; peek/wide covered, closed isn't)
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.37] Mobile viewport (`isMobile=true`), Sheet starts closed. | Label is "Open panel" regardless of the `next` prop passed; `data-open` reflects `openMobile`, not the desktop `open` prop. | unit | panel-toggle.test.tsx
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.38] Mobile viewport, click toggles the Sheet open. | `toggleSidebar()` invoked (not `onCycle`); label flips to "Close panel"; `openMobile` becomes true. | unit | panel-toggle.test.tsx
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.39] Mobile viewport, second click on an open Sheet. | Sheet closes again (`openMobile` → false), `onCycle` still never called. | unit | panel-toggle.test.tsx
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.40] Tooltip content on hover/focus. | `TooltipContent side="left"` shows the same label text as the button's `aria-label`. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.41] Viewport crosses the mobile breakpoint (768px) WHILE the panel is open (resize event mid-session). | The toggle's behavior source switches live from the desktop cycle to the mobile Sheet (or vice versa) without requiring a remount — `useIsMobile`'s `matchMedia` listener drives it. | e2e-ui | GAP

### components/sidebar/process-control-bar.tsx

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.42] SSE `/api/status/stream` emits a valid JSON frame with no `error`. | `status` state updates; `streamError` clears to false. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.43] SSE frame carries `{ error: <string> }`. | `streamError` set to true; badge text falls back to `t("offline")`; `status` is NOT updated from this frame. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.44] SSE `onmessage` payload is malformed (`JSON.parse` throws, e.g. truncated frame). | Caught; `streamError` set to true; no crash. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.45] `EventSource.onerror` fires (connection lost). | `streamError` set to true regardless of the last good frame. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.46] Component unmounts. | `source.close()` is called in the effect cleanup — no leaked EventSource. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.47] Two consecutive SSE frames whose signature (`issues_done/issues_total:gates.fail:run_active`) differs from the previous one, AND `onMapRefresh` is provided. | `onMapRefreshRef.current()` fires exactly once per signature change (not on the very first frame, since `lastSignatureRef` starts `null`). | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.48] Two consecutive SSE frames with an IDENTICAL signature. | `onMapRefresh` is NOT called a second time (dedup via `lastSignatureRef`). | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.49] `onMapRefresh` prop changes identity between renders (parent re-render with a new closure) while an SSE frame arrives. | The `onMapRefreshRef` ref pattern ensures the LATEST callback is invoked, not a stale closure captured at mount. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.50] `resolveRunPhase` yields `"idle"` and `isResumable` is false (no run ever started). | Renders the "Run" `IconControl` (Play icon), not Stop or Resume. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.51] Phase is `"running"` or `"stalled"`. | Renders the `StopControl` (Pause icon) instead of Run/Resume. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.52] Phase is `"idle"`/`"blocked"`/`"stalled"` AND `isResumable(status)` is true (partial progress). | Renders the "Resume" `IconControl` (RotateCcw icon). | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.53] `StopControl` clicked — opens the `AlertDialog`, user clicks Cancel. | Dialog closes; no `/api/control/stop` POST fires. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.54] `StopControl` clicked, confirms via the destructive Action button. | `act("stop","/api/control/stop", label)` fires; on success, `toast.success` with the Stop label; `pending` set to `"stop"` during the request then cleared. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.55] `development.issues.length === 0` (nothing extracted yet). | Extract button is enabled (`disabled={pending !== null}` only); clicking it POSTs `/api/control/extract`. | unit | process-control-bar.test.tsx
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.56] `development.issues.length > 0` (already extracted). | Extract button renders `aria-disabled`, `opacity-50`, remains a real focusable tooltip trigger (`data-slot="tooltip-trigger"`), and does NOT carry the native `disabled` attribute. | unit | process-control-bar.test.tsx
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.57] Click OR Enter/Space keyboard activation on the greyed Extract trigger. | `event.preventDefault()` swallows the action — no `/api/control/extract` POST fires via any activation path. | unit | process-control-bar.test.tsx
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.58] `extractedGateMessage(issueCount)` with `issueCount = 1`. | Singular wording: "Already extracted — 1 issue. Re-extraction isn't available yet." | unit | process-control-bar.test.tsx
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.59] `extractedGateMessage(issueCount)` with `issueCount = 0` (boundary — should be unreachable since the gate only renders when `>0`, but the pure fn itself). | Function still returns a grammatically valid string (verify no "0 issues" off-by-one or negative-count edge case breaks pluralization). | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.60] `act()` POST fails (`!res.ok || body.ok === false`). | `toast.error` with the failed title; description resolves via `errors.control.<code>` when present else `body.error`/status fallback. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.61] `act()` for `action === "extract"` succeeds with `body.steps` where at least one step has non-zero `code`. | `toast.warning` "extraction issues" with a joined `"name: exit code"` list per failing step; `onMapRefresh` still called. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.62] `act()` for `action === "extract"` succeeds with ALL steps `code === 0`. | `toast.success` "extraction success" listing step names joined by " -> "; `onMapRefresh` called. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.63] `act()` for a non-extract action (start/stop/resume) succeeds. | Plain `toast.success` with the ok title/label; `onMapRefresh` is NOT invoked from this path. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.64] `act()` throws (network failure, e.g. server process down). | `toast.error` with `error.message` or the generic network-error fallback; `pending` resets in `finally`. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.65] Any control button clicked while `pending !== null` (another action mid-flight). | All alternate action buttons are `disabled` — no concurrent duplicate POSTs (Run/Resume/Stop/Extract all gate on the shared `pending` state). | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.66] `issues_total = 0`. | Progress bar renders `value=0`; the counter text shows `done/"?"` (literal question mark placeholder) rather than `0/0`. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.67] `issues_total > 0`, `issues_done` between 0 and total. | `percent = round(done/total*100)` drives the `Progress` bar value; text shows `done/total`. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.68] Badge status dot color per phase (`idle`→muted, `running`/`done`→primary, `blocked`/`stalled`→destructive). | Correct `PHASE_DOT` class applied per phase; `streamError` overrides the label text to "Offline" but the dot's color still follows `phase` (not forced to a distinct offline color) — worth confirming this is intended, not a bug. | unit | GAP

### components/sidebar/quota-footer.tsx

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.69] No SSE frame received yet (`quota === null`). | Renders the neutral empty-state placeholder text; no collapse/expand toggle button rendered (gated on `hasAgents`). | unit | quota-footer.test.tsx
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.70] SSE frame carries `{ error }`. | Ignored — `quota` state is untouched (stays at whatever it was, including `null`). | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.71] SSE frame is malformed JSON. | Caught silently; last good `quota` state is kept. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.72] SSE frame has `quota: undefined`/missing `quota` key entirely (but no `error`). | Falls back to `{ agents: {} }` — `hasAgents` becomes false, not a crash. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.73] Collapsed view, both known CLIs present (claude + codex) plus one UNKNOWN extra agent name. | Known CLIs render first in the canonical `PROVIDERS` order (stable regardless of role assignment), unknown names appended after (`extraNames`). | unit | quota-footer.test.tsx ("swapped assignment: each CLI row shows its own settings entry, matched by live provider")
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.74] A window (`5h` or `weekly`) has `used_pct: null`. | Renders `"—"` (honest unknown), never a fabricated percentage; no `Progress` bar rendered for that window when expanded. | unit | quota-footer.test.tsx
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.75] A window has a real numeric `used_pct` (e.g. 38). | Renders `"38%"` and (expanded) a `Progress` bar at that value. | unit | quota-footer.test.tsx
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.76] `agent.status === "throttled"`. | Expanded view shows a `destructive` Badge "throttled"; collapsed row percentages render in destructive/medium-weight text. | unit | quota-footer.test.tsx
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.77] `friendlyModel` given a known id (`claude-opus-4-8`, `gpt-5.5`). | Returns "Opus 4.8" / "GPT 5.5" respectively. | unit | GAP (only exercised indirectly via component tests)
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.78] `friendlyModel` given an unrecognized id (e.g. `"claude-internal-x"`). | Returns the raw string unchanged — never fabricates a friendly name. | unit | quota-footer.test.tsx (indirect, via settings-dialog custom-model scenario, not quota-footer's own tests)
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.79] `agentLabel` — a CLI has a `settings` entry whose live `provider` matches it. | The settings-configured model wins over the live quota-state-reported model (settings > reported); the entry is found by provider match (`configForAgent`), never a hardcoded CLI→role map. | unit | quota-footer.test.tsx
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.80] Toggle collapse/expand button clicked. | `collapsed` flips; `localStorage["vivicy:quota-footer-collapsed"]` persists `"true"`/`"false"`; `aria-expanded` mirrors `!collapsed`. | unit | quota-footer.test.tsx
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.81] `readCollapsed()` when `localStorage` throws (private-mode/disabled storage). | Falls back to `true` (collapsed) rather than throwing. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.82] `readCollapsed()` server-side (`typeof window === "undefined"`). | Returns `true` (hydration-safe default). | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.83] `formatReset` with a `resetAt` in the past. | Returns `null` (no countdown shown for a stale reset time). | unit | quota-footer.test.tsx
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.84] `formatReset` with `resetAt = null`. | Returns `null`. | unit | quota-footer.test.tsx
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.85] `formatReset` with `resetAt` an INVALID date string (unparseable). | `Number.isFinite(ms)` guard catches `NaN` → returns `null`, no crash. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.86] `formatReset` with an exact hour boundary (e.g. 2h00m remaining). | Renders "resets in 2h00" (padded minutes), not "resets in 2h". | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.87] `formatReset` with less than 1 hour remaining (e.g. 45m). | Renders "resets in 45m" (minutes-only format, no hours segment). | unit | quota-footer.test.tsx
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.88] `ResetCountdown`'s internal 60s timer ticks while mounted. | The countdown label re-derives from the new `Date.now()` on each tick (verifies the label actually decrements over time, not just at mount). | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.89] `ResetCountdown` unmounts. | `clearInterval` cleans up the 60s timer — no leak. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.90] No agents exist yet, but `collapsed` was previously persisted `false` from an earlier session. | Toggle button itself still doesn't render (`hasAgents` false gates it) even though `collapsed` state is `false` — no dead expand/collapse control shown for an empty state. | unit | GAP

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.170] Selecting a NEW model via `onModel`/`setModel` (`withModel`). | `draft[role]` updates via `withModel(prev[role], model)` — presumably also normalizes/clamps effort+fast for the new model (contract lives in `lib/settings`, exercised here as an integration point). | integration | GAP

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.173] Reviewer on `gpt-5.3-codex-spark`. | No thinking-level Select rendered at all (`hasEffort` false) — instead a "no separate thinking level" note; fast also disabled with the Spark-specific reason. | unit | settings-dialog.test.tsx
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.174] `fastReason` for `provider==="claude"` and a non-fast-capable model (not Spark). | Returns the Claude-specific reason string interpolating the model id. | unit | GAP (Spark path tested; generic Claude non-fast path only indirectly via the disabled test, not asserting the exact message)
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.175] `fastReason` for a provider/model combo that's neither Codex-Spark nor Claude (future/unknown provider). | Falls back to the generic `fastReasonDefault` message. | unit | GAP

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.177] Reassigning a role's CLI (`assignCli`) to the SAME provider it already has. | No-op — `prev` returned unchanged (the `if (prev[role].provider === provider) return prev` guard). | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.178] Reassigning a role's CLI to a DIFFERENT provider. | BOTH roles reset: this role gets `agentDefaultsFor(provider)`, and the OTHER role is forced to `agentDefaultsFor(otherProvider(provider))` — enforcing the two-CLI distinctness invariant structurally, not just via validation. | unit | GAP (behavior is implied by the settings.test.ts around `agentDefaultsFor`/`otherProvider`, but the dialog's own `assignCli` wiring isn't directly tested)

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.182] Typing a NON-numeric or empty string into the stepper input. | `Number("")` → `0`; verify `clampMaxParallel` clamps this to `MIN_PARALLEL` (1) rather than committing `0` or `NaN` to the draft — HIGH-RISK boundary given `Number.isNaN` isn't obviously guarded before `clampMaxParallel` in the read code. | unit | GAP

### components/ui/number-stepper.tsx

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.238] `value === min`. | Decrease button disabled (`atMin`); Increase enabled (unless also at max). | unit | settings-dialog.test.tsx (via the concurrency stepper)
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.239] `value === max`. | Increase button disabled (`atMax`); Decrease enabled. | unit | settings-dialog.test.tsx
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.240] Click Decrease/Increase — clamps via `clamp()` so the reported value never exceeds `[min,max]` even if `step` would overshoot. | `onValueChange` always receives an in-range value. | unit | settings-dialog.test.tsx (indirect)
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.241] Typing directly into the underlying `Input` with a value the CALLER does not clamp (this component passes `Number(event.target.value)` straight through, unclamped, to `onValueChange`). | `NumberStepper` itself does NOT clamp typed input — clamping is the caller's responsibility (`clampMaxParallel` in the settings-dialog case). A caller that forgets to clamp would let out-of-range/NaN values through. Direct unit test of `NumberStepper` alone (no clamping caller) should verify this pass-through contract explicitly. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.242] Typing an empty string / non-numeric text directly. | `Number("")` = `0`, `Number("abc")` = `NaN` — both passed to `onValueChange` verbatim by this component; verify this documented pass-through behavior directly (not just through the settings-dialog integration). | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.243] Both arrow buttons have `tabIndex={-1}` (deliberately out of tab order — the Input itself is the tab stop). | Tab order skips the +/- buttons; they're mouse/keyboard-activatable only via direct focus (e.g. Space/Enter after a click) not via Tab traversal. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.244] `disabled` prop passed to `NumberStepper`. | Both buttons AND the input disable together. | unit | GAP

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.270] `toast.success(...)` rendered. | Uses `CircleCheckIcon`; theme forced `"light"` regardless of system dark-mode preference (an explicit, deliberate choice worth a regression test). | unit | sonner.test.tsx (`Toaster mount > anchors top-right, forced light theme`; `typed toasts > success ...`)
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.271] `toast.error(...)` / `.warning(...)` / `.info(...)` / a loading toast. | Correct icon per type (OctagonX/TriangleAlert/Info/spinning Loader2), each enlarged to `size-6` on BOTH the `[data-icon]` container (`classNames.icon`, important-flagged to beat sonner's fixed 16px box) and the svg — so the per-type icon reads as clearly dominant over the ~12px dismiss X instead of blending with it, with the box/glyph staying coherent (no overflow into the text gap) on one- and multi-line bodies. | unit | sonner.test.tsx (`typed toasts` each variant asserts the svg + `[data-icon]` carry `size-6` + `loading toast shows the spinning loader icon, sized like the typed icons`)
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.272] `closeButton` prop. | Every toast gets an explicit close (X) affordance, not just auto-dismiss. | unit | sonner.test.tsx (`typed toasts` asserts `[data-close-button]` per variant)
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.273] CSS vars for the neutral toast (`--normal-bg`/`--normal-text`/`--normal-border`) AND the four typed variants (`--{success,error,warning,info}-{bg,border,text}`) map to the app's design tokens — the neutral trio to `popover`/`border`, each typed trio derived from `--success`/`--destructive`/`--warning`/`--info` via `color-mix` in `oklab` (12% bg tint, 38% border, 70% text — oklab, not oklch, so the white/near-black neutral endpoint's undefined hue cannot drag the mix across the hue wheel). | Toasts are visually consistent with the design system, and typed toasts are colored by the repo tokens, not sonner's own default rich-color palette. | unit | sonner.test.tsx (`Toaster mount > typed colors derive from the design tokens, not sonner's own palette`)
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.356] Toaster `position="top-right"`. | The toaster anchors to the top-right (`data-x-position="right"`, `data-y-position="top"`), where descending alerts are conventional — not sonner's bottom-right default. | unit | sonner.test.tsx (`Toaster mount > anchors top-right, forced light theme`)
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.357] `richColors` enabled — a typed toast (`toast.success`/`.error`/`.warning`/`.info`) is fired. | Each toast carries `data-rich-colors="true"` and `data-type="<variant>"`, so sonner applies the per-type colored treatment (distinct color per success/error/warning/info) rather than a uniform neutral card. | unit | sonner.test.tsx (`typed toasts` per-variant `data-rich-colors`/`data-type` assertions)
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.358] Default display `duration={5000}`. | Toasts linger 5s — one second longer than sonner's 4s default — so alerts are easier to read before auto-dismiss; still dismissible early via the close button. | unit | GAP (duration is a JS prop with no cheap DOM/timing assertion; enforced at the single mount point)

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.280] `ToggleGroupItem`'s own `variant`/`size` props vs the CONTEXT's `variant`/`size` (set on the parent `ToggleGroup`). | Context wins over the item's own prop default (`context.variant || variant`) — verifies group-level styling can't be silently overridden per-item by accident. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.281] `type="single"` (SectionFilters' actual usage) — selecting one item deselects any other. | Only one `ToggleGroupItem` can be pressed/`aria-checked` at a time. | unit | section-filters.test.tsx
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.282] `orientation="vertical"`. | Flex direction and rounding rules flip accordingly (`data-vertical:flex-col`). | unit | GAP

### components/ui/toggle.tsx

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.283] Toggle `variant="outline"` vs `"default"`. | Border/background differ per variant. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.284] Toggle `data-state="on"` — pressed styling (`data-[state=on]:bg-muted`) matches `aria-pressed`. | Visual pressed state and a11y state agree. | unit | GAP (this exact toggle primitive underlies ToggleGroupItem, exercised in section-filters.test.tsx via the parent, but standalone `Toggle` usage isn't directly tested anywhere in this area)

### components/ui/tooltip.tsx

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.285] `TooltipProvider` `delayDuration` default override to `0` (instant tooltips app-wide — no hover delay). | Tooltip appears immediately on hover/focus, not after the Radix default ~700ms delay. | e2e-ui | GAP (relied upon by settings-dialog.test.tsx's `.focus()`-based tooltip reveal, but the delay-override itself isn't asserted)
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.286] `TooltipContent` `collisionPadding=8` — tooltip near a viewport edge. | Radix flips/shifts the tooltip to stay ≥8px from every edge; never bleeds off-screen. | e2e-ui | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.287] Long tooltip content (e.g. a long file path). | `max-w-xs` caps width; caller is expected to handle wrapping (per the code comment) — verify a long unbroken string doesn't force horizontal overflow of the tooltip box itself. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.288] Disabled-but-focusable pattern (as used in ProcessControlBar's greyed Extract, SettingsDialog's disabled fast Switch wrapped in a focusable span). | Tooltip is reachable via keyboard focus even though the underlying control is visually/functionally disabled — verified per-usage in process-control-bar.test.tsx and settings-dialog.test.tsx, but not as a standalone Tooltip-primitive contract test. | unit | process-control-bar.test.tsx, settings-dialog.test.tsx (indirect only)

### hooks/use-mobile.ts

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.289] `window.innerWidth < 768` at mount (client snapshot). | `useIsMobile()` returns `true` below 768px, `false` at/above. | unit | GAP (exercised indirectly via panel-toggle.test.tsx's `setViewport` helper, but `useIsMobile` itself has no dedicated unit test)
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.290] `getServerSnapshot` always returns `false` (SSR-safe default: never mobile on the server). | Server-rendered HTML always assumes desktop layout; the client "corrects" to mobile only after hydration via `useSyncExternalStore`, avoiding a hydration mismatch. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.291] Viewport crosses the 768px breakpoint after mount (resize / matchMedia `"change"` event fires). | Hook re-renders with the new boolean via the `subscribe`/`addEventListener("change", ...)` wiring. | unit | GAP (panel-toggle.test.tsx sets the viewport BEFORE render, never simulates a live resize mid-test)
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.292] Exactly at the boundary (`innerWidth === 767` vs `768`). | 767 → mobile (`max-width: 767px` matches); 768 → desktop (matches nothing). Off-by-one boundary worth an explicit test given `MOBILE_BREAKPOINT - 1` arithmetic. | unit | GAP

### hooks/use-panel-state.ts

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.293] Default first render (nothing persisted). | `state="peek"`, `open=true`, `width=PANEL_WIDTHS.peek`, `next="wide"`. | unit | use-panel-state.test.ts
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.294] Full 3-cycle: peek→wide→closed→peek. | Each transition's `state`/`open`/`width`/`next` match exactly, including the `closed` state falling back to the PEEK width (so reopening lands on peek, not wide). | unit | use-panel-state.test.ts
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.295] `setOpen(false)` from any state. | Forces `state="closed"`. | unit | use-panel-state.test.ts
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.296] `setOpen(true)` from `closed`. | Forces `state="peek"` (never restores to whatever state preceded closing — always reopens to the comfortable default, not "wide"). | unit | use-panel-state.test.ts
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.297] Persisted state written to `localStorage["vivicy:panel-state"]` survives a fresh hook mount (new component instance, same module/session). | Second mount reads the same state via the module-level cached snapshot. | unit | use-panel-state.test.ts
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.298] Corrupt/unknown persisted value (`"bogus"`). | `isPanelState` guard rejects it; falls back to `DEFAULT_STATE` ("peek"). | unit | use-panel-state.test.ts
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.299] `getPanelStateServerSnapshot()` always `"peek"` even when `"wide"` is persisted — the hydration-safety contract. | Server/first-client-render agreement, verified directly against `getPanelStateSnapshot()` diverging. | unit | use-panel-state.test.ts
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.300] `localStorage.getItem`/`setItem` THROWS (private browsing / storage disabled). | `readStoredState`/`writeStoredState` catch and fall back silently — panel state still works in-memory for the session, just doesn't persist across reloads. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.301] `setPanelState(next)` called with the SAME value as the current snapshot (`snapshot === next`). | Short-circuits — no redundant `localStorage.setItem` write, no listener notification storm. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.302] A `storage` event fires from ANOTHER browser tab/window changing the SAME key. | This tab's hook re-renders reflecting the new cross-tab value (`onStorage` listener updates the cached `snapshot` and notifies). | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.303] A `storage` event fires for a DIFFERENT key. | Ignored (`event.key !== STORAGE_KEY` guard) — no spurious re-render. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.304] Two components both call `usePanelState()` simultaneously (e.g. the page + a hypothetical second consumer). | Both stay in sync — a `cycle()` call from either instance is reflected in both (shared module-level store, not per-hook-instance state). | unit | GAP

### hooks/use-persisted-boolean.ts

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.305] Default value when nothing persisted for `key`. | Returns `defaultValue` exactly. | unit | use-persisted-boolean.test.ts
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.306] Setting a new value persists it and updates the returned snapshot immediately. | `localStorage.getItem(key)` reflects the new value as `"true"`/`"false"` string. | unit | use-persisted-boolean.test.ts
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.307] Fresh mount after a prior session persisted `true`. | Hook restores `true` (post-hydration) rather than resetting to `defaultValue`. | unit | use-persisted-boolean.test.ts
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.308] Explicitly persisting `false` when the default is also `false` (a no-op value but an explicit user action). | Still writes `"false"` to storage (not skipped merely because it matches the default) — verifies user intent is always recorded, not conflated with "never touched". | unit | use-persisted-boolean.test.ts
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.309] TWO DIFFERENT keys used by two different components (e.g. legend-open + some other persisted flag) — the `Map<string, BooleanStore>` per-key isolation. | Setting one key's value never affects the other key's stored/observed value. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.310] Two components share the SAME key (e.g. two SectionLegend instances, hypothetically). | Both read/write the same underlying store and stay in sync on a change (mirrors `use-panel-state`'s shared-store pattern). | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.311] `localStorage` read/write throws. | `readPersisted`/`writePersisted` catch and fall back to `defaultValue` / silently drop the write. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.312] Raw stored value is neither `"true"` nor `"false"` (corrupted, e.g. `"1"` or `"yes"`). | `raw === "true"` strict comparison means anything else (including truthy-looking strings) evaluates to `false` — worth an explicit boundary test since this differs from a naive `Boolean(raw)` cast. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.313] A cross-tab `storage` event for this hook's key. | The store updates and the listening component re-renders with the new value (mirrors use-panel-state's cross-tab sync but for the generic boolean store). | unit | GAP

### lib/utils.ts (cn)

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.314] Plain string class merge. | Space-joined output. | unit | utils.test.ts
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.315] Falsy/conditional values (false/null/undefined/""). | Dropped from output (clsx semantics). | unit | utils.test.ts
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.316] Arrays and object-syntax inputs. | Flattened/filtered correctly (clsx pass-through). | unit | utils.test.ts
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.317] Conflicting Tailwind utilities from the same axis (e.g. `px-2 px-4`). | Last one wins (`twMerge` dedup). | unit | utils.test.ts
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.318] Non-conflicting utilities on different axes. | Both retained. | unit | utils.test.ts
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.319] No input / all-empty input. | Returns `""`. | unit | utils.test.ts

### lib/brand.ts

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.320] `BRAND.name === "Vivicy"`, `BRAND.tagline === "visual vibe coding"`, object is `as const` (readonly at the type level). | Values match exactly; any attempted mutation is a compile-time error (not runtime-testable, but a lint/type-check case). | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.350] `DUO` constant (chrome-only affinity copy, `as const`) holds `nonna:{name:"Vivi", role:"la Nonna", blurb:"runs the kitchen — she governs the build, never cooks the code herself"}` and `nonno:{name:"the reviewer", role:"il Nonno", blurb:"the chef of finished dishes — he checks every issue the implementer plates up"}`. | Values match exactly; used only for chrome labels/tooltips, never leaking into method artifacts (specs/CRs/reports/prompts). | unit | GAP (`.320` pins BRAND.name/tagline only; DUO is unpinned)
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.351] `NonnaIcon` (components/chat/nonna-icon.tsx, chrome-only) renders a 24×24 monoline lucide-idiom SVG (`currentColor`, stroke-width 2, round caps, `aria-hidden`) and spreads `props` onto the `<svg>` so callers can size/label it. | Correct glyph; themes for free in light/dark; no state. | unit | GAP (no dedicated test; presentational chrome placeholder)

### Area cross-notes

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.321] **Settings single-source-of-truth**: `VivicySidebar` owns `settings` state, fetched once and updated via `SettingsDialog`'s `onSaved`; `QuotaFooter` only ever reads it as a prop. A future regression could reintroduce duplicate settings state (e.g. QuotaFooter re-fetching independently) — worth a guard test asserting `QuotaFooter` never calls `fetch("/api/settings")` itself. | integration | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.322] **Triple-independent SSE subscriptions**: `ProcessControlBar`, `QuotaFooter`, and `SectionPipeline` each open their OWN `EventSource("/api/status/stream")` rather than sharing one subscription. Verify this is an intentional, accepted cost (each test file's `FakeEventSource.last` pattern implies per-component instances) and that three simultaneous real `EventSource` connections don't hit a per-origin connection limit in production (browsers cap ~6 concurrent connections per origin over HTTP/1.1). | integration | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.323] **Map-refresh triggers**: both `ProcessControlBar` (on SSE signature change) and `ProcessControlBar`'s own `act()` extract-success path call `onMapRefreshRef.current?.()`, which in `page.tsx` is wired to `loadMap(false)` (background, non-foreground) — verify a refresh triggered by EITHER path never flips a `ready` map back to a `loading`/`error` state (background loads must be non-disruptive per case app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.8). | integration | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.324] **Panel width CSS var vs. Sidebar's mobile Sheet width**: `page.tsx` sets `--sidebar-width` from `usePanelState().width` (24rem/36rem) for DESKTOP, but the mobile Sheet branch in `components/ui/sidebar.tsx` hardcodes its OWN `--sidebar-width: 18rem` (`SIDEBAR_WIDTH_MOBILE`) inline, overriding the page-level var scoped to that Sheet. Verify there's no unintended width leak/conflict between the two — i.e. the desktop peek/wide cycle never affects the mobile Sheet's fixed width, and vice versa. | integration | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.325] **PanelToggle + panel-state cycle end-to-end**: `app/page.tsx` only renders `PanelToggle` when `state.kind === "ready"`; combined with `usePanelState`'s hydration-safe default, verify the toggle never flashes an incorrect icon/label on the FIRST paint after the map finishes loading (i.e., the "peek" server-safe default and the real persisted client state reconcile visibly, not just in isolated hook tests). | e2e-ui | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.326] **Accordion default-open section vs. content freshness**: `tasks` is the only section open by default; `SectionPipeline`, `QuotaFooter`, and `ProcessControlBar` all subscribe to SSE regardless of whether their accordion section is currently collapsed/hidden — verify collapsed (but mounted, since Radix Accordion keeps content in the DOM) sections don't leak/duplicate SSE subscriptions when repeatedly expanded/collapsed (mount/unmount vs. show/hide semantics of Radix `AccordionContent` matter here). | integration | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.327] **Zero-based rounded design language**: virtually every UI primitive in this area uses `rounded-none` explicitly (buttons, cards, badges, inputs, dialogs, selects) — a single visual-regression/lint check that no primitive accidentally reverts to a default Tailwind rounded corner would catch a whole class of drift at once. | e2e-ui | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.328] **`transcriptName`/`TranscriptRefs` reused identically between `SectionDetails` and `SectionTasks`**: both sections render the SAME `TranscriptRefs` component (imported by `SectionTasks` from `section-details.tsx`) — a shared-component regression (e.g. breaking the transcript modal's `open()` call) would silently break both sections at once; a single well-covered `TranscriptRefs` unit test suite (currently only exercised indirectly through each section's own tests) would pay for both. | unit | GAP

### Adversarial additions

- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.329] `components/ui/sidebar.tsx` has NO dedicated test file at all (`components/ui/sidebar.test.tsx` does not exist) — `SidebarProvider`, `useSidebar`, `Sidebar`'s three render branches (`collapsible="none"` / mobile Sheet / desktop offcanvas), the cookie write, and the Cmd/Ctrl+B shortcut are exercised ONLY incidentally through `panel-toggle.test.tsx` (which renders a bare `SidebarProvider` with no `open`/`onOpenChange`, so it never even exercises the CONTROLLED path `app/page.tsx` actually uses). | Every primitive behavior in this file (cookie persistence, controlled-vs-uncontrolled `setOpen`, the `role`/`aria-label` mobile relocation, `collapsible="none"`) is a GAP as a direct, isolated test — re-verified by reading the full file: confirmed no `components/ui/sidebar.test.tsx` exists and no other test file passes `open`/`onOpenChange` into `SidebarProvider`. | unit | GAP (highest-value miss in this pass — the whole primitive the app depends on for a11y/state is untested in isolation, only via one indirect consumer test)
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.330] `Sidebar`'s mobile branch passes NO `showCloseButton` prop to `SheetContent`, so Radix's default close `<button>` (with `X` icon + sr-only "Close") is still RENDERED in the DOM — the sidebar only hides it visually via `className="...[&>button]:hidden"` (CSS `display:none` via the `[&>button]` arbitrary variant). | Re-read `components/ui/sidebar.tsx` line ~195 and `components/ui/sheet.tsx`'s `SheetContent`: confirmed `showCloseButton` defaults `true` and is never overridden to `false` in the sidebar's mobile usage. If the CSS selector ever fails to match (e.g. a future markup change nests the button one level deeper, or a Tailwind purge/config change), the button reappears with NO test catching it; more importantly, verify TODAY whether `display:none` actually removes it from the tab order (it does, in modern browsers) — but this is coupled entirely to a CSS class string matching, not a structural `showCloseButton={false}`, which is the correct/idiomatic fix and the one actually testable in JSDOM (JSDOM does not compute `display:none` from arbitrary Tailwind variants, so a naive RTL query for the close button would still find the ghost node). | unit | GAP (no test asserts the mobile sidebar Sheet suppresses its own close button; existing `.257` only "flags" this without having traced the exact defaulted-prop mechanism)
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.331] `NumberStepper.onChange` computes `Number(event.target.value)` on EVERY keystroke, including the transient empty string produced mid-edit by `userEvent.clear()`/backspacing all digits — `settings-dialog.test.tsx`'s clamp test only asserts the FINAL value after `user.clear(input); user.type(input, "99")`, never inspecting `setMaxParallel`/`draft.maxParallel` during the intermediate empty-string keystroke. | Traced the full chain: `Number("")` is `0` (not `NaN`), and `clampMaxParallel` in `lib/settings.ts` does `if (!Number.isFinite(n) || n < MIN_PARALLEL) return MIN_PARALLEL` — so `0` IS correctly floored to `1` (finite, and `<1`). The original flagged case (.182 and .242) correctly identifies the input/output contract as safe but nobody has written the assertion; the REAL adversarial angle is verifying no intermediate render commits a literal `0`/`NaN` to `draft.maxParallel` state that could flash into a request payload if Save fires during that exact render (e.g. a fast Enter-to-save keybinding raced with the clear). No such race-tolerant test exists. | unit | GAP (sharpens .182/.242 — confirms the clamp math is safe on paper, flags the untested transient-state race as the actual residual risk)
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.332] `hooks/use-panel-state.ts` and `hooks/use-persisted-boolean.ts` both cache a module-level `snapshot`/`store.value` that is ONLY reset via the test-only `__reset*ForTests()` escape hatches — neither hook's own file tests what happens if a consuming component mounts, the module-level snapshot gets populated, and then a SECOND unrelated test file (not resetting the store) reads a stale cross-test-file leaked value. | `beforeEach`/`afterEach` in both `.test.ts` files call the reset function faithfully, so IN THIS SUITE no leak occurs — but this is a fragile invariant enforced by test-author discipline, not by the module design (no `afterEach` is registered globally; a new test file for either hook that forgets the reset call would silently read another suite's leftover state). No test asserts what happens if `__resetPanelStateStoreForTests`/`__resetPersistedBooleanStoresForTests` is NOT called between two `renderHook` calls in the same file (i.e., there is no test proving the SECOND hook instance in an unreset environment correctly shares rather than duplicates state) — case app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.304 asks for "two components stay in sync" but doesn't test the "two SEQUENTIAL renderHook calls, no unmount, no reset" shape specifically. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.333] `use-persisted-boolean.ts`'s `readPersisted` treats ANY `localStorage` value other than the exact string `"true"` as `false` — this includes a value written by a DIFFERENT, incompatible schema version (e.g. if a future refactor ever persisted `"1"`/`"on"` under the same key `vivicy:quota-footer-collapsed` or `vivicy:legend-open` before rolling back). | No test constructs a "stale/foreign-format value already in localStorage from a prior app version" scenario for either `use-persisted-boolean` or `quota-footer.tsx`'s own separate `readCollapsed()` (which uses the INVERTED convention `!== "false"`, i.e. default TRUE-unless-explicitly-"false" — the opposite polarity from `use-persisted-boolean`'s default-false-unless-explicitly-"true"). This polarity mismatch between the two persistence helpers in the SAME area (one is opt-out, one is opt-in) is itself untested and undocumented as an intentional divergence — worth an explicit regression test pinning both polarities so a future "unify the two boolean-persistence helpers" refactor doesn't flip one of them by accident. | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.334] `app/page.tsx`'s `runExtract`'s `body.blocked` / `body.code === "empty_canonical"` branches are mutually exclusive in the code (an `if/else if` chain), but a malformed server response could set BOTH `blocked: true` AND `code: "empty_canonical"` simultaneously — the code's `if (body.code === "empty_canonical")` check runs FIRST and returns early, so `blocked` is silently ignored in that combination. | No test constructs this over-specified/contradictory response shape to pin down which branch wins — worth an explicit precedence test given the two branches drive materially different UX (inline onboarding guidance vs. a blocking toast). | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.335] `isEmptyPayload`'s narrowing is checked BEFORE the `!res.ok` short-circuit has a chance to matter for foreground vs background loads, but note the ORDER in `loadMap`: a background (`foreground=false`) call that receives `!res.ok` returns silently (case .8), but a background call that receives `res.ok` AND an empty-payload/ready payload STILL calls `setState` unconditionally — meaning a background refresh (via `onMapRefresh`) CAN flip a `ready` map back to `empty` if the server transiently reports `empty:true` (e.g. a race during a project switch or corpus wipe). | This directly contradicts the "background loads are non-disruptive" invariant asserted in cross-note .323/.8, which only covers the ERROR path being suppressed for background loads — it does NOT cover the EMPTY-reason path, which has no such guard in the actual code (`if (isEmptyPayload(body)) { setState(...); return }` runs unconditionally regardless of `foreground`). This is a plausible real regression path (a `ready` map silently reverting to an onboarding screen mid-session from a background poll) with zero test coverage in either direction. | unit | GAP (high-value: contradicts a documented invariant; the error path is guarded, the empty path is not)
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.336] `components/sidebar/sidebar.tsx` (`VivicySidebar`)'s settings-fetch effect has NO cleanup-vs-in-flight-fetch abort — `cancelled` only gates the `setSettings` call, but the `fetch("/api/settings")` itself is never aborted (no `AbortController`), so an unmounted component's in-flight request still completes and consumes network/CPU resources uselessly. | No test verifies whether this is an accepted, deliberate simplicity trade-off (likely yes, given the same non-abort pattern repeats in `settings-dialog.tsx`) — but it's worth one explicit test/comment confirming the in-flight fetch is allowed to complete-and-be-discarded rather than aborted, since a rapid mount/unmount/remount cycle (e.g. fast tab-switching) could pile up several abandoned in-flight requests without any cap. | integration | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.337] `SectionFilters`' `SCOPES` list (case .114) and `section-filters.test.tsx` — re-checked: the component actually renders `SCOPES` (with `"all"` baked in as a real item) for the scope Select, but renders lane/status via a hand-written distinct `<SelectItem value="all">All</SelectItem>` PLUS the data-derived keys. This means "all" appears through two structurally different code paths across the three selects in the SAME component — a future edit to add a 4th filter (e.g. "owner") copy-pasting the lane/status pattern instead of the scope pattern (or vice versa) would silently diverge in behavior (e.g. duplicate "All" entries, or a missing one) with no shared-contract test enforcing "every filter Select in this component must expose exactly one All-equivalent option using ANY of the two valid patterns." | unit | GAP
- [app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.338] `hooks/use-mobile.ts`'s `subscribe` creates a NEW `matchMedia` query object (`window.matchMedia(...)`) on every subscribe call rather than reusing one across multiple simultaneous consumers (`VivicySidebar`'s `SidebarProvider`, `panel-toggle.tsx`'s indirect usage via `useSidebar`, and any other `useIsMobile()` caller) — each mounted consumer registers its OWN `matchMedia` listener rather than sharing one. | Not a correctness bug (each listener independently reflects the same live viewport), but no test verifies the listener-count/cleanup behavior when MULTIPLE components call `useIsMobile()` simultaneously and one unmounts while others remain mounted — i.e., that the shared boundary condition doesn't leak a stray `change` listener for the unmounted one while the others keep working. | unit | GAP

## Area: baselines-change-requests

Area covers: factory/doc-baseline.ts, factory/doc-baseline.test.ts, factory/change-control.ts, factory/change-control.test.ts, factory/cr-apply.ts, factory/cr-apply.test.ts, factory/prompts/change-request.md, factory/prompts/cr-applier.md, and the CR review surface (CrReviewCards in components/chat/vivi-notifications.tsx, formerly components/crs/cr-review-section.tsx).

### factory/doc-baseline.ts

- [baselines-change-requests.1] CLI invoked with no subcommand (`main()` with empty argv) prints usage to stderr and exits 1 | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.2] CLI invoked with an unknown subcommand (e.g. `bogus`) prints usage and exits 1 | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.3] `generate` without `--version` fails via `requireArg` ("Missing required --version") | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.4] `generate --version` with a malformed value (e.g. `1.0`, `v1.0.0`, `1.0.0.0`, empty string) fails `assertVersion` | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.5] `generate --status` with an invalid value (e.g. `published`) fails `assertStatus` | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.6] `generate` with no `--status` defaults to `draft` | covered-today: doc-baseline.test.ts ("generate hashes the doc set...")
- [baselines-change-requests.7] `generate --bump <class>` without `--previous-version` fails ("requires --previous-version") | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.8] `generate --bump` with an invalid class (not major/minor/patch) fails ("Invalid --bump") | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.9] `generate --bump minor --previous-version 1.0.0 --version <wrong-delta>` fails with the exact expected-version message | covered-today: doc-baseline.test.ts ("generate --bump validates the version delta...")
- [baselines-change-requests.10] `generate --bump major/minor/patch` with the exact correct delta succeeds for each of the three classes | covered-today: doc-baseline.test.ts (major + minor asserted; patch implicitly via other tests)
- [baselines-change-requests.11] `generate` default baseline_id is `baseline-v<version>` for status `frozen`, and `baseline-v<version>-draft` for status `draft` | covered-today: doc-baseline.test.ts (draft case); frozen case — GAP (no explicit frozen-id-format-without-`-draft` assertion, only indirectly)
- [baselines-change-requests.12] `generate --id <custom>` that does NOT match the governed `baseline-v<version>[-draft]` format fails `assertBaselineIdFormat` | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.13] `generate --id <id>-draft` combined with `--status frozen` fails ("carries a -draft suffix but status is frozen") | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.14] `generate --out` pointing outside `.vivicy/baselines/` fails unless `--allow-out-of-baseline-dir` is truthy | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.15] `generate --out <path outside baseline dir> --allow-out-of-baseline-dir true` succeeds and writes there | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.16] `generate --out <absolute path>` fails via `resolveRepoRelative` ("must be repository-relative") | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.17] `generate --out ../escape.json` (path traversal outside repo) fails via `resolveRepoRelative` ("must stay inside the repository") | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.18] `generate --status frozen` on a dirty working tree (uncommitted change) fails ("Refusing to generate a frozen baseline... working tree clean") | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.19] `generate --status frozen` when git is unavailable (no `.git`, or `git` not on PATH) fails on `git.available === false` | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.20] `generate --status frozen` without `--approved-by` or `--approval-ref` fails ("Refusing to generate a frozen baseline without owner-approval evidence") | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.21] `generate --status frozen --approved-by X --approval-ref Y` on a clean tree succeeds, and the manifest carries the `approval` block (`approved_by`, `approved_at`, `approval_ref`) | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.22] `generate --status draft` never requires approval, even without `--approved-by`/`--approval-ref` | covered-today: doc-baseline.test.ts (all draft generate calls omit approval)
- [baselines-change-requests.23] `generate --product <name>` overrides the derived package.json name | covered-today: doc-baseline.test.ts ("--product overrides the derived name")
- [baselines-change-requests.24] Product name derivation: package.json present with a usable `name` → title-cased | covered-today: doc-baseline.test.ts ("product is derived from...")
- [baselines-change-requests.25] Product name derivation: multi-word / scoped / snake_case package names title-case correctly (`my-cool-lib`, `@acme/formula-engine`, `pocket_ledger`) | covered-today: doc-baseline.test.ts ("product title-cases multi-word and scoped package names")
- [baselines-change-requests.26] Product name derivation: no package.json → neutral fallback `"Project"` | covered-today: doc-baseline.test.ts ("product falls back to a neutral name...")
- [baselines-change-requests.27] Product name derivation: package.json exists but has no `name` field → neutral fallback | covered-today: doc-baseline.test.ts ("product falls back to neutral when package.json has no usable name")
- [baselines-change-requests.28] Product name derivation: package.json is malformed JSON → `readPackageName` catches and returns null → neutral fallback | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.29] Product name derivation: package.json `name` is present but non-string (e.g. a number) → treated as absent → neutral fallback | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.30] `titleCase` with an empty/whitespace-only unscoped name (e.g. `"---"` or `"@scope/"`) falls back to the neutral fallback (words.length === 0 branch) | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.31] `generate` collects files matching `defaultInclude` (`.vivicy/canonical/**/*.md`) and excludes everything under `defaultExclude` (`_tmp/**`, `node_modules/**`, `dist/**`, `.DS_Store`, existing `.vivicy/baselines/*.json`, the tool script itself, `docs/change-requests/CR-####-*.md`, architecture-map viewer, `docs/governance/**`, `docs/reviews/**`, `docs/spikes/**`) | GAP (only the canonical include path is exercised in tests; the specific excludes are untested)
- [baselines-change-requests.32] `generate` with zero matching canonical files produces a manifest with an empty `files: []` array (boundary: 0 files) — verify this does not currently fail generate (only `assertManifestShape`'s "non-empty files[]" check applies to `verify`, not `generate`) | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.33] `generate` is idempotent-ish: running the identical generate command twice with unchanged docs produces the same `document_set_hash` and `manifest_hash` (differing only in `generated_at`) but each writes/overwrites the same output path | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.34] `walk()` recurses into nested subdirectories under `.vivicy/canonical/` and finds deeply-nested `.md` files | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.35] `walk()` skips a directory entirely when it matches an exclude `/**` pattern, without descending into it (e.g. a huge `node_modules` never touched) | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.36] `matchesPattern` boundary: a doc file literally named exactly as an exact-match pattern (no `*`) is matched via `addIfIncluded`'s non-glob branch | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.37] `matchesPattern` for `.vivicy/baselines/*.json` matches only direct children (single path segment), not nested `.vivicy/baselines/sub/x.json` | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.38] `matchesPattern` for `docs/change-requests/CR-[0-9][0-9][0-9][0-9]-*.md` matches `CR-0001-foo.md` but not `CR-1-foo.md` or `CR-0001.md` (no slug) | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.39] `generate` on freeze (`status: frozen`) calls `supersedePriorFrozenManifests`, which stamps every OTHER frozen, not-yet-superseded manifest (matching `knownGeneratedBy`) with a `superseded: { by_baseline_id, at }` marker and logs "Superseded ..." | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.40] `supersedePriorFrozenManifests` skips manifests that are already `superseded`, that are `draft` status, that are not valid JSON (parse failure silently skipped), or that don't match `knownGeneratedBy` | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.41] `supersedePriorFrozenManifests` is a no-op when `.vivicy/baselines/` does not exist yet | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.42] `generate` calls `pruneGitkeeps(repoRoot)` after writing the manifest (filesystem side-effect on the skeleton `.gitkeep` files) | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.43] `verify` with `--manifest` pointing to a non-existent file fails ("Manifest not found") | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.44] `verify` with a manifest missing/mismatched `schema_version` fails `assertManifestShape` | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.45] `verify` with a manifest whose `generated_by` is NOT in `knownGeneratedBy` (neither `.ts` nor legacy `.mjs`) fails | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.46] `verify` with a manifest whose `generated_by` IS the legacy `.vivicy/baselines/doc-baseline.mjs` value still passes (byte-unchanged pre-TS manifests keep verifying forever) | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.47] `verify` with an empty-string or missing `baseline_id` fails | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.48] `verify` with an invalid `version` (not MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH) fails `assertManifestShape` | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.49] `verify` with an invalid `status` (not in validStatuses) fails | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.50] `verify` with a manifest whose `files` array is empty fails ("must contain a non-empty files[] array") — boundary: 0 files at verify time | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.51] `verify` calls `assertBaselineIdFormat` on the manifest's own recorded `baseline_id`/`version`/`status` — a manifest whose `baseline_id` no longer matches `baseline-v<version>[-draft]` (e.g. hand-edited) fails even before the hash check | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.52] `verify` when the manifest is under `.vivicy/baselines/` but its filename does NOT equal `<baseline_id>.json` fails ("Manifest filename mismatch") | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.53] `verify` when the manifest is OUTSIDE `.vivicy/baselines/` (e.g. an `--allow-out-of-baseline-dir` output) skips the filename-match check entirely | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.54] `verify` on a manifest carrying a `superseded` marker: `effectiveStatus` becomes `"superseded"` regardless of the recorded `status`, and `--require-status frozen` on such a manifest fails with the superseded detail in the message | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.55] `verify` on a manifest with a malformed `superseded` marker (`by_baseline_id` not a string) fails ("superseded marker is malformed") | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.56] `verify --require-status <X>` mismatch (effective status differs) fails with the expected/got message | covered-today: doc-baseline.test.ts (indirectly, via tampering tests use default no --require-status; but direct mismatch case) — GAP for the mismatch path itself
- [baselines-change-requests.57] `verify --require-baseline-id <X>` mismatch fails | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.58] `verify --require-version <X>` mismatch fails | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.59] `verify --require-min-version <X>` boundary: exactly equal versions pass (`compareSemver` returns 0, not `< 0`) | GAP (only strictly-below and strictly-above tested)
- [baselines-change-requests.60] `verify --require-status frozen` on a manifest with `git.available !== true` or `git.working_tree_clean !== true` fails ("git evidence is insufficient") | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.61] `verify --require-status frozen` on a manifest missing any of `approval.approved_by`/`approved_at`/`approval_ref` (or blank-string values) fails ("owner-approval evidence is missing") | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.62] `verify` recomputes `manifest_hash` excluding `generated_at`, `git`, `approval`, `superseded` — changing any of THOSE does NOT trip the mismatch; changing a tracked field (incl. the `spec_kind`, which is inside the hashed set) DOES | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.63] `verify` reports "removed: <path>" for a manifest-listed file no longer present on disk | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.64] `verify` reports multiple simultaneous failures (changed + new + removed + document_set_hash mismatch) all in one failure list, not just the first | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.65] `verify` with a manifest whose `include[]` differs from the repo-owned `defaultInclude` fails `assertCorpusPolicy` ("include[] diverges") | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.66] `verify` with a manifest whose `exclude[]` contains an entry NOT in `defaultExclude` fails ("exclude[] contains entries outside the repo-owned corpus policy") | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.67] `verify` with a manifest whose `exclude[]` is a strict SUBSET of `defaultExclude` (legacy manifest missing a newer exclude entry) still passes — subset check, not equality | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.68] `verify` with a manifest whose `exclude` is not an array (`null`/string) fails (`unknownExcludes === null` branch) | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.69] `sameStringSet` treats duplicate entries and ordering as equivalent (dedups via Set, sorts before compare) | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.70] `stableJson`/`sortKeys` produce deterministic key-sorted JSON regardless of input key insertion order — same logical manifest content always serializes identically byte-for-byte | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.71] `parseArgs` fails on a bare non-flag argument (`doesnotstartwithdashdash`) ("Unexpected argument") | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.72] `parseArgs` fails when a flag's value is missing or itself looks like another flag (e.g. `--version --status draft`) ("Missing value for --version") | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.73] `readGitEvidence` scopes `git status --porcelain` to `-- .` at `cwd=repoRoot` so a dirty SURROUNDING repo (outside `VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT`) does not fail a target-scoped freeze | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.74] `VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT` unset falls back to the factory's own vendored project root (`resolve(scriptDir, "../..")`) — production default path | GAP (structural; hard to test without touching the real repo)
- [baselines-change-requests.75] Huge-corpus boundary: a doc tree with a very large number of canonical `.md` files (e.g. hundreds) still hashes/verifies correctly and within reasonable time | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.76] Malformed/binary content boundary: a canonical `.md` file containing non-UTF8 bytes still hashes via `sha256(bytes)` (byte-based, not text-based) without crashing | GAP

- [baselines-change-requests.254] `generate` stamps the mechanically detected `spec_kind` into the manifest — a bare target (no product code) yields `spec_kind: "project"` | covered-today: doc-baseline.test.ts ("generate stamps the mechanically detected spec_kind and it rides the manifest hash")
- [baselines-change-requests.255] `generate` stamps `spec_kind: "feature"` once product code (`src/main.go`) is present at the target root | covered-today: doc-baseline.test.ts ("generate stamps the mechanically detected spec_kind and it rides the manifest hash")
- [baselines-change-requests.256] `spec_kind` is part of `manifest_hash` (it sits in `manifestWithoutHash` fed to `computeManifestHash`) — flipping project→feature changes `manifest_hash`; the kind is contract, not metadata | covered-today: doc-baseline.test.ts ("generate stamps the mechanically detected spec_kind and it rides the manifest hash")
- [baselines-change-requests.257] `spec_kind` is OPTIONAL on verify — a freshly generated manifest carrying `spec_kind` round-trips `verify` with exit 0 (`assertManifestShape` deliberately not extended to require it) | covered-today: doc-baseline.test.ts ("a freshly generated manifest verifies with spec_kind present (shape stays legacy-tolerant)")
- [baselines-change-requests.258] absence tolerance, absent-field path: a manifest without `spec_kind` still passes `verify` AND its `manifest_hash` recomputes consistently (an absent field is absent on both sides) | GAP (the present test only proves the WITH-field round-trip)

### factory/doc-baseline.test.ts

- [baselines-change-requests.77] Test-file self-check: `runCli` correctly distinguishes a clean 0-exit from a `process.exit(1)` thrown error, surfacing `stdout`/`stderr`/`status` — implicit infra, not a product behavior; no separate case needed beyond what's covered above.

### lib/spec-kind.ts

- [baselines-change-requests.246] `detectSpecKind` on a git repo whose only tracked files are the scaffold surface (AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/README.md/vivicy.json/.gitignore + `.vivicy/**/.gitkeep`) returns `"project"` (no product code) | covered-today: spec-kind.test.ts ("a scaffolded-only repo is a PROJECT spec (no product code)")
- [baselines-change-requests.247] `detectSpecKind` git-ls-files witness: one tracked source file (`src/index.ts`) is code evidence → `"feature"` | covered-today: spec-kind.test.ts ("one tracked source file flips it to a FEATURE spec")
- [baselines-change-requests.248] `isCodeEvidence` excludes the `.vivicy/` governance surface — a tracked `.vivicy/canonical/01-product.md` never counts as code → `"project"` | covered-today: spec-kind.test.ts ("canonical docs under .vivicy never count as code")
- [baselines-change-requests.249] `gitTrackedFiles` is the witness of record: an UNTRACKED working-tree `scratch.ts` alongside a tracked README is not yet product code → `"project"` | covered-today: spec-kind.test.ts ("UNTRACKED code does not count (tracked files are the witness)")
- [baselines-change-requests.250] fs-fallback path (`gitTrackedFiles` null on a non-git dir → `fsHasCodeEvidence`), scaffold-only tree → `"project"` | covered-today: spec-kind.test.ts ("empty/scaffold-only dir is a PROJECT spec")
- [baselines-change-requests.251] fs-fallback with a real source file (`app/main.py`); the walk prunes `node_modules` and dot-dirs (`.cache`) so their contents never flip the kind → `"feature"` | covered-today: spec-kind.test.ts ("a source file makes it a FEATURE spec; node_modules and dot-dirs are ignored")
- [baselines-change-requests.252] `isCodeEvidence` scaffold/housekeeping exclusion completeness — a repo carrying only the remaining `SCAFFOLD_ROOT_FILES` (`license`/`license.md`/`license.txt`/`.gitattributes`) or a root-level `.gitkeep` outside `.vivicy` stays `"project"` | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.253] `isCodeEvidence` nested-placeholder exclusion — a tracked `<dir>/.gitkeep` OUTSIDE `.vivicy` (the `rel.endsWith("/.gitkeep")` branch) is an empty-dir marker, not code evidence | GAP
