- [baselines-change-requests.142] `rewriteFrontmatter` byte-preserves everything OUTSIDE the frontmatter block (the narrative body is untouched) | GAP

### factory/cr-apply.ts

- [baselines-change-requests.153] `applyChangeRequest` without `repoRoot` throws ("No target project configured... or pass repoRoot") | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.154] `applyChangeRequest` without `id` throws ("a CR id (e.g. CR-0001) is required") | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.155] Happy path: folds, freezes (patch bump), stamps docs_applied, spawns extraction, records phases | covered-today: cr-apply.test.ts ("folds the canonical, freezes a patch bump...")
- [baselines-change-requests.156] Commit runs BEFORE freeze (ordering invariant) | covered-today: cr-apply.test.ts ("commits the applied canonical edit BEFORE freezing...")
- [baselines-change-requests.157] Blocks honestly when the applied edit cannot be committed (phase "commit", CR stays accepted_current_build) | covered-today: cr-apply.test.ts ("blocks honestly when the applied edit cannot be committed")
- [baselines-change-requests.158] Applying a CR whose id does not exist blocks at phase "resolve" | covered-today: cr-apply.test.ts ("blocks on an unknown CR id")
- [baselines-change-requests.159] Applying an `idea` (not `accepted_current_build`) CR blocks at phase "resolve" without touching the CR or running any leg | covered-today: cr-apply.test.ts ("blocks on an idea CR without touching it")
- [baselines-change-requests.160] Applying a CR whose status is `docs_applied`/`rejected`/`accepted_future`/`implemented`/`superseded`/`under_review` ALSO blocks at "resolve" (only `accepted_current_build` proceeds) — only the `idea` case is directly tested; the other 6 non-eligible statuses are not | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.161] Reference-check red on BOTH attempts: bounded retry (exactly `DEFAULT_APPLY_ATTEMPTS`=2), second attempt carries feedback, terminal block at phase "verify", nothing downstream (freeze/extraction) runs, CR untouched | covered-today: cr-apply.test.ts ("leaves the CR accepted_current_build (NOT docs_applied) and reports blocked at verify")
- [baselines-change-requests.162] Reference-check red on FIRST attempt but GREEN on retry: recovers, proceeds to freeze — the "self-heals on retry" happy path is never exercised (only always-red and always-green are tested) | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.163] CR has an invalid/missing `previous_baseline_version` (not matching `\d+\.\d+\.\d+`) at the freeze step → blocks at phase "freeze" with the "no valid previous_baseline_version to bump from" message | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.164] `runFreeze` throws/rejects (e.g. doc-baseline shells out and fails) → caught and blocks at phase "freeze" with the thrown error's message folded into the summary | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.165] `stampChangeRequestApplied` throws (e.g. resulting fields incomplete, or stamped CR fails change-control) → caught and blocks at phase "stamp", with `baseline` included in the terminal report | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.166] Extraction spawn does not reach green → blocked at phase "extract" but CR IS already docs_applied (freeze/stamp already committed — partial progress is honest, not rolled back) | covered-today: cr-apply.test.ts ("stamps docs_applied (canonical is folded + re-frozen) but reports blocked at extract")
- [baselines-change-requests.167] Retires a failed spike named on `affected_verification_gates` to `deferred` BEFORE extraction spawns, commits the retirement separately, leaves an unnamed failed spike (bystander) untouched | covered-today: cr-apply.test.ts ("flips a failed spike named on affected_verification_gates to deferred BEFORE re-extraction...")
- [baselines-change-requests.168] A deferred spike does not count as unverified-blocking for the spike-verification gate's `transitivelyVerifiedGates`/blocking filter | covered-today: cr-apply.test.ts ("a deferred spike does NOT count as unverified-blocking...")
- [baselines-change-requests.169] A CR naming a gate whose spike is `verified` (not `failed`) leaves it untouched — no retire_spikes phase recorded | covered-today: cr-apply.test.ts ("leaves a spike alone when the CR names its gate but the spike is NOT failed...")
- [baselines-change-requests.170] A CR naming a gate that does NOT resolve to ANY spike at all (dangling gate reference) is a silent no-op (the `spikeByGate.get(gate)` undefined branch) — distinct from the "not failed" case, never directly tested | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.171] `retireAffectedSpikes` when `affected_verification_gates` is empty/absent returns `[]` immediately without reading spikes at all | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.172] `toGateList` normalizes a bare single-string gate value (not an array) to a one-element list | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.173] Retiring MULTIPLE named failed spikes in one CR retires all of them and reports all their gate_ids in `retired` | GAP

- [baselines-change-requests.175] `patchBump` boundary: bumping `1.0.9` -> `1.0.10` (double-digit patch, no rollover bug) and `1.0.0` -> `1.0.1` | covered-today: cr-apply.test.ts (only `1.0.0`->`1.0.1` asserted; the double-digit rollover not tested) — partial GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.176] `makeDefaultSpawnApplier` selects the codex leg vs claude leg branch based on `leg.provider` ("codex" -> `runCodexLeg`, else -> `runClaudeLeg`) — the REAL spawn path (not the fake) is never exercised in cr-apply.test.ts (tests always inject a fake spawnApplier) | GAP

- [baselines-change-requests.180] `defaultCommitApplied`: a commit with genuinely nothing to commit ("nothing to commit" in output) is tolerated as `{committed:true}`, not a failure | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.181] `defaultRunFreeze`: shells out to doc-baseline.ts with the correct args (`generate --version --status frozen --bump patch --previous-version --approved-by --approval-ref`); on non-zero exit throws with the combined stdout/stderr; on success but a MISSING manifest file at the expected path throws ("freeze reported success but ... is missing") | GAP (never exercised — always faked in tests)
- [baselines-change-requests.182] `defaultRunExtraction`: shells out to extract-issues.ts, reads back `.vivicy/development/reports/extraction-status.json` for `phase`/`summary`/`reopened`; when the status file is missing/malformed, falls back to inferring status from the child's exit code and last non-empty stdout/stderr line | GAP (real spawn path never exercised)
- [baselines-change-requests.183] `readExtractionStatus` returns null when the status file doesn't exist or fails to parse as JSON (both caught, not crashing) | GAP

- [baselines-change-requests.185] `defaultRecordReport` overwrites on each call (only the latest snapshot survives on disk, not an append log) — implied by the design, not directly asserted by writing twice and checking only the second snapshot remains | GAP

### factory/prompts/cr-applier.md

### CrReviewCards (components/chat/vivi-notifications.tsx — ported from the retired components/crs/cr-review-section.tsx into the panel's Notifications tab; now PRESENTATIONAL — `crs`/`onReload` arrive as props from the shared `useNotificationsFeed` hook, which owns the fetch/poll/SSE-refetch behavior formerly described below as the component's own `load()`; see pipeline-notifications-agents-ui's vivi-notifications.tsx section for that hook's cases)

- [baselines-change-requests.198] Renders a pending CR (status `idea`) as a Card with id/classification badges, the created_at date, the title, and Approve/Reject buttons under the section title | covered-today: vivi-panel.test.tsx ("a pending CR renders as a card; approving confirms, POSTs the decision, and shows the outcome inline")
- [baselines-change-requests.199] Renders nothing (returns null) when there is no pending CR and nothing was decided this mount | GAP (the panel's notifications-tab tests run with an empty CR list but never assert the section's absence directly)
- [baselines-change-requests.200] Approve requires confirm-dialog acceptance before POSTing (the confirmed click IS the owner decision, P2) | covered-today: vivi-panel.test.tsx (asserts no decide POST before the AlertDialog confirm)
- [baselines-change-requests.201] A CR with status `under_review` is ALSO shown as pending (PENDING_STATUSES includes both `idea` and `under_review`) — only `idea` is exercised in the test fixture | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.202] A CR with a DECIDED status (`accepted_current_build`, `docs_applied`, `accepted_future`, `rejected`, `implemented`, `superseded`) is filtered OUT of the pending list and not rendered | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.203] Multiple pending CRs render as multiple list items, and the badge count reflects `pending.length` (not `crs.length`) | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.204] The badge count updates correctly when some CRs are pending and others are decided (mixed list) | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.209] Reject flow, mirroring Approve: requires confirm dialog, POSTs `{id, decision:"rejected"}`, records the inline outcome from `rejectedToastTitle`/`body.summary` on success | GAP (only the approve flow is exercised end-to-end for the POST; reject's happy path is untested)
- [baselines-change-requests.210] Decide-request failure path: `res.ok === false` or `body.ok === false` records a FAILED inline outcome on the card (no toast): `approveBlockedToastTitle`/`decisionFailedToastTitle` plus the summary, or `errorText(tErrors, "control.<code>", fallback)` when only a code is present | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.211] Decide-request throws (network error during POST) is caught, recording a failed inline outcome with `decisionFailedToastTitle` plus the error's message or a generic `networkError` fallback | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.212] `deciding` state: while a decision is in flight for CR X, EVERY pending CR card's Approve/Reject buttons become `aria-disabled` (never native `disabled` — the AlertDialog returns focus to its trigger on close, and a natively-disabled trigger cannot receive it) — `disabled={deciding !== null || outcome !== undefined}` is NOT scoped per-row, so a DIFFERENT CR's buttons ALSO lock during CR X's decision (a global lock across pending cards despite appearances); only the spinner is per-row (`busy={deciding === cr.id}` lights up solely on the card actually deciding). The same aria-disabled gate also blocks reopening the confirm dialog while any decision is in flight or once this card already has an outcome (`onOpenChange` guards on `disabled`) | ALL pending cards lock during any ONE decision; only the busy spinner is per-row; dialog reopen guarded too | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.213] After a decide call completes (success or failure), `finally` always: clears `deciding` to null, calls the parent's `onReload` (the shared feed hook's refetch — CrReviewCards no longer owns its own list fetch), and calls `onDecided()` (the panel wires it to the notifications reload + `onActivity`, since the apply chain changed project state) — even when the decide POST failed | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.216] A CR with a very long `title` wraps (`break-words`) rather than overflowing the card — visual/layout, low-value but technically a rendered-state case | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.217] Approve uses `variant="default"` while Reject uses `variant="destructive"` (the owner-decision styling) | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.218] Canceling the confirm dialog (AlertDialogCancel) does NOT fire the decide POST | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.244] The decision outcome renders INLINE on the card as a `role="status"` Marker (ok → Check marker with the title + summary; blocked/failed → destructive CircleAlert marker) — announced to screen readers, not just painted — replacing the retired toasts | covered-today: vivi-panel.test.tsx (asserts the inline "CR-0001 approved — applied and re-extracted" marker; the `role="status"` attribute itself is unasserted)
- [baselines-change-requests.245] A CR decided in THIS mount stays visible with its inline outcome and permanently disabled buttons even after the post-decide list refresh drops it from `pending` (`shown = pending ∪ locally-decided`); the badge counts only the still-undecided cards | GAP

### Area cross-notes

- **doc-baseline <-> change-control**: `readFrozenBaselineIdentity` in change-control.ts independently re-implements "find the active frozen, non-superseded manifest" rather than calling into doc-baseline.ts — both must agree on the definition (frozen + no `superseded` marker). A drift test asserting these two independent implementations stay behaviorally identical (e.g. same manifest picked, same rejection on a superseded-only baselines dir) is a GAP: [baselines-change-requests.220] no cross-module consistency test exists between doc-baseline's freeze/supersede semantics and change-control's `readFrozenBaselineIdentity` reader.
- **doc-baseline <-> cr-apply**: cr-apply's `defaultRunFreeze` shells out to `doc-baseline.ts generate --bump patch`, which enforces `assertBumpClass` (exact patch delta) and the frozen-approval/clean-tree gates. cr-apply.test.ts always fakes `runFreeze`, so the REAL integration between cr-apply's freeze args and doc-baseline's CLI validation is never exercised end-to-end. [baselines-change-requests.221] GAP: an integration test spawning the real `doc-baseline.ts` from `cr-apply.ts`'s `defaultRunFreeze` (dirty tree, wrong bump, missing approval-by inputs) to confirm the two modules' contracts actually match at the CLI-arg boundary.
- **change-control <-> cr-apply**: `stampChangeRequestApplied` (change-control.ts) is the sole path advancing a CR to `docs_applied`, and cr-apply.ts calls it directly (not via CLI) — verified in cr-apply.test.ts's happy path (`runChangeControlCheck` passes after stamping). Good coverage here.
- **change-control <-> cr-apply (spike retirement)**: `retireAffectedSpikes` in cr-apply.ts reads `affected_verification_gates` from CR frontmatter parsed by change-control.ts's `parseFrontmatter`/`toList`. A gate value serialized as a single bare string (not `[a]`) by `serializeFmValue`/hand-authored CRs would need `toGateList`'s string-fallback branch in cr-apply.ts — this exact round-trip (create via change-control's array serialization -> read via cr-apply's list normalizer) is untested. [baselines-change-requests.222] GAP.
- **change-request.md / cr-applier.md <-> code**: Both prompt docs describe behavior (classification enum values, status lifecycle, "touch only canonical") that only partially maps to mechanically-enforced code paths. The "touch only canonical" constraint in particular has NO code-level enforcement — only a reference-check (broken link) would incidentally catch an out-of-bounds edit that also breaks a link; an out-of-bounds edit to an unrelated file (e.g. quietly rewriting an issue's title) would sail through completely undetected. This is the single most consequential gap in the area: [baselines-change-requests.223] GAP — no allowlist/diff-scope enforcement exists to confirm the CR-applier leg's edit touched only `.vivicy/canonical/**`, unlike (for comparison) the target-repo-write allowlists likely enforced elsewhere in the factory (dev-loop legs).
- **CrReviewCards (vivi-notifications.tsx) <-> app/api/control/crs routes**: The component's contract with `/api/control/crs` and `/api/control/crs/decide` is exercised only via mocked fetch in the panel test; the actual route handlers (`app/api/control/crs/route.ts`, `app/api/control/crs/decide/route.ts`) are OUT OF THIS AREA's file list and not reviewed here, but they are the real bridge to `change-control.ts`'s `decideChangeRequest`/`readChangeRequests`. [baselines-change-requests.224] GAP (out-of-area, flagged for the API/control-plane area): no end-to-end (e2e-ui or integration) test drives CrReviewCards against the REAL API routes and a REAL change-control registry on disk — the full stack is only unit-tested in two disconnected halves.

### Adversarial additions

- [baselines-change-requests.225] `change-control.ts`'s `repoRoot` is resolved at MODULE LOAD time via `resolveTargetRoot()` (factory/target-root.ts), not lazily per-call — `VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT` changed by a test AFTER the module is first imported has no effect on the module-level `repoRoot` constant used as the default for `runChangeControlCheck({})`/`readChangeRequests(undefined)` and the CLI entry's `repoRoot` check; every existing test avoids this by always passing an explicit `{ repoRoot }`, so the env-var-driven default path (`resolveTargetRoot()` returning null when unset, vs a stale value from a prior test's env mutation in the SAME process) is completely untested and could produce cross-test leakage in a single `node:test` worker. | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.226] `resolveTargetRoot(env)` with `VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT` set to whitespace-only (e.g. `"   "`) returns `null` (the `.trim().length > 0` guard) — same boundary as doc-baseline.ts's own targetOverride check, but never exercised for change-control's copy of the same logic (target-root.ts) nor for doc-baseline.ts's inline duplicate. | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.227] `matchesPattern`'s generic `/**` branch (used by `.vivicy/architecture-map/viewer/**`, `docs/governance/**`, `docs/reviews/**`, `docs/spikes/**`, `_tmp/**`, `node_modules/**`, `dist/**`) has a documented-by-code but never-asserted quirk: `path.endsWith(\`/${prefix}\`)` and `path.includes(\`/${prefix}/\`)` make a pattern like `dist/**` also match a path such as `nested/dist` (no trailing content) or `a/dist/b` even though `dist/**` intends only paths physically rooted under a top-level `dist/` directory — a same-named nested directory anywhere in the tree (e.g. `.vivicy/canonical/dist/notes.md`, or `vendor/node_modules/pkg/notes.md`) is silently excluded from the corpus with no test proving or disproving this over-broad matching. | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.228] `isExcludedDirectory`'s `path.endsWith(\`/${prefix}\`)` branch means a directory literally named e.g. `not_node_modules` is NOT excluded (correct, since it doesn't end with `/node_modules` as a full segment) — but a directory nested as `.vivicy/canonical/sub/node_modules` (a doc author accidentally naming a subfolder `node_modules`) IS excluded and walk() never descends into it, silently dropping any canonical `.md` files inside from the corpus with no error surfaced to `generate` or `verify` — a silent data-loss boundary with zero test coverage. | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.229] `collectIncludedFiles`'s non-glob include branch (`if (!pattern.includes("*"))`) is dead code for the CURRENT `defaultInclude` (`[".vivicy/canonical/**/*.md"]`, which always contains `*`) — this code path only activates if a future include pattern is a literal path; no test exists (and arguably none can exist without editing `defaultInclude`) confirming `addIfIncluded`'s exact-match branch actually fires end-to-end via `generate`, only via the narrower `matchesPattern` unit-level reasoning in case .36. | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.230] `verify`'s file-tamper detection loop only ever fires `failures.push` for `removed`/`changed`/`new included file` — a file whose PATH case changes on a case-insensitive filesystem (macOS default) but whose content is identical would produce two entries in `currentByPath`/`manifestByPath` keyed by literally different strings, surfacing as a spurious simultaneous "removed: X" + "new included file: x" pair; no test exercises case-sensitivity boundary behavior on the file path key. | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.231] `parseArgs` boundary: a flag repeated twice (e.g. `--version 1.0.0 --version 2.0.0`) silently lets the LAST occurrence win (object key overwritten), with no "duplicate flag" detection or error — untested silent-override behavior distinct from the missing-value case in .72. | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.232] `assertBumpClass`'s `previousVersion` parameter is validated via `assertVersion` (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH), but `patchBump`/`assertBumpClass`'s destructured `[M, m, p] = previousVersion.split(".").map(Number)` boundary at `p = 9007199254740991` (Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER) or a previous version with leading zeros (e.g. `"1.0.01"`) — the regex `\d+` allows leading zeros, so `"1.0.01"` passes assertVersion but `Number("01")` silently becomes `1`, potentially producing an expected-bump string that doesn't match the manifest's own recorded string form; untested. | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.233] `readGitEvidence`'s `git(["rev-parse", "HEAD"])` failing (e.g. a freshly-initialized repo with zero commits — `HEAD` doesn't resolve) while `git status --porcelain` still succeeds produces `available: false` (since `headSha.ok` is false) even though the working tree could otherwise be clean — the "empty repo, no commits yet" boundary for a first-ever freeze attempt is untested and would always refuse freezing with a somewhat misleading "git must be available" message rather than a more specific "no commits yet" one. | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.234] `supersedePriorFrozenManifests` iterates `readdirSync(baselineDirAbsolute, { withFileTypes: true })` in filesystem/OS-dependent (not sorted) order — when TWO OTHER prior frozen, non-superseded manifests exist (a data-integrity anomaly that should never happen given "at most one active frozen baseline" but isn't itself enforced anywhere), BOTH get stamped superseded in whatever order readdir yields, not a single deterministic one; no test constructs this multi-prior-frozen scenario to confirm the loop truly supersedes all of them (not just the first) as the comment intends. | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.235] `stableJson`/`sortKeys` recursion has no cycle/depth guard — a manifest is always a plain JSON-round-tripped object so a circular reference can't occur in practice, but `sortKeys` is also reused nowhere else defensively; not a real GAP to test (flagging as considered, not a miss) — no case needed.
- [baselines-change-requests.236] `change-control.ts`'s `CR_FILENAME` regex (`^CR-(\d{4})-[a-z0-9-]+\.md$`) requires the slug to be lowercase/digits/hyphens only — a hand-authored CR file with an UPPERCASE letter in its slug (e.g. `CR-0001-Fix-Bug.md`) fails the filename match silently: `readChangeRequests`'s `file.match(CR_FILENAME)` returns null, so `fileNumber` becomes `null`, and the CR is then flagged via `cr_id_filename_match` (fileNumber===null branch) rather than a dedicated "malformed filename slug" message — untested distinction between "wrong number" and "unparseable slug" causes of the same error. | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.237] `parseFrontmatter`'s line regex `^([a-z_]+):\s*(.*)$` only matches lowercase-plus-underscore keys — a frontmatter line with a key containing a digit or hyphen (e.g. `resulting-baseline-id:` or `field2:`) is silently DROPPED from the parsed object (the `if (!km) continue` branch), not merely mis-typed — meaning a subtly malformed CR (typo'd key name) fails open (missing field => "missing" in the checker) rather than failing loud; untested "unparseable frontmatter line is silently dropped, not reported" behavior. | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.238] `renderNewChangeRequest`'s frontmatter field ordering is a HARD-CODED literal list — `rewriteFrontmatter`'s "keys not yet in the block are appended just before closing ---" branch (case .140) is only reachable in production if `decideChangeRequest`/`stampChangeRequestApplied`'s `patch` object ever contains a key absent from the writer's template; today every `patch` key (`status`, `updated_at`, `owner_decision*`, `previous_baseline_*`, `resulting_*`) IS already present in the template, so the "append new key" branch is CURRENTLY DEAD CODE in the real call graph and only reachable via a direct unit test constructing a hand-written CR missing a key — worth flagging that no test proves this actually-reachable-only-via-drift branch still works today. | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.239] `readChangeRequests`'s directory-listing sort (`readdirSync(dirAbs).sort()`) is a plain lexicographic string sort — `CR-0010-x.md` sorts BEFORE `CR-0002-x.md` is false (both are zero-padded to 4 digits so lexicographic == numeric here), but a NON-zero-padded or hand-renamed file (e.g. a stray `CR-10-x.md` that fails `CR_FILENAME` anyway) would sort out of numeric order — low-value edge, but the ASSUMPTION that lexicographic sort equals numeric sort for the id scheme is never explicitly asserted by a test with CR-0001/CR-0002/.../CR-0010+ (10 or more CRs), only ever tested with 1-3 CRs. | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.240] `cr-apply.ts`'s `retireAffectedSpikes` calls `flipSpikeStatus` (factory/spike-prover.ts, OUT of this area's reviewed file list but load-bearing) which THROWS if the target spike file has no `## Traceability` heading or no `status:` line inside it — `retireAffectedSpikes` does NOT catch this; an exception here would propagate up through `applyChangeRequest`'s synchronous call (not awaited, but `flipSpikeStatus` is sync and un-try/catch'd) as an UNHANDLED throw that aborts the whole `applyChangeRequest` promise with a raw error instead of a `blocked` terminal report — meaning a malformed spike file (missing Traceability block) crashes the apply chain ungracefully rather than producing an honest `phase: "retire_spikes"` block; completely untested. | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.241] `defaultRunFreeze`/`defaultRunExtraction`/`defaultCommitApplied` all shell out via `spawnSync`/`spawnSync("node", ...)` with NO timeout configured — a hung child process (e.g. extract-issues.ts or doc-baseline.ts stuck waiting on stdin, or an infinite loop) blocks `applyChangeRequest` forever with no kill/timeout safety net; no test exercises a timeout/kill-on-hang scenario for any of the three real subprocess seams (all always faked in cr-apply.test.ts). | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.242] `CrReviewCards`' `decide()` has no guard against a DOUBLE-CLICK / re-entrant call while `deciding === id` is already truthy — the `AlertDialogAction onClick={onConfirm}` is not itself disabled while busy (the trigger Button disables on `deciding !== null || outcome`, but the trigger is already closed once the dialog is open), so a user could in principle trigger a second decide POST for the same CR by re-opening/re-confirming rapidly before `finally` clears `deciding`; no test asserts single-flight/idempotent-decide behavior under rapid double-confirm. | GAP
- [baselines-change-requests.243] `CrReviewCards`' i18n calls (`t("approveBlockedToastTitle", {id})`, `errorText(tErrors, \`control.${body.code}\`, fallback)`, etc.) assume every referenced translation key exists in the `crs`/`errors` message catalogs for every supported locale — no test (in this area) verifies the catalog actually DEFINES `sectionTitle`, `via`, `approve`, `reject`, `approveDialogTitle`, `approveDialogDescription`, `rejectDialogTitle`, `rejectDialogDescription`, `cancel`, `approvedToastTitle`, `rejectedToastTitle`, `approveBlockedToastTitle`, `decisionFailedToastTitle`, `networkError` for EVERY locale the app ships (a missing key in one non-English locale would render next-intl's fallback/error behavior, not a translated string) — a catalog-completeness test for this component's specific key set is a GAP. | GAP

## Area: cli-supervisor-process-infra

Scope: factory/cli.ts, factory/cli.test.ts, factory/dev-status.ts, factory/dev-preflight.ts, factory/progress-ledger.ts, factory/progress-ledger.test.ts, factory/reopen.ts, factory/reopen.test.ts, factory/atomic-write.ts, factory/atomic-write.test.ts, factory/target-root.ts, factory/test-target-root.ts, factory/sleep-sync.ts, factory/project-config.ts, factory/project-config.test.ts, factory/prompts.test.ts, lib/spawner.ts, lib/node-spawner.ts, lib/runtime-dir.ts, lib/runtime-dir.test.ts, lib/run-status.ts, lib/run-status.test.ts.

Every file below was read in full, line by line.

### factory/cli.ts

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.1] `--help` prints HELP text and exits 0 | exit 0, stdout lists every verb | e2e-process | factory/cli.test.ts ("--help exits 0 and lists verbs")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.2] `-h` alias behaves identically to `--help` | exit 0, HELP printed | e2e-process | GAP (only `--help` is asserted, not `-h`)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.3] no argv at all | prints HELP, exit 2 (usage, since argv.length===0) | e2e-process | factory/cli.test.ts ("no args exits 2 (usage) and prints help")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.4] unknown verb (e.g. `frobnicate`) | stderr "Unknown command", HELP on stdout, exit 2 | e2e-process | factory/cli.test.ts ("unknown verb exits 2")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.5] `--runtime-dir` hidden flag is stripped from argv before verb dispatch and threaded through every lock-aware verb | lock/log land under the given dir, not the default | integration | factory/cli.test.ts (implicit via every `--runtime-dir` usage)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.6] `takeFlag` when the flag's next token itself starts with `--` (bare flag, no value) | returns null, only removes the flag token (not the following `--xxx`) | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.7] `takeFlag` when flag is the last argv token (no following value at all) | returns null, splices 1 | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.8] `takeBool` toggles presence/absence of a bare flag | true when present (and removed from argv), false otherwise | unit | GAP (exercised transitively via --json/--help but no direct unit test)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.9] `emitJson` writes exactly one pretty JSON object + trailing newline to stdout | stdout is valid JSON, single object | unit | factory/cli.test.ts (implicit, every `--json` test parses `r.out`)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.10] `note()` always writes to stderr, never stdout | stdout stays clean for --json parsing | integration | factory/cli.test.ts ("stdout is JSON only" assertion in status test)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.11] `fail()` in `--json` mode emits `{ok:false,error,...extra}` on stdout and calls process.exit(code) | correct JSON shape + exit code | unit | factory/cli.test.ts (various missing_target / usage tests)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.12] `fail()` in non-JSON mode writes `vivicy: <message>` to stderr only | no stdout output | integration | GAP (no test asserts human-mode fail() path)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.13] `runtimeDir()` precedence: opts.runtimeDir > VIVICY_RUNTIME_DIR > default `<appDir>/.vivicy-runtime` | correct dir chosen | unit | GAP (only opts.runtimeDir path is exercised by tests; env-var-only and default-only branches are GAP)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.14] `readRunState` when run-state.json is missing | returns null | unit | factory/cli.test.ts ("stop with no recorded run" implies null)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.15] `readRunState` when run-state.json contains malformed JSON | returns null (catch swallows parse error) | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.16] `isAlive(pid)` with a non-number pid | returns false | unit | GAP

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.18] `isAlive(pid)` with a pid that does not exist | returns false (ESRCH caught) | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.19] `isRunActive` when lock exists and pid is alive | returns true, does not delete the lock | integration | factory/cli.test.ts (second `start` sees "already_running")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.20] `isRunActive(opts, target)` when the per-project lock exists but the pid is dead (stale lock) | deletes `projects/<key>/run-state.json` and returns false | integration | GAP (no test kills the tracked pid out-of-band and re-probes)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.21] `isRunActive` when no lock file exists | returns false | unit | factory/cli.test.ts ("stop with no recorded run")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.22] `resolveTarget`: `--dir` flag wins | target resolved from --dir | integration | factory/cli.test.ts (all `--dir` tests)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.23] `resolveTarget`: `--target` flag used when `--dir` absent | target resolved from --target | unit | GAP (only `--dir` is exercised in cli.test.ts; `app`/`loop`/`supervise` use `--target` but for a different purpose — passthrough, not resolveTarget)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.24] `resolveTarget`: falls back to `VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT` env when no flag given | target resolved from env | unit | GAP (tests always pass `--dir`; the pure-env path for the JSON verbs is untested directly, though blanking `VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT=""` is tested for the negative case)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.25] `resolveTarget`: no flag and no env | returns null -> caller emits missing_target usage error | integration | factory/cli.test.ts ("missing target is a usage error")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.26] `resolveTarget`: flag value is whitespace-only (`--dir "  "`) | falls through to env/null (per `.trim().length>0` check) | unit | GAP

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.28] `scriptPath(name)` when the script is missing (bad VIVICY_FACTORY_ROOT or packaging bug) | throws Error with vivicyCode=EXIT_UNEXPECTED(3) | unit | factory/cli.test.ts ("degrades to dev:null... when the dev-status sub-probe is unavailable" exercises this indirectly for `status`, catching internally) — direct top-level throw-to-exit-3 path is GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.29] `childEnv(target)` sets VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT on top of inherited env | child sees correct env var | integration | factory/cli.test.ts (all spawning-verb tests read env in the stub)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.30] `runScript` streams child stdout+stderr to our stderr AND collects both for parsing | stderr shows child output; ScriptResult.stdout/stderr populated | integration | factory/cli.test.ts (extract/skills-install stub tests read stdout)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.31] `runScript` when spawn itself errors (e.g. ENOENT command) | resolves `{code:null, stdout, stderr, error}` rather than throwing | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.32] `readJsonFile` on a missing/unparsable file | returns fallback (default null) | unit | factory/cli.test.ts ("skills --json with no report is an honest null")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.33] `parseJsonLine` finds the first `{...}` line among noisy multi-line text | returns parsed object | unit | factory/cli.test.ts (cr approve/reject tests via change-control decide stdout/stderr)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.34] `parseJsonLine` when no line starts with `{` | returns null | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.35] `parseJsonLine` when a `{`-prefixed line is malformed JSON | keeps scanning subsequent lines rather than throwing | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.36] `parseJsonBlock` slices from first `{` to last `}` tolerating surrounding noise | correct object parsed | unit | factory/cli.test.ts (status merges dev-status.ts stdout)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.37] `parseJsonBlock` with no `{` or malformed content between braces | returns null | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.38] `parseFrontmatter` on a CR file with well-formed `---\n...\n---` block | returns key/value map, quotes stripped | unit | factory/cli.test.ts ("crs --json lists well-formed CRs")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.39] `parseFrontmatter` on text with no frontmatter delimiters | returns `{}` | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.40] `passthrough()` for `app`/`loop`/`supervise`/`rehearsal`: child inherits stdio, and on child exit with a signal, re-kills self with that signal; on plain exit, `process.exit(code ?? 0)` | correct propagation of signal vs exit code | e2e-process | GAP (no test spawns the passthrough verbs — they launch next dev / real scripts)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.41] `main()` dispatch: every JSON verb (status/extract/start/resume/stop/crs/cr/skills/retry-stage/notifications) routes to its handler | correct handler invoked | integration | factory/cli.test.ts (per-verb suites)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.42] top-level `main().catch()`: an unmodeled thrown Error with no `.vivicyCode` | stderr `vivicy: <message>`, exit 3 (EXIT_UNEXPECTED) | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.43] top-level `main().catch()`: a thrown VivicyError carrying `.vivicyCode` (e.g. from `scriptPath`) | exits with that specific code, not the generic 3 | unit | GAP

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.380] `stop` WITHOUT a target is refused because the run lock is per-project | exit 2, `missing_target` ("the run lock is per project") | e2e-process | cli.test.ts ("stop without a target is a usage error (the lock is per project)")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.381] `stop` with a target but no recorded run for that project | exit 1, `not_running` | e2e-process | cli.test.ts ("stop with no recorded run is an actionable refusal (exit 1, not_running)")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.382] `childEnv(target, opts)` threads `VIVICY_RUNTIME_DIR = projectDir(opts, target)` so spawned factory scripts notify into the project's log | spawn env `VIVICY_RUNTIME_DIR` equals the per-project namespace, not the runtime root | integration | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.383] `cmdNotifications` with `--dir` reads the legacy root log FIRST then the project log, concatenated, read-only (never migrates) | rows from `[<runtime>/notifications.jsonl, <runtime>/projects/<key>/notifications.jsonl]` in that order; no file moved | integration | GAP (existing notifications tests pass no `--dir`)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.384] `loop`/`supervise` passthrough sets `VIVICY_RUNTIME_DIR = projectDir(opts, resolve(target))` when `--target` is given | env carries the per-project runtime dir | integration | GAP

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.48] status when a run-state lock IS present and its pid is alive | run block populated {pid, alive:true, mode, started_at, target_root}, run_active true | integration | GAP (start/stop test checks the lock file directly but no `status` call is made while a run is active)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.49] status when a run-state lock is present but pid is dead (stale) | run block still reflects lock content but alive:false; run_active false (lock cleared as a side effect of isRunActive) | integration | GAP

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.53] status blocked-extraction fixture (`extraction_blocked`) | status verb itself still exits 0 (it only REPORTS the phase) | integration | factory/cli.test.ts ("blocked extraction fixture surfaces the phase")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.54] status surfaces `dev.quota` only when devStatus.quota is truthy (conditional spread) | quota key omitted vs present correctly | unit | GAP

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.57] `readLatestCrApply`: a report file with unparsable `updated_at` (Date.parse NaN -> 0) is still considered but loses ties to any dated report | correct fallback ordering | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.58] `readLatestCrApply`: file name not matching `apply-CR-\d{4}\.json` (e.g. extra suffix) is skipped | ignored | unit | GAP

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.60] `readCrSummaries` skips `cr-template.md` and `readme.md` (case-insensitively) and any non-`.md` file | excluded from list | unit | factory/cli.test.ts ("lists well-formed CRs, skipping template + readme")

### verbs: start / resume / stop

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.72] start with missing target | exit 2 missing_target | unit | GAP (only tested for status verb pattern; start's own missing-target path isn't directly asserted, though the code path is identical)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.73] start with non-existent `--dir` path | exit 2 "target root does not exist" | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.74] start writes the run-state lock (schema pid/started_at/target_root/factory_root/log_file/mode) under `<runtimeDir>/projects/<key>/run-state.json` before spawning (TOCTOU-safe wx claim) | lock file matches the byte-compatible schema at the per-project path | integration | factory/cli.test.ts ("start writes the run-state lock in the runtime dir; a second start refuses; stop clears it")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.75] a second `start` while a run is active | exit 1 "already_running", existing run summary in extra.run | integration | factory/cli.test.ts (same test, "second start")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.76] a lost race on the `wx` exclusive claim (EEXIST) between the isRunActive check and the write | exit 1 already_running (no crash) | unit | GAP (this is the TOCTOU race itself — not concurrently exercised)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.77] `writeFileSync(..., {flag:"wx"})` throws a non-EEXIST error (e.g. EACCES on the unwritable per-project runtime namespace) | error re-thrown (falls to main().catch(), exit 3) | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.78] spawn of the supervisor script itself throws synchronously | lock is released (rmSync) and exit 3 "spawn_failed" | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.79] spawned child has no numeric pid (`typeof child.pid !== "number"`) | lock released, exit 3 "spawn_failed" | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.80] successful start: log file opened in append mode (`openSync(logFile,"a")`) for both stdout+stderr, matching resume-accumulates-log intent | log content persists across a stop+resume start | integration | GAP (no test verifies the "a" append behavior across two starts)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.81] start's child is `detached:true` + `.unref()`'d so CLI exits without keeping the run alive | CLI process itself terminates promptly | e2e-process | factory/cli.test.ts (implicit — the CLI test process exits and asserts the lock)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.82] `resume` mode records `mode:"resume"` in the lock instead of `"start"` | lock.mode === "resume" | integration | GAP (only `mode:"start"` is asserted in the existing lifecycle test)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.83] `stop` with a recorded run | killGroup called (negative pid first, falls back to single pid), lock removed, exit 0, stopped.pid reported | e2e-process | factory/cli.test.ts ("start writes the run-state lock... stop clears it")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.84] `stop` with no recorded run at all | exit 1, code not_running | unit | factory/cli.test.ts ("stop with no recorded run is an actionable refusal")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.85] `killGroup`: `process.kill(-pid,...)` throws (e.g. no such group) | falls back to `process.kill(pid,...)`; if that also throws, returns false without throwing | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.86] `stop` when the recorded pid is already dead | killGroup returns false internally but stop still clears the lock and reports exit 0 (stop doesn't check the return value) | unit | GAP

### verb: crs / cr approve|reject

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.87] `crs --json` with a populated, well-formed registry | full CrSummary array, exit 0 | integration | factory/cli.test.ts ("lists well-formed CRs...")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.88] `crs --json` with an empty change-requests dir | `{ok:true, crs:[]}`, exit 0 | unit | factory/cli.test.ts ("empty registry returns an empty list")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.89] `crs` missing target | exit 2 missing_target | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.90] `crs` non-JSON mode with entries | human-readable padded lines per CR to stderr | e2e-process | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.91] `crs` non-JSON mode with no entries | "(no change requests)" note | e2e-process | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.92] `cr` with a decision word other than approve/reject (typo) | exit 2 usage | unit | GAP (only a malformed ID is tested for usage-2, not a malformed decision word)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.93] `cr approve <malformed-id>` (fails `^CR-\d{4}$`) | exit 2 usage | unit | factory/cli.test.ts ("a malformed id is a usage error")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.94] `cr approve <id>` without `--by` | exit 2 usage | unit | factory/cli.test.ts ("missing --by is a usage error")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.95] `cr approve <id> --by ""` (blank actor) | exit 2 usage (trim check) | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.96] `cr approve` with missing target | exit 2 missing_target | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.97] `cr approve` with target path that does not exist | exit 2 "target root does not exist" | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.98] `cr approve <unknown-id>` against the REAL change-control.ts decide (no CR file exists) | exit 1, code unknown_cr | integration | factory/cli.test.ts ("unknown id is an actionable refusal")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.99] `cr` decide script exits with code 2 (child usage error) | CLI maps to EXIT_USAGE (2), not EXIT_REFUSAL | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.100] `cr` decide script fails for a reason OTHER than unknown/undecidable (classifyDecisionCode falls to "decision_failed") | code:"decision_failed" surfaced | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.101] `cr reject <id> --by <actor>` on a decidable CR | decision recorded, NO apply chain run, exit 0, summary "CR <id> rejected" | integration | GAP (only approve-with-unknown-id is exercised; a full reject-happy-path against a real or stub CR is untested)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.102] `cr approve <id>` decide succeeds, then cr-apply.ts (APPLY leg) also succeeds (status green) | ok true, applied.ok true, exit 0 | e2e-process | GAP (no stub factory test drives the full approve+apply happy path)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.103] `cr approve <id>` decide succeeds but cr-apply reports `status:"blocked"` | ok false, blocked true, exit 1 | e2e-process | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.104] `cr approve <id>` decide succeeds, apply script exits non-zero AND writes no report file at all | applyStatus falls back to "blocked" (since applyRes.code!==0), summary falls back to lastLine(stdout) or literal default | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.105] `classifyDecisionCode`: message matches /no CR with id/i | returns "unknown_cr" | unit | factory/cli.test.ts (implicit, via the unknown-id integration test)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.106] `classifyDecisionCode`: message matches /can be decided\|no frozen baseline/i | returns "cr_not_decidable" | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.107] `classifyDecisionCode`: message matches neither pattern | returns "decision_failed" | unit | GAP

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.113] `skills` non-JSON mode with no report | "(no skills report — no install has run yet)" | e2e-process | GAP

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.117] `skills install` phase "failed" | ok false, exit 1 | unit | factory/cli.test.ts ("failed install is a refusal")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.118] `skills install` missing target | exit 2 missing_target | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.119] `skills install` when the report is missing after the run entirely (script crashed before writing) | phase falls back to "error", ok false, summary falls back to lastLine(stdout) or default string | unit | GAP

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.121] `cmdSkills` dispatch: first arg "install" routes to cmdSkillsInstall (shifted off), anything else (including none) routes to cmdSkillsReport | correct routing | unit | factory/cli.test.ts (implicit via both suites)

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.367] `vivicy skills remove <ids>` dispatches `cmdSkillsRemove`, runs install-skills.ts `--remove`, mirrors the final report; ok iff exit 0 AND phase green | ok true, report echoed | e2e-process | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.368] `skills remove` with no target | exit 2, `missing_target` | e2e-process | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.369] `skills remove` when the target root does not exist | exit 2, `missing_target` | e2e-process | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.370] `skills remove` with no ids | exit 2, `missing_ids` | e2e-process | GAP

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.372] `claimCliSkillsLock` reclaims a stale lock (pid dead) and proceeds; the release fn removes the lock file after the run | run proceeds, lock cleared | e2e-process | GAP

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.388] `cycle open` happy path | exit 0, writes `spec-cycle.json`, `cycle.opened_by === "owner:cli"`, status "drafting" | e2e-process | cli.test.ts ("open → status → start refused → cancel round-trip")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.389] `cycle open` refuses a second open | exit 1, `cycle_state` | e2e-process | cli.test.ts ("open → status → start refused → cancel round-trip")

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.391] `cycle status` prints/returns the open cycle | exit 0, `cycle.id` matches the opened id | e2e-process | cli.test.ts ("open → status → start refused → cancel round-trip")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.392] `cycle status` (and bare `cycle`) with no open cycle | exit 0, `cycle:null` / "no spec cycle open" | e2e-process | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.393] `start` guard refuses while a cycle is open | exit 1, `cycle_state` (EXIT_REFUSAL) | e2e-process | cli.test.ts ("open → status → start refused → cancel round-trip")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.394] `cycle cancel` legal while undrifted (stub doc-baseline verify exits 0) | exit 0, `cancelled === id`, state file gone | e2e-process | cli.test.ts ("open → status → start refused → cancel round-trip")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.395] `cycle cancel` refused when the canonical drifted (stub verify exits 1) | exit 1, `cycle_state`, state file stays | e2e-process | cli.test.ts ("cancel refuses when the canonical has drifted (verifier red)")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.396] `cycle cancel` when no cycle is open | exit 1, `cycle_state` (/no drafting spec cycle/) | e2e-process | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.397] `cycle cancel` when `activeFrozenManifestRel` is null (no manifest) | skips verify, clears the cycle | e2e-process | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.398] `cycle <unknown>` action | exit EXIT_USAGE, "unknown cycle action … (expected open|cancel|status)" | e2e-process | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.399] `cycle` with no resolvable target (no `--dir`/`VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT`) | exit EXIT_USAGE, `missing_target` | e2e-process | GAP

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.133] `vivicy app` with no `--target`: spawns `npx next dev` (+ `--port` if given) with cwd=appDir, empty env override | Next dev server launched from package root | e2e-process | GAP (never exercised in cli.test.ts — would start a real Next server)

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.137] `vivicy supervise [--target <d>]`: passthrough to `dev-loop-supervised.ts` | script + env correct | e2e-process | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.138] `vivicy rehearsal [--dry]`: passthrough to `dev-rehearsal.ts`, no env override | forwarded args intact | e2e-process | GAP

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.140] passthrough child is killed by a signal | our own process re-sends itself that signal (`process.kill(process.pid, signal)`) instead of calling exit | e2e-process | GAP

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.351] `removeSkills` uninstalls an installed skill; the report (mode `"remove"`, `removed[]`), `vivicy.json` requiredSkills, and the AGENTS.md block all shrink together | phase green, removed=[{id}], remover called once | unit | install-skills.test.ts ("removes an installed skill: report, vivicy.json, and AGENTS.md all shrink together")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.352] `removeSkills` accepts a `https://skills.sh/owner/repo/skill` URL (`normalizeSkillId`) and frees a cap slot | removed=[{owner/repo@skill}], requiredSkills reduced | unit | install-skills.test.ts ("accepts a skills.sh URL and frees a cap slot")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.353] `removeSkills` refuses an id absent from the installed set with reason `not_installed`; never passed to the remover | rejected reason not_installed, remover call count 0 | unit | install-skills.test.ts ("refuses a not-installed id and an invalid id with machine reasons (never silent)")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.354] `removeSkills` refuses an unparseable id with reason `invalid_id` | rejected reason invalid_id, nothing shrank | unit | install-skills.test.ts ("refuses a not-installed id and an invalid id with machine reasons (never silent)")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.355] `removeSkills` records `remove_failed` when the remover exits non-zero and that skill keeps its requiredSkills slot | rejected [{id, remove_failed}], requiredSkills still lists the failed id | unit | install-skills.test.ts ("records a remove_failed rejection and leaves the state intact for that skill")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.356] removing the LAST skill renders the empty-set AGENTS.md block text `_No project skills are currently installed._` (`buildSkillsBlock` empty-entries branch) | AGENTS.md contains "No project skills are currently installed" | unit | install-skills.test.ts ("renders the empty-set AGENTS.md block when the last skill is removed")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.357] `removeSkills` throws `SkillsConfigError` when no target (repoRoot) is configured | throws SkillsConfigError | unit | install-skills.test.ts ("throws SkillsConfigError without a target or without ids")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.358] `removeSkills` throws `SkillsConfigError` when ids is empty/blank | throws SkillsConfigError | unit | install-skills.test.ts ("throws SkillsConfigError without a target or without ids")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.359] `removeSkills` dedupes a repeated id within one run (`toDrop.has` guard) — the remover fires once, one removed entry | single removed entry, no double-uninstall | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.360] `removeSkills` treats an id present only in the prior report's `installed` (not in vivicy.json requiredSkills) as removable (installedSkillIds union defence) | not_installed NOT raised; removed | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.361] `dropRequiredSkills` is a no-op returning [] when vivicy.json is absent or unparseable (the owner file is never clobbered) | vivicy.json untouched | boundary | GAP

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.363] `defaultRunRemove` returns code 1 (remove_failed) when the CLI refuses and `.agents/skills/<skill>` does not exist | code 1 with actionable output | boundary | GAP

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.365] install-skills.ts CLI entry parses `--remove <ids>` / `--remove=<ids>` and dispatches `removeSkills` instead of `installSkills` | remove run executed | e2e-process | GAP

### factory/dev-status.ts

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.142] no issues at all (`issues:[]`), no done files, no processes | verdict "NOT STARTED", issues_total 0, issues_done 0, doneIds [] | unit | GAP (no dedicated test file for dev-status.ts exists in this area listing)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.143] all issues have a `done/<id>.md` file | verdict "DONE" | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.144] an issue is "done" via ledger verification across ALL its graph_refs (not a done/ file) | issueDone() true via verifiedIssuesByRef path | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.145] an issue has SOME but not all graph_refs verified | issueDone() false (require every ref) | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.146] an issue has zero graph_refs and no done file | issueDone() false (refs.length>0 guard fails) | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.147] a `dev-(loop|rehearsal).ts` process is alive per `ps` output | processAlive true, verdict RUNNING (when not stale) | e2e-process | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.148] the loopLine regex explicitly excludes any line matching `dev-status.ts` (so dev-status doesn't see itself as "the loop") | processAlive false when only dev-status.ts itself is running | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.149] process alive AND idleSeconds > STALE_IDLE_SECONDS(600) | verdict "STALE?" | integration | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.150] process alive and idle <= 600s | verdict "RUNNING" | integration | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.151] process not alive, blockedGates.length>0, doneIds<issues.length | verdict "STOPPED (last gate failed)" | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.152] process not alive, doneIds>0, no blocked gates (or all done) | verdict "STOPPED (resume to continue)" | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.153] `ps` call itself throws (execFileSync failure, e.g. sandboxed env with no `ps`) | psOut="" caught, processAlive false rather than crashing | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.154] `codexLegs` count: counts only `ps` lines containing "codex exec" AND the target root string | correct count, 0 when none match | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.155] `newestMtimeMs` on a path that does not exist | returns 0 (catch) rather than throwing | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.156] `newestMtimeMs` recurses into subdirectories to find the max mtime | correct max across nested files | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.157] quota-state.json present with a `throttled` agent + reset_at | formatQuotaLine renders "name model: throttled, resets <ts> (5h X% · wk Y%)" | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.158] quota window has no `used_pct` (provider exposes nothing) | formatWindow renders "5h —" (never fabricates a %) | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.159] quota-state.json absent/unreadable | quota = {updated_at:null, agents:{}}, formatQuotaLine renders "(unknown)" | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.160] quota field present but not an object with `.agents` (malformed) | status.quota falls back to `{updated_at:null, agents:{}}` per the ternary guard | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.161] `--json` flag: prints the full `status` object as pretty JSON, exit 0 always | machine-readable full dump | integration | factory/cli.test.ts exercises this indirectly (as the `dev` sub-block read by the CLI's `status` verb), but no direct test targets dev-status.ts's own CLI entrypoint/exit code
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.162] non-`--json` (human) mode: multi-line summary (verdict/progress/done/active/process/gates/quota) | correctly formatted text block | e2e-process | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.163] `--dir <root>` flag overrides `resolveTargetRoot()`/VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT | root explicitly resolved from --dir | integration | factory/cli.test.ts (status verb passes `--dir` through to the dev-status.ts child)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.164] neither `--dir` nor VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT set | `root` resolves to `null` cast to string, then `join(root,...)` reads throw a TypeError — deliberately uncaught, crashing the script | boundary/negative | GAP (explicitly documented in source comment as intentional; no test proves the crash happens/propagates as expected)
