- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.177] `checkSkills`: project declares NEITHER required nor recommended skills | ok true immediately, runner never invoked | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.178] `checkSkills`: runner fails (CLI unavailable) AND required.length>0 | ok false, reason "skills CLI not available..." | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.179] `checkSkills`: runner fails AND required.length===0 but recommended.length>0 | ok true, informational note pushed, missingRecommended = all of recommended | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.180] `checkSkills`: runner succeeds, all required+recommended found | ok true, no missing, no notes | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.181] `checkSkills`: runner succeeds, some recommended missing (but all required present) | ok true, notes array carries the recommended-missing message | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.182] `checkSkills`: runner succeeds, some required missing | ok false, reason "required skills not found in `skills list`" | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.183] `defaultRunner`: `npx skills list` exits non-zero or errors | returns `{ok:false}` | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.184] `defaultRunner`: succeeds | returns `{ok:true, output: stdout+stderr}` | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.185] CLI entrypoint (`import.meta.url===...`) with ok:true and nothing declared | prints "no development skills declared..." | e2e-process | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.186] CLI entrypoint with ok:true and skills declared | prints "all required development skills are installed." | e2e-process | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.187] CLI entrypoint with ok:false | prints missing list to stderr + remediation hint, `process.exit(1)` | e2e-process | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.188] CLI entrypoint prints every note (recommended-missing etc.) regardless of ok | notes always surfaced first | unit | GAP

### factory/progress-ledger.ts

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.189] `applyProgressEvent`: `issue_started` records active_item state "working" and graph_item_states "in_progress" | correct ledger shape | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("records in-progress heartbeat...")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.190] `review_started` with `role:"reviewer"` | active_item.role="reviewer", state "reviewing", graph_item status "reviewing" | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("carries the actor role on the active item")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.191] `issue_completed`/`gate_passed` clears ALL active items for the issue_id, across different actors (implementer vs reviewer) | no dangling active items after completion | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("completing an issue clears active items from both legs")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.192] event.role outside the ProgressRole vocabulary | throws `/Unsupported role/` | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("rejects an unknown actor role")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.193] transcript_refs accumulate on both the graph_item_state and the active_item | correct arrays populated | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("accumulates transcript_refs...")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.194] the orchestrator write path (`recordProgressEvent`) records a review event with correct role/state end-to-end via disk | ledger on disk matches expectation | integration | progress-ledger.test.ts ("the orchestrator write path records a review event...")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.195] `issue_completed` with a matching verification-grammar evidence ref | status "verified", evidence_refs recorded, active items cleared | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("records verified state and clears active item...")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.196] completion evidence ref is a prose doc NOT under the gate dir (fails grammar) | throws `/verification evidence_ref/` | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("#31: rejects completion evidence that is a docs/ prose doc")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.197] event's graph_refs include a ref not linked to the issue | throws `/not linked to issue/` | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("rejects graph refs outside the linked issue")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.198] `issue_completed`/`gate_passed` with empty evidence_refs | throws `/requires evidence_refs/` | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("rejects completion without evidence")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.199] completion evidence that doesn't match the grammar at all (e.g. README.md) | throws `/verification evidence_ref/` | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("rejects completion evidence that does not match verification grammar")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.200] evidence ref pointing to a line number beyond the file's actual line count | throws `/missing line/` | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("rejects evidence refs pointing to missing lines")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.201] evidence ref that is an ABSOLUTE path (even one that resolves inside the repo) | throws `/repository-relative/` | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("rejects absolute evidence refs...")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.202] `issue_blocked` with empty evidence_refs | throws `/requires evidence_refs/` | unit | GAP (only gate_passed/issue_completed empty-evidence is tested explicitly; issue_blocked's identical requirement at line 463-465 is untested directly)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.203] evidence ref pointing to a NON-existent file entirely (not just a bad line) | throws `/missing file/` | unit | GAP (only the "missing line" variant is tested; the more basic "missing file" existsSync check at validateEvidenceRefs is not directly exercised)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.204] a late heartbeat (older timestamp) arriving after `gate_passed` | does not un-verify status, does not rewind `updated_at` | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("late heartbeat after gate_passed does not un-verify or rewind updated_at")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.205] a NEWER heartbeat arriving after `gate_passed` | still does not downgrade verified status, but DOES advance updated_at | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("newer heartbeat after gate_passed still does not downgrade verified status")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.206] explicit `issue_reopened` after verified | downgrades status back to in_progress, advances updated_at, creates a new "working" active item (the ONE event allowed to downgrade) | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("explicit issue_reopened downgrades verified back to in_progress")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.207] `recordProgressEvent` writes atomically, leaves no stray `.tmp`/`.lock` file after a successful write | filesystem clean post-write | integration | progress-ledger.test.ts ("recordProgressEvent writes atomically and leaves no partial/temp file")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.208] `recordProgressEvent` revision increments monotonically across sequential writes (1,2,3...) | revision matches call count | integration | progress-ledger.test.ts ("recordProgressEvent increments revision monotonically across writes")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.209] `recordProgressEvent` reclaims a lock left by a DEAD pid (999999999) immediately, without waiting the stale TTL | write succeeds promptly, lock released after | integration | progress-ledger.test.ts ("recordProgressEvent reclaims a stale lock left by a dead writer")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.210] lock held by a LIVE, DIFFERENT process, still within LOCK_STALE_MS(30s) | acquireLock retries (sleepSync) rather than reclaiming, up to LOCK_ACQUIRE_TIMEOUT_MS(10s) then throws "Timed out acquiring..." | unit/concurrency | GAP (no test simulates a genuinely live concurrent holder blocking past the timeout)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.211] lock file's age exceeds LOCK_STALE_MS(30s) even though `owner.pid` is the CURRENT process itself (`owner.pid===process.pid` guard: note `ownerDead` requires pid!==process.pid) | reclaimed via the `tooOld` branch since ownerDead alone would be false for self | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.212] lock file exists but is malformed JSON (unreadable owner) | `owner=null`, falls back to file mtime age for staleness decision | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.213] lock file vanishes between the EEXIST and the `statSync` call (race) | `reclaimStaleLock` treats as reclaimable (catch -> true), acquire retries | unit/concurrency | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.214] `isProcessAlive`: EPERM error code (process exists, not ours) | treated as alive (true) | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.215] `isProcessAlive`: ESRCH (no such process) | treated as dead (false) | unit | GAP (implicitly covered by the 999999999-pid stale-lock test via kill(pid,0) throwing ESRCH, but not asserted directly as a unit case)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.216] CAS loop: `readProgressLedger` revision changes between the read and the atomic write attempt (another writer won the race) — retries up to `CAS_MAX_ATTEMPTS`(50) then throws | throws "ledger revision kept changing (compare-and-swap exhausted)" after 50 attempts | unit/concurrency | GAP (no test forces a genuine CAS collision across concurrent writers)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.217] `validateVerificationGateBinding`: issue declares NO `verification_gate_ids` at all | binding check is a no-op (any grammar-matching evidence suffices) | unit | GAP (only the gate-bound-issue-index variant is tested; the "no gate ids declared" default path relies on the base tests incidentally, not asserted explicitly as its own case)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.218] gate-run record with the DECLARED gate_id, status pass, exit_code 0 | binding satisfied, status verified | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("#31b: accepts a green gate-run record whose gate_id is declared on the issue")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.219] gate-run record for a DIFFERENT gate_id than declared | throws `/gate_id declared on the issue/` | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("#31b: rejects a green gate-run record for a gate the issue did not declare")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.220] gate-run record for the declared gate but status "fail"/exit_code 1 | throws `/gate_id declared on the issue/` (fails the `record.status==="pass"` AND `exit_code===0` checks) | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("#31b: rejects a failed gate-run record for the declared gate")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.221] gate-run evidence file exists but is unparsable/missing (JSON.parse throws in try/catch) | treated as non-satisfying (returns false), NOT a hard crash | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.222] event missing `session_ref` | throws `/session_ref must be a non-empty string/` | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("rejects events without session_ref")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.223] event with empty `graph_refs:[]` (no fallback to the issue's full ref set) | throws `/explicit graph focus/` | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("rejects events with empty graph_refs...")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.224] event missing `actor` (empty/whitespace string) | throws `/actor must be a non-empty string/` | unit | GAP (requiredString is exercised for session_ref but not explicitly for actor/issue_id/event_type)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.225] event missing `issue_id` | throws `/issue_id must be a non-empty string/` | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.226] event's `event_type` outside `progressEventTypes` (unknown string) | throws `/Unsupported progress event_type/` | unit | GAP (role-rejection is tested; event_type-rejection at the very top of validateProgressEvent is not directly tested)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.227] `evidence_refs`/`graph_refs`/`transcript_refs` passed as a non-array or array with non-string entries | `stringArray()` throws `/Expected string array/` | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.228] `issueIndex.issues` is not an array at all (malformed index) | throws `/Issue index must define issues\[\]/` | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.229] `event.issue_id` not found in the issue index at all | throws `/Unknown issue_id/` | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("progress recording is inert against the real empty issue index")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.230] `verification_evidence_ref_grammar` missing/empty/non-string on the issue index | `createVerificationEvidenceMatcher` throws `/must define verification_evidence_ref_grammar/` | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.231] `verification_evidence_ref_grammar` is a syntactically invalid regex string | throws `/is invalid/` | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.232] evidence ref carrying a `#anchor` suffix | anchor is stripped before file/line parsing (`ref.split("#")[0]`) | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.233] evidence ref of the form `path:line` with no anchor | parsed into `{filePath, line}` correctly | unit | GAP (exercised transitively by the missing-line test, but the pure parseEvidenceRef function itself isn't isolated)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.234] evidence ref that is just a bare path with no `:line` suffix | `{filePath}` only, no line-range check performed | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.235] `resolveRepoPath` when `repoRoot` (VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT) is unset | throws "No target project configured..." | unit | GAP (test-target-root.ts always sets it before any test runs, so the null-repoRoot branch is never hit in this suite)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.236] `resolveRepoPath` given a path that escapes the repo via `..` even though not literally absolute | throws `/Path must stay inside repository/` | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.237] two issues sharing a graph_ref: one goes verified while the other is still in_progress | shared graph item's aggregate status stays the conservative (lowest) one, per-issue issue_states tracked independently | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("#34: one issue going verified does not overstate a graph item another issue still works on")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.238] aggregate status tie-break: multiple issues at the SAME lowest rank, one of them "blocked" | "blocked" wins the tie (visibility of the blocker) | unit | GAP (aggregateGraphStatus's blocked tie-break branch is implemented but not exercised by any test)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.239] a ledger written before per-issue states existed (legacy shape lacking `issue_states`) | `ensureIssueStates` synthesizes it from `issue_ids`+scalar `status` | unit | GAP (migration shim untested)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.240] unknown top-level ledger fields (e.g. baseline_id/baseline_version/manifest_hash/document_set_hash) | survive untouched across `applyProgressEvent`'s spread | unit | progress-ledger.test.ts ("baseline identity fields on the ledger survive a recorded event")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.241] real (fixture) issue-index artifact schema/frozen-baseline-identity/grammar cross-checks against the architecture map | consistent identity fields | integration | progress-ledger.test.ts ("real issue-index artifact carries schema...")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.242] real issue-index coverage_summary numeric fields, with issues.length===0 forcing total_doc_lines===0 | placeholder invariant holds | integration | progress-ledger.test.ts ("real issue-index coverage_summary follows the computed total_doc_lines rule")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.243] real progress-ledger placeholder shares the frozen baseline identity with the manifest | fields match | integration | progress-ledger.test.ts ("real progress-ledger placeholder is bound to the same frozen baseline")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.244] `createEmptyProgressLedger()` shape | schema_version 1, revision 0, updated_at null, empty arrays | unit | GAP (implicitly used everywhere as a starting ledger but its own shape is never asserted directly)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.245] `maxTimestamp(a,b)`: a null | returns b (or null if b also null) | unit | GAP (pure helper, indirectly exercised via timestamp-ordering tests but not unit-isolated)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.246] `readProgressLedger`: ledger file does not exist | returns `createEmptyProgressLedger()` instead of throwing | unit | GAP (indirectly true for every "first write" test, not asserted standalone)

### factory/progress-ledger.test.ts

(All cases above are covered here where marked; no additional standalone behaviors beyond what's listed.)

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.247] test file ordering dependency: `test-target-root.ts` MUST be imported first so `VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT` is bound before any factory module's module-load-time `resolveTargetRoot()` call | if import order were violated, tests would silently target the wrong (unset) root | unit | n/a (test-infra invariant, documented in the file's own header comment; not itself a "case" but a fragility worth flagging — see cross-notes)

### factory/reopen.ts

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.248] `impactedIssues`: an issue referencing a `changed` requirement id | included in the impacted list | unit | reopen.test.ts ("impactedIssues returns issues referencing a changed or removed requirement")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.249] `impactedIssues`: an issue referencing a `removed` requirement id | included | unit | reopen.test.ts (same test)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.250] `impactedIssues`: an issue referencing only `unchanged` requirement ids | excluded | unit | reopen.test.ts (same test)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.251] `impactedIssues` with a null/undefined drift or issueIndex | returns [] gracefully (optional chaining + defaults) | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.252] `runReopen`: an impacted issue that IS currently in done/ | file renamed from done/ back to the active issues dir, `issue_reopened` event recorded with correct graph_refs/evidence_refs(crRef) | unit | reopen.test.ts ("runReopen reopens exactly the impacted done issues...")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.253] `runReopen`: an unchanged issue stays in done/ (not touched) | file remains under done/ | unit | reopen.test.ts (same test)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.254] `runReopen`: an impacted issue that is NOT currently done (no done/<id>.md file) | silently skipped (the `if (!existsSync(donePath)) continue` guard) — not renamed, no event recorded | unit | GAP (only the "is done" path is exercised; the "impacted but not done" skip branch is untested)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.255] `runReopen` with no requirement changes at all (identical prior/current source maps) | reopened:[] | unit | reopen.test.ts ("runReopen does nothing when no requirement changed")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.256] `runReopen`: issue index entry lookup miss for an impacted id (defensive `issuesById.get(id) ?? {id, graph_refs:[]}`) | falls back to empty graph_refs rather than crashing | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.257] `runReopen` without a `crRef` | `session_ref` is `"change-control"` (no suffix), `evidence_refs` is `[]` | unit | GAP (only the with-crRef variant is tested)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.258] CLI entrypoint with no `VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT` configured | stderr "no target project configured...", exit 2 | e2e-process | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.259] CLI entrypoint missing `--prior <path>` | usage message, exit 2 | e2e-process | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.260] CLI entrypoint: prior or current source-map file missing/unreadable | error message reporting which is missing (`prior=<bool>, current=<bool>`), exit 1 | e2e-process | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.261] CLI entrypoint happy path with `--cr CR-####` | prints "reopen: reopened N impacted issue(s): ..." (or "none") | e2e-process | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.262] CLI entrypoint happy path with reopened:[] | prints "...reopened 0 impacted issue(s): none" | e2e-process | GAP

### factory/reopen.test.ts

(All covered above.)

### factory/atomic-write.ts

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.263] normal write to a fresh path | file exists with pretty-printed JSON + trailing newline, exact byte match | unit | atomic-write.test.ts ("writes the file with exactly the serialized JSON...")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.264] destination's parent directory does not exist | throws ENOENT (writer never mkdirs), no residue left in the existing ancestor dir | unit | atomic-write.test.ts ("throws when the parent directory is missing...")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.265] destination's parent directory already exists | clean write, no stray temp file | unit | atomic-write.test.ts ("writes cleanly when the parent directory already exists")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.266] a second write to the same path | fully overwrites (no key bleed from the prior value) | unit | atomic-write.test.ts ("a second write overwrites the prior contents in place")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.267] after a successful write | no `.tmp` sibling remains, directory contains ONLY the destination | unit | atomic-write.test.ts ("leaves no temp file behind after a successful write")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.268] 5 repeated writes to the same path | no accumulation of temp files across repeated calls | unit | atomic-write.test.ts ("repeated writes do not accumulate temp siblings...")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.269] rename fails because the "parent" is actually a file, not a directory | throws, and the failed write's own `.tmp` sibling is cleaned up (unlinkSync in the catch) before rethrow | unit | atomic-write.test.ts ("propagates the error when the destination directory is unwritable...")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.270] the temp filename includes `process.pid` + a random suffix, so concurrent writers to the SAME destination never collide on the temp file itself | two processes writing concurrently each get a unique temp path | concurrency | GAP (no test spawns two concurrent writers to prove no temp-name collision; this is asserted only by code inspection of the naming scheme)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.271] `unlinkSync` cleanup itself fails (e.g. tmp file already gone) during the rename-failure catch path | swallowed silently (best-effort comment), original rename error still thrown | unit | GAP

### factory/atomic-write.test.ts

(All covered above.)

### factory/target-root.ts

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.272] `resolveTargetRoot()` with `VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT` set to a non-empty value | returns `resolve(override)` (absolute, normalized) | unit | GAP (no dedicated test file for target-root.ts; only exercised transitively through test-target-root.ts's side effect and every consumer's tests)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.273] `resolveTargetRoot()` with the env var unset | returns null | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.274] `resolveTargetRoot()` with the env var set to whitespace-only | treated as unset -> returns null (per `.trim().length>0` guard) | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.275] `resolveTargetRoot(customEnvObject)` — passing an explicit env object rather than `process.env` | resolves from the PASSED env, not the global | unit | GAP (the function accepts an injectable env param specifically for testability, but no test exercises that seam)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.276] `FACTORY_DIR`/`FACTORY_PROMPTS_DIR`/`FACTORY_REHEARSAL_DIR` constants resolve to real, existing paths under factory/ | paths exist on disk | unit | GAP (prompts.test.ts uses FACTORY_PROMPTS_DIR but never asserts its value/existence directly — only reads files from it, which would throw if wrong)

### factory/test-target-root.ts

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.277] `testTargetRoot` is a freshly created, unique tmp dir on each importing test FILE (node:test isolates per file) | two different test files never collide on the same target root | integration | implicit across every *.test.ts using it (progress-ledger.test.ts) — not a standalone assertion but structurally verified by the whole suite passing without cross-contamination
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.278] the side-effecting `process.env.VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT = testTargetRoot` assignment happens at MODULE EVALUATION time (import-order dependent) | any factory module imported AFTER this one sees the correct root at its own load time | unit | GAP (this is an implicit architectural invariant with no direct regression test — a future refactor that reorders imports would silently break tests; see cross-notes)

### factory/sleep-sync.ts

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.279] `sleepSync(ms)` with a positive ms | blocks the calling thread synchronously for approximately that duration (busy-park via Atomics.wait, never resolved) | unit | GAP (no dedicated test file exists; only indirectly exercised via progress-ledger.ts's lock-retry path, and even that is never timing-asserted)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.280] `sleepSync(0)` | returns immediately (the `if (ms<=0) return` guard), no Atomics.wait call at all | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.281] `sleepSync(negative)` | also returns immediately (same guard) | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.282] `sleepSync` internal loop chunks the wait in <=1000ms `Atomics.wait` slices even for a very large `ms`, checking `Date.now()` between slices | total elapsed time approximates the requested `ms`, not one giant blocking call | unit | GAP

### factory/project-config.ts

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.283] `validateGateCommand` accepts a non-empty string, trims whitespace | trimmed string returned | unit | project-config.test.ts ("validateGateCommand accepts a non-empty string and trims it")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.284] `validateGateCommand` rejects `""`, `"   "`, null, undefined, 42, {}, [] | throws ProjectConfigError code invalid_gate_command for every case | unit | project-config.test.ts ("validateGateCommand rejects empty / non-string")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.285] `loadProjectConfig` reads a non-Node `gateCommand` from vivicy.json (e.g. "go test ./...") | no hidden Node assumption; command honored verbatim | unit | project-config.test.ts ("reads a NON-NODE gateCommand from vivicy.json")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.286] `loadProjectConfig` honors several polyglot runners verbatim (cargo/pytest/phpunit/swift/npm) | each returned unchanged | unit | project-config.test.ts ("honors several polyglot runners verbatim")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.287] `loadProjectConfig` falls back to package.json's `vivicy` field when no vivicy.json exists | fallback config returned | unit | project-config.test.ts ("falls back to a `vivicy` field in package.json")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.288] `loadProjectConfig`: vivicy.json WINS over package.json#vivicy when both exist | vivicy.json's value used | unit | project-config.test.ts ("vivicy.json WINS over a package.json vivicy field...")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.289] `loadProjectConfig` with neither file present | returns null | unit | project-config.test.ts ("returns null when no config is present at all")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.290] `loadProjectConfig` with a present-but-malformed vivicy.json (bad JSON) | throws ProjectConfigError code invalid_json | unit | project-config.test.ts ("THROWS on a present-but-malformed vivicy.json")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.291] `loadProjectConfig` with vivicy.json present but `gateCommand:""` | throws ProjectConfigError code invalid_gate_command | unit | project-config.test.ts (same test, second assertion)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.292] `loadProjectConfig` with a malformed package.json (invalid JSON) as the ONLY config source | returns null (malformed package.json is "not our config to validate") rather than throwing | unit | GAP (only the vivicy.json-malformed case is tested; the package.json-malformed fallback path at line 96-98 is untested)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.293] `loadProjectConfig` with package.json present but its `vivicy` field is not an object (e.g. a string or missing) | fallback not applied, returns null | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.294] `resolveGateCommand` precedence: issue.gate_command > vivicy.json > explicitDefault | correct precedence order honored | unit | project-config.test.ts ("resolveGateCommand precedence...")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.295] `resolveGateCommand` uses explicitDefault ONLY when nothing else is configured (no vivicy.json, no per-issue override) | explicitDefault returned | unit | project-config.test.ts ("uses the explicit caller default ONLY when nothing else exists")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.296] `resolveGateCommand` with truly nothing configured (no issue override, no vivicy.json, no explicitDefault) | throws ProjectConfigError code invalid_gate_command with the full remediation message | unit | project-config.test.ts (same test, second assertion)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.297] `resolveGateCommand` with `issue.gate_command` present but blank/whitespace-only | falls through to vivicy.json/explicitDefault rather than using the blank string | unit | GAP (only a genuinely non-empty override is tested; the whitespace-only per-issue override edge is not)
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.298] `normalizeConfig` with `raw` being a non-object (e.g. a string or array) | throws ProjectConfigError code invalid_json ("must be a JSON object") | unit | GAP (this specific guard, reached only via the package.json#vivicy branch when `pkg.vivicy` IS an object per the outer check, is effectively unreachable from that call site — but vivicy.json's own parseConfig path could hit it if the JSON parses to e.g. `"a string"` or `[1,2]`; untested)

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.328] `isAlive(pid)`: signal-0 probe throws (dead/no such process) | returns false | unit | GAP

### lib/run-status.ts

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.334] `resolveRunPhase`: `run_active:true`, verdict starting with "STALE" | "stalled" (checked before the generic "running" branch) | unit | lib/run-status.test.ts ("is stalled when active but the verdict is stale")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.335] `resolveRunPhase`: `run_active:true`, verdict NOT starting with "STALE" | "running" | unit | lib/run-status.test.ts ("is running when the lock is active")

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.338] `resolveRunPhase`: not active, not done, not stale, `gates.fail>0` and `issues_done<issues_total` | "blocked" | unit | lib/run-status.test.ts ("is blocked when a gate failed mid-way...")

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.341] `isResumable`: phase idle/blocked/stalled AND `0<issues_done<issues_total` | true | unit | lib/run-status.test.ts ("is resumable when stopped part-way through")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.342] `isResumable`: `issues_done===0` (no progress yet) | false | unit | lib/run-status.test.ts ("is not resumable before any progress")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.343] `isResumable`: `issues_done===issues_total` (complete) | false | unit | lib/run-status.test.ts ("is not resumable when complete")
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.344] `isResumable`: phase is "running" (active lock) | false regardless of issues_done | unit | lib/run-status.test.ts ("is not resumable while running")

### lib/run-status.test.ts

(All covered above.)

### Area cross-notes

- **CLI/API parity seam (documented, not tested here).** `factory/cli.ts` and `lib/control.ts` deliberately do NOT share code (per the file's own CLI↔app parity header) — parity is asserted by both spawning the same factory scripts with the same args/env and reading the same state files. This area's tests only exercise the CLI side; there is no test IN THIS AREA that proves `lib/control.ts` (out of scope) actually agrees byte-for-byte with `cli.ts`'s lock schema, script names, or exit-code mapping. A parity-drift bug (e.g. someone changes `RUN_STATE_FILE` or the lock schema in one file but not the other) would not be caught by any test in this list. This is the single biggest structural gap for the area, and it is a GAP by construction since one half of the parity pair is out of scope.
- **Lock byte-compatibility is asserted only structurally.** cli.test.ts checks the on-disk lock JSON schema (`pid/target_root/mode`) but never cross-loads it through `lib/runtime-dir.ts`'s `getRuntimeDir()` to prove the SAME directory is computed by both the CLI (`runtimeDir()` in cli.ts) and the app side, beyond the shared `--runtime-dir`/`VIVICY_RUNTIME_DIR` override used by tests. The default-path agreement (`<appDir>/.vivicy-runtime` == `getRuntimeDir()`'s `<cwd>/.vivicy-runtime` when both are launched from the package root) is asserted only in a code comment, never in a test.
- **`lib/spawner.ts` / `lib/node-spawner.ts` have NO test file at all** in this area's file list (no `spawner.test.ts` / `node-spawner.test.ts` exist), despite being critical detached-process infrastructure (real agent spawning, kill-group signal handling, fake-spawn E2E seam). Every case under those two files (30–37 above) is a GAP. This is the largest concentration of untested surface in the whole area.
- **`factory/dev-status.ts` and `factory/dev-preflight.ts` have NO dedicated test files** despite being read-only but logic-heavy (verdict state machine, quota formatting, skills-declaration merge rules). All cases under those two files are GAPs. `dev-status.ts`'s output is only exercised indirectly, and shallowly, through `cli.test.ts`'s `status` verb tests (which stub or use the real script but only assert a couple of fields: `issues_total`/`issues_done`), never its verdict state machine, quota formatting, or stale-detection directly.
- **`factory/target-root.ts` and `factory/sleep-sync.ts` have NO dedicated test files.** `target-root.ts`'s `resolveTargetRoot()` is exercised only as a side effect of `test-target-root.ts` always setting the env var before any test runs — the "unset" and "explicit env param" code paths are never hit by any test. `sleep-sync.ts` is invoked by `progress-ledger.ts`'s lock-retry loop but never itself timing-tested.
- **Import-order fragility is real and undocumented by a regression test.** `test-target-root.ts`'s own header comment explains that `progress-ledger.ts` (and other factory modules) bind `resolveTargetRoot()` at MODULE LOAD TIME, so the temp-root-setting import must run first. If a future refactor reorders imports in any `*.test.ts` file, or if `progress-ledger.ts` moved its `const repoRoot = resolveTargetRoot()` inside a function, this invariant would silently break (tests would target the real environment's unset root) with no test catching the regression itself — the fragility is structural, not policed by a meta-test.
- **Concurrency is asserted only for the SEQUENTIAL-retry paths, never true parallelism.** progress-ledger.test.ts proves the CAS loop's *shape* (revision incrementing sequentially, stale-lock reclamation from a dead pid) but never spins up two REAL concurrent writers hammering `recordProgressEvent` on the same ledger — the up-to-12-parallel-worktree dev-loop design this factory supports is never load-tested at the ledger-locking layer in this area.
- **`--json` vs human-mode output is asymmetrically tested across every verb.** For nearly every `cli.ts` verb (status/extract/crs/skills/notifications), only the `--json` path is exercised by `cli.test.ts`; the human-readable/non-JSON branch (the `else` arm of each verb's `if (json) {...} else {...}`) is a GAP across the board. Given the CLI's own contract states `--json` is the agent surface and human mode is a secondary convenience, this is a coherent but real gap in output-contract coverage.
- **`start`/`resume`/`stop` failure-injection branches (spawn throws, non-numeric pid, EACCES on the lock write) are entirely untested** — cli.test.ts's lifecycle test only exercises the happy start->refuse->stop path with a working stub supervisor script; no test simulates a spawn failure to verify the lock-release-on-failure behavior (lines 730-739 of cli.ts).

### Adversarial additions

- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.346] `retry-stage <stage>` where `<stage>` is a JS Object-prototype property name (`constructor`, `toString`, `hasOwnProperty`, `__proto__`, `valueOf`) | `RETRYABLE_STAGES[stage]` (a plain `{}`-shaped object, not `Object.create(null)`) returns the inherited prototype FUNCTION for those keys, which is truthy — so the `!action` guard at cli.ts:1008 passes and the stage is treated as "supported"; since `action` (a function, not the string `"extract"`/`"skills"`) fails both `=== "extract"` and `=== "skills"` checks at cli.ts:1016-1017, execution falls through to `startSupervisor(argv, opts, "resume")`, i.e. `retry-stage constructor` silently LAUNCHES THE SUPERVISOR instead of returning the documented "not retryable" usage error | GAP — a plain-object property-name collision in the stage dispatcher that bypasses validation and triggers an unintended supervisor start; verified live: `({extract:"extract",skills:"skills",dev:"resume"})["constructor"]` returns `[Function: Object]` (truthy) in node
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.347] `vivicy cr approve|reject` where a flag (e.g. `--dir <path>`) is given BEFORE the positional `<id>` argument on the command line | `cmdCr` only inspects `argv[0]` to detect the id (`argv[0] && !argv[0].startsWith("--") ? argv.shift() : null`); if the first token is a flag, `id` resolves to `null` and the id-format usage error ("a CR id of the form CR-#### is required") fires even though a well-formed id WAS supplied later in argv — verified live: `argv=["approve","--dir","/tmp/x","CR-0001","--by","me"]` yields `id:null`. No test in cli.test.ts exercises any flag-before-positional ordering for `cr approve/reject` (every existing test places the id immediately after the decision word) | GAP — argument-order-sensitive usage bug, undocumented and untested, that would confuse an agent caller that (reasonably) puts `--dir`/`--by` before the id
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.348] `applyProgressEvent` for each of the 10 event types NOT individually exercised by any per-event-type test — `gate_started`, `gate_failed`, `graph_item_focus`, `heartbeat` (as a FRESH active item, not just a late/newer heartbeat AFTER gate_passed), `spike_proof_started`, `spike_proof_completed`, `readiness_check_started`, `readiness_check_completed`, `readiness_update_applied`, `issue_parked_on_cr`, `post_merge_gate_failed`, `merge_conflict_resolved`, `merge_conflict_unresolved` | each maps to its declared `activeStateByEvent` entry (verifying/working/blocked as declared in the table at progress-ledger.ts:76-98) and (for the ones with no explicit branch in `applyProgressEvent`) falls through to the generic `upsertGraphStates(..., "in_progress", ...)` + `upsertActiveItem(..., activeStateByEvent[event_type] ?? "working", ...)` tail at lines 378-379 | unit | GAP — only `issue_started`/`issue_claimed`-shaped events, `review_started`/`review_completed`, `issue_blocked`, `issue_reopened`, and `issue_completed`/`gate_passed` are covered in progress-ledger.test.ts; 13 of the 16 declared `progressEventTypes` are never independently asserted against their active-item state or graph-item status, including the entire S3 spike-proving pair, the entire S8 readiness triad, and the entire S10 merge-integrity triad that the module's own header comments describe as load-bearing for their respective gates
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.349] `dev-status.ts` quota line rendering when an agent's `windows` object has NEITHER `"5h"` NOR `"weekly"` keys at all (not just a window with no `used_pct`) | `formatWindow` returns `null` for each missing window, `.filter(Boolean).join(" · ")` yields `""`, so `pctSuffix` is `""` and the agent line renders as just `"name model: <status>"` with no trailing `(...)` parenthetical at all (distinct from the already-listed #158 case of a window present but lacking `used_pct`) | unit | GAP
- [cli-supervisor-process-infra.350] `factory/cli.ts` `parseFrontmatter`: a frontmatter key containing a digit or hyphen (e.g. `created-at:` or `id2:`) | silently fails to match `/^([a-z_]+):\s*(.*)$/` (no digits/hyphens in the character class) and the line is dropped from the returned map entirely, rather than erroring — verified live: `"created_at: X".match(re)` succeeds but a hypothetical `"created-at: X"` or `"id2: X"` does not | unit | GAP — latent parser fragility that would silently drop a frontmatter field if a CR template ever introduced a hyphenated or digit-suffixed key; no current CR field triggers it, but the parser has no defensive test proving the drop-silently behavior is intentional

## Area: control-plane-api-routes

### lib/control.ts

- [control-plane-api-routes.1] `getFactoryRoot` returns `<cwd>/factory` when `VIVICY_FACTORY_ROOT` is unset | resolves to cwd-relative "factory" | unit | control.test.ts (implicit via scaffoldFactory/cwd setup)
- [control-plane-api-routes.2] `getFactoryRoot` returns the resolved absolute path of `VIVICY_FACTORY_ROOT` when set, including a relative value | path.resolve applied | unit | GAP (only absolute values used in tests)
- [control-plane-api-routes.3] `getFactoryRoot` treats an empty/whitespace-only `VIVICY_FACTORY_ROOT` as unset (falls back to cwd/factory) | falls back | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.4] `assertInside` accepts a child path that resolves strictly inside root | returns absolute path | unit | GAP (only exercised indirectly through resolveScript in control.test.ts)
- [control-plane-api-routes.5] `assertInside` throws `ControlError("missing_script")` when the relative script escapes root via `../` | throws with code missing_script | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.6] `assertInside` throws when given an absolute path outside root | throws missing_script | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.7] `resolveScript` throws `missing_script` when the script file does not exist on disk | throws /not found/ | unit | control.test.ts ("fails clearly when the supervisor script is missing", "...extraction status is missing", "...installer script is missing")
- [control-plane-api-routes.8] `devEnv` merges `process.env` with `VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT` and `VIVICY_RUNTIME_DIR`, the latter set to the PER-PROJECT `projectRuntimeDir(targetRoot)` namespace (not the runtime root) | env has both keys plus inherited env | unit | control.test.ts (assertions on `call.env.VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT`; the per-project VIVICY_RUNTIME_DIR value is asserted truthy only — an exact-value assertion is a partial GAP)
- [control-plane-api-routes.9] `readRunState` returns null when the lock file does not exist | null | unit | control.test.ts (via isRunActive tests indirectly) — GAP for direct null-path assertion
- [control-plane-api-routes.10] `readRunState` returns null (not throw) when the lock file contains malformed JSON | null, no throw | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.11] `readRunState` returns the parsed RunState when the file is well-formed | parsed object | unit | control.test.ts ("sets the lock and spawns...", asserts `persisted?.pid`)
- [control-plane-api-routes.12] `claimRunLock` creates the PROJECT runtime namespace (`projectRuntimeDir(targetRoot)`, i.e. `projects/<key>/`) recursively if absent before writing the lock | dir created | unit | control.test.ts ("keeps the lock inside the PROJECT's runtime namespace")
- [control-plane-api-routes.13] `claimRunLock` writes the lock atomically with `wx` flag when no lock exists | lock file created with placeholder state | unit | control.test.ts ("holds the lock atomically before the spawn returns")
- [control-plane-api-routes.14] `claimRunLock` refuses (already_running) when lock exists and `isRunActive` is true | throws ControlError already_running | unit | control.test.ts ("refuses a double start while a run is active")
- [control-plane-api-routes.15] `claimRunLock` clears a stale lock (dead pid) and retries the claim successfully | new lock written, no throw | unit | control.test.ts ("allows a restart once the prior pid is no longer alive")
- [control-plane-api-routes.16] `claimRunLock` loses a concurrent race on retry (second `wx` write also EEXIST) → throws already_running | throws already_running | unit | GAP (race-losing branch not directly exercised — needs a spawner/fs mock that recreates the lock between the stale-check and the retry write)
- [control-plane-api-routes.17] `claimRunLock` re-throws a non-EEXIST fs error from the initial write (e.g. EACCES) | propagates raw error | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.18] `clearRunState` is a no-op when the file does not exist (no throw) | no-op | unit | GAP (implicitly covered whenever stop is called after start, but no isolated case)
- [control-plane-api-routes.19] `isRunActive` returns false when no lock recorded | false | unit | control.test.ts (stopSupervisor "refuses to stop when nothing is recorded" indirectly)
- [control-plane-api-routes.20] `isRunActive` returns true and keeps the lock when the recorded pid is alive | true, lock untouched | unit | control.test.ts ("sets the lock and spawns...")
- [control-plane-api-routes.21] `isRunActive` returns false and clears the lock (self-healing) when the recorded pid is dead | false, lock file removed | unit | control.test.ts ("allows a restart once the prior pid is no longer alive" — asserts new spawn but not explicit clearing check) — partially covered, GAP for direct "lock file removed" assertion
- [control-plane-api-routes.22] `resolveContext` throws `missing_target` when `getTargetRoot()` returns null (no project selected) | throws missing_target | unit | GAP (control.test.ts always sets VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT; no test unsets it to hit this branch directly — covered only via other functions' "target root does not exist" tests, which is a DIFFERENT branch: existsSync check, not the null-target branch)
- [control-plane-api-routes.23] `startSupervisor` happy path (mode="start"): claims lock, spawns supervisor script with correct args/cwd/env, patches pid, persists state | RunState returned; lock persisted; spawnDetached called once | unit | control.test.ts ("sets the lock and spawns the supervisor...")
- [control-plane-api-routes.24] `startSupervisor` mode="resume" records `mode: "resume"` and re-spawns even after a prior stop | mode=resume, new pid | unit | control.test.ts (describe "resume")
- [control-plane-api-routes.25] `startSupervisor` throws `missing_target` before touching the lock when `targetRoot` does not exist on disk | throws missing_target; no lock file created | unit | control.test.ts ("fails clearly when the target root does not exist")
- [control-plane-api-routes.26] `startSupervisor` throws `missing_script` when `dev-loop-supervised.ts` is absent from factoryRoot | throws missing_script; no lock persisted | unit | control.test.ts ("fails clearly when the supervisor script is missing")
- [control-plane-api-routes.27] `startSupervisor` refuses with `already_running` on a genuine double-start (lock live) | throws already_running; only 1 spawnDetached call total | unit | control.test.ts ("refuses a double start while a run is active")
- [control-plane-api-routes.28] `startSupervisor` releases the claimed lock when `spawner.spawnDetached` throws, so a retry can succeed | lock cleared; retry spawns successfully | unit | control.test.ts ("releases the lock when the spawn throws so a retry can proceed")
- [control-plane-api-routes.29] `startSupervisor` merges `settingsToEnv(readSettings())` into the child env (per-agent model/effort/fast/maxParallel/allowUnsafeSkills) | env carries VIVICY_CLAUDE_*/VIVICY_CODEX_*/VIVICY_MAX_PARALLEL/VIVICY_ALLOW_UNSAFE_SKILLS | unit | GAP (control.test.ts never asserts these env keys on startSupervisor's spawnDetached call — only checked for startSkillsInstall)
- [control-plane-api-routes.30] `startSupervisor` invalid/corrupt settings.json on disk still produces a valid env (defaults) rather than throwing | env has default values | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.31] `startSupervisor` the placeholder pid (`process.pid`) keeps the lock live between claim and patch — a concurrent claimant sees active | second claimant refused during the window | unit | GAP (would need a spawnDetached that itself calls startSupervisor recursively/synchronously to prove the window; today only proven indirectly by test #13)
- [control-plane-api-routes.32] `stopSupervisor` happy path: kills recorded pid with SIGTERM, clears lock, returns pid | killGroup called with correct pid+signal; lock cleared | unit | control.test.ts ("kills the recorded pid and clears the lock")
- [control-plane-api-routes.33] `stopSupervisor` throws `not_running` when no run is recorded | throws not_running | unit | control.test.ts ("refuses to stop when nothing is recorded")
- [control-plane-api-routes.34] `stopSupervisor` when the recorded pid is already dead (killGroup returns false) still clears the lock and returns ok | lock cleared, `killGroup` returned false doesn't error | unit | GAP

- [control-plane-api-routes.36] `readDevStatus` throws `missing_target` when targetRoot does not exist on disk | throws missing_target | unit | GAP (only tested for other verbs, not readDevStatus itself)
- [control-plane-api-routes.37] `readDevStatus` throws `missing_script` when `dev-status.ts` is absent | throws missing_script | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.38] `readDevStatus` throws with `spawn_failed` code when stdout is not valid JSON, including exit code/stderr in the message | throws /did not return JSON/ | unit | control.test.ts ("throws a clear error when dev-status returns non-JSON")
- [control-plane-api-routes.39] `readDevStatus` reflects `run_active: true` when a supervised run's lock is live | run_active true | unit | control.test.ts ("reflects an active run via the lock in run_active")
- [control-plane-api-routes.40] `readDevStatus` reflects `run_active: false` and self-heals a stale lock while reading status | run_active false, lock cleared | unit | GAP (only tested for isRunActive directly, not composed through readDevStatus)
- [control-plane-api-routes.41] `readDevStatus` parsed JSON that happens to include a `run_active` key is overwritten by the real computed value (spread order) | real run_active wins | unit | GAP

- [control-plane-api-routes.43] `runExtract` empty-canonical guard: throws `empty_canonical` when canonical contains only `README.md` | throws /only the scaffold README/ | unit | control.test.ts ("refuses to extract when canonical holds only the scaffold README")
- [control-plane-api-routes.44] `runExtract` empty-canonical guard: accepts a real doc nested arbitrarily deep in subdirectories (recursive stack walk) | passes guard | unit | control.test.ts ("accepts a real canonical doc even when nested in a subdirectory")
- [control-plane-api-routes.45] `runExtract` empty-canonical guard: accepts a real doc even when `README.md` (any case? — only exact name checked) is also present alongside it | passes guard | unit | GAP (only-README case tested; mixed case not tested)
- [control-plane-api-routes.46] `runExtract` empty-canonical guard: a file named "README.MD" (different case) is NOT excluded (exact match `!== "README.md"`), so it counts as a "real" doc and lets extraction proceed | guard passes when it maybe shouldn't (case-sensitivity edge) | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.47] `runExtract` empty-canonical guard: a non-.md file (e.g. `01-area.txt`) does not count as real canonical content | still throws empty_canonical | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.48] `runExtract` throws `missing_target` when targetRoot does not exist | throws missing_target | unit | GAP (only tested via getExtractionStatus/startSupervisor, not runExtract directly)
- [control-plane-api-routes.49] `runExtract` throws `missing_script` when `extract-issues.ts` is absent from factoryRoot | throws ControlError | unit | control.test.ts ("fails clearly when the orchestrator script is missing")
- [control-plane-api-routes.50] `runExtract` happy path green: spawns `extract-issues.ts` with `VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT`, reads back status file phase=green, returns ok:true/blocked:false/status:green/summary from the status file | ok true | unit | control.test.ts ("drives the single extract-issues orchestrator...")
- [control-plane-api-routes.51] `runExtract` surfaces `blocked:true` honestly when status file phase is `extraction_blocked`, even though process exit code is non-zero | ok false, blocked true, status extraction_blocked | unit | control.test.ts ("surfaces the blocked case honestly when the orchestrator stays red")
- [control-plane-api-routes.52] `runExtract` never claims ok:true unless BOTH exit code 0 AND status phase is exactly "green" — a 0-exit with an unparseable/missing status file is reported as a non-blocked "error", not silent success | ok false, blocked false, status "error" | unit | control.test.ts ("reports a non-blocked failure when the orchestrator errors without a status file")
- [control-plane-api-routes.53] `runExtract` a 0-exit but status phase = something other than "green"/"extraction_blocked" (e.g. mid-run "authoring" left stale) yields ok:false, blocked:false, status:"authoring" | ok false, status reflects stale phase | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.54] `runExtract` `blocked_on_unverified_spikes` phase in the status file surfaces as status="blocked_on_unverified_spikes" (not "blocked") — consumed distinctly by the extract route's notification branch | ok false, blocked false (only extraction_blocked sets blocked=true), status = blocked_on_unverified_spikes | unit | GAP (route-level test exists — extract/route.test.ts — but no lib/control.test.ts case builds this directly through runExtract)
- [control-plane-api-routes.55] `runExtract` lastLine fallback: when `result.lastLine` is empty, falls back to the last non-empty stderr line | summary/lastLine derived from stderr | unit | GAP

### app/api/control/start/route.ts

- [control-plane-api-routes.104] `POST /api/control/start` maps any OTHER `ControlError` code (missing_target, missing_script, spawn_failed) to HTTP 422 | 422, body.code echoed | integration | GAP

- [control-plane-api-routes.169] `GET /api/status/stream` returns an SSE response with `Content-Type: text/event-stream`, `Cache-Control: no-cache, no-transform`, `Connection: keep-alive` | headers correct | e2e-process | GAP (no test file; SSE streaming untested anywhere in this area)
- [control-plane-api-routes.170] `GET /api/status/stream` emits an eager `: connected` comment frame immediately on connect, before the first poll tick resolves | first bytes are the connected comment | e2e-process | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.171] `GET /api/status/stream` polls `readDevStatus` every `POLL_INTERVAL_MS` (2000ms) and sends a `data: {...}` frame each tick | periodic data frames | e2e-process | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.172] `GET /api/status/stream` sends a `: ping` heartbeat comment every `POLL_INTERVAL_MS * 5` (10s) independent of the data poll, to keep idle proxies from dropping the connection | ping frames at the right cadence | e2e-process | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.173] `GET /api/status/stream` when `readDevStatus` throws mid-stream, sends a `data: {"error": "..."}` frame instead of closing the connection, then continues polling | stream stays open, error frame sent | e2e-process | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.174] `GET /api/status/stream` closes the stream cleanly (clears timer + heartbeat, removes the abort listener) when the client disconnects (`request.signal` aborts) | controller closed once; no further enqueues after close | e2e-process | GAP

- [control-plane-api-routes.176] `GET /api/status/stream` a `controller.enqueue` that itself throws (e.g. because the underlying stream already errored) triggers `close()` defensively rather than propagating | stream closes gracefully | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.177] `GET /api/status/stream` `close()` is idempotent — calling it twice (e.g. once from abort, once from a subsequent enqueue failure) does not double-clear timers or throw on `controller.close()` | idempotent, no throw on `controller.close()` twice | unit | GAP

- [control-plane-api-routes.207] `detectClaudeAuth` layer (c): file-credentials fallback on darwin when Keychain is unprobeable but a flat creds file exists (a macOS user with only a file) | subscription from file, not "unknown" | unit | GAP (only the "no file" unprobeable case is tested on darwin; the "unprobeable Keychain + file present" combination isn't)
- [control-plane-api-routes.208] `detectClaudeAuth` layer (d): Keychain confirms item absent AND no file → confident unauthenticated (false), not unknown | authenticated false | unit | agents-health.test.ts
- [control-plane-api-routes.209] `detectClaudeAuth` layer (d): Linux with no creds file at all → unauthenticated (the file IS the store there, so absence is confident, not unknown) | authenticated false | unit | agents-health.test.ts
- [control-plane-api-routes.210] `detectClaudeAuth` layer (d): darwin Keychain unprobeable AND no file → honest unknown (null), never fabricated | authenticated null | unit | agents-health.test.ts
- [control-plane-api-routes.211] `detectClaudeAuth` honours `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` override for both settings.json and .credentials.json paths | override respected | unit | agents-health.test.ts ("Claude honours CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR...")
- [control-plane-api-routes.212] Windows: Claude creds read from `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\.credentials.json` (file store), NEVER the Keychain seam (proven via a call-counting probe) | keychain() never called on win32 | unit | agents-health.test.ts ("Claude: the Keychain seam is NEVER consulted on Windows")
- [control-plane-api-routes.213] Windows: `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` env and `settings.json` `apiKeyHelper` under the Windows config dir both still yield api_key | api_key | unit | agents-health.test.ts
- [control-plane-api-routes.214] Windows: no credentials file present → confident unauthenticated (false), the file IS the store there | authenticated false | unit | agents-health.test.ts ("Claude: NO file on Windows → confident unauthenticated")
- [control-plane-api-routes.215] `codexHome`/Codex auth: honours `CODEX_HOME` override, reads `auth.json` on the OS-correct path (incl. Windows `%USERPROFILE%\.codex\auth.json`) | override + path respected | unit | agents-health.test.ts (Windows + CODEX_HOME cases)
- [control-plane-api-routes.216] Codex keyring/auto credential mode (no `auth.json` on disk because creds live in the OS keyring) degrades to `authenticated:false`, never fabricated true | authenticated false | unit | agents-health.test.ts ("Codex keyring mode...degrades to not-authenticated")
- [control-plane-api-routes.217] `getAgentsHealth` full snapshot: both CLIs present+authed+versioned on a macOS subscription machine, versions normalized before reaching the caller | full object match | unit | agents-health.test.ts
- [control-plane-api-routes.218] `getAgentsHealth` reports `present:false, version:null` for an absent CLI, and never calls `version()` for it (guarded by `present ? ... : null`) | version null, no spurious probe | unit | agents-health.test.ts ("reports absent CLIs with null version and no version probe")
- [control-plane-api-routes.219] `getAgentsHealth` never leaks a raw access token anywhere in its serialized output, across both agents, on Windows | no token substrings in JSON | unit | agents-health.test.ts ("no token leak (cross-platform)")
- [control-plane-api-routes.220] `nodeKeychain` (real implementation, darwin-only): returns null immediately on non-darwin platforms without invoking `security` at all | null, no exec | unit | GAP (the real `nodeKeychain`/`runSecurity` functions are NOT exercised by any test — only the injected `HealthProbe.keychain` fake is used throughout agents-health.test.ts; the real macOS `security` shell-out path has zero test coverage)
- [control-plane-api-routes.221] `nodeKeychain` step 1 (`-w` secret read) succeeds → returns `{secret, exists:true}` | secret returned | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.222] `nodeKeychain` step 1 times out → returns null (undetectable) without trying step 2 | null | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.223] `nodeKeychain` step 1 fails/blocked (non-zero, not timeout) → falls to step 2 existence probe; step 2 exit 0 → `{secret:null, exists:true}` | exists true, secret null | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.224] `nodeKeychain` step 2 exit code 44 → `{secret:null, exists:false}` (definitively absent) | exists false | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.225] `nodeKeychain` step 2 an unexpected exit code (neither 0 nor 44) → null (undetectable) | null | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.226] `nodeKeychain` step 2 times out → null | null | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.227] `safeExec`/real `resolveOnPath`/`nodeHealthProbe.which`/`.version` — the REAL exec-based implementations (not the injected fakes) have no direct test coverage; only `resolveOnPath`'s platform branching is tested via an injected `exec` | GAP — real spawn behavior (timeout, non-zero exit, multi-line stdout trimming) untested | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.470] `getAgentsHealth` with `VIVICY_FAKE_MISSING_CLI=claude,codex` (dev override, read via `probe.env`, comma-separated) forces BOTH agents' ENTIRE health to confirmed-absent — `present:false, version:null, authenticated:false, authMethod:null, plan:null` — even on a machine where both CLIs are installed and authenticated; no auth/keychain signal leaks into the faked snapshot | both confirmed-absent | unit | agents-health.test.ts ("claude,codex forces BOTH to confirmed-absent on a fully installed+authed machine — no auth/keychain leak")
- [control-plane-api-routes.471] `VIVICY_FAKE_MISSING_CLI` with a single token (`claude` or `codex`) fakes only that CLI; the other keeps full real detection (present, version, auth) | one faked, one real | unit | agents-health.test.ts ("claude alone fakes only Claude — Codex keeps full real detection" + "codex alone fakes only Codex — Claude keeps full real detection")
- [control-plane-api-routes.472] `VIVICY_FAKE_MISSING_CLI` unset or empty string → detection identical to real behavior (the override parses to an empty set; `nodeHealthProbe.env` already maps empty env values to null) | real detection untouched | unit | agents-health.test.ts ("unset or empty value leaves real detection untouched")
- [control-plane-api-routes.473] `VIVICY_FAKE_MISSING_CLI` unknown tokens (e.g. `gemini`) are ignored silently; tokens are trimmed and lowercased before matching against the closed `claude`/`codex` set | tolerant parse, no throw | unit | agents-health.test.ts ("ignores unknown tokens silently and tolerates whitespace/case")
- [control-plane-api-routes.474] a faked CLI's detection is short-circuited BEFORE any probe — `which`/`version`/`keychain`/`readFile` are never invoked for it, so the real detection paths carry zero override logic | zero probe calls | unit | agents-health.test.ts ("never invokes which/version/keychain/readFile for a faked CLI — detection is short-circuited")

- [control-plane-api-routes.234] `POST /api/agents/update` an unexpected runner error (e.g. spawn EACCES) maps to 500 | 500 | integration | agents/update/route.test.ts ("returns 500 on an unexpected runner error")

- [control-plane-api-routes.236] `POST /api/agents/update` request body is a non-object (array/string/number) — zod's `z.object` rejects it as 400 | 400 | integration | GAP

- [control-plane-api-routes.239] `runAgentUpdate("codex", exec)` invokes ONLY `{cmd:"codex", args:["update"]}` | exact single call | unit | agents-update.test.ts

- [control-plane-api-routes.242] `runAgentUpdate` rejects non-string/object/undefined agent values | UnknownAgentError | unit | agents-update.test.ts ("rejects non-string / object agents")
- [control-plane-api-routes.243] `runAgentUpdate` surfaces a non-zero exit honestly: `ok:false`, exit code, stderr passed through | ok false | unit | agents-update.test.ts ("surfaces a non-zero exit honestly")
- [control-plane-api-routes.244] `isAgentKey` accepts exactly "claude"/"codex" and rejects everything else including empty string, null, numbers | boolean matrix | unit | agents-update.test.ts ("isAgentKey accepts only the two allow-listed keys")
- [control-plane-api-routes.245] `capBytes` returns short ASCII text unchanged when under budget | unchanged | unit | agents-update.test.ts ("returns short ASCII unchanged")
- [control-plane-api-routes.246] `capBytes` truncates at a byte budget WITHOUT splitting a multi-byte UTF-8 code point (drops the whole character rather than emitting U+FFFD) | no replacement char, byte length <= max | unit | agents-update.test.ts ("truncates at a byte budget without producing replacement chars")
- [control-plane-api-routes.247] `capBytes` keeps a multi-byte char only when it fits ENTIRELY within budget (boundary: exactly fits vs. one byte over) | exact-fit boundary correct | unit | agents-update.test.ts ("keeps a multibyte char only when it fits entirely")
- [control-plane-api-routes.248] `capBytes` preserves an astral/surrogate-pair emoji as a whole (4-byte UTF-8), dropping it entirely rather than splitting the surrogate pair when it doesn't fit | whole-or-nothing on surrogate pairs | unit | agents-update.test.ts ("preserves astral (surrogate-pair) emoji as a whole")
- [control-plane-api-routes.249] `capBytes` boundary: `max=0` returns an empty string (no character can ever fit) | "" | unit | GAP

Its timeout (5 min), `maxBuffer`, `windowsHide`, and error-vs-success branching (via the execFile callback) have zero test coverage | GAP — real child-process spawn/timeout/buffer-cap behavior untested | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.251] `nodeExec` a killed/timed-out process (execFile error with no numeric `.code`) resolves with `code: null`, not a thrown exception | code null, ok false | unit | GAP

### lib/dev-status-fs.ts

- [control-plane-api-routes.253] verdict ordering: DONE when every issue is done via a `done/` file | DONE | unit | dev-status-fs.test.ts (1)
- [control-plane-api-routes.254] verdict ordering: "STOPPED (last gate failed)" when some issues done AND any gate has failed | that verdict | unit | dev-status-fs.test.ts (2)
- [control-plane-api-routes.255] verdict ordering: "STOPPED (resume to continue)" when some done, no failing gate | that verdict | unit | dev-status-fs.test.ts (3)
- [control-plane-api-routes.256] verdict ordering: "NOT STARTED" when nothing is done | that verdict | unit | dev-status-fs.test.ts (4)
- [control-plane-api-routes.257] verdict ordering: a failing gate with ZERO progress still yields "STOPPED (last gate failed)", not "NOT STARTED" (fail branch doesn't require doneIds>0) | STOPPED (last gate failed) | unit | dev-status-fs.test.ts ("NOT STARTED even with a failing gate...")
- [control-plane-api-routes.258] verdict ordering: DONE takes precedence over a failing gate when all issues are complete | DONE | unit | dev-status-fs.test.ts ("DONE takes precedence over a failing gate")
- [control-plane-api-routes.259] verdict ordering: zero-issue index yields NOT STARTED, never DONE (DONE requires issues.length>0) | NOT STARTED | unit | dev-status-fs.test.ts ("NOT STARTED when there are zero issues")
- [control-plane-api-routes.260] per-issue done rule: an issue counts done purely via the ledger when EVERY graph_ref is verified, even with no `done/` dir at all | done via ledger | unit | dev-status-fs.test.ts (5)
- [control-plane-api-routes.261] per-issue done rule: partial verification (some but not all refs verified) does NOT count as done | not done | unit | dev-status-fs.test.ts ("does NOT count an issue done when only SOME...")
- [control-plane-api-routes.262] per-issue done rule: an issue with an EMPTY graph_refs array can never be done via the ledger path (refs.length>0 guard) | not done | unit | dev-status-fs.test.ts ("does NOT count an issue with an empty graph_refs list")
- [control-plane-api-routes.263] per-issue done rule: a `done/<id>.md` file wins even when the ledger shows the issue still in_progress | done | unit | dev-status-fs.test.ts ("a done/ file wins even when the ledger shows...unverified")
- [control-plane-api-routes.264] per-issue done rule: non-`.md` entries in the `done/` dir are ignored as markers | not done | unit | dev-status-fs.test.ts ("ignores non-.md entries in the done/ dir")
- [control-plane-api-routes.265] `active_items` from the ledger are surfaced verbatim in `status.active` | passthrough | unit | dev-status-fs.test.ts ("surfaces active_items verbatim")
- [control-plane-api-routes.266] gates counting: counts pass/fail across multiple files, ignores other statuses (e.g. "skipped") | {pass:2, fail:1} | unit | dev-status-fs.test.ts ("counts pass/fail across multiple gate files")
- [control-plane-api-routes.267] gates counting: zero gates when the gates dir is absent | {pass:0, fail:0} | unit | dev-status-fs.test.ts ("reports zero gates when the gates dir is absent")
- [control-plane-api-routes.268] quota fallback: empty agents map when quota-state.json missing (never fabricated) | {updated_at:null, agents:{}} | unit | dev-status-fs.test.ts (6)
- [control-plane-api-routes.269] quota fallback: real quota block passed through when present and well-formed | full block | unit | dev-status-fs.test.ts ("returns the real quota block when present")
- [control-plane-api-routes.270] quota fallback: malformed JSON in quota-state.json falls back to empty-agents, no throw | empty fallback | unit | dev-status-fs.test.ts ("falls back to an empty agents map when the quota file is malformed")
- [control-plane-api-routes.271] quota fallback: a quota object missing its `agents` key entirely repairs to the empty-agents fallback (not a half-populated object) | empty fallback | unit | dev-status-fs.test.ts ("repairs a quota object missing its agents map")
- [control-plane-api-routes.272] fully absent `.vivicy/development` tree yields a complete NOT STARTED snapshot with every field zeroed/emptied, no throw | full NOT STARTED snapshot | unit | dev-status-fs.test.ts ("returns a fully NOT STARTED snapshot...")
- [control-plane-api-routes.273] a malformed (non-JSON) `issue-index.json` falls back to `{issues:[]}` via `readJson`'s try/catch, not a throw | empty issues fallback | unit | GAP (readJson's fallback is exercised for quota-state.json but not explicitly for issue-index.json/progress-ledger.json)
- [control-plane-api-routes.274] a malformed (non-JSON) `progress-ledger.json` falls back to `{graph_item_states:[], active_items:[]}` | empty ledger fallback | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.275] `issues` field present but not an array (e.g. a stray object) falls back to `[]` via the `Array.isArray` guard | empty issues | unit | GAP

- [control-plane-api-routes.276] `readNotifications` returns `[]` when the log file doesn't exist | [] | unit | notifications.test.ts ("returns [] when the log is missing")
- [control-plane-api-routes.277] `readNotifications` returns `[]` when the log file exists but is empty | [] | unit | notifications.test.ts ("returns [] when the log is empty")
- [control-plane-api-routes.278] `readNotifications` reads well-formed lines oldest-first, skips blank lines and malformed/partial JSON lines without failing the whole read | 2 valid rows from 4 lines | unit | notifications.test.ts ("reads well-formed lines oldest-first and skips malformed/blank ones")
- [control-plane-api-routes.279] `appendNotification` creates the runtime dir AND the log file on the very first call (the legacy root when no project is selected; the per-project namespace `projects/<key>/` when one is) | dir+file created | unit | notifications.test.ts ("creates the runtime dir and log on the first call") — the per-project-namespace create branch is a partial GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.280] `appendNotification` stamps `id` and `ts` itself — caller input never supplies them | id/ts present, within time bounds | unit | notifications.test.ts (same test)
- [control-plane-api-routes.281] `appendNotification` appends without disturbing prior lines — one line per call, oldest-first, every line newline-terminated | 3 lines, trailing newline, no partial writes | unit | notifications.test.ts ("appends without disturbing prior lines")
- [control-plane-api-routes.282] `appendNotification` same-millisecond back-to-back calls get DISTINCT ids (the per-process counter), even though `ts` may collide | distinct ids | unit | notifications.test.ts ("same-instant appends get distinct ids")
- [control-plane-api-routes.283] `dismissNotifications([id])` flips `dismissed:true` on exactly the referenced id, leaves others untouched | 1 flipped | unit | notifications.test.ts ("flips dismissed:true on the referenced id...")
- [control-plane-api-routes.284] `dismissNotifications` dismisses exactly one of two rows sharing an identical `ts` (cross-process same-millisecond case), proving `id` — not `ts` — is the real key | 1 flipped, correct row | unit | notifications.test.ts ("dismisses exactly one of two rows sharing the same ts")
- [control-plane-api-routes.285] `dismissNotifications` falls back to matching on `ts` for a LEGACY row that predates the `id` field | 1 flipped via ts match | unit | notifications.test.ts ("falls back to ts matching for a legacy line")
- [control-plane-api-routes.286] `dismissNotifications()` with no args clears ALL notifications (the sidebar "clear all") | all flipped | unit | notifications.test.ts ("clears all when no refs are given")
- [control-plane-api-routes.287] `dismissNotifications` is idempotent: dismissing an already-dismissed id, or an unknown id, is a no-op returning 0 | 0, 0 | unit | notifications.test.ts ("is a no-op on an unknown id or an already-dismissed one")
- [control-plane-api-routes.288] `dismissNotifications` is a no-op (returns 0, no throw) when the log file doesn't exist yet | 0 | unit | notifications.test.ts ("is a no-op when the log does not exist yet")
- [control-plane-api-routes.289] `dismissNotifications` round-trips cleanly through `readNotifications` — the dismissed row reads back identical to the appended row plus `dismissed:true` | exact round-trip | unit | notifications.test.ts ("round-trips through readNotifications exactly like a fresh append")
- [control-plane-api-routes.290] `dismissNotifications` writes ZERO bytes / does not rewrite the file at all when `changed === 0` (no matching rows) — an optimization that also means an untouched file's mtime/inode is preserved | file unchanged | unit | GAP (the `if (changed > 0)` guard's negative branch — that NO write occurs — is not directly asserted, only that the return value is 0)
- [control-plane-api-routes.291] concurrent writers: two processes calling `appendNotification` "simultaneously" never interleave/corrupt each other's line (relies on POSIX O_APPEND atomicity) | no corruption | integration | GAP (this is stated as a design guarantee in the doc comment but never tested under actual concurrent/parallel writers, e.g. via Promise.all of multiple appends or child processes)

- [control-plane-api-routes.446] `getNotificationsPath` falls back to the legacy root `<runtime>/notifications.jsonl` when no project is selected | legacy root path | unit | notifications.test.ts (implicit — the suite runs on this branch but never asserts the path selection)
- [control-plane-api-routes.447] `migrateLegacyLog`: legacy root log present and NO project log yet → `renameSync` moves it into the project namespace, then a loud marker is appended | legacy file gone, project log = old lines + a `notifications_migrated` marker | integration | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.448] `migrateLegacyLog` MERGE branch: legacy root log AND a project log already exist → legacy lines PREPENDED to the project log, legacy removed, marker appended | project log = legacy + prior-project + marker; legacy removed | integration | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.449] `migrateLegacyLog` no-op when no legacy root log exists | project log untouched, no marker | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.450] `migrateLegacyLog` is best-effort: a throwing `renameSync`/merge is swallowed, leaving the legacy log in place and the project log starting fresh | no throw propagates; append proceeds | boundary | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.451] the migration marker is a loud notification (`event:"notifications_migrated"`, `stage:"runtime"`, level info) with a minted id | marker line present with those fields | unit | GAP
