### lib/settings.ts

- [control-plane-api-routes.298] `DEFAULT_SETTINGS` pins the latest models with documented default effort levels and fast off for both roles | exact match | unit | settings.test.ts ("pins the latest models with the documented default thinking levels")
- [control-plane-api-routes.299] each CLI's default model is the FIRST in its curated `MODEL_IDS` list (newest-first ordering contract) | first entry | unit | settings.test.ts ("each CLI's default model is the first in its curated list")
- [control-plane-api-routes.300] `modelCapability`/`modelSupportsFast` per researched model: full effort range + fast true/false correctly per generation (opus 4-6/4-7/4-8 fast; 4-5 not; gpt-5.5/5.4 fast; 5.4-mini/spark not) | matrix of exact booleans | unit | settings.test.ts ("declares the researched models with the right effort + fast support")
- [control-plane-api-routes.301] every listed model's declared `capability.efforts` is a SUBSET of its CLI's full union (`EFFORT_LEVELS`) — a self-consistency invariant over the whole `MODELS` table | subset holds for every model | unit | settings.test.ts ("every listed model declares a self-consistent capability")
- [control-plane-api-routes.302] a custom/unlisted model (`modelCapability` returns null) is fast-INCAPABLE by default but still gets a usable (CLI-union) effort set, never stranded | fast false, efforts = union | unit | settings.test.ts ("treats a custom (unlisted) model as fast-incapable...")
- [control-plane-api-routes.303] `isValidEffort` accepts only levels the SELECTED MODEL allows (not just the CLI-wide union) — e.g. a Codex-only level rejected for a Claude model even if it happens to share a name | strict per-model validation | unit | settings.test.ts ("accepts only the levels the SELECTED model allows")
- [control-plane-api-routes.304] `isValidEffort` on a model with NO effort control (spark) rejects every level including the empty string "" itself | always false | unit | settings.test.ts (same test, spark cases)
- [control-plane-api-routes.305] `isValidEffort` rejects a non-string effort value (e.g. a number) | false | unit | settings.test.ts (`isValidEffort("claude", "claude-opus-4-8", 5)`)
- [control-plane-api-routes.306] `normalizeSettings` repairs an incompatible model+effort combo to the model's default effort, while KEEPING a valid one for the other role untouched | repaired vs kept | unit | settings.test.ts ("normalizeSettings rejects an incompatible model+effort...")
- [control-plane-api-routes.307] `normalizeSettings` empties the effort string entirely for a model with no reasoning control (spark), and the result is still `isAgentCompatible` | effort "" | unit | settings.test.ts ("normalizeSettings empties the effort for a model with no reasoning control")
- [control-plane-api-routes.308] `normalizeSettings` PRESERVES a custom/unlisted model name (never silently replaced) while still validating its effort against the CLI-union fallback | custom model kept, effort valid | unit | settings.test.ts ("preserves a custom model but keeps a valid effort for it")
- [control-plane-api-routes.309] `normalizeSettings` strips `fast:true` on a fast-incapable model (both a known-incapable model AND a no-reasoning model like spark) | fast false | unit | settings.test.ts ("normalizeSettings strips fast on a fast-INcapable model")
- [control-plane-api-routes.310] `normalizeSettings` keeps `fast:true` on a genuinely fast-capable model+CLI pair | fast true | unit | settings.test.ts ("normalizeSettings keeps fast on a fast-capable model")
- [control-plane-api-routes.311] `withModel` switching to a fast-incapable model drops fast AND repairs effort to the new model's set (keeping a still-valid level rather than blindly resetting) | fast false, effort preserved if still valid | unit | settings.test.ts ("withModel drops fast when switching to a fast-incapable model")
- [control-plane-api-routes.312] `withModel` switching Codex frontier → spark drops BOTH fast and effort (spark has no reasoning control at all) | fast false, effort "" | unit | settings.test.ts (same test, second half)
- [control-plane-api-routes.313] `isAgentCompatible` rejects fast=true on a no-fast model, rejects a non-empty effort on a no-reasoning model, accepts a genuinely compatible combo | 3-way boolean matrix | unit | settings.test.ts ("isAgentCompatible rejects an impossible fast/effort combo")
- [control-plane-api-routes.314] `resolveAssignment` keeps a valid distinct (implementer≠reviewer) assignment as-is | unchanged | unit | settings.test.ts ("resolveAssignment keeps a valid distinct assignment")
- [control-plane-api-routes.315] `resolveAssignment` REPAIRS a same-CLI-for-both request to distinct CLIs, implementer's choice winning and reviewer flipped to the other CLI — proven for BOTH claude-claude and codex-codex collisions | repaired both ways | unit | settings.test.ts ("resolveAssignment repairs same-CLI-for-both to distinct CLIs")
- [control-plane-api-routes.316] `resolveAssignment` falls back to the default assignment entirely for an unknown provider string, or for `null` input | defaults | unit | settings.test.ts ("resolveAssignment falls back to defaults for unknown CLIs")
- [control-plane-api-routes.317] `normalizeSettings` NEVER lets one CLI hold both roles, verified both via a valid-but-swapped input and a same-CLI-collision input | isDistinctAssignment always true post-normalize | unit | settings.test.ts ("normalizeSettings never lets one CLI hold both roles")
- [control-plane-api-routes.318] `clampMaxParallel` bounds: exact min(1)/max(12) pass through, above-max clamps to 12, below-min(0) clamps to 1, a numeric STRING is coerced, a non-numeric string falls back to 1 | full boundary matrix | unit | settings.test.ts ("exposes the concurrency bounds as [1, 12]...")
- [control-plane-api-routes.319] `normalizeSettings` clamps `maxParallel` through the full range incl. negative, zero, exact bounds, way-over-cap, and a non-integer (2.7 → floored to 2) | full matrix | unit | settings.test.ts ("normalizeSettings clamps maxParallel to an integer in [1, 12]")
- [control-plane-api-routes.320] `agentDefaultsFor` returns each CLI's latest model + documented default effort + fast off | exact match | unit | settings.test.ts ("agentDefaultsFor returns each CLI's latest model...")
- [control-plane-api-routes.321] `isSettingsValid(DEFAULT_SETTINGS)` and `isSettingsValid(normalizeSettings({}))` are both true — the defaults and the empty-input normalization are self-consistent | true, true | unit | settings.test.ts ("normalized defaults are valid settings")
- [control-plane-api-routes.322] `allowUnsafeSkills` defaults to false and normalizes ANYTHING but strict boolean `true` (string "1", number 1, null) to false | false for all non-true inputs | unit | settings.test.ts ("defaults to false and normalizes anything but true to false")
- [control-plane-api-routes.323] `allowUnsafeSkills` persists through the settings-store round-trip (write then read) | true preserved | unit | settings.test.ts ("persists through the settings store round-trip")
- [control-plane-api-routes.324] `settingsToEnv` maps the default assignment to all documented env var names/values with fast off | exact env object | unit | settings.test.ts ("maps the default assignment to the dev-loop env vars")
- [control-plane-api-routes.325] `settingsToEnv` emits `"1"` for the fast flag only when BOTH fast is on AND the model supports it | "1"/"1" | unit | settings.test.ts ("emits the fast flag '1' only when fast is on AND the model supports it")
- [control-plane-api-routes.326] `settingsToEnv` NEVER emits fast="1" for an incapable model even when the (already-should-be-repaired) input claims fast:true — defense in depth re-check inside settingsToEnv itself | "0"/"0" | unit | settings.test.ts ("never emits fast '1' for a model that cannot do fast")
- [control-plane-api-routes.327] `settingsToEnv` carries `VIVICY_MAX_PARALLEL` clamped to [1,12] across representative values (3, 12, 0→1, 99→12) | clamped values | unit | settings.test.ts ("carries the concurrency knob, clamped to [1, 12]")
- [control-plane-api-routes.328] `settingsToEnv` with a SWAPPED assignment (codex implementer, claude reviewer): each CLI's own model/effort/fast values follow the CLI itself, not the role slot | per-CLI values follow the CLI | unit | settings.test.ts ("carries a swapped assignment...")
- [control-plane-api-routes.329] `isProvider` accepts only "claude"/"codex" | boolean | unit | GAP (no standalone test of isProvider as an exported function, though it's exercised transitively via resolveAssignment)
- [control-plane-api-routes.330] `otherProvider` maps claude↔codex exactly | swap | unit | GAP (only exercised transitively via resolveAssignment's repair path)

### lib/settings-store.ts

- [control-plane-api-routes.332] `getSettingsPath` resolves to `<runtimeDir>/settings.json` | exact path | unit | settings-store.test.ts ("points at settings.json inside the runtime dir")
- [control-plane-api-routes.333] `readSettings` returns DEFAULT_SETTINGS when the file doesn't exist (no file created as a side effect of reading) | defaults, no file | unit | settings-store.test.ts ("returns the defaults when the file is absent")
- [control-plane-api-routes.334] `readSettings` returns defaults on a corrupt/non-JSON file | defaults | unit | settings-store.test.ts ("returns the defaults on a corrupt (non-JSON) file")
- [control-plane-api-routes.335] `readSettings` normalizes a present-but-partial file: fills missing fields (e.g. reviewer entirely absent) and repairs invalid ones (bad effort) rather than returning raw | fully normalized doc | unit | settings-store.test.ts ("normalizes a present-but-partial file")
- [control-plane-api-routes.336] `readSettings` on an already-valid, already-normalized file on disk returns it unchanged (idempotent normalize) | same doc | unit | settings-store.test.ts ("normalizes a valid present file and keeps its valid choices")
- [control-plane-api-routes.337] `writeSettings` normalizes input, persists it, and returns the SAME normalized document that a subsequent `readSettings` call reads back | round-trip equality | unit | settings-store.test.ts ("normalizes the input, returns the normalized document...")
- [control-plane-api-routes.338] `writeSettings` returns the NORMALIZED (repaired) document, never the raw invalid input, and what's on disk matches exactly what's returned | repaired output, disk matches return | unit | settings-store.test.ts ("returns the normalized document, never the raw (invalid) input")
- [control-plane-api-routes.339] `writeSettings` creates the runtime dir on demand (even if deleted just before the call) and writes a trailing-newline-terminated JSON file | dir created, trailing `}\n` | unit | settings-store.test.ts ("creates the runtime dir on demand and writes a trailing-newline-terminated file")
- [control-plane-api-routes.340] `writeSettings({})` (completely empty input) normalizes to the full default-shaped document, and reads back identically | full defaults | unit | settings-store.test.ts ("normalizes a completely empty input to the full default-shaped document")
- [control-plane-api-routes.341] concurrent `writeSettings` calls from two processes (a plausible scenario given the CLI + UI both writing settings) — the last writer wins with a full-file overwrite, no partial-write corruption given `writeFileSync` is not atomic-rename-based here (unlike the run-state lock's `wx` claim) | last-write-wins, no corruption asserted | integration | GAP (no test exercises concurrent writers on the settings store; unlike run-state.json this file has NO atomic-rename or lock semantics at all — a genuine gap in a shared multi-process design)

### lib/transcript.ts

- [control-plane-api-routes.342] `parseTranscript` Claude JSONL: detects format "claude" (via presence of `type:"assistant"`/`"user"` lines) and normalizes user/thinking/tool_use/text entries in order | 4 entries in order | unit | transcript.test.ts ("detects the claude format and normalizes entries")
- [control-plane-api-routes.343] `parseTranscript` Claude JSONL: attaches a `tool_result`'s content back onto the matching `tool_use` entry by `tool_use_id`, and marks the LAST assistant entry `final:true` | tool.output set; assistant.final true | unit | transcript.test.ts ("attaches tool output by tool_use_id and marks the final assistant")
- [control-plane-api-routes.344] `parseTranscript` Codex JSONL: detects format "codex" (via `session_meta`/`response_item` types) and normalizes user/reasoning/function_call/function_call_output/message entries | 4 entries in order | unit | transcript.test.ts ("detects the codex format and normalizes entries")
- [control-plane-api-routes.345] `parseTranscript` Codex JSONL: `function_call_output` content is attached to the matching `function_call` tool entry by `call_id` | tool.output set | unit | transcript.test.ts (same test, `tool` assertion)
- [control-plane-api-routes.346] `parseTranscript` Codex de-duplication: when BOTH an `event_msg`(reasoning/agent_message) AND a `response_item` carry the SAME semantic content, the `response_item` wins and the `event_msg` duplicate is suppressed via the `riHasReasoning`/`riHasAssistant` pre-scan flags | no duplicate thinking/assistant entries | unit | GAP (the pre-scan suppression logic — riHasReasoning/riHasAssistant — is not directly exercised by a test that includes BOTH an event_msg and a matching response_item for the same content; the existing Codex fixture only has one source per entry kind)
- [control-plane-api-routes.347] `parseTranscript` returns `{entries:[], format:"unknown"}` for empty input or input with no recognizable type markers | empty/unknown | unit | transcript.test.ts ("returns unknown format and no entries for empty/garbage input")
- [control-plane-api-routes.348] `parseTranscript` skips individual malformed JSON lines within an otherwise valid file, rather than failing the whole parse | malformed lines skipped, valid ones kept | unit | GAP (only "all garbage" is tested — `"not json\n{bad"` — not a MIX of valid and invalid lines)
- [control-plane-api-routes.349] `parseTranscript` drops consecutive duplicate entries of the SAME kind+text (e.g. Codex's double user_message emission) | duplicates collapsed to 1 | unit | transcript.test.ts ("drops consecutive duplicate entries")
- [control-plane-api-routes.350] `parseTranscript` drops consecutive duplicate TOOL entries (same name+input), a distinct de-dup branch from the text-based one | duplicates collapsed | unit | GAP (only the text-based `sameText` de-dup branch is tested; the `sameTool` branch — same name+input for two consecutive tool entries — has no dedicated test)
- [control-plane-api-routes.351] `parseTranscript` marks final:true on the LAST assistant entry even when non-consecutive entries of other kinds follow it in the source, and correctly picks NO final marker at all when there are zero assistant entries | final marked correctly / absent | unit | GAP (no case with zero assistant entries, verifying no crash/no marker)
- [control-plane-api-routes.352] `parseTranscript` Claude: an assistant message with `content` that is neither a string nor an array (e.g. `null`/object) is handled by `asText` fallback in `msgText`/`reasoningText` without throwing | no throw, stringified fallback | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.353] `parseTranscript` Claude: a `tool_use` block whose `id` is absent doesn't get registered in `toolById`, so a later `tool_result` referencing a nonexistent id is silently dropped (no crash) | tool entry with no output; no throw | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.354] `transcriptUrl` builds `/api/transcript/<ref>` and strips ANY number of leading slashes from the ref | leading slashes stripped | unit | transcript.test.ts ("builds an API url and strips leading slashes")
- [control-plane-api-routes.355] `transcriptName` derives just the final path segment as the display name | filename only | unit | transcript.test.ts ("derives the file name from a ref")
- [control-plane-api-routes.356] `transcriptName` on a ref with NO slash at all returns the ref itself unchanged (fallback `?? ref` never actually reached since `split("/").slice(-1)[0]` always yields something for a non-empty string, but an EMPTY string ref exercises the `?? ref` fallback) | edge case: empty-string ref | unit | GAP

### app/api/transcript/[...path]/route.ts

- [control-plane-api-routes.357] `GET /api/transcript/*` happy path: serves the file contents with `Content-Type: application/x-ndjson; charset=utf-8`, status 200 | 200, correct content-type, body = file contents | integration | GAP (NO test file exists for this route at all — a complete coverage gap)
- [control-plane-api-routes.358] `GET /api/transcript/*` rejects a path containing `..` (traversal attempt) with 400 "bad transcript path", before ever touching the filesystem | 400 | integration | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.359] `GET /api/transcript/*` rejects any path that doesn't start with `.vivicy/development/transcripts/` with 400 | 400 | integration | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.360] `GET /api/transcript/*` rejects any path that doesn't end with `.jsonl` (e.g. `.json`, `.txt`, no extension) with 400 | 400 | integration | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.361] `GET /api/transcript/*` returns 404 "transcript not found" when no project/target is selected (`getTargetRoot()` returns null) — deliberately the SAME 404 signal as a genuinely absent file, not a distinct error, so the client can't distinguish "no project" from "file missing" | 404 | integration | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.362] `GET /api/transcript/*` defense-in-depth: even after the string-prefix check passes, the RESOLVED absolute path is re-verified to live under the target's `transcripts` dir (catches a prefix-check bypass via a crafted relative segment that still starts with the right string but resolves elsewhere) — rejected with 400 if it escapes | 400 | integration | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.363] `GET /api/transcript/*` a path that resolves to EXACTLY the transcripts directory itself (no filename) — allowed by the boundary check (`absolute !== transcriptsDir` is false, so it passes that guard) but then fails to read as a file — returns 404, not 400 or 500 | 404 | integration | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.364] `GET /api/transcript/*` a well-formed path pointing to a genuinely nonexistent file (but under the right directory, right extension) returns 404 "transcript not found" (readFile ENOENT caught) | 404 | integration | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.365] `GET /api/transcript/*` URL-encoded path segments (e.g. spaces, unicode filenames) are correctly decoded via `decodeURIComponent` before the allowlist checks and the filesystem read | decoded path used consistently | integration | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.366] `GET /api/transcript/*` a malformed percent-encoding in a path segment (e.g. a lone `%`) causes `decodeURIComponent` to throw — route has NO try/catch around this call, so this would be an unhandled 500 rather than a clean 400 | unhandled exception → Next's default 500, not the route's structured error | integration | GAP (security/robustness-relevant: an untrusted path segment can crash the handler)
- [control-plane-api-routes.367] `GET /api/transcript/*` empty `params.path` array (should not normally be routable given the catch-all segment, but defensively: `(segments ?? []).join("/")` on `[]` yields `""`, which fails the `.startsWith` check) → 400 | 400 | integration | GAP

- [control-plane-api-routes.368] (type-only module — no runtime logic) `NodeStatus` is aliased to `OverlayStatus`; a compile-time contract only, not independently unit-testable beyond a `tsc` typecheck | n/a | unit | GAP — not applicable as a runtime test; flagged only because the file was in-scope and has zero associated test file (by design, being types-only)

### lib/type-guards.ts

- [control-plane-api-routes.369] `isRecord` returns true for a plain object literal | true | unit | GAP — no test file exists for this trivial but load-bearing guard
- [control-plane-api-routes.370] `isRecord` returns false for `null` | false | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.371] `isRecord` returns false for an array (arrays are typeof "object" but explicitly excluded) | false | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.372] `isRecord` returns false for primitives (string/number/boolean/undefined) | false | unit | GAP

### Area cross-notes

- **CLI/API parity:** `lib/control.ts`'s header doc explicitly states the Next API routes and the `vivicy` CLI (`factory/cli.ts`, out of this area's file list) are two independent clients of the SAME control-plane functions and the SAME on-disk state (run-state.json, extraction-status.json, CR registry, cr-apply reports). No test in this area cross-checks that a CLI-started run (which would write run-state.json directly via `cli.ts`, not through `lib/control.ts`) is correctly picked up as `run_active:true` by `readDevStatus` invoked from the UI route — this parity claim rests entirely on both clients reading the identical file path/schema, which `lib/control.test.ts` and the route tests do not jointly verify (the CLI script itself is out of scope for this area, but the SHARED FILE CONTRACT would be a natural integration point). GAP.
- **Two independent single-run locks, two different concurrency-safety mechanisms:** the supervisor lock (`run-state.json`, atomic `wx` + pid liveness) and the skills-install lock (`skills-install.lock`, same `wx`+pid pattern) are structurally identical but exercised with very asymmetric test depth — the supervisor lock has direct tests for "holds atomically before spawn returns" and "releases on spawn throw" (control-plane-api-routes.13, .28), while the skills lock has NEITHER (control-plane-api-routes.74, .76 are GAPs). Since `startSkillsInstall` is reachable both directly and via `retry-stage` stage="skills", a lock bug there would surface identically in both call paths and neither exercises the raw lock file.
- **`settings.json` has no lock/atomicity at all**, unlike run-state.json and skills-install.lock — `writeSettings` does a plain `writeFileSync` with no `wx`/rename-swap. Given both the UI (`PUT /api/settings`) and (presumably) a CLI settings verb could write concurrently, this is a real asymmetry against the two locked files in the same module family, and it's entirely untested (control-plane-api-routes.341).
- **Route response-shape inconsistency across the area:** most `control/*` routes wrap payloads in `{ok, ...}`; `app/api/status/route.ts` does NOT (`readDevStatus` payload returned raw, and its error branch also omits `ok`). Any shared client-side response parser assuming a uniform `{ok:boolean}` envelope across `/api/status` and `/api/control/*` would break silently — this cross-route shape contract has no test anywhere verifying the two families deliberately diverge (or asserting the divergence is intentional and stable).
- **`getSpawner()` fake-vs-real switch (`VIVICY_FAKE_SPAWN=1`) is invisible to every route/lib test in this area** — all tests inject a hand-built fake `Spawner` directly into `lib/control.ts` functions, never through `getSpawner()`/`lib/spawner.ts`. The E2E dry-run fake (`fakeSpawner` in `lib/spawner.ts`) that backs the "no real agents" demo path — including its `dev-status.ts`/`extract-issues.ts`/`vivi-turn.ts` script-name dispatch and its own file side effects (writing a fake extraction-status.json, a fake vivi-turn reply file) — has ZERO unit coverage in this area (it's arguably e2e-only territory, but no e2e spec.ts under this area's file list touches it either). GAP.
- **Notification event-name contract is spread across three routes** (`extract`, `retry-stage`, `crs/decide`) each inventing its own `stage`/`event` vocabulary (e.g. "started"/"green"/"blocked"/"blocked_on_unverified_spikes"/"refused_empty_canonical" for extract; "retry_<stage>_started"/"retry_extract_green"/"retry_extract_blocked" for retry-stage; "approved_applied"/"approved_apply_blocked"/"rejected"/"decide_error" for crs/decide) with NO shared enum/type — a typo in any event string would be silently accepted by `appendNotification`'s untyped `event: string` field and never caught by a type-checker. Nothing in this area tests that a UI notification-list renderer's known-event-icon mapping (out of scope, presumably in a component file) stays in sync with this ad-hoc string vocabulary across all three routes.
- **`decideCr`'s `decidedBy` hardcoding is a security-relevant provenance guarantee** ("the route always passes owner:ui, never trusting request input") stated only in a comment, with zero test proving a malicious/malformed body field named `decidedBy` is ignored server-side (control-plane-api-routes.165). This is exactly the kind of implicit contract that regresses silently if the route is refactored to spread the body into the `decideCr` input.
- **Total absence of route tests for three of the ten `app/api/*` route files in scope**: `crs/route.ts`, `crs/decide/route.ts`, and `transcript/[...path]/route.ts` have NO `route.test.ts` at all (confirmed via `find`), unlike every sibling route in `control/*`. `status/route.ts`, `status/stream/route.ts`, `start/route.ts`, `stop/route.ts`, `resume/route.ts`, and `agents/health/route.ts` are likewise untested at the route level (only their underlying lib functions have unit tests) — this is the single largest concentration of GAPs in the area.

### Adversarial additions


- [control-plane-api-routes.381] `app/api/control/retry-stage/route.ts` on an unexpected (non-ControlError) throw from ANY of the three stage dispatches, the error notification's `event` field is literally `retry_${stage}_error` (e.g. `retry_dev_error`, `retry_skills_error`) — a THIRD distinct naming convention beyond what the area cross-notes document (which only lists the success-path events `retry_<stage>_started`/`retry_extract_green`/`retry_extract_blocked`); this failure-path event name is untested anywhere | notification event exactly `retry_<stage>_error` | integration | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.382] `app/api/control/retry-stage/route.ts` a ControlError thrown from ANY dispatch ALSO appends `retry_${stage}_error` (same event name as the generic-throw branch above, both routes through the same catch block) — not a distinct name per error class | same event name for ControlError vs generic throw | integration | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.383] `app/api/control/retry-stage/route.ts` `stage: "extract"` dispatch never calls `startSkillsInstall` or `startSupervisor` (cross-stage isolation) — only asserted for the `dev`/`skills` dispatches in the existing suite, not for `extract` itself | zero calls to the other two verbs | integration | retry-stage/route.test.ts (partially — asserts `startSupervisor` not called on extract dispatch, but never asserts `startSkillsInstall` not called on the extract path)

- [control-plane-api-routes.384] `app/api/control/crs/decide/route.ts` body validation is a SINGLE combined `if` (`!idRegex.test(id) || badDecision`) — a request with a VALID id but an invalid decision, and a request with an INVALID id but a valid decision, both fall into the identical generic 400 message with no way to tell which field was wrong from the response body | 400, same generic error message for either bad field | integration | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.385] `app/api/control/crs/decide/route.ts` `body.id` defaults to `""` when not a string at all (`typeof body.id === "string" ? body.id : ""`), so a non-string id (number, object, array, null) never reaches `decideCr` — always rejected by the regex test on the empty string before any spawn | 400, 0 calls to decideCr | integration | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.386] `app/api/control/crs/decide/route.ts` a ControlError thrown by `decideCr` NEVER produces a 500 — the catch block's `error instanceof ControlError` check always returns 422, so `spawn_failed`-coded ControlErrors (e.g. from `classifyDecisionError`'s catch-all branch) also surface as 422, not 500, unlike the generic Error path just below it | 422 even for a ControlError coded spawn_failed | integration | GAP (this route's error-status mapping never distinguishes ControlError codes the way start/stop/resume do — ALL ControlError codes here get 422, none get 409)
- [control-plane-api-routes.387] `app/api/control/crs/decide/route.ts` on the unexpected-throw (non-ControlError, non-Error) path, e.g. a thrown string or thrown `undefined`, the message falls back to `"decision failed"` — not exercised by any existing test (only `Error` instances and `ControlError` instances are used as mocked throws elsewhere in this codebase's route tests) | 500, message "decision failed" | integration | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.388] `app/api/control/crs/decide/route.ts` id regex `^CR-\d{4}$` rejects lowercase (`cr-0001`), 3-digit (`CR-001`), 5-digit (`CR-00001`), and a valid-looking id with trailing content (`CR-0001x`, since `$` anchors end-of-string strictly after 4 digits) — none of these boundary shapes are tested at the ROUTE level (only `listChangeRequests`' analogous internal regex is tested in control.test.ts, a different code path) | 400 for every malformed shape | integration | GAP

- [control-plane-api-routes.389] `app/api/status/stream/route.ts` the heartbeat interval (`setInterval(..., POLL_INTERVAL_MS * 5)`) is never cleared until `close()` runs — if `tick()`'s recursive `setTimeout` chain is somehow still scheduled after `close()` (e.g. a `tick` in flight when abort fires), the in-flight `tick`'s own `send()` call correctly no-ops via the `closed` guard, but nothing in this codebase's test suite proves the heartbeat/timer interaction under a genuine abort mid-tick (an async gap between `await readDevStatus(...)` resolving and the `if (!closed) timer = setTimeout(...)` check) | no timer leak, no post-abort enqueue | e2e-process | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.390] `app/api/status/stream/route.ts` `readDevStatus` synchronously throwing a NON-Error value (e.g. throwing a plain string) inside `tick()` is caught by `catch (error)` and falls back to `"status failed"` via `error instanceof Error ? ... : "status failed"` — untested edge (every existing usage of readDevStatus mocks throw real Error/ControlError instances) | data frame `{error:"status failed"}` | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.391] `app/api/status/stream/route.ts` the route accepts NO abort at all for its whole lifetime (a client that never disconnects) — timers accumulate call after call indefinitely; nothing bounds total stream lifetime (no max-duration cutoff), which is a legitimate resource-exhaustion edge for a long-lived local dev server but is completely unspecified/untested | unbounded stream lifetime, no cap | e2e-process | GAP

- [control-plane-api-routes.392] `lib/control.ts` `claimRunLock`'s SECOND `writeFileSync` retry (after clearing a stale lock) uses the SAME `placeholder` object reference/body computed BEFORE `isRunActive` ran — since `isRunActive` can itself call `clearRunState()` (removing the file) as a side effect, the retry write always targets a freshly-absent path; but if `isRunActive` returns true (lock still live) and throws, the placeholder's `started_at` timestamp from the FIRST claim attempt is never reused or exposed anywhere in a test asserting the exact discarded state is safely dropped (no partial/dangling write survives a refused claim) | no dangling partial lock state after a refused claim | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.393] `lib/control.ts` `startSupervisor`/`startSkillsInstall` both resolve `factoryRoot`/`resolveScript` (which does an `existsSync` disk check) AFTER `resolveContext()` but the ORDER of the `existsSync(targetRoot)` check vs. `resolveScript` (script-exists check) matters: a target root that doesn't exist AND a missing script both being true simultaneously always surfaces `missing_target` first (never `missing_script`) — this check-ordering precedence is implied by reading the source top-to-bottom but has no test that sets up BOTH failure conditions at once to confirm which ControlError code wins | missing_target wins over missing_script when both are true | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.394] `lib/control.ts` `decideCr` when `decision` is an invalid value that is nonetheless a non-empty string (e.g. `"maybe"`), the thrown `ControlError` message interpolates the raw untrusted string directly into the error message (`` `invalid decision "${decision}" (expected approved|rejected)` ``) — not a security issue (message only, no injection surface) but the interpolation of arbitrary-length/control-character input into a message that flows to notifications/logs is untested for pathological values (e.g. a decision string containing newlines or extremely long text) | message safely contains the raw string, no crash | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.395] `lib/control.ts` `readRunState` — a lock file that parses as valid JSON but is a JSON ARRAY or a JSON primitive (e.g. `readFileSync` returns `"42"` or `"[]"`) rather than an object is cast via `as RunState` with NO runtime shape validation; callers (`isRunActive`, `claimRunLock`) then read `.pid`/`.started_at` off it, which for a primitive/array yields `undefined` — `spawner.isAlive(undefined)` behavior on a non-numeric pid is unspecified and untested | no crash; either treated as stale/inactive or throws inside isAlive — behavior unverified | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.396] `lib/control.ts` `listChangeRequests` sorts filenames via plain `Array.prototype.sort()` (lexicographic, not numeric) BEFORE filtering — so display order for `CR-0002.md`, `CR-0010.md`, `CR-0001.md` is purely by filename string, and a mix of different zero-padding widths (if the registry ever contained a legacy `CR-1.md`-style name, itself excluded by the id regex but still affecting sort position among files that ARE lowercased/compared) is unexercised; more concretely, no test asserts the sort key is the FILENAME (not the parsed `id` field), so a file literally named `bbb-CR-0001.md` (invalid filename but valid frontmatter id) would sort by "bbb..." not "CR-0001" | display order follows filename, not frontmatter id | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.397] `lib/control.ts` `parseFrontmatter`'s key regex `^([a-z_]+):\s*(.*)$` requires lowercase-only unquoted keys — a frontmatter line with an uppercase or mixed-case key (e.g. `Title: Foo`) or a key containing a hyphen (`created-at: ...`) is silently ignored (falls to `continue`), meaning such a CR would get its defaulted values (title falls back to id, etc.) rather than an error — untested key-shape edge | field silently defaulted when key doesn't match `[a-z_]+` | unit | GAP

- [control-plane-api-routes.398] `lib/dev-status-fs.ts` `readDevStatusFromDisk` when `issue-index.json` exists and parses but `issues` is present and IS an array yet contains entries missing the required `id` field entirely (`{}` instead of `{id: "..."}`) — `issue.id` is `undefined`, `doneFiles.includes("undefined.md")` is false, and the issue flows through as "remaining" with `id: undefined`; downstream JSON serialization would render `null` for that field — untested malformed-entry shape | issue surfaces with id undefined, doesn't crash, unclear if desired | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.399] `lib/dev-status-fs.ts` `readDevStatusFromDisk`'s gates-counting step calls `readJson` per gate file with a fallback of `{}` (no `status` key) — a gate JSON file that is valid JSON but omits `status` entirely counts toward NEITHER pass NOR fail (silently invisible in the tally, not an error) — this "vanishes from both counters" behavior for a well-formed-but-incomplete gate record has no dedicated test (existing tests cover "skipped" status explicitly per the matrix, but not a MISSING status field) | gate counted in neither pass nor fail | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.400] `lib/dev-status-fs.ts` the `doneDir` readdir + `.md` filter, when `doneDir` exists but is actually a FILE (not a directory) — an unusual but possible on-disk state (e.g. a stray `done` file where a `done/` dir is expected) — `readdirSync` on a file throws `ENOTDIR`, which is NOT caught anywhere in this function (no try/catch around the `readdirSync(doneDir)` call, unlike the JSON reads which go through `readJson`'s try/catch) | unhandled throw propagates out of readDevStatusFromDisk | unit | GAP (a real robustness gap: this function has no defensive read for the `done`/`gates` directory listings, only for the JSON file contents)
- [control-plane-api-routes.401] `lib/dev-status-fs.ts` same ENOTDIR/unhandled-throw exposure applies identically to the `gatesDir` readdir call — a `gates` path that is a plain file rather than a directory crashes `readDevStatusFromDisk` uncaught | unhandled throw | unit | GAP

- [control-plane-api-routes.402] `lib/notifications.ts` `dismissNotifications` — a target `Set` built from `refs` containing DUPLICATE entries (e.g. `["id-1", "id-1"]`) still only flips the one matching row once (Set de-dupes), so `changed` counts 1, not 2 — no test passes a refs array with a repeated id to confirm the count isn't inflated | changed === 1 for a doubled ref, not 2 | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.403] `lib/notifications.ts` `appendNotification`'s `nextId()` counter (`seq`) is MODULE-LEVEL, persisting across every call within the same process for the lifetime of the Node process/test run — a test that imports the module fresh via `vi.resetModules()` between assertions would see the counter reset, but tests that DON'T isolate modules share state across `it()` blocks; nothing in notifications.test.ts documents/asserts monotonic-across-tests id uniqueness as an explicit invariant beyond the "same instant" case, so a subtly-relied-upon shared module state is unverified as a design choice vs. accidental coupling between test cases | ids remain unique/monotonic across the whole test-file run | unit | GAP (test-infrastructure risk, not just app behavior)
- [control-plane-api-routes.404] `lib/notifications.ts` `readNotifications` on a log file whose LAST line has no trailing newline (a partial/interrupted final write, e.g. process killed mid-`appendFileSync`) still parses correctly since `.split("\n")` handles a non-newline-terminated final segment — but if that partial line is a TRUNCATED JSON object (cut off mid-write), it's indistinguishable from "malformed" and silently skipped, which is probably desired but is a genuinely different failure mode (torn write) from the "garbage line" case already tested — no test simulates a truncated-mid-object last line specifically | truncated last line skipped, no throw, no data loss for OTHER lines | unit | GAP

- [control-plane-api-routes.405] `lib/settings.ts` `normalizeSettings` — when BOTH `raw.implementer.provider` and `raw.reviewer.provider` are the SAME invalid/unknown string (e.g. both `"gemini"`), `resolveAssignment` falls both back to their own per-role DEFAULT (implementer→claude default, reviewer→codex default per `DEFAULT_SETTINGS`) BEFORE the same-CLI-collision check — since claude≠codex the collision-repair branch never fires; this "both invalid falls back to defaults, which happen to already be distinct" path is a different code path from "both valid but identical" and is untested | implementer claude-default, reviewer codex-default, no repair triggered | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.406] `lib/settings.ts` `coerceAgent` — `raw.model` that is a string of ONLY whitespace (`"   "`) fails the `.trim().length > 0` check and falls back to `DEFAULT_MODEL[provider]`, exactly like an absent model — but a model string with LEADING/TRAILING whitespace around an otherwise-valid known model id (e.g. `"  claude-opus-4-8  "`) is trimmed and then looked up — is the TRIMMED value what's persisted (yes, per `raw.model.trim()`), and does `modelCapability` then find it as a known model — untested whitespace-trimming-then-lookup interaction | trimmed value persisted and correctly matched against MODELS | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.407] `lib/settings.ts` `clampMaxParallel` — a `NaN`-producing string input like `"abc123"` (non-numeric but non-empty) — `Number("abc123")` is `NaN`, `Math.floor(NaN)` is `NaN`, `Number.isFinite(NaN)` is false → falls back to `MIN_PARALLEL` (1); but a numeric-LOOKING string with trailing garbage like `"5abc"` also yields `NaN` via `Number()` (stricter than `parseInt`) — both collapse to 1, untested distinction from a clean numeric string | both non-numeric-ish strings clamp to 1 | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.408] `lib/settings.ts` `settingsToEnv` — when `implementer.provider === reviewer.provider` (an invariant violation that should never reach this function post-normalization, but `settingsToEnv` takes a raw `AgentsSettings` with NO internal re-validation of `isDistinctAssignment`) — `byProvider.claude` and `byProvider.codex` would BOTH resolve from the SAME ternary branch (`implementer.provider === "claude" ? implementer : reviewer`), meaning if both roles claim "claude", `byProvider.codex` incorrectly falls back to `reviewer` (itself claude) rather than there being an actual codex block — `settingsToEnv` has no defensive assertion here and silently produces env vars for a "codex" that's actually a claude config; untested defense-in-depth gap since callers (`startSupervisor`) always read normalized settings, but `settingsToEnv` is exported and independently callable | either an assertion/guard, or documented silent misbehavior on a same-CLI input bypassing normalization | unit | GAP

- [control-plane-api-routes.409] `lib/agents-health.ts` `parseCodexAuth` — `OPENAI_API_KEY` present as an EMPTY string (`""`) — `hasApiKey` requires `.length > 0`, so an empty string does NOT count as an api key, correctly falling through to check `auth_mode`/tokens — this exact empty-string boundary for the JSON field (as opposed to the env var, which is separately guarded by `HealthProbe.env`'s own empty-string handling) is untested | empty string OPENAI_API_KEY field does not trigger api_key | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.410] `lib/agents-health.ts` `detectClaudeAuth` — `probe.env("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY")` returning an empty string is impossible via the real `HealthProbe.env` contract (which returns null for empty), but a FAKE probe injected in a test could return `""` directly bypassing that contract — `if (probe.env(...))` treats `""` as falsy so this degenerate fake-probe input would NOT trigger api_key (falls through correctly) — the real-vs-fake contract boundary here (does every test's fake probe correctly emulate "empty means null"?) is worth an explicit assertion but has none | falls through correctly even if a malformed fake probe returns "" | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.411] `lib/agents-health.ts` `runSecurity`'s `err.status` narrowing — an execFileSync throw where `error.status` is a non-integer or negative number (unlikely from a real shell-out, but the type is `unknown` cast) would still pass `typeof err.status === "number"` and be used directly as the SecurityRun code, which is then compared with strict `=== 0`/`=== 44` in `nodeKeychain` — no test constructs a thrown error object with a non-standard `.status` shape (e.g. a string status, missing status) to confirm the `null` fallback path | code falls back to null for a malformed thrown error shape | unit | GAP

- [control-plane-api-routes.412] `lib/transcript.ts` `parseClaude` — a `tool_use` block's `id` field is present but is NOT a string (e.g. a number) — `if (b.id)` is truthy for a non-zero number, so `toolById.set(b.id as string, tool)` would set a NUMERIC key cast to string type but stored as the actual number at runtime (Map keys aren't coerced) — a later `tool_result` with a STRING `tool_use_id` would then fail to match via `toolById.get(string)` even though `id` "looked like" it should match, since the Map holds a number key — untested type-coercion mismatch | tool_result silently fails to attach output when tool_use.id was non-string | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.413] `lib/transcript.ts` `parseCodex`'s `riHasReasoning`/`riHasAssistant` pre-scan uses `.some(...)` over ALL lines including ones with malformed/missing `payload` — since `(o.payload as Record<string,unknown>)?.type` uses optional chaining, a `response_item` entry with `payload: null` or `payload` absent safely evaluates to `undefined !== "reasoning"`, never throining — but this defensive-null-payload path in the pre-scan itself (as opposed to the main loop's `if (!p) continue`) is never independently exercised | no throw on a response_item with null/absent payload during the pre-scan | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.414] `lib/transcript.ts` `transcriptUrl` — a `ref` that itself contains a `?` or `#` (URL-significant characters, e.g. a filename with a literal `#` in a legacy path) is NOT escaped/encoded at all before being concatenated into the URL string — only leading slashes are stripped; a caller relying on `transcriptUrl` to produce a fetch-safe URL for a ref with special characters gets a broken/ambiguous URL, untested | no encoding of special characters in the ref segment | unit | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.415] `lib/transcript.ts` `parseTranscript`'s final-line-JSON parse loop (the outer `for (const raw of jsonl.split("\n"))`) treats a BOM-prefixed first line (`﻿{"type":...}`) as unparseable JSON (JSON.parse chokes on a leading BOM character before `{`), silently dropping the first line of a transcript file saved with a byte-order mark — untested encoding edge that would silently lose the first event of an otherwise well-formed transcript | first line silently dropped when BOM-prefixed, no error surfaced to the caller | unit | GAP

- [control-plane-api-routes.416] `app/api/transcript/[...path]/route.ts` a `rel` value equal to the empty string after decode (covered as .367 for empty `params.path`) is DISTINCT from a `rel` that is just `.vivicy` or `.vivicy/development` (a prefix of the allowed path but not the full `transcripts/` segment) — `.startsWith(".vivicy/development/transcripts/")` correctly rejects any of these shorter prefixes, but no test enumerates the several plausible "almost right" prefix-truncation attempts (`.vivicy/development/transcripts` with no trailing slash, `.vivicy/development/transcript/x.jsonl` singular-typo) | 400 for every under-qualified prefix variant | integration | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.417] `app/api/transcript/[...path]/route.ts` a path using BACKSLASH segments (meaningful on Windows path resolution, e.g. a segment containing `..\\`) is not caught by the `rel.includes("..")` check if the traversal token were somehow split across encoded segments in a way `decodeURIComponent` reassembles into `..` only after the string check already ran — actually the check runs AFTER decode so a literal `%2e%2e` decodes to `..` before the check, which IS caught; but the CASE of a Windows-style `..\\..\\etc` (backslash instead of forward slash) on a Windows deployment of this Next server is unexercised, since `path.resolve` on win32 treats backslash as a separator while the string guard only checks for the substring `..` (which WOULD still catch it) — low-severity but the interaction between `path.sep`-based defense-in-depth check and a non-POSIX separator on Windows hosting is untested | traversal still rejected via the substring check regardless of separator style | integration | GAP
- [control-plane-api-routes.418] `app/api/transcript/[...path]/route.ts` `readFile(absolute, "utf8")` on a file that exists but is NOT valid UTF-8 (a corrupted or binary `.jsonl`) does not throw — `readFile` with an encoding always returns SOMETHING (replacement characters for invalid sequences), so a corrupted transcript file is served as 200 with mangled content rather than any error signal — untested "successfully serves garbled content" case | 200 with replacement-character-mangled body, no error | integration | GAP

- [control-plane-api-routes.419] `lib/type-guards.ts` `isRecord` — a boxed object wrapper like `new String("x")` or `new Number(1)` (typeof "object", not null, not an array) is treated as a valid record per this guard's literal definition, even though callers likely intend "plain JSON-shaped object" — an extremely unlikely real input (nothing in this codebase constructs boxed primitives) but the guard's contract doesn't exclude it and no test documents the intended scope boundary | returns true for boxed primitives — arguably correct per the guard's stated contract, but unverified as intentional | unit | GAP

- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.78] defaultCreateWorktree is idempotent/resumable: a leftover worktree dir / branch from a prior crashed run is force-removed and rebuilt fresh from current HEAD. | fresh worktree, no stale state | integration | GAP (no explicit "prior crashed worktree" resume test)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.79] defaultCreateWorktree throws with stderr/stdout detail when `git worktree add` fails. | throw | unit | GAP

- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.82] defaultResetWorktreeFrozenArtifacts returns true and commits a "drop out-of-scope frozen-artifact edits" commit when a frozen path WAS edited, discarding that edit and keeping legit src/test changes. | true, edit reverted, legit changes intact | integration | dev-loop.test.ts:1982, 2027

- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.84] defaultIntegrateWorktree runs `git merge --abort` and returns {ok:false, conflict:true} on a real merge conflict, leaving main untouched. | aborted merge, main unchanged | integration | dev-loop.test.ts:2643 family (via forced conflict scenarios)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.85] defaultCaptureHead returns the current HEAD sha of the main root. | sha string | unit | GAP (only exercised as a fixture stub in most tests; real git version untested directly)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.86] defaultResetHard hard-resets the main root to a given sha. | git reset --hard | unit | GAP (stubbed in tests; real git path untested directly)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.87] defaultRebaseWorktree rebases the worktree branch onto the current integration branch name; returns {ok:false} with conflict message on rebase conflict (left in-progress for the resolver). | {ok, message} | integration | dev-loop.test.ts:2643 family
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.88] defaultRemoveWorktree force-removes the worktree dir and deletes its branch; is best-effort tolerant of an already-gone worktree/branch. | cleanup, no throw | integration | dev-loop.test.ts:1872 (cleanup-failure-tolerance angle)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.89] currentBranch falls back to the detached-HEAD sha when `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` returns "HEAD" (no branch). | sha string | unit | GAP

### Integration lock
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.95] withIntegrationLock serializes concurrent callers in-process via the tail-promise mutex (a second concurrent call waits for the first to release). | serialized execution | integration | dev-loop.test.ts:1897 (many concurrent completions, no lost events)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.96] acquireExclusiveLock creates the lock file with `wx` (fails if exists) and retries with a busy-wait until acquired or a 30s deadline throw. | lock acquired or throw after 30s | unit | GAP (30s deadline path not exercised in tests — would be slow; likely intentionally untested at real-time scale)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.97] reclaimStaleExclusiveLock reclaims (unlinks) a lock file when its recorded epoch_ms is older than INTEGRATION_LOCK_STALE_MS (120s) OR its owning pid is dead. | reclaimed | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.98] reclaimStaleExclusiveLock does NOT reclaim a fresh lock held by a live different pid. | not reclaimed | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.99] isPidAlive returns true for the current process's own pid and for a live pid; returns false for a pid that errors with something other than EPERM (e.g. ESRCH). | true/false | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.100] a lock-release failure inside the `finally` (already-removed file) never throws — best-effort unlink. | tolerated | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.101] ensureWorktreesIgnored appends `<worktreesDir>/` to .gitignore only if not already present (idempotent), tolerating a missing .gitignore. | .gitignore updated once | integration | GAP (no direct assertion found; only used as a precondition in parallel tests)

### Quota / rate-limit detection

- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.105] detectRateLimit fires on each DEFAULT_QUOTA_PATTERNS phrase (rate_limit_error, 429, usage limit reached, quota exceeded, overloaded, resets at/in, try again later, retry-after) with a non-zero exit, and ignores an ordinary "FAIL"/"TypeError" test failure. | hit:true / hit:false respectively | unit | dev-loop.test.ts:822
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.106] detectRateLimit with a null/undefined exitCode scans unconditionally (pure-pattern test mode). | hit reflects pattern match regardless of exit | unit | GAP (explicit null-exitCode case)

- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.108] parseResetMs parses "retry-after: <seconds>", relative durations ("in 2h14m", "90s"), absolute clock times ("at 3pm"), and ISO-8601 timestamps, in that priority order. | absolute epoch ms | unit | dev-loop.test.ts:856
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.109] parseResetMs rolls an already-past clock time (e.g. "at 9am" when it's currently 3pm) to the next day. | tomorrow's epoch ms | unit | dev-loop.test.ts:871
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.110] parseResetMs returns null when no pattern matches and no ISO timestamp is present. | null | unit | GAP (explicit "nothing parseable" case; implied by empty-string in windowRecord tests but not parseResetMs itself)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.111] parseResetMs's clockToEpochMs rejects an invalid clock (hour>23 or minute>59). | null (falls through) | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.112] computeWaitMs uses the parsed reset time (capped at quotaBackoffCapMs) with a 5s pad when a reset is parseable. | waitMs, resetAtMs | unit | dev-loop.test.ts:880
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.113] computeWaitMs floors a near-zero parsed reset to QUOTA_MIN_WAIT_MS (30s) so a provider reporting "resets in 0s" never busy-spins. | waitMs >= 30000 | unit | dev-loop.test.ts:971
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.114] computeWaitMs backs off exponentially (start * 2^(attempt-1), capped) when no reset time is parseable. | doubling backoff, capped | unit | dev-loop.test.ts:891
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.115] runLegWithQuota waits the parsed duration then retries the SAME leg (never burns a gate attempt) on a rate-limit hit, then proceeds once the leg succeeds. | eventual success, no gate-attempt consumed | unit | dev-loop.test.ts:922
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.116] runLegWithQuota never waits or writes throttled state on a clean (non-rate-limited) leg. | immediate return, no wait | unit | dev-loop.test.ts:942
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.117] runLegWithQuota leaves a SUCCESSFUL leg alone even if its output text happens to mention quota/rate-limit vocabulary (no false throttle). | not throttled | unit | dev-loop.test.ts:956
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.118] runLegWithQuota gives up with `quotaBlocked:true` once cumulative wait would exceed quotaMaxWaitMs — never throws, never loops forever. | quotaBlocked, no throw | unit | dev-loop.test.ts:982
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.119] runLegWithQuota writes "throttled" quota-state then "available" quota-state to disk across a hit-then-recover sequence. | quota-state.json transitions | unit | dev-loop.test.ts:1005

### Frozen-corpus protection (integration)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.162] frozenIntegrationPaths returns the canonical/baselines/requirements/architecture-map.yml/issue-index paths, and explicitly EXCLUDES loop lifecycle files (issues/, done/, ledger, gates, reports, transcripts) and package.json. | path list | unit | dev-loop.test.ts:1958
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.163] runLoopParallel: two parallel branches BOTH editing a frozen artifact integrate WITHOUT a merge conflict — both edits are dropped, each branch's legit work is kept. | clean integration, edits dropped | e2e-process | dev-loop.test.ts:2027

### Readiness check (sequential + parallel)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.164] readiness verdict "implementable" -> the implementer runs and the issue verifies normally. | verified | integration | dev-loop.test.ts:2309
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.165] readiness verdict "issue_update" with a prose-only patch is applied to the issue file (rewriting it), THEN the issue is implemented. | issue file patched, then implemented | integration | dev-loop.test.ts:2334
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.166] readiness verdict "issue_update" whose patch TOUCHES the traceability block is REFUSED — routed to parked instead, and the patch is discarded (never applied). | parked, file unpatched | integration | dev-loop.test.ts:2365
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.167] readiness verdict "needs_cr" parks the issue; the implementer is NEVER invoked; the loop continues to the next ready issue (skip-and-continue, not a break). | parked, implementer not called, loop continues | integration | dev-loop.test.ts:2403
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.168] readiness leg death (no verdict file written, e.g. crashed leg) triggers exactly ONE retry, then parks with reason "readiness_leg_failed" if still no verdict (transient-failure honesty, never silently needs_cr). | parked after 2 attempts | integration | dev-loop.test.ts:2436
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.169] a parked report is CLEARED (unparked) the instant its issue file's content hash changes (CR-driven re-extraction/reopening). | unparked | integration | dev-loop.test.ts:2462
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.170] readParkedIssueIds deletes a stale `-parked.json` report when the issue file identity no longer matches (best-effort unpark), and tolerates an unreadable report by skipping it. | stale report removed | unit | GAP (unreadable-report-tolerance branch specifically)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.171] readReadinessVerdict returns null (not a crash) for a missing file, unparseable JSON, or a verdict string outside READINESS_VERDICTS. | null | unit | GAP (malformed-verdict-string case specifically; missing-file case implied by 2436)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.172] applyReadinessUpdate returns false (no-op) when verdict.updates.body_patch is absent, empty, or the issue file cannot be read. | false | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.173] parallel readiness: a batch member that parks (needs_cr) gets NO worktree created at all (excluded pre-worktree, before any git side effect). | no worktree for parked member | e2e-process | dev-loop.test.ts:2505
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.174] parallel readiness runs SEQUENTIALLY across the batch (not concurrently) so a readiness leg and a running implementer leg never fight over the same CLI quota window. | sequential readiness pass | integration | GAP (no test isolates/asserts the sequential-not-concurrent readiness execution order specifically)

### Merge integrity (S10)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.175] on a merge conflict, the worktree is FIRST rebased onto integration HEAD, THEN a bounded merge-resolver leg runs IN THE WORKTREE; if it claims resolved AND the orchestrator's own worktree gate re-run is green, the merge is retried ONCE and, on success, emits merge_conflict_resolved. | merge succeeds on retry | e2e-process | dev-loop.test.ts:2643
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.176] the merge-resolver's `resolved:true` claim is NEVER trusted alone — if the orchestrator's own worktree gate re-run is RED, the issue is blocked regardless of the resolver's claim (trust nothing). | blocked despite resolved:true | e2e-process | dev-loop.test.ts:2691
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.177] when steps.runMergeResolver===false (test opt-out) or the resolver is otherwise unavailable, an unresolved conflict blocks immediately with no retry attempt. | blocked, no retry | integration | GAP (explicit runMergeResolver:false path not found as its own test — only the resolver-present flows are tested)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.178] readMergeResolutionVerdict returns null for a missing/unparseable file or a non-boolean `resolved` field (treated as unresolved). | null | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.179] POST-MERGE RE-GATE: after a successful merge, the gate is re-run on the INTEGRATION TREE; if it passes, the issue is moved to done/, gate_passed is emitted referencing the POST-MERGE evidence, and commitDoneMove commits. | verified via post-merge evidence | e2e-process | dev-loop.test.ts:1756 (green path), 2726 (explicit post-merge re-gate)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.180] POST-MERGE RE-GATE RED (merge was green pre-merge, red after merging — the merge itself damaged something): the merge commit is REVERTED (`reset --hard` to the captured pre-merge sha), the issue is blocked with BOTH the green pre-merge AND red post-merge gate evidence embedded, and post_merge_gate_failed + issue_blocked are emitted. | integration branch reverted to green, issue blocked, others continue | e2e-process | dev-loop.test.ts:2726
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.181] a FAILED revert (`reset --hard` itself exits non-zero) after a damaging merge is surfaced as a loud CRITICAL stderr message (never silently swallowed, never thrown to crash the scheduler). | CRITICAL log, no throw | unit | GAP (no test forces resetHard to fail)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.182] readGateEvidenceSnapshot captures the gate evidence CONTENT (not just the path) before it would be clobbered by the next gate run reusing the same `<id>-gate.json` path; returns {path, record:null} when the file is unreadable at snapshot time. | snapshot embeds both green+red content | unit | GAP (unreadable-at-snapshot-time branch specifically; the main dual-evidence embedding is covered by 2726)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.183] preMergeSha is captured FIRST, under the integration lock, before resetFrozenArtifacts/merge run, so it is always the exact sha to revert to (the merge is the only new commit after it). | correct revert target | e2e-process | dev-loop.test.ts:2726 (implied by successful revert)

### factory/dev-loop-supervised.ts

- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.195] nextSupervisorAction returns "done" when total>0 and done>=total, taking priority over a simultaneous blocked/stall condition (checked first in the decision order). | "done" | unit | dev-loop.test.ts:713
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.196] nextSupervisorAction returns "blocked" whenever blocked>0, even before total is reached (checked second). | "blocked" | unit | dev-loop.test.ts:713

- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.198] nextSupervisorAction returns "stalled" when stall>=stallLimit (checked fourth, after done/blocked/cap). | "stalled" | unit | dev-loop.test.ts:713
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.199] nextSupervisorAction returns "relaunch" as the default/fallthrough when none of the stop conditions hold. | "relaunch" | unit | dev-loop.test.ts:713
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.200] nextSupervisorAction with total===0 never returns "done" purely from done>=total (guarded by `total > 0`), e.g. before extraction has produced any issues. | not "done" from the total guard alone | unit | GAP (0-issue-index boundary not tested)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.201] skillsStageNeeded returns false when no frozen baseline exists yet (nothing to select skills from). | false | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.202] skillsStageNeeded returns true when a baseline exists but no skills report exists yet. | true | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.203] skillsStageNeeded returns true when the report's phase is neither "green" nor "skipped" (unsettled, e.g. a crashed prior run). | true | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.204] skillsStageNeeded returns true when the report's baseline_id does not match the current frozen baseline's id (stale report from a superseded baseline). | true | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.205] skillsStageNeeded returns false when the report is settled (green or skipped) AND matches the current baseline id. | false | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.206] main() exits 2 with a clear stderr message when VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT (resolveTargetRoot) is unset. | exit 2 | e2e-process | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.207] main() runs the skills stage (install-skills.ts) synchronously BEFORE the first relaunch loop iteration only when skillsStageNeeded is true and NOT in --rehearsal mode; a non-green skills stage is logged but NEVER blocks the dev loop (non-fatal by design). | skills stage runs or is skipped correctly; loop proceeds either way | e2e-process | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.208] main() with --rehearsal targets dev-rehearsal.ts and REHEARSAL_DIR instead of dev-loop.ts/VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT, and skips the skills stage entirely. | rehearsal wiring, skills stage skipped | e2e-process | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.209] main()'s doneCount/blockedCount/totalIssues correctly count done/*.md, *-blocked.json, and issue-index.issues.length, tolerating a missing directory (returns 0) or a missing/malformed issue-index (empty array). | correct counts, 0 on missing dirs | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.210] main() writes the heartbeat run-state file (`dev-loop-supervisor.json`) on EVERY loop iteration (both "running" before a relaunch and the terminal status), including pid/target/progress_root/updated_at, so `vivicy status` sees liveness across relaunches. | run-state file updated every iteration | e2e-process | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.211] main() relaunches the child (`node dev-loop.ts` or `dev-rehearsal.ts`) via spawnSync with stdio:"inherit" and logs its exit code/signal, looping until a stop condition fires. | repeated relaunch + logging | e2e-process | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.212] the stall counter increments only when doneCount is UNCHANGED from the previous iteration, and resets to 0 the moment doneCount increases (progress detection). | stall increments/resets correctly | unit | GAP (this logic lives inline in main(), not exposed as a pure function — the pure decision nextSupervisorAction IS tested, but the stall-tracking loop itself is not)

### factory/leg-supervisor.ts

- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.213] main() reads the spec file at argv[2]; a missing argv[2] exits with code 64 and a stderr message, without touching argv[2] at all. | exit 64 | unit | GAP (no leg-supervisor.test.ts exists)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.214] the leg's stdout/stderr are piped straight through to the supervisor's own stdout/stderr UNMODIFIED (transparent tee) — the supervisor itself never writes its own bytes to those streams. | transparent passthrough | integration | leg-timeout.test.ts (indirectly, via spawnLegSync/Async assertions on captured stdout/stderr)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.215] on normal exit (no timeout trip), the result file records {status: exitCode, signal, timedOut:false, timeoutReason:null} and the supervisor itself exits 0 regardless of the leg's exit code. | correct result file, supervisor exits 0 | integration | leg-timeout.test.ts:58, 67
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.216] a spawn() failure for the leg command itself (e.g. command not found) is caught and finish()'d with {status:null, signal:null, timedOut:false, timeoutReason:null, spawnError:<message>}, never an uncaught throw. | spawnError recorded, no crash | unit | GAP (no test forces spawn() to throw inside leg-supervisor — leg-timeout.test.ts only exercises valid `node -e` commands)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.217] the hard CAP timer fires when capMs elapses regardless of ongoing output (armIdle resets only the IDLE timer, never the cap timer) — a leg that emits continuously past the cap is still killed. | killed at cap, timeoutReason mentions "hard cap" | integration | leg-timeout.test.ts:76
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.218] the IDLE timer resets on EVERY stdout/stderr data event and fires only after idleMs elapses with NO output — a silent leg is killed distinctly from a cap trip. | killed at idle, timeoutReason mentions "idle" | integration | leg-timeout.test.ts:90
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.219] whichever of cap/idle fires FIRST wins; if the leg naturally exits between the trip and the kill completing, `close` still reports timedOut:true (the timedOut flag, once set, is authoritative regardless of the exit code the kill produced, e.g. 143/137). | timedOut:true reported even if a race lets the process exit with an odd code | unit | GAP (race window itself not explicitly forced in a test; the general behavior is covered by the cap/idle tests above)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.220] killTree sends SIGTERM to the NEGATIVE pid (the whole process group, since the leg is spawned detached) and escalates to SIGKILL only after graceMs if the group has not exited. | group signaled, escalation after grace | integration | leg-timeout.test.ts:102 (grandchild-reaping test)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.221] killTree falls back to signaling the immediate child directly when `process.kill(-pgid, ...)` throws (e.g. the group already vanished in a race) rather than throwing itself. | fallback signal, no throw | unit | GAP (no test forces the group-kill to throw)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.222] a killed leg's WHOLE process tree (including a grandchild it spawned) is reaped — no orphaned process survives the grace period. | grandchild process gone | integration | leg-timeout.test.ts:102
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.223] finish() is idempotent (`settled` guard): calling it twice (e.g. both a timeout trip AND a subsequent close event) writes the result file and exits only once. | single write/exit | unit | GAP (no test forces a double-finish race)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.224] a `writeFileSync(resultPath, ...)` failure inside finish() is swallowed (best-effort) — the supervisor still exits 0 rather than crashing on a filesystem error. | tolerated, still exits | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.225] humanMs formats sub-minute durations in seconds (minimum 1s) and >=60000ms durations in whole minutes, for a readable timeout reason at both test-scale and production-scale timeouts. | "N s" or "N min" | unit | GAP (no direct unit test of humanMs; only indirectly observed via the /hard cap|idle/ regex matches on real timeout runs)

- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.231] envMs falls back to the default for a NEGATIVE numeric env value (guarded by `n < 0`). | default | unit | GAP

- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.236] a killed leg's process tree (a spawned grandchild) leaves NO orphan — verified by polling `process.kill(pid,0)` after the grace period. | grandchild reaped | integration | leg-timeout.test.ts:102
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.237] spawnLegAsync: a normal finish under the cap is not killed (async sibling parity with the sync case). | status:0, not timed out | integration | leg-timeout.test.ts:139
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.238] spawnLegAsync: a stalled leg is killed and returns a timeout failure without hanging the test (async sibling parity). | timedOut:true | integration | leg-timeout.test.ts:148

### factory/agent-spawn.ts

No dedicated agent-spawn.test.ts exists. Every behavior below is exercised ONLY indirectly through dev-loop.test.ts's injected `steps.runImplementer/runReviewer` fakes (which bypass agent-spawn.ts entirely) — so the REAL spawn/build/capture code in this file has no direct unit coverage in this area. This is the single biggest structural gap in the area.

- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.245] spawnTee / spawnTeeAsync correctly forward {cwd, env, timeout} to spawnLegSync/spawnLegAsync and return the leg-timeout LegResult shape unchanged. | passthrough | unit | GAP

- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.247] agentEnv() returns a shallow copy of process.env (agents get NO injected progress/ledger identity env — confirms the "no governance" invariant at the env level). | copy of process.env, no PROGRESS_* keys | unit | GAP

- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.249] captureClaudeTranscript finds the session .jsonl by uuid across ~/.claude/projects/*/ subdirs and copies it; returns false (not true) when the copied file is 0 bytes (not-yet-flushed session), and false when projectsDir or the uuid file does not exist. | true/false correctly | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.250] findNewestCodexRollout walks ~/.codex/sessions recursively, picks the NEWEST .jsonl with mtime>=sinceMs that also matches cwdFilter, and returns null when none qualify or the sessions dir is absent. | newest matching path or null | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.251] findNewestCodexRollout tolerates a directory/file vanishing mid-walk (ENOENT on readdir/stat) without throwing — just skips it. | no throw, skip | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.252] rolloutMatchesCwd returns true unconditionally when cwdFilter is null (sequential path, no filtering needed). | true | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.253] rolloutMatchesCwd returns true when the rollout records NO parseable cwd at all (never excludes on missing data — best-effort, no false negative). | true | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.254] rolloutMatchesCwd returns true only when the recorded cwd resolves to the SAME path as cwdFilter; false when it resolves to a different worktree (prevents cross-worktree transcript mis-capture under concurrency). | true/false correctly | unit | GAP — this is the SPECIFIC mechanism that prevents a parallel Codex leg's transcript being stolen by a sibling worktree; no isolated test exists for it (only implied indirectly by the "many concurrent completions" ledger-consistency test, which doesn't specifically check transcript attribution)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.255] rolloutMatchesCwd tolerates an unreadable rollout file (returns true — best-effort, never excludes on a read error). | true | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.256] ensureTranscriptDir creates the transcript dir recursively (idempotent — tolerates it already existing). | dir exists | unit | GAP

- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.258] buildCodexArgs produces `exec <prompt> --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox -C <root> --skip-git-repo-check ...modelArgs` in exactly that order. | argv | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.259] runClaudeLeg/runClaudeLegAsync compose the prompt via readPrompt(cfg, leg.role) + composePrompt(issue), build a UNIQUE transcript path per invocation (`<transcriptsDir>/<issue.id>/claude-<role>-<uuid>.jsonl`), spawn, then capture the transcript — returning transcriptRel:undefined (not the path) when capture fails, so a caller never references a non-existent file. | {result, output, transcriptRel|undefined} | unit | GAP
