- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.265] the implementer prompt's QUALITY BAR requires (1) end-to-end public-entry-point testing, (2) type-fuzzing every public entry point with null/undefined/wrong-type/empty/malformed input, (3) refusing side-channel contract-mismatch hacks (surfacing as a blocker instead), (4) no dead/unreferenced exported symbols. | prompt text contains all four | unit | dev-loop.test.ts:2109 (levers 1 and 2 explicitly named "the two audit-defect levers" — levers 3 and 4 presence: GAP)

### factory/prompts/reviewer.md

- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.266] the reviewer prompt states the reviewer did NOT author the implementation (independence) and must apply only bounded, intent-preserving fixes — never expanding scope. | prompt text | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.267] the reviewer prompt's public-API review checklist requires verifying garbage-input degradation is PROVEN BY TESTS (not just asserted), the public path is exercised end-to-end, no dead exports/side-channels (with an explicit "grep the whole repo" reachability check), and cross-checking ALL canonical docs that touch the feature (not just the primary referenced doc). | prompt text contains all four checklist items | unit | dev-loop.test.ts:2109 (partial — same two-lever assertion as implementer; the reviewer-specific "grep the whole repo" and "cross-check ALL docs" items: GAP)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.268] the reviewer prompt instructs returning `not_faithful` with a precise file:line when a checklist item fails, rather than silently passing it. | prompt text | unit | GAP

### factory/prompts/merge-resolver.md

- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.269] the merge-resolver prompt scopes the leg to running INSIDE the worktree (cwd = worktree root) and explicitly forbids performing the merge onto integration itself (the orchestrator does that under its lock). | prompt text | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.270] the merge-resolver prompt requires rebase-then-resolve preserving BOTH sides' tests/code, and instructs reporting an irreconcilable conflict as unresolved rather than sacrificing one side. | prompt text | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.271] the merge-resolver prompt requires taking the integration head's version VERBATIM for any frozen-path conflict (never resolving it by editing the frozen file). | prompt text | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.272] the merge-resolver prompt documents the EXACT verdict file shape (`resolved: boolean`, `reason: string`) and states `resolved:true` requires rebase complete + both sides reconciled + gate green IN THE WORKTREE — and that the orchestrator re-runs the gate itself regardless, catching a false "resolved:true" claim. | prompt text | unit | GAP

### factory/prompts/readiness.md

- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.273] the readiness prompt defines the exact three verdicts (implementable / issue_update / needs_cr) and the disambiguation rule ("when in doubt, choose needs_cr — a plan tweak is cheap, an intention problem silently promoted is not"). | prompt text | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.274] the readiness prompt's `issue_update` bound requires the ENTIRE traceability block reproduced byte-identical and the COMPLETE new body (never a diff/fragment) in `updates.body_patch`. | prompt text | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.275] the readiness prompt states the orchestrator VERIFIES the traceability block is untouched and will REFUSE + re-route to needs_cr + discard the patch if it is altered. | prompt text | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.276] the readiness prompt documents the exact verdict-file shape and rules (verdict is exactly one of three strings; reason always present and concrete; updates present ONLY for issue_update; a missing/unparseable file is a transient failure retried once then parked). | prompt text | unit | GAP

### Area cross-notes

- **Prompt-content tests exist only for implementer.md + reviewer.md** (dev-loop.test.ts:2091, 2109, 2144); merge-resolver.md and readiness.md have ZERO content-assertion tests despite both encoding load-bearing contracts (the verdict-file JSON shapes the orchestrator parses via `readMergeResolutionVerdict` / `readReadinessVerdict`, and the traceability-preservation rule `issueUpdatePreservesTraceability` enforces). A prompt edit that silently breaks the documented verdict shape would only be caught by the e2e-process tests happening to exercise that exact JSON shape via their fakes — the fakes hardcode the shape independent of the prompt text, so a prompt/orchestrator-contract drift is currently invisible to the suite.
- **agent-spawn.ts is entirely untested at its own layer.** Every dev-loop.test.ts scenario injects `steps.runImplementer`/`runReviewer`/`runReadiness`/`runMergeResolver` fakes that bypass `runClaudeLeg`/`runCodexLeg` and therefore never exercise `buildClaudeArgs`, `buildCodexArgs`, `captureClaudeTranscript`, `findNewestCodexRollout`, or — most importantly for the parallel/worktree area — `rolloutMatchesCwd`'s cwd-filter exclusion logic that prevents a sibling worktree's Codex rollout from being mis-captured as another issue's transcript. This is the load-bearing correctness mechanism for "transcripts are attributed to the right issue under N-way concurrency" and it has no test at all in this area.
- **leg-supervisor.ts (the actual detached tree-kill process) has no standalone test file.** It is only exercised end-to-end through `leg-timeout.test.ts`, which spawns it as a real child process and observes outcomes — so its cap/idle/kill-tree behaviors ARE integration-tested, but internal branches (missing argv[2] exit 64, a spawn() throw for the leg command itself, the double-`finish()` idempotence guard, `writeFileSync` failure tolerance) are never independently forced.
- **dev-loop-supervised.ts's pure decision function is well-tested (`nextSupervisorAction`), but `skillsStageNeeded` (also pure and exported) has ZERO tests**, despite gating a real side-effecting spawn (`install-skills.ts`) in `main()`. The whole `main()` function (heartbeat writes, relaunch loop, rehearsal-mode wiring, stall-counter bookkeeping inline in the loop) is untested — the file relies entirely on the exported pure helpers being correct and the reader trusting the glue code around them.
- **The CLI entrypoint blocks of dev-loop.ts** (`if (import.meta.url === ...)`) — assertCleanTree, checkSkills gating, the final exit-code logic (0/1/2) — are exercised only by the pure `runLoop`/`runLoopParallel` calls inside tests, never via actually invoking the module as a subprocess (`node dev-loop.ts`). The supervisor's relaunch behavior therefore has no test proving the child process actually produces the documented exit codes the supervisor's `res.status` logic depends on.
- **Merge-integrity interacts with the frozen-artifact guard in an untested combination**: a merge conflict that the resolver leg fixes (S10) where the CONFLICT ITSELF is on a frozen path is never separately tested — `defaultResetWorktreeFrozenArtifacts` is asserted to neutralize frozen edits BEFORE the first merge attempt and again before the retried merge, but no test forces a frozen-path conflict specifically into the merge-resolver flow (only a non-frozen-path conflict is exercised in the merge-conflict tests, and only a non-conflicting frozen edit is exercised in the frozen-artifact tests — the two are never combined).
- **Quota-aware retry and per-leg timeout are two independent watchdogs that can both apply to the same leg but are never tested together**: a leg that BOTH rate-limits (triggering `runLegWithQuota`'s wait-and-retry) AND separately times out (triggering `leg-timeout.ts`'s cap/idle kill) on a later attempt has no combined test — each mechanism is verified in isolation only.

### Adversarial additions

- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.X1] `emit()` is called at 21 sites in dev-loop.ts but only 4 are try/catch-guarded (`parkIssueOnCr`, `writeIntegrationBlock`, `writePostMergeIntegrationBlock` — each explicitly commented "best-effort... must not crash"). The other 17 sites — `issue_started`/`review_started`/`gate_passed`/`gate_failed`/`issue_blocked` inside `runIssueCycle`/`runIssueCycleAsync`, plus `emitReadinessStarted`'s `readiness_check_started`, `routeReadinessVerdict`'s `readiness_check_completed`/`readiness_update_applied`, and `merge_conflict_resolved` — are UNGUARDED: a ledger write failure (e.g. a concurrent revision CAS conflict, disk full, or a corrupt ledger file) throws straight out of `runIssueCycle`, uncaught, which in the SEQUENTIAL `runLoop` crashes the whole process (unlike a real gate failure, which cleanly blocks-and-continues/breaks) and in the PARALLEL path is only caught because `runOne`'s outer try/finally happens to catch it as an "unexpected error" block — an inconsistency between "governance emits are best-effort" (the documented invariant for 4 sites) and "governance emits can crash the run" (the undocumented behavior for the other 17). | an unguarded emit() throwing inside `runIssueCycle`/`runIssueCycleAsync` should degrade like the guarded sites (log + continue), not crash the sequential loop or fall through to the parallel path's generic catch-all | unit | GAP — no test forces `recordProgressEvent`/`emit` to throw at any of the 17 unguarded call sites; the guarded-vs-unguarded asymmetry itself is untested and undocumented as intentional
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.X2] `runLoopParallel`'s scheduler: when an ENTIRE selected batch parks on readiness (`readyToRun` stays empty after the readiness pass, `running.size` is still 0 from before), the loop hits the `running.size === 0` branch and BREAKS immediately on that same turn — relying on the invariant that a parked issue is excluded from `computeReadySet` on every subsequent turn (via the disk `-parked.json` report + `parkedThisRun`) so no future turn could ever schedule it again. This exact path (whole batch parks, zero worktrees ever created, loop terminates cleanly rather than spinning) has no isolated test — the existing readiness test (dev-loop.test.ts:2505) only covers ONE parked member alongside others that proceed, never a batch where every member parks. | loop terminates cleanly with all batch members recorded "parked", no worktree created for any of them, no infinite scheduling spin | e2e-process | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.X3] `runLoopParallel`'s `runOne`: the merge-resolver branch calls `wt.rebaseWorktree` and then `runLegWithQuotaAsync(...mergeResolverLeg...)` UNCONDITIONALLY once `steps.runMergeResolver !== false`, even when `wt.rebaseWorktree` itself reports `{ok:false}` (a rebase conflict left in-progress, per its own doc comment "left in-progress for the resolver") — there is no test proving the resolver leg is actually expected to run (and clean up) against an in-progress/conflicted rebase state in the worktree, only against a plain merge conflict. If the rebase itself fails for a reason the resolver prompt does not anticipate (e.g. binary conflict, submodule conflict), the resulting worktree state fed to the resolver leg is unexercised. | resolver leg runs against a genuinely rebase-conflicted worktree and either reconciles it or reports unresolved; no test forces this specific rebase-failure-then-resolver sequence | integration | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.X4] `defaultRebaseWorktree` / `defaultIntegrateWorktree` / `defaultResetWorktreeFrozenArtifacts` all shell out via `spawnSync("git", ...)` with NO check of `git`'s own presence/version on the host — if `git` is entirely missing from PATH (a misconfigured CI/container), every one of these silently returns `{status: null or 1, stdout:"", stderr:""}`-shaped failures indistinguishable from "real git ran and failed", so a missing `git` binary would surface as a confusing "merge conflict" or "worktree setup failed" block rather than a clear "git is not installed" diagnostic. | a clear, distinguishable error when `git` itself cannot be spawned (ENOENT), not a misleading merge-conflict/worktree-setup-failed report | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.X5] `commitDoneMove` and `defaultCommit` both call `pruneGitkeeps(root)` before `git add -A` — but neither dev-loop.ts nor its test file asserts WHAT `pruneGitkeeps` actually does (it lives in `../lib/skeleton.ts`, outside this area's file list) or that a `.gitkeep` file legitimately still needed (an otherwise-empty directory that must stay tracked) is not wrongly pruned by a run that happens to add its first real file to that directory in the SAME commit — a race between "directory just became non-empty" and "gitkeep pruned" that could leave a directory that later empties again untracked-and-invisible to git. | `.gitkeep` pruning only removes a gitkeep in a directory that now has other tracked content, never removes one from a directory that is (or reverts to) genuinely empty | unit | GAP — pruneGitkeeps itself is outside this area's file list, but both its dev-loop call sites (commit + commitDoneMove) have zero test assertions on its effect within this area's suite
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.X6] `parkIssueOnCr`'s `issueFileIdentity` call passes a synthetic `{issue_id, issue_path}` object (not a full `ParkedReport`) to compute the identity stamp — if `issue.path`/`issue.issue_path` are BOTH absent and the issue was never written under the default `${cfg.issuesDir}/${issue.id}.md` convention (e.g. a caller-supplied issue-index entry with a nonstandard path), `issueFileIdentity` resolves a path that does not exist, returns null, and the parked report is written with `issue_hash: null` — meaning `readParkedIssueIds`' later check (`report.issue_hash === identity.hash`) can never re-match (since a null-identity issue also resolves to null on the read side) UNLESS the two nulls are treated as equal — they are NOT (the code checks `identity && report.issue_hash === identity.hash`, so a null `identity` on read never re-parks; it always unparks immediately). No test exercises a parked issue with an unresolvable path to confirm it deliberately self-clears every turn rather than staying stuck parked. | an issue parked with an unresolvable file path unparks on the very next scheduling turn (never permanently stuck), because a null identity always fails the re-match check | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.X7] `readGateEvidenceSnapshot` is called for the PRE-merge evidence using `result.evidenceRel` (the worktree gate's own evidence path) and for POST-merge using `postMergeGate.evidenceRel` — both are `${cfg.gatesDir}/${issue.id}-gate.json`, i.e. the SAME path (`writeGateEvidence` always writes to that fixed name). The pre-merge snapshot is read BEFORE `runGateAt(integrationCfg)` executes, but there is no lock/atomicity between "read old content" and "gate overwrites the file" other than program order in a single async function — if the post-merge gate run's `writeFileSync` happens to interleave with a concurrent reader of the SAME path from a DIFFERENT sibling issue's block-report generation (two issues resolving post-merge blocks back-to-back within the same integration-lock window is impossible since it's serialized, but a stale read from an unrelated concurrent process reading gate evidence for status display purposes is not accounted for) — worth an explicit test that the pre-merge snapshot's `record` field is the OLD (green) content, not a partially-written or already-overwritten one, given both genuinely share one file path. | preMergeEvidence.record reflects the gate-evidence content strictly BEFORE the post-merge gate run touches the same path — verified by content, not just by path | unit | GAP (dev-loop.test.ts:2726 exercises the end-to-end shape but does not isolate `readGateEvidenceSnapshot`'s read-before-overwrite ordering as its own assertion)
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.X8] `agent-spawn.ts`'s `runClaudeLegWith`/`runCodexLegWith` build the transcript path via string interpolation (`` `${cfg.transcriptsDir}/${issue.id}/claude-${leg.role}-${uuid}.jsonl` ``) with NO sanitization of `issue.id` or `leg.role` — an issue id containing a path-traversal sequence (`../../etc`) or a slash would escape the intended per-issue transcript directory. Since `issue.id` ultimately originates from the issue-index (an artifact the loop treats as trusted/frozen), this is a low-likelihood but real boundary-value gap: no test asserts the loop rejects, sanitizes, or is at least aware of a malformed/adversarial issue id reaching the transcript-path construction. | a malformed issue id (containing `/`, `..`, or other path-breaking characters) either is rejected earlier (issue-index validation) or cannot escape `cfg.transcriptsDir` when used to build a transcript path | unit | GAP
- [dev-loop-worktrees-merge.X9] `dev-loop-supervised.ts`'s `main()` heartbeat write (`writeState`) is called with `status: "running"` BEFORE each relaunch and then again with the terminal status/`action` after the loop exits — but between those two writes, if the supervised child process itself calls `process.exit()` inside a signal handler or crashes the Node process mid-write (e.g. OOM-killed), the run-state file is left stamped `status:"running"` indefinitely with a stale `updated_at`. There is no staleness check documented anywhere for `vivicy status` reading this file (unlike the integration lock's `INTEGRATION_LOCK_STALE_MS`), so a truly-dead supervisor could appear perpetually "running" to any consumer of `dev-loop-supervisor.json`. | either the run-state file carries a staleness/liveness signal (e.g. supervisor pid check, like the integration lock's dead-pid reclaim) or this is a documented, accepted limitation | unit | GAP — no test covers a supervisor process dying mid-iteration and the resulting stale run-state file

## Area: e2e-test-infra-rehearsal

Scope: the Playwright e2e specs and their shared infra (config, global-setup, helpers), the Vitest unit config, the Next.js config seams that support e2e, package.json scripts, the artifact-cleaning wrapper, and the factory's own end-to-end rehearsal harness plus its two bundled fixtures (formula, pocket-ledger) — i.e. the tests OF the test infrastructure, and the infrastructure's own testable behaviors.

### e2e/control.spec.ts

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.8] Suite runs in serial mode (`test.describe.configure({ mode: "serial" })`) because the run lock is process-global | tests execute one at a time, never interleaved with concurrent control-plane races | e2e-ui | e2e/control.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.9] Map renders from the demo target and the panel opens (mobile Sheet vs desktop dock) | at least one node visible, "Vivicy" panel wordmark visible | e2e-ui | e2e/control.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.10] The status pill (`aria-label` matching `/^status:/`) reflects the real demo ledger via SSE | badge visible within 15s | e2e-ui | e2e/control.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.11] Pre-condition cleanup: if a Stop button is already present (prior run left active), the test clicks past overlap, confirms the Stop AlertDialog, and waits for Run/Resume to reappear | idempotent pre-state regardless of prior test/run history | e2e-ui | e2e/control.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.12] Clicking Run/Resume (fake spawner records a lock; no real claude/codex launched) | status pill flips to `/running/i` within 15s, Stop button becomes visible | e2e-ui | e2e/control.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.13] Clicking Stop opens an AlertDialog titled "Stop the development loop?" requiring explicit confirm | dialog visible before any state change | e2e-ui | e2e/control.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.14] Confirming Stop in the dialog | status pill no longer matches `/running/i` within 15s; Run/Resume button reappears | e2e-ui | e2e/control.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.15] Dismissing/cancelling the Stop confirm dialog without confirming | GAP: run should remain in the running state, pill still `running`, Stop still visible — not exercised anywhere | e2e-ui | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.16] Extract button is gated when the target already has issues (demo target carries 8 issues): button has `aria-disabled="true"` | Extract control visible but disabled | e2e-ui | e2e/control.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.17] Hovering the disabled Extract trigger surfaces a shadcn tooltip with the exact honest reason | text matches `/Already extracted — \d+ issues?\. Re-extraction isn't available yet\./` within 10s | e2e-ui | e2e/control.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.18] Extract flow when the target has NO issues yet (re-extraction actually runs) | GAP: this suite only covers the "already extracted, gated" branch; the enabled/successful Extract click path (POST to extract endpoint, resulting issue list) is not driven end-to-end anywhere in e2e | e2e-process | GAP (see empty-state.spec.ts, which only asserts the Extract button is offered, never clicks it)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.19] Opening the Tasks accordion section lists the demo issues, matched by exact id text (avoids colliding with the file-path text in the same card) | "ISS-0001" exact-text visible in sidebar | e2e-ui | e2e/control.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.20] Run against a target with zero extractable issues (empty issue index) | GAP: not exercised — only the "8 issues, gated Extract" demo shape and the "no map yet" onboarding shape exist as fixtures | e2e-integration | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.21] Concurrent Run clicks / double-submission of Run while a run is already starting (race on the process-global lock) | GAP: no test simulates a double-click or two tabs racing Run | e2e-process | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.22] Stop while no run is active (Stop button absent — should never be clickable) | implicitly covered by pre-condition guard but not asserted as its own scenario (e.g. Stop's actual absence when idle) | e2e-ui | GAP

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.32] Clicking "Extract from docs" from this exact onboarding card actually triggers extraction | GAP: this spec only asserts the button's presence, never clicks it — the full "no_map -> extracted -> map renders" transition is never driven in e2e | e2e-process | GAP

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.36] Clicking "Edit layout" flips the toggle to the active "Editing layout" labeled state | button re-labels | e2e-ui | e2e/layout-edit.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.37] Dragging the first node by a real on-screen offset (retried via `toPass` up to 30s to absorb React Flow drag-init flake) reveals the Save button AND produces a real positional delta > 10px (guards against a "jiggle" that reveals Save without actually moving the node) | Save button visible; before/after bounding-box delta > 10 | e2e-ui | e2e/layout-edit.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.38] Clicking Save round-trips through `POST /api/architecture-map/layout`, which patches the source yml and regenerates the served viewer JSON | "Saved" text visible within 30s; no "Save failed" text | e2e-integration | e2e/layout-edit.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.39] The patched yml on disk differs from the pre-drag snapshot (proves the write hit the real file, not a shadow copy) | `patched !== ymlSnapshot` | e2e-integration/filesystem | e2e/layout-edit.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.40] The patched yml still carries the same node count as before (a coordinate-only patch, not a structural rewrite) | `countNodes(patched) === countNodes(ymlSnapshot)` | e2e-integration | e2e/layout-edit.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.41] Save failure path (e.g. malformed yml patch, filesystem write error, concurrent external edit to the yml) | GAP: "Save failed" text is asserted absent in the happy path, but no test drives an actual failure to prove the error state renders correctly | e2e-ui | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.42] Dragging a node then clicking "Edit layout" again to cancel/toggle OFF before saving (discarding the drag) | GAP: no test exercises turning editing off with unsaved changes | e2e-ui | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.43] Dragging a CLUSTER (not just a leaf node) — the comment in dev-rehearsal.ts references "node/cluster drag" but this spec only drags a single node | GAP: cluster-drag round-trip not exercised in e2e | e2e-ui | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.44] Concurrent layout edits across two browser tabs/sessions racing the same yml write | GAP: no locking/last-write-wins assertion exists | e2e-integration | GAP

### e2e/onboarding.spec.ts

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.45] Suite runs serial; `beforeEach` deletes `current-project.json` from the onboarding server's runtime dir so every test (and every serial-mode retry) starts from the pristine `no_target` state | reset is deterministic independent of run/retry order | e2e-process | e2e/onboarding.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.46] Target has NO `.vivicy/` at all (unresolved target) | `/api/map` returns `no_target`; the calm map-area empty state renders with the Vivi panel CLOSED (no panel heading in the a11y tree) — the empty-state "Talk to Vivi" CTA (located within the `no_target` empty-state container via its `data-empty-reason` marker, so the locator does not depend on where `<ViviPanel>` sits in the DOM) opens the onboarding view, and the bottom-left launcher bubble is not rendered on this screen (the CTA is the sole opener) | e2e-ui | e2e/onboarding.spec.ts ("no_target keeps the Vivi panel closed until the empty-state CTA opens the start choices")
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.47] After the user opens the panel, its three acquisition choices render: "Open an existing project", "Start a new project", "Import documents" — plus the "Start a project" welcome header; NO chat composer exists without a target | heading + all 3 buttons visible, `Message Vivi` count 0 | e2e-ui | e2e/onboarding.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.48] Cross-browser capture of the pristine panel onboarding (first test in the serial file, so it precedes the scaffold mutation) | screenshot written to `/tmp/vivicy-xbrowser/06-onboarding--<project>.png` | e2e-ui | e2e/onboarding.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.49] The scaffold choice expands the IN-PANEL scaffold form (no dialog) | "Project name" field visible after the click | e2e-ui | e2e/onboarding.spec.ts ("the scaffold choice creates a new project and lands on the empty-canonical map state")
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.50] Filling "Project name", navigating the folder browser to the scaffold parent, and naming the new folder ("New folder name"), then clicking "Scaffold project" | `POST /api/project/scaffold` succeeds; success toast "Project scaffolded" visible within 30s | e2e-integration | e2e/onboarding.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.51] Per-browser isolation: each browser project scaffolds into its OWN parent dir (`onboardScaffoldParent(browserKey)`), so parallel browser projects never race the same scaffold target | scaffold succeeds independently per browser | e2e-process | e2e/onboarding.spec.ts + playwright.config.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.52] After scaffolding, the app re-fetches map + project and lands on the fresh project's EMPTY-CANONICAL onboarding state (canonical holds only the scaffold seed, no authored spec doc yet — the passive twin of the auto-opened panel) | `[data-empty-reason="empty_canonical"]` bare sentence visible, containing "Talk to Vivi to get grilled" (no no_map Extract card) | e2e-ui | e2e/onboarding.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.54] No graph, no raw "Request failed" text, and no page-level runtime throw after scaffolding | all three assertions hold | e2e-ui | e2e/onboarding.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.55] The open-an-existing-project choice (in-panel `OpenProjectForm` from the `no_target` state) | GAP: never driven from the panel onboarding view in e2e (only the change-project picker is covered elsewhere, from an ALREADY-resolved project) | e2e-ui | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.56] The import-documents choice end-to-end (acquire target in-panel, then stage → verify → apply) | GAP: button presence only; no e2e drives the panel import flow | e2e-process | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.58] Scaffold validation errors: empty project name, a new-folder name colliding with an existing non-empty directory, a target with no write permission | GAP: no negative-path test for the panel scaffold form's own field validation | e2e-ui | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.59] Backing out of an expanded choice (the Back affordance) without acquiring | GAP: not exercised in e2e | e2e-ui | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.60] Re-running the first (choices) test after a prior scaffold in the SAME serial run (retry semantics) | covered implicitly by the `beforeEach` reset rationale in the file's own comment, but no assertion actually forces a retry to prove it | e2e-process | GAP (partial — the `beforeEach` exists specifically for this, un-exercised by a deliberate forced-retry test)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.292] After the scaffold acquisition the panel flips from the onboarding view to CHAT mode with Vivi's seeded welcome already greeting; the composer renders, the choice buttons are gone; the welcome is transcript-backed so it survives a reload (rehydration), not a UI-only ghost | `Message Vivi` visible within 15s; "Start a new project" count 0; the welcome ("what do you want to build?") visible before and after `page.reload()` | e2e-ui | e2e/onboarding.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.307] The seeded welcome carries the one-shot import card ("Already wrote some of this down?" + "I have docs to import"); setting files on its hidden picker imports into the just-scaffolded (already governed) project deterministically (no agent leg) — the card stamps decided ("… documents imported"), the button goes `aria-disabled`, and Vivi's acknowledgment ("… in the kitchen") appears; the decided state + ack survive `page.reload()` (transcript-backed) | card visible; after `setInputFiles` the decided marker + `aria-disabled` button + "in the kitchen" ack render, and persist across reload | e2e-ui | e2e/onboarding.spec.ts ("the scaffold choice creates a new project and lands on the empty-canonical map state")

### e2e/overflow.spec.ts

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.61] Suite runs serial within the file (the long-target `POST /api/project` switch re-points the process-global current project on disk); cross-FILE isolation comes from the config's dependency phase (case .299) because selecting the long target re-points the process-global current project | no concurrent current-project mutation | e2e-process | e2e/overflow.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.299] The file RESTORES the demo target after ALL its tests (`afterAll` POST /api/project with DEMO_TARGET_ROOT — a hook, so no future test can bypass it) so the main demo phase starts from the canonical current project instead of the long target leaking into control.spec's run | restore response ok; later specs observe the demo project | e2e-process | e2e/overflow.spec.ts (afterAll hook)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.62] `expectNoPageOverflow` helper: `document.documentElement.scrollWidth` and `body.scrollWidth` never exceed `innerWidth + 2px` tolerance | assertion helper used at every step below | unit-of-helper (inline) | e2e/overflow.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.64] At 1320x820 on the demo target: initial map render has no page-level horizontal overflow | assertion passes | e2e-ui | e2e/overflow.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.69] Switching to the long-rooted (governed) project via `POST /api/project {root, requireGoverned:true}` then reloading (the absolute-path input is gone) | map reloads for the long target within 30s with no page overflow | e2e-integration | e2e/overflow.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.71] Details panel with long source refs/paths (after clicking a node then "Details") | "Source refs" visible within 15s; no page overflow | e2e-ui | e2e/overflow.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.72] Tasks panel with long-path issue cards | "ISS-0001" exact text visible within 15s; no page overflow | e2e-ui | e2e/overflow.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.73] Transcript modal opened via a retry loop (`toPass` up to 30s) that force-clicks past SSE-driven re-renders that can detach the button mid-click | dialog visible within 3s per attempt; no page overflow; dialog box fits within 1320px width | e2e-ui | e2e/overflow.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.74] If NO transcript button exists on the demo issues (conditional `if (await transcriptButton.count())`), the transcript-modal overflow check is silently skipped | GAP: a demo fixture regression that drops all transcript refs would silently skip this coverage rather than fail loudly | e2e-ui | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.75] Narrow viewport (760x720): map has no page-level horizontal overflow | assertion passes | e2e-ui | e2e/overflow.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.76] Overflow at true mobile widths (< 400px, e.g. the Pixel 7 project's 412px) | GAP: overflow.spec.ts only tests 1320px and 760px fixed sizes — nothing excludes it from the mobile project, so it DOES run on chromium-mobile at the device's native (412x915) viewport, but no test explicitly sets/asserts THAT narrowest width scenario — the two explicit sizes (1320, 760) both override the mobile device's real viewport | e2e-ui | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.77] Overflow inside the Agent Settings dialog with long strings (long model names) | GAP: overflow.spec.ts never opens it; only settings.spec.ts touches it, and it does not assert overflow | e2e-ui | GAP

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.81] Changing "Implementer thinking level" via the shadcn Select to `max`, then Save | PUT `/api/settings`; "Settings saved" toast visible within 15s; dialog closes | e2e-integration | e2e/settings.spec.ts

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.83] Test restores the documented default (`xhigh`) at the end so the store stays clean for other runs | store ends at the documented default | e2e-process (test hygiene) | e2e/settings.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.84] Default CLI assignment: implementer = Claude Code, reviewer = Codex | both comboboxes show correct defaults | e2e-ui | e2e/settings.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.85] Assigning the implementer to Codex triggers the "distinct-CLI invariant": reviewer auto-moves to Claude Code (one CLI can never hold both roles) | reviewer combobox flips automatically | e2e-ui / business-rule | e2e/settings.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.86] Save + reopen round-trips the swapped assignment | comboboxes read back Codex/Claude Code | e2e-integration | e2e/settings.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.87] Assigning the REVIEWER to Claude Code (the inverse swap direction) forces the implementer to move instead | GAP: only the implementer-first swap direction is tested; the symmetric reviewer-first swap is never independently asserted (though the test's own restore step does perform half of this without checking the invariant explicitly) | e2e-ui | GAP

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.89] Switching to `claude-opus-4-5` (no fast mode) disables the Fast switch | switch becomes disabled | e2e-ui / compatibility-matrix | e2e/settings.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.90] Switching back to a fast-capable model re-enables Fast AND resets it to unchecked (model switch clears fast state) | switch enabled, unchecked | e2e-ui | e2e/settings.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.91] Saving persists the fast-capable default with fast off | round-trip via toast + dialog close | e2e-integration | e2e/settings.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.92] Attempting to save with an INCOMPATIBLE fast=true + no-fast model combination via a race (e.g. direct API call bypassing the UI's auto-reset) | GAP: no test proves the SERVER also rejects/normalizes an invalid fast+model combo — only the client-side UI auto-reset is covered | integration | GAP

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.94] Max parallel issues defaults to `1` | input value "1" | e2e-ui | e2e/settings.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.95] Setting max parallel to `3`, saving, reopening reads back `3` | round-trip via JSON store | e2e-integration | e2e/settings.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.96] Restoring to `1` at the end for store cleanliness | store ends at 1 | e2e-process (test hygiene) | e2e/settings.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.97] Stepper input advertises `min="1"` and `max="12"` attributes | both attributes present | e2e-ui | e2e/settings.spec.ts

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.99] Filling `2` then clicking Decrease floors at exactly `1`; Decrease then becomes disabled | value "1", Decrease disabled | e2e-ui, boundary=min | e2e/settings.spec.ts

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.101] Screenshot documentation frame for the concurrency stepper at mid-range value `6` (both arrows enabled), desktop-only | screenshot written to `/tmp/vivicy-concurrency.png` | e2e-ui | e2e/settings.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.102] Closing the Settings dialog via Escape/backdrop-click without saving discards in-flight edits | GAP: no test verifies unsaved changes are discarded on dialog dismissal (every test in this file explicitly Saves or restores) | e2e-ui | GAP

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.120] Desktop-only: sidebar width toggle cycles peek(24rem, expanded) -> wide(36rem, expanded) -> closed(collapsed) -> peek(24rem) again, verified via `--sidebar-width` CSS var and `data-state` attribute | full 4-state cycle asserted | e2e-ui | e2e/sidebar-legend-minimap.spec.ts

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.122] Hydration-mismatch regression: pre-seeding `localStorage["vivicy:panel-state"] = "wide"` via `addInitScript` BEFORE first paint reproduces the exact SSR-vs-client-first-render disagreement that used to log a hydration error | console listener records zero matches for the strict hydration-mismatch regex across error/warning levels and `pageerror` | e2e-ui (regression) | e2e/sidebar-legend-minimap.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.123] The hydration-mismatch regex is precise enough to NOT false-positive on Firefox's benign `console.timeStamp("Hydrated")` dev perf marker | zero false positives on Firefox project | e2e-ui, cross-browser | e2e/sidebar-legend-minimap.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.124] Desktop only: after the hydration-safe fix, the persisted "wide" state still visibly takes effect post-hydration (36rem) — proves the fix moved persistence past hydration rather than removing it | `--sidebar-width` reads exactly "36rem" | e2e-ui | e2e/sidebar-legend-minimap.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.125] Legend trigger "Legend · Kind colors" is present, collapsed by default (`data-state="closed"`) | attribute matches | e2e-ui | e2e/sidebar-legend-minimap.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.126] The old floating `.map-legend` overlay on the map canvas was fully removed (regression guard) | count is 0 | e2e-ui (regression) | e2e/sidebar-legend-minimap.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.127] Expanding the legend (click-past-overlap on mobile Sheet) flips `data-state` to "open" and reveals the swatch-row content with at least one visible label span | content visible; `data-state="open"` | e2e-ui | e2e/sidebar-legend-minimap.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.128] Legend lives in the fixed sidebar footer region, positioned ABOVE the Quota label (asserted via bounding-box Y comparison, not DOM order alone) | legend box `y` < quota box `y` | e2e-ui | e2e/sidebar-legend-minimap.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.129] Collapsing the legend again after expanding it (the reverse toggle) | GAP: only the closed->open transition is exercised; open->closed is never asserted | e2e-ui | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.130] Minimap renders visible, sized at or under the compact 140x100 box (`width<=180`, `height<=140` with margin) | bounding box within limits | e2e-ui | e2e/sidebar-legend-minimap.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.131] Minimap has at least 1 painted node rect, and every rect has a non-`none`/non-empty stroke color (regression: previously-empty/all-white minimap) | count >= 1; every stroke is non-empty | e2e-ui (regression) | e2e/sidebar-legend-minimap.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.132] At least one minimap rect's stroke is concretely non-white/non-transparent | `someColored === true` | e2e-ui | e2e/sidebar-legend-minimap.spec.ts

### e2e/global-setup.ts

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.154] Demo target: `rmSync` + `mkdirSync` + `copyTarget` (which explicitly filters out any nested `.git`) rebuilds `/tmp/vivicy-demo` fresh from the committed fixture every run | demo target directory tree matches the committed fixture bytes, with no `.git` subtree | integration/filesystem | e2e/global-setup.ts (implicitly exercised by every demo-shape spec)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.155] No-map target: fresh `.vivicy/canonical/01-overview.md` is written with placeholder content, no generated map | `/api/map` resolves to `no_map` for this target | integration/filesystem | e2e/global-setup.ts (exercised by e2e/empty-state.spec.ts)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.156] Onboarding target: directory recreated empty with NO `.vivicy/` at all | `/api/map` resolves to `no_target` | integration/filesystem | e2e/global-setup.ts (exercised by e2e/onboarding.spec.ts)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.157] Long-path target: only materialized `if (existsSync(DEMO_TARGET_ROOT))` (i.e. depends on the demo-target step having already run in this same invocation) — copies the demo target under the deeply nested long directory chain | long target tree exists as a copy of the demo target | integration/filesystem | e2e/global-setup.ts (exercised by e2e/overflow.spec.ts)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.158] If the demo-target copy step were to fail/throw BEFORE the long-target step runs, `existsSync(DEMO_TARGET_ROOT)` guard silently skips creating the long target rather than failing global-setup loudly | GAP: a partial/failed demo-target materialization degrades silently into a missing long-target fixture, surfacing later as a confusing overflow.spec.ts failure rather than a clear setup error | e2e-process | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.159] Every per-(shape × browser) runtime dir is wiped (`rmSync recursive+force`) so no stale current-project/settings/run-lock survives across runs | 12 runtime dirs (3 shapes x 4 browsers) all start empty | integration/filesystem | e2e/global-setup.ts (implicitly exercised — a stale lock would manifest as control.spec.ts's pre-condition-cleanup branch firing)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.160] Demo shape ONLY: `current-project.json` is pre-seeded per browser with the REALPATH-resolved demo root (works around macOS's `/tmp` -> `/private/tmp` symlink) so `/api/project` reports it immediately (the app does NOT fall back to `VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT` alone) | file written with `{root: realpath}` for chromium-desktop, chromium-mobile, firefox-desktop, webkit-desktop | integration/filesystem | e2e/global-setup.ts (foundational to every demo-shape spec that expects the demo project to be reported immediately)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.161] Empty/onboarding shapes are deliberately left UNSEEDED (no current-project.json) so their `no_map`/`no_target` states derive purely from the env target, not from a persisted pick | both shapes correctly show their env-derived state | integration | e2e/global-setup.ts (exercised by empty-state.spec.ts / onboarding.spec.ts)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.162] Onboard-scaffold parent dirs wiped + recreated per browser key so the onboarding scaffold spec always scaffolds into a fresh empty parent | `onboardScaffoldParent(browserKey)` exists and is empty before the suite | integration/filesystem | e2e/global-setup.ts (exercised by onboarding.spec.ts's Mode B test)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.163] `globalSetup` throwing/partially failing mid-way (e.g. disk full, permission denied on `/tmp`) leaves SOME targets rebuilt and others stale, with no atomicity/rollback across the whole function | GAP: no test (nor code comment) addresses partial-globalSetup-failure recovery; a half-completed run silently proceeds with a mixed old/new fixture state | e2e-process | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.164] Global setup running while stale dev servers from a KILLED prior run still hold their ports (no server cleanup/kill step exists in global-setup itself — only `reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI` in playwright.config) | GAP: no assertion or safeguard here for "orphaned dev server holding /tmp/vivicy-demo open with a stale in-memory route cache" | e2e-process | GAP

### e2e/helpers.ts

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.165] `isMobileProject`: returns true iff the Playwright project name contains "-mobile" | pure string-match helper, exercised transitively by every spec that branches on it | unit-of-helper | (transitively covered by nearly every spec; no standalone unit test)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.166] `ensurePanelOpen` on desktop: no-op beyond confirming the wordmark is already visible (docked, open by default) | wordmark visible within 30s, no click performed | e2e-ui (helper) | transitively covered by every desktop spec using it
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.167] `ensurePanelOpen` on mobile when the Sheet is ALREADY open (idempotence: `if (await wordmark.isVisible()...) return`) | returns immediately without re-clicking the toggle | e2e-ui (helper) | GAP — no spec deliberately calls `ensurePanelOpen` twice in a row on mobile to prove the idempotent early-return path fires rather than double-toggling shut
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.168] `ensurePanelOpen` on mobile when the Sheet is closed: clicks `[data-panel-toggle]`, waits for `[data-mobile="true"]` then the wordmark, both within 15s | Sheet opens successfully | e2e-ui (helper) | transitively covered by every mobile spec using it
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.169] `ensurePanelOpen` called BEFORE the map/toggle has rendered (requires `.react-flow__node` visible first per the doc comment) — calling it too early | GAP: the doc comment states this precondition but no test verifies the helper's behavior (timeout vs silent no-op) when a caller violates it | e2e-ui (helper) | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.170] `openSettingsDialog`: waits for map, opens panel, force-clicks past overlap on the Settings gear, confirms "Agent settings" dialog text | dialog visible | e2e-ui (helper) | transitively covered by every settings.spec.ts test
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.171] `clickPastOverlap`: scrolls into view then force-clicks, bypassing Playwright's normal actionability/stability gate entirely | used wherever a scrollable sibling can visually overlap the real target | e2e-ui (helper) | transitively covered by control.spec.ts, settings.spec.ts, sidebar-legend-minimap.spec.ts, smoke.spec.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.172] `clickPastOverlap` force-clicking a target that is ACTUALLY disabled (not just overlapped) — force:true bypasses Playwright's disabled-element guard too, which could mask a real "the button should be disabled and unclickable" regression as a false pass | GAP: no test/comment addresses this risk; every current caller only uses it on enabled, merely-overlapped elements, but nothing in the helper itself prevents misuse against a genuinely disabled target | e2e-ui (helper), risk | GAP

### playwright.config.ts

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.173] 12 total projects = 3 shapes (demo/empty/onboarding) x 4 browsers (chromium-desktop, chromium-mobile [Pixel 7], firefox-desktop, webkit-desktop), each with its OWN dev server on its OWN port and its OWN runtime/dist dir | all 12 projects boot and pass independently | e2e-process | playwright.config.ts (structurally exercised by every spec run)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.174] `DEMO_TEST_IGNORE` excludes `empty-state.spec.ts` and `onboarding.spec.ts` from all `demo-*` projects | those 2 files never run against the demo server | e2e-process | playwright.config.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.300] `OVERFLOW_SPEC` phase isolation: each `demo-<browser>` project excludes overflow.spec and DEPENDS on a `demo-<browser>-overflow` companion project (same server/baseURL) that runs ONLY overflow.spec — the one spec that re-points the server's current project can never overlap the rest of that browser's demo suite; `--project=demo-<browser>` still covers it because Playwright auto-runs dependency projects | overflow.spec executes strictly before the main demo phase on the same server | e2e-process | playwright.config.ts (structurally exercised by every demo project run)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.301] `canonicalIfExists` on the webServer `VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT` env: the env fallback carries the CANONICAL (realpath) spelling when the target dir exists, matching global-setup's realpath'ed current-project seeds — even a transient env-fallback read (current-project store mid-rewrite) derives the SAME per-project runtime key instead of forking the run lock across /tmp vs /private/tmp spellings | env and persisted spellings agree on every suite server | e2e-process | playwright.config.ts (structural)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.175] `empty` shape's `testMatch` restricts it to ONLY `empty-state.spec.ts`; `onboarding` shape restricts to ONLY `onboarding.spec.ts` | no other spec file executes against those two server shapes | e2e-process | playwright.config.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.177] `CHROMIUM_DESKTOP_ONLY` regex excludes `layout-edit.spec.ts` from every project EXCEPT `chromium-desktop` (avoids a cross-project race on the shared demo yml) | file runs on exactly 1 of the 12 projects | e2e-process | playwright.config.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.178] `VIVICY_DIST_DIR` gives each server its own `.next-e2e-<shape>-<browser>` dist dir so 12 concurrent Next dev servers never collide on Next's single-instance dev lock | 12 distinct dist dirs created, no lock collision | e2e-process | playwright.config.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.179] `workers: 4` caps concurrency (matches the reference project's e2e worker count) specifically to reduce mid-compile contention against 12 lazily-compiling dev servers | fewer than 12 tests hammer servers simultaneously | e2e-process (perf/flake mitigation) | playwright.config.ts (not independently testable; inferred from config)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.180] `retries: 1` self-heals a transient first-hit compile-jitter timeout; a genuinely broken test still fails BOTH attempts | flaky-looking single failures pass on retry; real regressions fail twice | e2e-process | playwright.config.ts (implicit; no test deliberately proves "fails twice = reported failure")
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.181] `reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI` means LOCAL runs reuse an already-running dev server on that port (possibly serving STALE code from before the latest edit) while CI always boots fresh | GAP: no safeguard/test catches a developer running against a stale locally-reused server and getting false-negative/false-positive results that would not reproduce in CI | e2e-process | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.182] `timeout: 60_000` (test-level) vs `webServer timeout: 180_000` (server-boot-level) — a test whose FIRST assertion needs on-demand Next compilation that legitimately takes 45-59s (compile + assertion) is right at the edge of the per-test timeout | GAP: no stress test proves behavior right at that boundary; only the config comment acknowledges the risk ("a heavy spec's first map-load can briefly exceed its timeout") | e2e-process, boundary | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.183] `browser-issues-reporter.ts` aggregates per-test issue attachments and fails the run on any non-allowlisted browser error | run status flips to failed when ≥1 error-level non-allowlisted issue exists across the whole run | e2e-integration | GAP (behavior inventoried in .280–.286 below; proven manually 2026-07-05 by an injected console.error and by two real catches — the scaffold-dialog t.rich React error and the Firefox SSE aborts — but no automated negative test)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.280] `e2e/browser-issues.ts` console classification: `error`/`warning`/`assert` console messages are captured with location + first serialized args; `log`/`info`/`debug` are ignored | captured issues carry kind "console", the right level, and a body | unit | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.281] pageerror capture: uncaught page exceptions become error-level issues with stack; the known WebKit `_rsc` fetch-abort pattern is filtered as noise | real exceptions recorded, WebKit RSC aborts absent | unit | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.282] requestfailed classification: abort error texts (`ERR_ABORTED`, `NS_BINDING_ABORTED`, `NS_ERROR_ABORT`, `NS_BASE_STREAM_CLOSED`, cancelled/canceled) on SAME-ORIGIN requests are ignored entirely; cross-origin aborts downgrade to warning; non-abort failures are error-level | Firefox EventSource teardown aborts on `/api/status/stream` never fail the gate (regression found live 2026-07-05: `NS_ERROR_ABORT` was missing from the matcher and failed a green cross-browser run) | unit | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.283] same-origin HTTP 4xx/5xx responses are recorded as issues, with the 5xx response body captured (capped) | status, statusText, method, and capped body present on the issue | unit | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.284] issue deduplication inside one test via the issue key (kind/level/text/url/location/method/status) | repeating the same error N times yields one issue | unit | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.285] on test end the collected issues attach as the `browser-issues` JSON attachment; a clean test attaches nothing | attachment present iff issues occurred | unit | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.286] reporter allowlist: Next static-chunk load-failure warnings and Firefox/WebKit network-cancel signatures are allowlisted; anything else error-level blocks | an allowlisted pattern never masks a real error on the same URL shape (proven manually: an injected console.error at a `/_next/static/chunks/` location still failed the run) | e2e-integration | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.287] `npm run matrix:stamp` (scripts/test-matrix.ts CLI) computes the behavior fingerprint (app/components/lib/factory/hooks/scripts/e2e sources + root configs, test files excluded) and writes/updates the `Reconciled fingerprint: <sha256> @ commit <HEAD>` line in test/TEST-MATRIX.md (legacy no-commit stamp lines are upgraded in place; no git repo → commit "unknown") | stamped value equals a fresh recompute and carries the reconciliation commit | unit | scripts/test-matrix.test.ts "the behavior fingerprint is stamped and matches"
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.288] editing any behavior-bearing source file without re-stamping makes the matrix guard fail the vitest suite, printing the exact behavior files changed since the stamped commit (`git diff --name-only <stamped>` ∪ dirty tree, filtered to behavior paths) | `npm test` red on unreconciled behavior change, with an actionable file list | e2e-process | GAP (positive path covered by the guard test; the failure path was only demonstrated live, no automated negative test)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.302] `computeBehaviorFingerprint` EXCLUDES regenerated run artifacts (`factory/rehearsal/reports/`): a rehearsal run alone never invalidates a fresh stamp (found live: the report re-reddened the guard minutes after an honest reconcile) | rehearsal report churn leaves the fingerprint unchanged; product-file churn still changes it | unit | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.308] `scripts/test-matrix.ts` `walk()` (behavior-fingerprint discovery) skips `node_modules` and every dotdir (`entry.startsWith(".")`) and only descends the rooted `BEHAVIOR_DIRS`, so a nested git worktree under `.vivicy-worktrees/` or `.claude/worktrees/` never enters the fingerprint — the walker has NO double-count hole, unlike vitest discovery which needed the .189 excludes | files inside a nested worktree/tool dir are absent from `computeBehaviorFingerprint` | unit | GAP — verified by inspection; no test seeds a nested-worktree file and asserts the fingerprint ignores it
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.303] `clean-artifacts.ts` prunes DEAD `.next-*` entries from tsconfig.json's `include` (the Next plugin auto-appends every dist dir it ever serves and never removes them): entries whose dir no longer exists are dropped; `.next` and the 12 official e2e matrix dist dirs are always kept (Next re-adds them anyway — keeping them avoids a per-run tsconfig diff); byte-identical no-op when nothing is dead; a malformed tsconfig is left untouched (typecheck owns that failure) | zombie include pruned on `npm run clean`; matrix/. next entries survive; second run is a no-op | unit | GAP (proven live: seeded zombie pruned, idempotence byte-checked)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.304] vivi.md carries la Nonna's VOICE section (Italian-kitchen register: la ricetta/mise en place/in the oven) bounded by four guards — engineer-first posture ("not a toy": the accent recedes on dense subjects), seasoning density capped, plain-facts-first on errors, and NO metaphor in any written file (canonical/spikes/CRs stay technical English; the action block stays strict JSON) — locked so the brand can never leak into method artifacts (P1) | prompt-lock assertions on the section + all three guards | unit | prompts.test.ts ("vivi.md carries la Nonna's voice WITH the no-seasoning-in-files guard")
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.289] matrix status-table integrity: per-area cases/gaps recounted from the actual bullets, covered = cases − gaps, TOTAL = column sums | any drift lists every mismatch and fails | unit | scripts/test-matrix.test.ts "the status table matches the actual per-area bullet and GAP counts" (caught 4 real inconsistencies on first run)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.290] matrix case ids are globally unique across areas and cross-matrices | duplicate ids listed and failed | unit | scripts/test-matrix.test.ts "case ids are globally unique"
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.291] `npm run matrix:delta` prints the reconciliation work-list (behavior files changed since the stamped commit, committed ∪ dirty, behavior-filtered, sorted; "(none…)" when reconciled; exit 2 with a message when no stamp line exists; fingerprint already matching => "nothing to reconcile", no list) | the printed list matches `changedBehaviorFilesSince(stamp.commit)` exactly — the agent work-list is git-derived, never a full-repo rescan | unit | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.184] `trace: "retain-on-failure"` and `screenshot: "only-on-failure"` global use-options | on any failing test, a trace.zip and failure screenshot are retained; on a passing test neither is kept | e2e-process | playwright.config.ts (not independently tested; would require a deliberately-failing spec to verify artifact retention)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.185] `forbidOnly: !!process.env.CI` — a stray `.only` in a spec fails the CI run but is silently permitted locally | GAP: no test proves a committed `.only` is actually caught in CI mode (would need a CI-mode dry run against a fixture file containing `.only`) | e2e-process | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.186] Port collision: two DIFFERENT shapes' base ports (`DEMO_BASE_PORT=3100`, `EMPTY_BASE_PORT=3110`, `ONBOARD_BASE_PORT=3120`) are 10 apart, but each shape allocates 4 sequential ports (indices 0-3) for its 4 browsers — 3100-3103, 3110-3113, 3120-3123 — no overlap by construction, but this invariant is unexecuted/unverified beyond "if it collided, all 12 servers would fail to boot" | GAP: no explicit assertion that the port ranges never overlap (relies on the base-port spacing being manually kept in sync with `BROWSERS.length`) | e2e-process | GAP

### vitest.config.ts

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.187] `jsdom` environment + `setupFiles: ["./vitest.setup.ts"]` applies polyfills to every unit test | ResizeObserver/pointer-capture/localStorage polyfills active in all `.test.ts(x)` runs | unit (config, transitively exercised) | vitest.config.ts (exercised by the whole `*.test.ts(x)` suite)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.188] `include: ["**/*.test.ts", "**/*.test.tsx"]` picks up test files anywhere in the repo (not scoped to a `test/` dir), bounded by the `exclude` list | every `*.test.ts(x)` file in the tree is discovered except those under an excluded path (see .189) | unit (config) | vitest.config.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.189] `exclude` = the standard defaults (`configDefaults.exclude`: `node_modules`, `.git`) plus `.next`, the nested-worktree/tool dirs `.vivicy-worktrees/**` (the product's own factory loop) and `.claude/**` (any local tool dir, incl. `.claude/worktrees/`), `e2e/` (Playwright's own domain), and factory (except explicitly `generate-viewer-data.test.ts`, via the glob negation `factory/!(generate-viewer-data).test.ts`) | vitest run never picks up e2e specs, the `node:test`-based factory suite (except that one named file), or DUPLICATED test copies inside a nested git worktree — a mid-loop `npm test` can't double-count or execute stale copies from `.vivicy-worktrees/` or `.claude/worktrees/` | unit (config) | vitest.config.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.190] The glob-negation exclude pattern `factory/!(generate-viewer-data).test.ts` correctly matches ALL factory `*.test.ts` files except exactly `generate-viewer-data.test.ts`, including files with hyphens/multiple words in their basename (e.g. `dev-loop.test.ts`, `atomic-write.test.ts`) | every other factory `*.test.ts` is excluded from vitest; only `generate-viewer-data.test.ts` runs under vitest | unit (config) | GAP — an extglob pattern like this is exactly the kind of thing that silently breaks on a shell/glob-engine version change or a new factory test file with an unusual name; no assertion (e.g. a `vitest --listTests` snapshot) guards this
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.191] `alias: {"@": path.resolve(__dirname, ".")}` resolves `@/...` imports the same way the app's own tsconfig path mapping does | components importing via `@/` resolve correctly under vitest | unit (config) | vitest.config.ts (transitively exercised by every component test using `@/` imports)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.192] Running `generate-viewer-data.test.ts` under BOTH vitest (per this config's exclude carve-out) AND `node --test` (per package.json's `factory:test` script, which does not list it) | GAP: need to confirm this file is not ALSO double-registered/double-run under `node --test`, which would be redundant or could reveal a runner-specific behavior difference; package.json's factory:test list was read and does NOT include generate-viewer-data.test.ts, so it appears vitest-only — but no assertion documents this split intentionally | unit (config), cross-runner consistency | GAP

### vitest.setup.ts

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.193] `ResizeObserver` polyfill installed only `if (typeof globalThis.ResizeObserver === "undefined")` — a no-op `observe/unobserve/disconnect` class | components using Radix Tooltip/Select mount without throwing "ResizeObserver is not defined" | unit (polyfill) | vitest.setup.ts (transitively exercised by every component test rendering a Radix primitive)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.194] `hasPointerCapture`/`setPointerCapture`/`releasePointerCapture`/`scrollIntoView`/`scrollTo` polyfilled onto `Element.prototype` only if absent, as harmless no-ops | Radix Select opens and the message-scroller mounts/scrolls in jsdom without throwing | unit (polyfill) | vitest.setup.ts (transitively exercised by the settings-dialog and vivi-panel component tests)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.195] `window.localStorage` polyfilled with a real in-memory `Map`-backed Storage implementation (get/set/remove/clear/key/length) only when jsdom's own (opaque-origin) environment doesn't expose one | panel-state hook and quota footer can read/write localStorage under vitest without a SecurityError | unit (polyfill) | vitest.setup.ts (transitively exercised by any component test touching panel-state/quota-footer persistence)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.196] The localStorage polyfill's `key(index)` method behavior when `index` is out of bounds (negative, or >= length) | returns `null` per the Storage spec (`Array.from(store.keys())[index] ?? null`) — correct for out-of-range but NOT explicitly asserted by any test | unit, boundary | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.197] Two component tests in the SAME vitest worker/file sharing the SAME in-memory localStorage Map instance across tests (no `beforeEach` reset visible in this file) | GAP: if a component test writes a panel-state/quota key and a LATER test in the same file expects a clean localStorage, cross-test state leakage could occur — no reset-between-tests mechanism is visible in vitest.setup.ts itself (would need to be handled per-test-file, not verified here) | unit, isolation | GAP

### next.config.ts

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.198] `turbopack.root` pinned to the app dir avoids the "multi-lockfile root" warning when checked out alongside sibling projects | no Turbopack root-detection warning in dev server logs | integration (config) | next.config.ts (not directly asserted by any e2e/unit test; inferred from absence of the warning in normal runs)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.199] `distDir` overridden via `VIVICY_DIST_DIR` env var when set, defaults to `.next` otherwise | e2e's 12 servers each get a distinct dist dir; a plain `npm run dev` still uses `.next` | integration (config) | next.config.ts (exercised structurally by playwright.config's `webServersForShape`)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.200] `allowedDevOrigins: ["127.0.0.1", "localhost"]` permits Playwright's loopback dev traffic (HMR/RSC) without cross-origin blocking | e2e specs never hit a cross-origin-blocked dev resource | integration (config) | next.config.ts (implicitly exercised by every e2e spec; a regression here would surface as broad HMR/RSC failures across the whole suite)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.201] A THIRD loopback host/IP (e.g. `0.0.0.0`, a LAN IP, or `[::1]`) used to reach the dev server | GAP: only `127.0.0.1`/`localhost` are allowed; not tested against any other loopback form, and no test proves a disallowed origin is actually blocked (there's no negative test at all for `allowedDevOrigins`) | integration, security | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.202] `next-intl` plugin wiring (`createNextIntlPlugin("./i18n/request.ts")`) wraps the config; a request-time i18n misconfiguration | GAP: out of this area's file list (i18n/request.ts not read here) but the wrapping itself is unverified for a broken i18n config surfacing as a build/dev-server-boot failure | integration | GAP (adjacent area)

### package.json

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.203] `npm test` = `node scripts/clean-artifacts.ts -- vitest run` — cleans artifacts unconditionally after vitest, regardless of pass/fail | exit code equals vitest's own exit code; `test-results`/`playwright-report` dirs absent afterward | e2e-process | package.json + scripts/clean-artifacts.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.204] `npm run e2e` = same wrapper around `playwright test` | exit code equals Playwright's own; dist-dir/`test-results` artifacts cleaned afterward regardless of outcome | e2e-process | package.json + scripts/clean-artifacts.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.205] `factory:test` explicitly enumerates 18 factory `*.test.ts` files run under `node --test` (not a glob) | exactly those 18 files execute; a NEW factory test file not added to this list is silently never run under `factory:test` | e2e-process, maintenance-risk | GAP — this is an explicit hardcoded list, so adding factory/some-new-thing.test.ts without editing package.json means it silently never runs in CI/local factory:test; no test/lint catches a factory `*.test.ts` file that exists on disk but is absent from this list
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.206] `factory:rehearsal`, `factory:rehearsal:formula`, `factory:rehearsal:all` scripts wire `dev-rehearsal.ts` with `--fixture=formula` and both-in-sequence variants | each script invokes the correct fixture argument | e2e-process | package.json (mirrors dev-rehearsal.ts's own `--fixture=` parsing, see below)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.207] `engines.node: ">=24"` is declared but nothing in the repo's scripts enforces it at invocation time (no preinstall/prestart engine-strict check) | GAP: running any script on Node < 24 (e.g. Node 20 LTS) is not guarded — the factory relies on Node 24+ native TS type-stripping per the repo's own commit history ("migrate the factory from plain-JS .mjs to TypeScript (Node 24+ type stripping)") — an engine mismatch would fail with a confusing runtime error rather than a clear preflight message | e2e-process | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.208] `bin.vivicy: factory/cli.ts` — the published CLI entry point executing correctly when installed as a global/npx package (shebang + Node type-stripping interplay) | GAP: not covered by any file in this area; no e2e/integration test invokes the package via its `bin` entry as an external consumer would | e2e-process | GAP (adjacent area — factory/cli.test.ts likely covers this internally, but not from the `bin` entry point itself)

### scripts/clean-artifacts.ts

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.209] No-args form (`node scripts/clean-artifacts.ts`): removes `test-results/` and `playwright-report/` (both gitignored) and exits 0 | both dirs absent after run; exit code 0 | unit/integration, filesystem | scripts/clean-artifacts.ts (exercised by `npm run clean`)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.210] No-args form also sweeps every top-level entry starting with `.next-e2e-` (the per-matrix-server dist dirs) | all `.next-e2e-*` dirs removed regardless of how many the e2e matrix created | unit/integration, filesystem | scripts/clean-artifacts.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.279] Direct `npx playwright test` (bypassing the npm `e2e` wrapper) still leaves a clean tree: `globalTeardown` (e2e/global-teardown.ts) spawns scripts/clean-artifacts.ts after every playwright invocation, wrapped or not | no `.next-e2e-*`, `test-results/`, or `playwright-report/` entries remain after any playwright run | e2e-process, filesystem | GAP (verified manually 2026-07-05: direct smoke run then ls; no automated assertion)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.211] `readdirSync(REPO_ROOT)` failure (e.g. permission error, repo root briefly unreadable) is caught and treated as an empty entry list rather than crashing the cleaner | function completes without throwing even if the top-level listing fails | unit, error-branch | scripts/clean-artifacts.ts (explicit `try/catch` -> `entries = []`)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.212] Wrapper form (`-- <cmd> <args...>`): runs the command with `stdio: "inherit"`, ALWAYS cleans afterward (even on command failure), then exits with the command's own status code | cleanup runs on both success and failure; final exit code equals the child's exit code | unit/integration, filesystem | scripts/clean-artifacts.ts (exercised in practice by `npm test`/`npm run e2e`, including their failure paths)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.213] Wrapper form when the child process itself fails to SPAWN (`result.error` set, e.g. command not found) | error message printed to stderr; process exits 1; cleanup still ran first | unit, error-branch | scripts/clean-artifacts.ts (explicit `if (result.error)` branch) — GAP: no test actually drives an unspawnable command through this wrapper to confirm the branch fires
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.214] Wrapper form when the child is killed by a SIGNAL rather than exiting normally (`result.status` is `null`, `result.signal` set) | exits 1 (`result.signal ? 1 : 0`) so a terminated run never reports green | unit, error-branch, boundary | GAP — no test simulates a signal-killed child (e.g. `SIGTERM` mid-run) to prove this branch's exit code
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.215] `argv.indexOf("--")` when `--` is literally the LAST argument with nothing after it (`... -- ` with no command) | falls through the `if (!cmd)` guard: cleans and exits 0 (treated as "no command") | unit, boundary=empty | GAP — not exercised by any script invocation shown; only inferred from reading the code
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.216] `ARTIFACTS` list omits `.next` deliberately (documented as "a reusable cache, not a per-run artifact") | plain `.next` dir (non-suffixed) is NEVER removed by either form of this script | unit (design invariant) | scripts/clean-artifacts.ts (comment-documented; not independently asserted by a test that seeds a `.next` dir and proves it survives)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.217] Running the wrapper form with a command that both succeeds AND itself creates NEW `.next-e2e-*` dirs mid-run (e.g. Playwright's webServer boot happening as a side effect of the same invocation) — cleanup after the command's exit removes exactly those dirs the command just created | GAP: this is exactly production usage (`npm run e2e`) but there's no isolated test proving clean-artifacts.ts's sweep runs AFTER the servers are done writing, not concurrently/racing them | e2e-process | GAP

### scripts/eslint-comment-density.ts + eslint.config.mjs

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.305] `vivicy/comment-density` rule: a file whose non-directive comment lines exceed max(5, ceil(4% of total lines)) fails lint with one report at line 1; comment-free files, one-line invariants within the allowance, and ratio-scaled invariants in large files all pass; every line of a multi-line block comment counts; tool directives (`eslint-`, `@ts-`, shebang, `/// <reference`) are exempt | violations reported exactly once per offending file; sanctioned invariant density never trips | unit | scripts/eslint-comment-density.test.ts (6 cases)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.306] eslint.config.mjs wiring: the Next configs (core-web-vitals + typescript) are scoped OFF `factory/**` via the `extends` block (factory must never be linted as React/Next); `factory/**/*.ts` gets `@typescript-eslint/parser` and is linted ONLY by `vivicy/comment-density`; the density rule applies repo-wide to ts/tsx/mjs/mts so `npm run lint` is the mechanical comment-cap gate | `npm run lint` exits 0 on the invariant-only tree and covers factory/ | boundary | eslint.config.mjs (exercised by `npm run lint`; rule mechanics unit-tested in scripts/eslint-comment-density.test.ts)

### factory/dev-rehearsal.ts

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.218] Default fixture (no `--fixture=`) resolves to `pocket-ledger` | `fixtureDir` points at `factory/rehearsal/pocket-ledger` | unit/integration | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts (exercised by `npm run factory:rehearsal`)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.219] `--fixture=formula` resolves to the formula fixture; the name is SANITIZED via `.replace(/[^a-z0-9-]/gi, "")`, stripping any character outside `[a-z0-9-]` | a malicious/malformed `--fixture=../../etc` argument is neutralized to a harmless string rather than enabling path traversal | unit, security (path-traversal guard) | GAP — no test explicitly feeds a path-traversal-style `--fixture=` value and asserts the sanitized result never escapes `FACTORY_REHEARSAL_DIR`
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.220] `--dry` mode uses fake implementer/reviewer legs (`dryImplementer`/`dryReviewer` for sequential, `*Parallel` for concurrent) that write synthetic transcripts instead of spawning real Claude/Codex CLIs | rehearsal completes without any real agent process spawned | e2e-process | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts (exercised by `--dry` mode; presumably CI's default given real-agent cost)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.221] Real (non-dry) mode spawns the actual Claude implementer + Codex reviewer CLIs end to end | full real two-agent loop completes | e2e-process | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts (expensive; run manually per the file's own docs, not by default in CI presumably)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.222] `--concurrency=N` (N>1) drives the PARALLEL scheduler: async dry legs additionally write a disjoint per-issue file marker under `cfg.execRoot` (proving worktree isolation) and `delay(15)`ms to make the concurrency real rather than merely scheduled | markers land in distinct worktree paths, no collision | e2e-process, concurrency | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.223] `parseConcurrency`: a non-numeric or `<1` value for `--concurrency=` falls back to `1` (`Number.isFinite(n) && n >= 1 ? n : 1`) | e.g. `--concurrency=abc`, `--concurrency=0`, `--concurrency=-3` all yield sequential (N=1) | unit, boundary/malformed-input | GAP — no test feeds these malformed concurrency values through `dev-rehearsal.ts` directly; the parsing function itself isn't unit-tested in isolation
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.224] `parseConcurrency` floors a fractional value (`Math.floor`) — e.g. `--concurrency=2.9` becomes `2` | GAP: not exercised | unit, boundary | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.225] `REHEARSAL_DIR` pinning: if the pinned dir already has a `.git`, the run RESUMES rather than re-materializing from the fixture — counts existing `done/*.md` issues and reports them | resumed run continues from the correct issue-completion count | e2e-process, resume-after-kill | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.226] `REHEARSAL_DIR` NOT set (or set but without a `.git` yet): a fresh temp repo is materialized — via `mkdtempSync` if unpinned, or `mkdirSync` at the pinned path if pinned-but-empty — fixture copied in, git initialized, `add -A` + initial commit | fresh isolated repo created and committed | e2e-process | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.227] Stage 2: `doc-baseline.ts verify --require-status frozen --require-baseline-id baseline-v1.0.0` against the freshly committed fixture | exit 0; recorded as PASS | e2e-integration | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts (drives factory/doc-baseline.ts, out of this area's direct scope but invoked here)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.228] Baseline verify FAILING (e.g. a fixture whose committed docs don't match their frozen manifest hash — a corrupted/edited fixture) | GAP: no test deliberately corrupts a fixture doc and confirms this stage fails loudly (status false, non-zero exit propagated) rather than being silently swallowed | e2e-integration, error-branch | GAP
