- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.230] Stage 3 regex `/(\d+) UNCOVERED/` failing to match AT ALL (e.g. the underlying script changes its output format) | GAP: `uncovered` becomes `undefined`, and `uncovered === "0"` is simply false — the stage fails, but with a potentially confusing "expected uncovered count met" message rather than a clear "output format changed" diagnostic | unit, error-branch/parsing-fragility | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.231] Stage 3b: `traceability-check.ts` passes for both fixtures (every issue requirement resolves, MVP reqs covered) | exit 0 | e2e-integration | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.232] Stage 4: `generate-viewer-data.ts` (pre-loop) produces a non-empty `development.issues` array in the generated JSON | issue count > 0 recorded in the PASS detail | e2e-integration | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.233] Stage 4b: the extraction corpus is committed (`git add -A` + commit) mirroring extract-issues' own mechanical commit; the working tree is clean afterward AND the generated map JSON is tracked in git | `git status --porcelain` is empty; `git ls-files` includes the map path | e2e-integration, git | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.234] The static map's baked `graph_item_states` has ZERO entries with `status: "verified"` BEFORE the dev-loop runs (no progress baked in at generation time — progress is purely a ledger-derived read-time overlay) | `staticBakedVerified === 0` | e2e-integration, invariant | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.235] Stage 5: the two-agent dev-loop (`dev-loop.ts`'s `runLoop`) processes every extracted issue to completion for BOTH fixtures, with `readiness: false` explicitly (this rehearsal's own harness self-test carries no readiness leg by design) | `processed` array covers all issues; `doneCount === totalIssues`; `blocked.length === 0` | e2e-process | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts (drives factory/dev-loop.ts, which has its own dedicated `dev-loop.test.ts`)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.236] `runLoop` throwing an uncaught error mid-run (e.g. a leg crashes unexpectedly) | caught, recorded as a FAIL stage with the error message, `processed` stays `[]`, execution continues to the later assertions (which then also fail meaningfully rather than crashing the whole script) | e2e-process, error-branch | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts (explicit try/catch)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.237] Concurrency > 1: every issue settles EXACTLY ONCE (`new Set(order).size === order.length`) — no duplicate processing across worktrees | assertion holds for the formula fixture's larger 13-issue DAG under `--dry --concurrency=N` | e2e-process, concurrency | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.238] Concurrency > 1: no leftover `.vivicy-worktrees/` directories remain after the run (clean worktree teardown) | worktree count is 0 | e2e-process, filesystem, concurrency | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.239] Concurrency > 1: dependency order is respected on the integration branch — every dependent issue's done-move commit appears AFTER (older, since git log is newest-first) its dependencies', verified via the formula fixture's genuinely branching depends_on graph (e.g. ISS-0011 depends on 5/3/6/1; ISS-0013 depends on 6 issues) | `dependencyOrderRespected(temp) === true` | e2e-process, concurrency, git-log-ordering | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.240] Dependency-order check when an issue's dependency was NEVER integrated at all (`depPos === undefined`) — the function returns `false` (a genuine ordering violation) rather than silently skipping | GAP: this specific branch (`return false` for a missing dependency position) is unexercised by any deliberately-broken rehearsal run — only the passing case is exercised in normal use | unit, error-branch | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.241] Guard: if the temp workspace VANISHES mid-run (e.g. an external process cleans `/tmp`), the script fails clearly with a named stage rather than an ENOENT stack trace, writes a partial report, and exits 1 | clear failure message printed; report written; exit 1 | e2e-process, error-branch | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts — GAP: not independently exercised by deliberately deleting the temp dir mid-run in any test
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.242] Stage 6: ledger verification — every `verified` graph-item state carries at least one non-empty `transcript_refs` entry | `verifiedStates.length > 0` and every verified state has transcripts | e2e-integration | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.243] Stage 6: gate-run evidence records — every issue has a matching `pass` status JSON record under `.vivicy/development/gates/` | `passingGates === totalIssues` | e2e-integration | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.244] Stage 7: the static map file bytes are IDENTICAL pre- and post-loop (`staticMapBytesPreLoop.equals(staticMapBytesPostLoop)`) — no per-issue regeneration leaked | byte-for-byte equality holds | e2e-integration, invariant | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.246] Stage 7b(a): the map is tracked in git AND projecting the COMMITTED ledger (read via `git show HEAD:...`, not the working tree) onto the COMMITTED map yields verified progress | `committedVerified > 0` | e2e-integration, git-history-consistency | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.247] Stage 7b(b): ledger AND at least one gate-evidence JSON are tracked in git (real evidence lands in git for the user, not just on disk) | both tracked booleans true | e2e-integration, git | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.248] Stage 7b(c): transcripts are produced ON DISK but NEVER committed to git (the privacy/size boundary) | `transcriptsOnDisk === true` AND `transcriptsCommitted.length === 0` | e2e-integration, invariant (gitignore boundary) | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.249] Stage 7b(d): the final working tree is completely clean per `git status --porcelain` (only gitignored files may be untracked) | porcelain output is empty string | e2e-integration, invariant | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.250] Stage 8: the markdown report is written to `factory/rehearsal/reports/method-rehearsal-report.md` with a verdict, per-stage PASS/FAIL table (pipe characters in detail text escaped), and issue-outcome summary | file exists at the expected path with the expected sections | e2e-process, report-generation | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.251] Overall verdict: temp repo is KEPT (not deleted) when `keep` (`REHEARSAL_KEEP=1`), OR any stage failed, OR a `REHEARSAL_DIR` was pinned; otherwise it's `rmSync`'d | correct keep/delete decision printed and executed in each of the 4 combinations | e2e-process, boundary/branch-matrix | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts — GAP: only the "normal pass, no env vars, unpinned" delete-path and the "pinned" keep-path are exercised by the two documented modes; the FAILED-run-so-kept-even-though-unpinned-and-not-REHEARSAL_KEEP branch is not deliberately exercised by any test
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.252] Process exit code: `0` iff every stage passed, `1` otherwise, REGARDLESS of dry/real/concurrency mode | exit code matches `stages.every(s => s.ok)` | e2e-process | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.253] Running the SAME fixture through `--dry` at `--concurrency=1` vs `--concurrency>1` and confirming both reach IDENTICAL final `done/` issue sets and IDENTICAL ledger verified-count, despite fully different execution paths (sync vs async/worktree legs) | GAP: no test cross-checks that the sequential and parallel dry runs converge to equivalent final state for the SAME fixture — each concurrency level is only self-consistency-checked, never compared against the other | e2e-integration, cross-mode consistency | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.254] Running the `formula` fixture (13 issues, deep multi-dependency DAG) through `--dry --concurrency>1` specifically to stress the scheduler against a WIDER, more branching dependency graph than `pocket-ledger`'s (8 issues, mostly linear via ISS-0008's 7-way fan-in) | this is the documented PURPOSE of the second fixture per its own README ("exercising extraction, traceability, and architecture-map checks on requirement shapes pocket-ledger does not produce") — but the file doesn't show whether `--concurrency` is actually exercised against `formula` anywhere (package.json's `factory:rehearsal:formula` passes no `--concurrency`, defaulting to 1) | e2e-process | GAP — the formula fixture's rich dependency graph is authored but package.json never actually drives it through the concurrent scheduler
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.255] `--fixture=nonexistent` (a fixture name that sanitizes to a valid string but has no corresponding directory under `factory/rehearsal/`) | GAP: `cpSync(fixtureDir, temp, ...)` would throw ENOENT with no friendly pre-check; not exercised by any test, and the script has no explicit "fixture not found" guard/message | unit, error-branch, invalid-input | GAP

### factory/rehearsal/formula/.vivicy/canonical/01-architecture.md

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.259] Architecture doc declares 3 module clusters (pipeline: Tokenizer/Parser/Evaluator; model: CellRef/Sheet/DepGraph; functions: Registry + math/logical/text/lookup) each owning one concern, and each cluster/module maps 1:1 to a `node:` graph_ref used by an issue (tokenizer, parser, evaluator, cellref, sheet, depgraph, registry, fn-math, fn-logical, fn-text, fn-lookup, api, errors = 13 nodes for 13 issues) | every architecture-declared module has exactly one corresponding issue and `graph_refs` node id | e2e-integration (traceability) | factory/semantic-extraction-check.ts + factory/traceability-check.ts (driven by dev-rehearsal.ts's stages 3/3b)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.260] Boundary rule "the Sheet is the single source of truth... no other module may mutate a cell's stored value directly" is a governance-level architectural invariant that no single issue's automated test can directly verify (it's a design constraint on the IMPLEMENTATION agents, not a runtime-checkable property of test fixtures) | GAP: this class of "boundary/architecture invariant" is asserted only in prose; no test (e2e or unit) can mechanically prove a hypothetical rogue module didn't mutate Sheet state directly, short of a full code-review gate | e2e-process (governance), unverifiable-by-tests | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.261] "It runs entirely in memory... talks to no external service, database, file system, or network" — a negative architectural constraint | GAP: no test in the fixture or rehearsal harness asserts the absence of network/filesystem I/O from the formula engine's own generated `src/` code (e.g. no test mocks `fs`/`net` and asserts zero calls) | e2e-process, unverifiable-by-current-tests | GAP

### factory/rehearsal/formula/.vivicy/canonical/02-tokenizer.md through 13-public-api.md

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.262] Every canonical doc's line ranges cited by `source_line_refs` in every issue (e.g. `02-tokenizer.md:5-7`) resolve to EXACTLY the prose that issue's Scope paraphrases — semantic-extraction-check's core job | 0 UNCOVERED lines, every reference resolves | e2e-integration | factory/semantic-extraction-check.ts (driven by dev-rehearsal.ts stage 3)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.263] The tokenizer's `#NAME?`-on-unlexable-character rule, the parser's `#VALUE!`-on-incomplete-expression rule, the evaluator's `#DIV/0!`/`#VALUE!`/`#REF!`/`#NAME?` rules, and VLOOKUP's `#REF!`/`#VALUE!` rules are each independently specified per-module AND collectively must never introduce a 5th error code (per 07-errors.md's "fixed, closed set... no module invents a new error code") | GAP: no cross-cutting rehearsal-level check confirms that the SUM of all per-module error-code usage across the 13 issues never exceeds the 4 declared codes — this is implicitly relied upon (each issue textually only ever cites the 4 known codes) but not mechanically verified by dev-rehearsal.ts itself, only by whatever the (real or dry) implementer produces | e2e-integration, cross-cutting invariant | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.264] The precedence/associativity prose in `03-parser.md` (comparison loosest -> +/- -> */÷ -> exponent tightest, left-assoc except right-assoc exponent, unary minus tighter than any binary) is complex enough that a subtly wrong PRATT PARSER implementation could still pass 100%-line-coverage unit tests while producing wrong trees for some input | GAP: coverage-gate satisfaction (ISS-0005's stated verification bar) is a necessary but not sufficient proof of correct precedence — this is a known class of gap between "100% line/branch coverage" and "semantically correct precedence for every input shape," not specifically caught anywhere in this area | e2e-process, coverage-vs-correctness gap | GAP

### factory/rehearsal/formula/.vivicy/development/issues/ISS-0001.md .. ISS-0013.md

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.265] ISS-0001 (errors, no deps) is a root node with zero `depends_on`; ISS-0013 (public API) has the deepest fan-in (6 direct deps: 0004,0005,0011,0003,0012,0001) — the widest and deepest node in the 13-issue DAG | topological order in `issue-index.json` places ISS-0001 first-eligible and ISS-0013 last-eligible | e2e-integration | factory's issue-index ordering logic (extract-issues, exercised indirectly — issue-index.json for this fixture is pre-authored, not regenerated by dev-rehearsal.ts itself)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.266] Each issue's `verification_gate_ids` (e.g. `gate:test:tokenizer`) is a DISTINCT deterministic gate id per issue, never reused across issues | 13 distinct gate ids exist, one per issue, matching `.vivicy/development/gates/*.json` records after a run | e2e-integration | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts stage 6 (gate-run evidence count check covers the AGGREGATE count, not that each id is distinct — see next)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.267] Gate-run evidence records are counted (`passingGates === totalIssues`) but the dev-rehearsal.ts check does NOT verify that each gate record's filename/id actually corresponds 1:1 to the issue's own `verification_gate_ids` value (only that the COUNT matches) | GAP: a hypothetical bug that produced 13 passing gate records but with MISMATCHED ids (e.g. two issues accidentally sharing a gate id, another issue's gate silently missing) could still show `passingGates === totalIssues` by pure count coincidence | e2e-integration, weak-assertion gap | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.268] ISS-0011 (Evaluator) explicitly calls out "Integration coverage using the real Sheet and the real Registry (not mocks)... stateful/lifecycle boundaries the Anti-Cheating Rules forbid faking" — a fixture-authored anti-cheating requirement on the IMPLEMENTER agent | GAP: whether a real (non-dry) implementer run actually satisfies this (uses real Sheet/Registry, not mocks) is not independently checked by dev-rehearsal.ts itself — it trusts the gate-pass record and coverage gate, not a static-analysis check for mock usage | e2e-process, unverifiable-by-current-harness | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.269] ISS-0012 (DepGraph/recalculation) requires "the same stored cells and the same formulas... produce the same values every time, independent of the order in which cells were set" — a property-based/randomized-order requirement, not just example-based unit tests | GAP: the issue's own verification section describes this determinism property but a "100% branch coverage" gate does not, by construction, prove ORDER-INDEPENDENCE across arbitrary set-orderings — property-based testing isn't mandated or verified at the rehearsal-harness level | e2e-process, coverage-vs-property gap | GAP

### factory/rehearsal/pocket-ledger/README.md

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.270] Documented manual validation recipe (mktemp, git init, doc-baseline verify, semantic-extraction-check, generate-viewer-data, npm test) matches EXACTLY what `dev-rehearsal.ts` automates for stages 1-4 | manual recipe and automated harness agree | doc-consistency | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts (automates the documented manual recipe; no lint enforces they stay in sync if either changes)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.271] Regenerating the frozen manifest (`doc-baseline.ts generate --version 1.0.0 --status frozen --approved-by --approval-ref`) then re-pinning the printed hashes into `architecture-map.yml`, `issue-index.json`, and `progress-ledger.json` — a 3-file manual re-pin after any canonical doc edit | GAP: if a maintainer edits a canonical doc and regenerates the manifest but forgets to re-pin ONE of the three files, the mismatch would only surface as a doc-baseline verify FAILURE the next rehearsal run — there's no single command that re-pins all three atomically, and no test simulates "edited doc, regenerated baseline, forgot one re-pin" | e2e-process, maintenance-risk | GAP

### factory/rehearsal/pocket-ledger/.vivicy/canonical/*.md

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.273] Money-representation invariant ("all monetary amounts are integers in minor units... floating-point money is forbidden") declared in 01-architecture.md and directly cited by ISS-0003's `source_line_refs` | ISS-0003's own verification explicitly asserts "no floating-point drift" | e2e-integration | factory/dev-loop.test.ts + this fixture's own issue verification (not independently re-checked by dev-rehearsal.ts beyond the gate passing)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.274] CLI's "never prints partial output before an argument-validation error" (06-cli.md) is a subtle ordering guarantee — validate-then-output vs output-then-validate — for ISS-0008 | ISS-0008's own verification explicitly calls this out ("an argument-validation failure emits no partial standard output before the error") | e2e-integration | factory/dev-loop.test.ts + this fixture's own issue verification

### factory/rehearsal/pocket-ledger/.vivicy/development/issues/ISS-0001.md .. ISS-0008.md

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.276] ISS-0008 (CLI) has the widest fan-in of this fixture: depends on ALL 7 other issues (0001-0007) — the single integration point exercising the whole DAG-completion path before dev-loop can mark the fixture fully done | ISS-0008 is the last-eligible issue in topological order; `doneCount === totalIssues` only after it completes | e2e-integration | factory/dev-rehearsal.ts (implicit — this fixture's default/no-concurrency rehearsal run is the primary vehicle exercising this)
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.277] ISS-0004 (Categorizer) and ISS-0005 (Reporter) both carry an `edge:` graph_ref (`edge:cli->categorizer:...`, `edge:reporter->ledger:...`) in ADDITION to a `node:` ref — the only two issues in this fixture with edge-level traceability, unlike the formula fixture where no issue carries an edge graph_ref | edge_graph_ref-bearing issues still resolve correctly through `generate-viewer-data.ts`'s traceability projection | e2e-integration | factory/generate-viewer-data.ts (driven by dev-rehearsal.ts stage 4) — GAP: no dedicated check confirms edge-level graph_refs specifically (vs node-level) resolve correctly; this fixture is the ONLY one in the area exercising that shape, and formula never does, so a bug specific to edge_graph_ref resolution could hide behind "pocket-ledger's overall rehearsal passed" without anyone isolating the edge-ref-specific behavior
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.278] The `test/scaffold.test.js` placeholder (a trivial green test that exists so the `npm test` gate has something to pass BEFORE any issue is implemented) — its behavior once REAL issue tests are added by the dev-loop (does it get superseded, deleted, or does it linger as dead weight in the final green suite?) | GAP: not addressed anywhere in the read files; the scaffold's post-implementation fate is unspecified | e2e-process, fixture-hygiene | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.293] Feature-cycle open: guarded by `hasActiveFrozenBaseline && !isSpecCycleOpen` on the just-built repo, then `writeSpecCycle` → cycle open | `isSpecCycleOpen(temp)` true | e2e-process | dev-rehearsal.ts ("feature-cycle: drafting cycle opened on the frozen baseline (guarded)")
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.294] Canonical evolution: a new numbered canonical doc joins the corpus and is committed so the re-freeze sees a clean tree | doc exists, tree clean | e2e-process | dev-rehearsal.ts ("feature-cycle: canonical evolved + committed (new doc joins the corpus)")
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.295] Re-extraction over the evolved canonical with FAKE agent legs + REAL gates | `status green`, `uncovered_lines===0`, the new doc covered, committed, clean tree | e2e-process | dev-rehearsal.ts ("feature-cycle: re-extraction green over the EVOLVED canonical (fake agents, real gates)")
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.296] The re-freeze is a MINOR bump, `approval_ref === cycle.id`, and the prior baseline is SUPERSEDED (never overwritten) | version bumped, approval_ref matches, prior `superseded.by_baseline_id === new id` | e2e-process | dev-rehearsal.ts ("feature-cycle: freeze is a MINOR bump, approval_ref = cycle id, prior baseline superseded")
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.297] The freeze CLOSED the cycle mechanically (P1) — the state file is gone | `spec-cycle.json` absent, `isSpecCycleOpen` false | e2e-process | dev-rehearsal.ts ("feature-cycle: freeze CLOSED the cycle mechanically (state file gone)")

### Area cross-notes

- **Serial-suite lock contention across files, not just within one file.** `control.spec.ts`, `settings.spec.ts`, `layout-edit.spec.ts`, and `overflow.spec.ts` are EACH individually `test.describe.configure({mode:"serial"})`, and all target the SAME demo server/port for a given browser project. Playwright's `fullyParallel: true` + `workers: 4` means these four files can still run CONCURRENTLY as separate files against the same demo server, each internally serial but racing each other's writes to the same on-disk current-project/settings/run-lock/yml. Nothing in the config file-level `test.describe.configure` scope prevents inter-FILE races — only intra-file ordering is guaranteed. This is a structural GAP: no evidence anywhere (config or spec comments) that cross-file serialization on the shared demo target is actually enforced beyond "hope the timing doesn't collide," despite the file-level comments only ever reasoning about intra-file races.
- **The onboarding chooser's untested Modes A/C/D (open-existing, import-docs, talk-to-Vivi) are the single biggest coverage hole in the onboarding surface** — only Mode B (scaffold) is driven end-to-end; the other three intake/acquisition paths are asserted present but never clicked through, meaning a broken import or Vivi-chat intake flow would not be caught by e2e at all.
- **`clickPastOverlap`'s `force: true` is used pervasively as the standard idiom for mobile-Sheet/scroller overlap**, but the same escape hatch would silently mask a genuinely-disabled control if ever misapplied — this is a latent risk shared across control.spec.ts, settings.spec.ts, sidebar-legend-minimap.spec.ts, and smoke.spec.ts, not a single-file issue.
- **The screenshot-based specs (xbrowser-screenshots and the two fixed-frame captures inside settings.spec.ts) are documentation captures, not visual-regression gates** — none of the PNG outputs across this area are ever diffed against a baseline or against each other. This is a consistent, area-wide gap: "prove the whole UI works across browsers" (the xbrowser file's own stated goal) is only partially met — presence/reachability is proven by the preceding assertions, but the actual PIXEL content is never verified, only captured for human review.
- **Global-setup's fixture materialization order is a hidden dependency chain**: long-target creation depends on demo-target materialization having already succeeded in the SAME globalSetup call (guarded by `existsSync`), and the demo-shape's `current-project.json` seeding depends on the demo-target copy's realpath. A partial failure early in `globalSetup` degrades every later step silently rather than aborting the whole matrix loudly — this cross-cuts overflow.spec.ts (long target), every demo-shape spec (current-project seed), and is not guarded by any single test.
- **The rehearsal harness (`dev-rehearsal.ts`) and the two fixtures (`formula`, `pocket-ledger`) constitute Vivicy's OWN meta-test-suite** — they are the mechanism that proves the whole freeze -> extract -> dev-loop -> merge -> ledger chain works end-to-end, self-tested against the factory's own tooling. Because this harness is itself "the test," there is no higher-level test-of-the-tests: a subtle bug in one of the ~15 chained assertions inside `dev-rehearsal.ts` (e.g. the weak `passingGates === totalIssues` COUNT-only check noted in ISS-0001-013 cross-notes, or the unexercised `dependencyOrderRespected` false-branch) would not itself be caught by any other test in this area — the rehearsal harness has no meta-rehearsal. This is the deepest structural gap in the whole area: correctness of the verifier of correctness is unverified.
- **Neither fixture's `--dry --concurrency>1` path is exercised by any package.json script** — `factory:rehearsal` and `factory:rehearsal:formula` both default to sequential (concurrency=1); the parallel-worktree scheduler is only ever exercised by someone manually running `node factory/dev-rehearsal.ts --dry --concurrency=N`, which means CI (as configured via package.json alone) likely never runs the concurrent path at all for either fixture.

### Adversarial additions

- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.X1] `main()` is invoked bare at the bottom of `factory/dev-rehearsal.ts` (`main();`) with no `.catch`/`.then` attached — an unhandled rejection anywhere in the async body (e.g. the dynamic `import(pathToFileURL(...dev-loop.ts))` at stage 5 rejecting because the temp repo's `dev-loop.ts` resolution fails, or the dynamic `import("../lib/development-overlay.ts")` inside `projectLedgerOntoMap` throwing) surfaces as a raw Node unhandled-rejection stack trace and a non-standard exit path, bypassing the harness's own clean-failure reporting (`writeReport` + the `[FAIL]` stage log) entirely | GAP: no test drives a genuine throw from either dynamic `import()` call site to confirm the harness degrades to its own report-then-exit-1 path rather than an uncaught-rejection crash — this is distinct from the already-noted `.241` guard, which only covers the temp-workspace-vanished case (an explicit `existsSync` check on the ledger file), not an arbitrary async throw elsewhere in `main` | unit, error-branch, process-exit-hygiene | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.X2] Stage 4 (`generate-viewer-data (pre-loop)`) calls `generatedData(temp)` — an un-guarded `readJson` (via `JSON.parse(readFileSync(...))`) — immediately after the `sh(...)` child-process call, with NO try/catch around it, even though the preceding `record(...)` for that same stage hasn't executed yet: if `generate-viewer-data.ts` exits 0 but writes truncated/malformed JSON (e.g. killed mid-write by an OOM or an external process, or a bug that emits a partial file), `JSON.parse` throws synchronously and crashes the ENTIRE rehearsal script before stage 4's own `record()` call is reached — a data-corruption bug in the driven script is reported as a total harness crash with no PASS/FAIL row at all, rather than a named "generate-viewer-data (pre-loop)" FAIL | GAP: no test feeds a truncated/malformed architecture-data.json through this exact call site to confirm it fails as a clean stage-FAIL rather than an uncaught JSON.parse exception aborting the whole run | unit, error-branch, malformed-input | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.X3] `readMapFromHead` and `readJsonFromHead` (both used only in the stage 7b(a) closure assertion) collapse TWO distinct failure modes into the same `null` return: (a) `git show HEAD:<path>` exiting non-zero because the path was never committed, and (b) the git command succeeding but its stdout being unparsable JSON (a corrupted git object or an in-flight commit race) — both are swallowed by the same `catch { return null }`, so the closure record's detail string (`committedVerified` = 0 either way) cannot distinguish "map isn't tracked yet" from "map IS tracked but its committed content is unreadable," which are very different bugs to chase | GAP: no test isolates the JSON-parse-failure branch specifically (only the "not tracked at all" shape is implicitly exercised by a normal passing run) — a maintainer debugging a real closure-assertion failure gets no signal on which of the two branches fired | unit, error-branch, weak-diagnostic | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.X4] `writeReport`'s markdown-table row builder escapes pipe characters in `s.detail` (`.replace(/\|/g, "\\|")`) but does NOT escape pipes in `s.name`, and escapes neither field's embedded newlines — a stage detail containing a literal newline (e.g. the caught-error message string interpolated at the `dev-loop two-agent run` FAIL branch, `String((error as Error)?.message ?? error)`, for a real agent CLI error that embeds a multi-line stack excerpt) would inject an extra, unescaped table row boundary into `method-rehearsal-report.md`, corrupting the rendered Markdown table rather than merely showing an ugly-but-safe cell | GAP: no test exercises a stage detail containing `|` in the NAME (always a fixed literal today, so low risk) or containing embedded newlines in either field to confirm the report still renders as a well-formed table | unit, report-generation, malformed-input | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.X5] The rehearsal's fixed integer sleep in the async dry legs (`delay(15)` in both `dryImplementerParallel`/`dryReviewerParallel`) is the ONLY mechanism making concurrency "real rather than merely scheduled" per the file's own comment — on a sufficiently fast/idle CI runner, Node's microtask/timer scheduling could still resolve all N dry legs before any of them observably overlaps (i.e. the 15ms delay is a heuristic, not a guaranteed interleaving), so the `.237`/`.239` concurrency assertions (settle-once, dependency-order) could pass even in a build where the scheduler regressed to fully sequential execution disguised as parallel | GAP: no test asserts an actual wall-clock overlap (e.g. two issues' start/end timestamps genuinely intersecting) — only the OUTCOME (no duplicates, correct order) is checked, never that true concurrent execution occurred | e2e-process, concurrency, weak-assertion | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.X6] `sh()` always resolves `cwd` to `env?.VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT ?? factoryDir` — every driven factory script (`doc-baseline.ts`, `semantic-extraction-check.ts`, `traceability-check.ts`, `generate-viewer-data.ts`) is spawned via bare `spawnSync("node", args, {cwd, env, encoding: "utf8"})` with NO timeout option set, so a hang in any of those four child scripts (e.g. an infinite loop introduced by a future bug, or a process waiting on stdin it never receives since stdio isn't set to "inherit"/"ignore" and defaults to pipes) blocks `main()` forever with no rehearsal-level watchdog — the whole CI job would hang until the OUTER CI timeout kills it, producing an opaque hang rather than a clear "stage N timed out" failure | GAP: no test/comment addresses a hung child process; `spawnSync` has no `timeout:` option configured anywhere in this file | e2e-process, error-branch, hang-risk | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.X7] `REHEARSAL_DIR` resume mode (`fixedDir && existsSync(join(fixedDir, ".git"))`) counts `done/*.md` files to report a resume count, but never validates that the pinned dir's fixture CONTENT matches the currently-bundled `fixtureDir` (e.g. someone re-runs `REHEARSAL_DIR=/tmp/x node factory/dev-rehearsal.ts --fixture=formula` against a dir that was originally materialized from `pocket-ledger`, or the bundled fixture's canonical docs were edited since the pinned dir was first created) — the resumed run would silently proceed against a STALE/mismatched fixture rather than detecting the drift and failing loudly | GAP: no test pins a dir with one fixture's content then resumes with a DIFFERENT `--fixture=` value to confirm this mismatch is caught (currently nothing in the code even attempts to check it) | e2e-process, resume-after-kill, error-branch | GAP
- [e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.X8] `parseConcurrency` accepts `--concurrency=Infinity` or an extremely large finite value (e.g. `--concurrency=999999`) — `Number.isFinite(n) && n >= 1` passes for any huge finite number, and `Math.floor` is a no-op on integers already, so nothing caps concurrency against the fixture's actual issue count (13 for `formula`, 8 for `pocket-ledger`); the dev-loop presumably degrades this gracefully (fewer schedulable issues than requested slots), but this boundary (`concurrency > totalIssues`) is never exercised by any test in this area, unlike the well-covered fractional/negative/non-numeric malformed-input cases already noted in `.223`/`.224` | GAP: no test drives `--concurrency` set higher than the fixture's own issue count to confirm the scheduler simply saturates at `totalIssues` rather than erroring or hanging on unschedulable slots | unit, boundary=huge | GAP

## Area: extraction-gates

Area: extraction-gates. Scope: `factory/extract-issues.ts`, `factory/semantic-extraction-check.ts`, `factory/traceability-check.ts`, `factory/reference-check.ts`, `factory/excerpt-drift.ts`, `factory/spike-check.ts`, `factory/spike-prover.ts`, and the four prompt files (`extractor.md`, `extraction-verifier.md`, `spike-prover.md`, `spike-verifier.md`).

### factory/extract-issues.ts

- [extraction-gates.1] extractIssues throws when repoRoot is not configured (no options.repoRoot, no VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT) | throws "No target project configured..." | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.2] extractIssues honors options.maxRetries as an integer; maxAttempts = maxRetries+1 | correct attempt bound | unit | extract-issues.test.ts ("bounded retries / blocked")
- [extraction-gates.3] extractIssues defaults maxRetries to DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES(3) when options.maxRetries is not an integer (undefined, float, string) | 4 total attempts | unit | GAP (only explicit maxRetries values are exercised)
- [extraction-gates.4] extractIssues resolves the freeze version via `resolveFreezeVersion(repoRoot)` (1.0.0 on a virgin target, else a MINOR bump over the highest) rather than a hardcoded default; the freeze seam is called with that version (+ the open cycle's `approvalRef` when present) | freeze called with the resolved version | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("freezes via the injected freeze seam...")
- [extraction-gates.5] extractIssues merges options.cfg over DEFAULT_CONFIG (custom cfg fields reach spawnExtractor/spawnVerifier) | leg cfg carries custom fields | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.6] resolveAgentLegs enforces distinct CLIs for implementer(extractor) and reviewer(verifier) — R12 | extractor and verifier always run different CLI providers | integration | GAP (dev-loop.test.ts may cover resolveAgentLegs itself, but not wired through extractIssues)
- [extraction-gates.7] Happy path: extractor authors a valid corpus -> deterministic green -> verifier faithful:true -> map ok -> status green, attempts=1, no fix context on first call | status green; checkOutput null on attempt 1 | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("two-agent happy path")
- [extraction-gates.8] Reuses an existing frozen baseline (does not call runFreeze) when a valid frozen manifest is found and verifyFrozenManifest returns true — UNLESS a drafting spec cycle is open, in which case an unchanged canonical triggers the refuse-if-unchanged throw instead of a silent reuse | froze:false, freezeCalls=0 (no open cycle) | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("does NOT freeze when a frozen baseline already exists")
- [extraction-gates.9] Re-freezes when the found frozen manifest fails verifyFrozenManifest (spec edited after freeze) | froze:true, exactly one re-freeze | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("re-freezes a STALE frozen baseline")
- [extraction-gates.10] Mid-loop re-freeze: extractor edits canonical (Pass 1 contradiction fix) so the per-attempt verifyFrozenManifest check fails once after spawnExtractor | re-freeze + re-pin + re-author, consumes one attempt, then green | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("re-freezes when the EXTRACTOR edits canonical mid-loop")
- [extraction-gates.11] Map-review actionable finding triggers an extractor fix pass; a clean review on the next attempt reaches green | reviewCalls=2, calls.length=2, summary mentions "map review clean" | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("re-prompts the extractor when the per-lens map review finds an actionable issue")
- [extraction-gates.12] options.spawnAgent is accepted as a back-compat alias for options.spawnExtractor | extractor leg driven via spawnAgent | unit | extract-issues.test.ts ("accepts the legacy spawnAgent alias")
- [extraction-gates.13] Both spawnExtractor and spawnAgent provided simultaneously — precedence order (spawnExtractor wins per `options.spawnExtractor ?? options.spawnAgent`) | spawnExtractor takes priority | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.14] S2 spike mode resolves to "integrate" when readSpikes(repoRoot).length > 0 before the run, and provided spikes are passed through byte-for-byte | spike_mode "integrate"; spike files untouched; extractor leg told spikeMode="integrate"; status.json records it | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("INTEGRATE mode: pre-existing spikes pass through byte-for-byte")
- [extraction-gates.15] S2 spike mode resolves to "extract" when no spikes exist pre-run | spike_mode "extract" everywhere | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("EXTRACT mode: no spikes on disk")
- [extraction-gates.16] S5 map mode resolves to "reused" when architecture-map.yml pre-exists; a layout baseline snapshot is taken (mkdtemp) before the extractor runs | map_mode "reused"; extractor leg told mapMode="reused" | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("REUSED map mode")
- [extraction-gates.17] S5 map mode resolves to "authored" when no map pre-exists | map_mode "authored"; no layout baseline snapshot taken | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("AUTHORED map mode")
- [extraction-gates.18] Both spike_mode and map_mode are recorded on the extraction_blocked (red) path too, not only on green | blocked status carries spike_mode/map_mode | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("records both modes on the BLOCKED path too")
- [extraction-gates.19] S3 spike proving runs strictly BEFORE the S4 freeze (interleaving order) | order[0] === "prove"; prove index < freeze index | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("runs the S3 proving stage BEFORE the freeze")
- [extraction-gates.20] the spike-verification gate: extraction BLOCKS (status blocked_on_unverified_spikes) when any non-deferred spike is not transitively verified after S3 proving; extractor leg never runs (calls.length===0); offending gate_ids named in result and in the emitted status; summary matches gate ids | status blocked_on_unverified_spikes with unverified_spike_gate_ids populated | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("BLOCKS extraction when a non-deferred spike is not verified")
- [extraction-gates.21] the spike-verification gate proceeds to green when every required (non-deferred) spike is transitively verified; spike_proving summary counts ride on the result | status green; spike_proving {proved:1,failed:0,skipped:0} | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("PROCEEDS to green when every required spike is verified")
- [extraction-gates.22] A deferred spike (status "deferred") does NOT block extraction even though not verified | status green | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("a DEFERRED spike does NOT block extraction")
- [extraction-gates.23] the spike-verification gate's boundary: a spike with status "blocked" or "failed" (not just "pending") is also treated as an unverified required gate and blocks extraction | status blocked_on_unverified_spikes | unit | GAP (only "pending" is exercised as the non-verified state; "failed"/"blocked" statuses not directly tested at this gate)
- [extraction-gates.24] the spike-verification gate with an empty verifiedGates set but ZERO spikes on disk at all — extraction proceeds (no spikes means no unverified gates) | status not blocked_on_unverified_spikes purely from spike absence | unit | extract-issues.test.ts ("EXTRACT mode" implicitly covers no spikes -> not blocked), but not an explicit assertion — soft GAP
- [extraction-gates.25] The mechanical corpus commit runs REAL git (init/add/commit) on green: commits the corpus, regenerated map, extraction-status.json; excludes transcripts and decorative .md mirrors; leaves clean tree | result.committed=true; tracked files match expectation; porcelain empty | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("mechanical corpus commit on green (Item 2, real git)")
- [extraction-gates.26] commitCorpus is a no-op (still committed:true) when there is nothing new to commit ("nothing to commit" treated as success, not failure) | committed:true even on empty diff | unit | GAP (defaultCommitCorpus's "nothing to commit" branch not directly exercised by a dedicated test)
- [extraction-gates.27] commitCorpus reports committed:false and writes to stderr when `git add -A` fails | committed:false | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.28] commitCorpus reports committed:false when `git commit` fails for a reason other than "nothing to commit" | committed:false | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.29] Mechanical SPEC-SNAPSHOT commit runs BEFORE the freeze, snapshotting the owner's uncommitted canonical spec so the freeze sees a clean tree | tree is clean at the moment runFreeze is invoked; a "spec snapshot" commit exists | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("commits the owner's uncommitted spec so the freeze sees a CLEAN committed tree")
- [extraction-gates.30] Spec-snapshot commit is a no-op (no redundant empty commit) when the tree is already clean | commit count unchanged; no "spec snapshot" message in log | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("makes NO redundant empty commit when the repo is already clean")
- [extraction-gates.31] Spec-snapshot commit `git init`s the target when it is not yet a git repo (defensive path), then commits and the freeze succeeds | repo is now a git repo; HEAD resolves; clean at freeze | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("inits a repo when the target is NOT a git repo")
- [extraction-gates.32] Spec-snapshot commit sets a LOCAL git identity (user.email/user.name) only when none is configured (global/system isolated) — never clobbers an existing identity | commit succeeds; local identity == vivicy@local/Vivicy | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("auto-commits even when the fresh repo has NO git identity configured")
- [extraction-gates.33] Spec-snapshot commit does NOT overwrite an existing local/global git identity when one is already configured | pre-existing identity preserved | unit | GAP (only the "no identity" branch is tested; the "identity already set, do not clobber" branch is asserted only by omission/inference)
- [extraction-gates.34] defaultCommitSpecSnapshot returns committed:false and logs to stderr when ensureGitRepo fails (e.g. `git init` itself fails) | committed:false, no throw | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.35] defaultCommitSpecSnapshot returns committed:false when `git add -A` fails | committed:false | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.36] End-to-end: real spec snapshot + REAL doc-baseline freeze succeed on a from-scratch repo (no freeze stub) | status green; froze:true; frozen manifest exists; spec committed | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("end-to-end: real spec snapshot + REAL doc-baseline freeze succeed")
- [extraction-gates.37] Freezes via the injected freeze seam exactly once when no frozen baseline exists (freeze-if-needed branch); the version comes from `resolveFreezeVersion(repoRoot)` (1.0.0 on a virgin target) and an open cycle is closed (`clearSpecCycle` + `cycle_closed_by_freeze`) after the freeze | freezeCalls.length=1, froze:true, status green | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("freeze-if-needed branch")
- [extraction-gates.38] No status is emitted before the freeze runs (regression guard: freeze must be the first observable side effect, so extraction-status.json never dirties the tree pre-freeze) | order[0]==="freeze"; no "status:freezing" phase exists at all | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("freeze runs before ANY status emission")
- [extraction-gates.39] Deterministic fix loop: attempt 1 fails semantic/traceability check (pin mismatch) -> re-prompt with exact check output -> attempt 2 valid -> green; verifier short-circuited on the red attempt | attempts=2; calls[1].isFix=true; calls[1].checkOutput matches /pin mismatch/i; verifyCalls.length=1 | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("re-prompts with the exact check output when attempt 1 fails")
- [extraction-gates.40] Fidelity fix loop: verifier rejects attempt 1 (faithful:false with problems) even though deterministic checks pass; extractor (not verifier) is re-prompted with the verdict verbatim; attempt 2 faithful -> green; map gate runs on BOTH deterministic-green attempts (before fidelity is judged each time) | attempts=2; calls[1].checkOutput matches /faithful:false/, /invented_requirement/, /ISS-0003/; mapCalls.length=2 | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("verifier returns problems on attempt 1, extractor fixes")
- [extraction-gates.41] Fidelity stays false through all bounded retries -> extraction_blocked; blocked summary carries faithful:false and the specific problem kind | status extraction_blocked; attempts=maxRetries+1; summary matches /faithful:false/, /scope_drift/ | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("blocks when fidelity STAYS false through the bounded retries")
- [extraction-gates.42] A verifier that runs but writes NO verdict file at all is treated as not faithful (never green); readVerdict/defaultReadVerdict path exercised end-to-end via the orchestrator | status extraction_blocked; result.verdict.faithful=false | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("treats a MISSING verdict (verifier wrote nothing) as not faithful")
- [extraction-gates.43] defaultReadVerdict: verdict file present but unparseable JSON -> faithful:false with kind "unparseable_verdict" and the parse error message in detail | faithful:false, problems[0].kind="unparseable_verdict" | unit | GAP (exercised implicitly only via the "no verdict at all" integration case; the "present but invalid JSON" branch of defaultReadVerdict itself is untested directly)
- [extraction-gates.44] defaultReadVerdict: verdict.faithful is a truthy-but-not-exactly-true value (e.g. string "true", 1, missing) -> coerced to faithful:false (honest shape coercion) | faithful:false | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.45] defaultReadVerdict: verdict.problems is present but not an array -> problems coerced to [] | problems=[] | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.46] Map-generation GATE: a map-gen failure (exit 1, "Unsupported architecture-map.yml line...") on an otherwise deterministic-green + would-be-faithful corpus is NOT green; extractor (not verifier) is re-prompted with the EXACT generator error; verifier short-circuited on the map-fail attempt; next attempt's map parses -> green with no contradictory "map FAILED" wording in a green summary | attempts=2; calls[1].checkOutput matches the exact generator output; verifyCalls.length=1 (only on the map-clean attempt); summary does NOT match /map FAILED/i | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("map-gen failure is NOT green")
- [extraction-gates.47] GREEN requires deterministic + map + fidelity ALL clean — proven by withholding exactly one gate at a time (4 sub-cases: all clean->green; deterministic red->blocked; map red->blocked; fidelity red->blocked) | matrix of outcomes as specified | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("GREEN requires deterministic + map + fidelity all clean")
- [extraction-gates.48] Map stays unparseable through all bounded retries -> extraction_blocked; verifier NEVER runs (short-circuited every attempt); blocked summary carries the exact map error | status extraction_blocked; verifyCalls.length=0; summary matches /architecture-map generation/ and the exact error text | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("blocks when the map STAYS unparseable through the bounded retries")
- [extraction-gates.49] Deterministic-red bounded retries: extractor always writes an invalid corpus (pin mismatch) -> extraction_blocked after maxRetries+1 attempts; verifier and map generator NEVER run; blocked status event emitted | status extraction_blocked; verifyCalls.length=0; mapCalls.length=0 | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("returns extraction_blocked after maxRetries+1 red attempts")
- [extraction-gates.50] An UNCHANGED placeholder issue index (agent authored nothing usable) is treated as a failed attempt, not green, even though semantic-extraction-check itself would exit 0 with placeholder:true — extractIssues's own `!deterministicGreen` check (`!semantic.placeholder`) forces a retry | status extraction_blocked; verifyCalls.length=0; mapCalls.length=0 | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("treats an UNCHANGED placeholder index as a failed attempt")
- [extraction-gates.51] A TIMED-OUT extractor leg (leg-timeout.ts kill) is retried like any other failing attempt (never hangs waiting), and the final extraction_blocked names the timeout reason verbatim | result.timeoutReason set; summary matches /A leg was killed: .../ | integration | extract-issues.test.ts ("a TIMED-OUT extractor leg is retried")
- [extraction-gates.52] A TIMED-OUT verifier leg is likewise treated as a non-faithful attempt (not asserted directly for the verifier leg, only the extractor leg's timeout path is tested) | verifier-side timeout should also surface a timeoutReason and re-prompt loop | integration | GAP (only extractor-leg timeout is covered; a verifier-leg timeout/leg death is not asserted)
- [extraction-gates.53] legTimeoutReason: returns null for a leg result with no `.result.timedOut` (normal completion) | null | unit | GAP (implicitly covered by every happy-path test never setting result.timedOut, but no direct unit assertion)
- [extraction-gates.54] findFrozenManifest: returns the active frozen manifest and ignores a superseded one (status frozen + superseded marker) | picks non-superseded manifest | unit | extract-issues.test.ts ("findFrozenManifest — returns the active frozen manifest")
- [extraction-gates.55] findFrozenManifest: returns null when only a draft (non-"frozen" status) baseline exists | null | unit | extract-issues.test.ts ("returns null when only a draft baseline exists")
- [extraction-gates.56] findFrozenManifest: returns null (or skips) when a baseline JSON file is unparseable / missing baseline_id / baseline_id is empty string | manifest skipped, not thrown | unit | GAP (the try/catch-continue and the empty-baseline_id-string branch inside findFrozenManifest are not directly tested)
- [extraction-gates.57] findFrozenManifest: returns null when the baselines directory itself does not exist | null | unit | GAP (only the "empty via draft" and "active vs superseded" cases are tested; bare missing-dir case not asserted directly, though implied by other fixtures with no .vivicy/baselines)
- [extraction-gates.58] formatCheckOutput: flattens semantic + traceability errors/warnings into one readable text block, including the placeholder note line | text contains "semantic-extraction-check:", error/warning lines, "traceability-check:" | unit | extract-issues.test.ts ("formatCheckOutput — flattens semantic + traceability errors")
- [extraction-gates.59] formatCheckOutput: is honest about a missing check object (`null`) — returns "(no check output)" | exact string | unit | extract-issues.test.ts ("is honest about a missing check object")
- [extraction-gates.60] formatCheckOutput: includes spike/reference/change-control blocks ONLY when they failed (exitCode !== 0); a passing remaining gate contributes nothing | passing remaining gates omitted from output | unit | GAP (only semantic+traceability failure formatting is directly tested; the "remaining" loop over spike/reference/changeControl is untested)
- [extraction-gates.61] formatVerdict: null verdict -> null; faithful:true -> "fidelity-verifier: faithful:true"; faithful:false with problems -> each problem rendered with issue/kind/detail | exact strings per case | unit | GAP (formatVerdict has no dedicated describe block; only reached indirectly via formatFixContext tests)
- [extraction-gates.62] formatVerdict: faithful:false with an EMPTY problems array -> a synthesized "verifier reported faithful:false but listed no specific problems" line | that exact fallback text | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.63] formatMapError: returns null for a clean (code 0) map generation, and for a null map | null in both cases | unit | extract-issues.test.ts ("formatMapError — returns null for a clean (code 0) map generation")
- [extraction-gates.64] formatMapError: flattens a failed map generation with the exact generator output, each output line indented | text matches expected format | unit | extract-issues.test.ts ("flattens a failed map generation with its exact generator output")
- [extraction-gates.65] formatMapError: a failed map with EMPTY/whitespace-only output falls back to "(no generator output captured)" | fallback text present | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.66] formatFixContext: includes the deterministic check block only when something actually failed (never re-feeds a passing deterministic block as if it were the problem) | text has the failing block only | unit | extract-issues.test.ts ("includes the deterministic check block when a deterministic check failed")
- [extraction-gates.67] formatFixContext: includes the fidelity verdict block on a fidelity-only failure without re-feeding the passing deterministic block | verdict block present, no passing det. block | unit | extract-issues.test.ts ("includes the fidelity verdict block ... on a fidelity-only failure")
- [extraction-gates.68] formatFixContext: honest empty case — no checks, no map problem, no verdict problem (including faithful:true, and code:0 map) -> "(no check, map, or verdict output)" | exact fallback string in 3 sub-cases | unit | extract-issues.test.ts ("is honest when there is neither check, map, nor verdict output")
- [extraction-gates.69] formatFixContext: includes the map-generation error block on a map-only failure, without re-feeding the passing deterministic block | map block present alone | unit | extract-issues.test.ts ("includes the map-generation error ... on a map-only failure")
- [extraction-gates.70] formatFixContext: all three blocks present simultaneously (deterministic failed + map failed + fidelity failed) are concatenated together, in order | combined text with all three | unit | GAP (only single-failure and all-clean combinations are tested; the simultaneous-triple-failure concatenation and ordering is untested)
- [extraction-gates.71] countIssues: returns the correct count from a real issue-index.json, and 0 on a missing/unparseable file (never throws) | count or 0 | unit | GAP (no direct unit test; only reached indirectly via the "8 issue(s)" summary assertion in the happy path)
- [extraction-gates.72] CLI entry: `node extract-issues.ts` with no VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT prints the "no target project configured" error and exits 2 | exit 2, stderr message | e2e-process | GAP
- [extraction-gates.73] CLI entry: `node extract-issues.ts` with VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT set runs extractIssues and exits 0 on green (prints result.summary to stdout) | exit 0, summary printed | e2e-process | GAP
- [extraction-gates.74] CLI entry: exits 1 on any non-green terminal status (extraction_blocked / blocked_on_unverified_spikes) | exit 1 | e2e-process | GAP
- [extraction-gates.75] CLI entry: an exception thrown inside extractIssues (e.g. freeze subprocess throws) is caught, printed as "error: ..." to stderr, and exits 1 | exit 1, stderr message | e2e-process | GAP
- [extraction-gates.76] defaultRunFreeze: throws with the tool's stdout+stderr when doc-baseline generate exits non-zero | throws Error with combined output | unit | GAP (only exercised end-to-end on the success path; the failure-to-freeze branch is not directly tested)
- [extraction-gates.77] defaultRunFreeze: throws when doc-baseline reports success (exit 0) but the expected manifest file is missing on disk | throws "freeze reported success but ... is missing" | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.78] defaultVerifyFrozenManifest: shells out to doc-baseline verify with --require-status frozen/--require-baseline-id/--require-min-version and returns true only on exit 0 | boolean matches subprocess exit | unit | GAP (only exercised indirectly through injected fakes; the real defaultVerifyFrozenManifest subprocess path is not isolated in a unit test)
- [extraction-gates.79] defaultRunGenerateMap: passes `--reconcile-against <layoutBaselinePath>` only when reconcileAgainst is provided (map_mode reused), and omits the flag when authoring from scratch | args array conditionally includes the flag | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.80] defaultEmitStatus: writes extraction-status.json with an updated_at timestamp, creates the reports dir if missing, and calls pruneGitkeeps | file written with correct shape | unit | GAP (status writes are observed only via the injected fake emitStatus in tests; the real defaultEmitStatus is untested directly)
- [extraction-gates.81] defaultEmitStatus: dispatches the correct NOTIFY_BY_PHASE entry to notify() for each of the six mapped phases (spike_proving/authoring/fixing/blocked_on_unverified_spikes/extraction_blocked/green), and is a no-op for an unmapped phase (e.g. "validating", "mapping", "verifying", "refreezing", "map-review") | notify called only for mapped phases | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.82] ensureGitRepo: returns true without side effects when repoRoot is already a git repo; runs `git init` and returns its success/failure when not | boolean | unit | GAP (covered only indirectly through the spec-snapshot integration tests)
- [extraction-gates.83] ensureLocalGitIdentity: sets user.email/user.name only for whichever of the two is empty (independently), never touching one that is already set | selective identity fill | unit | GAP (only the "both absent" case is exercised end-to-end)
- [extraction-gates.84] Boundary: maxRetries=0 means exactly ONE attempt total (maxAttempts=1) before extraction_blocked/green | attempts never exceeds 1 | unit | extract-issues.test.ts ("records both modes on the BLOCKED path too" uses maxRetries:0) — partial coverage, not a dedicated boundary test
- [extraction-gates.85] Boundary: a huge maxRetries value (e.g. Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER) does not itself cause an infinite loop given a scripted finite sequence of attempts (the loop is bounded by the fakes' behavior, not by maxRetries alone in a real failure) | terminates normally once green/blocked scripted outcome is reached | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.86] Reopen integration: on a re-extraction over a CHANGED baseline (a prior source-map existed), the orchestrator mechanically reopens exactly the issues whose requirement excerpts changed/were removed via runReopen, and the count rides on result.reopened + the green summary | result.reopened populated; summary mentions "reopened N impacted issue(s)" | integration | GAP (extract-issues.test.ts has no describe block exercising priorSourceMap/runReopen; reopen.test.ts likely covers runReopen itself but not this orchestrator wiring)
- [extraction-gates.87] A first extraction (no prior source-map) reopens nothing (`reopened` key omitted from the result) | no `reopened` key in ExtractionResult | integration | extract-issues.test.ts happy path implicitly (no reopened key asserted, but not an explicit "must be absent" check) — soft GAP

- [extraction-gates.282] `resolveFreezeVersion` on a virgin target (no `.vivicy/baselines` dir) returns `DEFAULT_FREEZE_VERSION` | "1.0.0" | unit | extract-issues.test.ts ("returns 1.0.0 on a virgin target (no baselines dir)")
- [extraction-gates.283] `resolveFreezeVersion` MINOR-bumps the highest baseline version ever cut regardless of status — a superseded 1.0.0 + a draft 1.2.0 → 1.3.0 (ids never overwritten) | "1.3.0" | unit | extract-issues.test.ts ("minor-bumps the HIGHEST baseline version ever cut, whatever the statuses (superseded/draft included)")
- [extraction-gates.284] `resolveFreezeVersion` skips malformed manifests (unparseable JSON, non-semver `version`); all-malformed falls back to 1.0.0; one well-formed 1.1.0 drives 1.2.0 | correct skip + fallback | unit | extract-issues.test.ts ("skips malformed manifest files (unparseable JSON, non-semver versions)")
- [extraction-gates.285] `readManifestSpecKind` reads the frozen manifest's `spec_kind` and `extractIssues` injects it into `extractorContext` — the extractor leg's ctx carries `specKind` and the prompt block names the kind | ctx.specKind threaded | integration | GAP (test ExtractorCtx has no specKind field; no assertion on calls[0].specKind)
- [extraction-gates.286] `readManifestSpecKind` falls back to a live `detectSpecKind(repoRoot)` when the frozen manifest lacks `spec_kind` or fails to parse — the same shared derivation, so the two agree | agreeing kind | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.287] `extractorContext` renders the FEATURE guidance (`**feature**`, "EVOLUTION", scope-to-changes, respect the existing codebase) when `specKind==="feature"`, the PROJECT guidance ("whole product from scratch") when "project", and NO spec-kind block when undefined | correct guidance branch | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.288] `extractIssues` reads the open drafting cycle (`readSpecCycle`) and threads its id as the freeze `approvalRef` (→ doc-baseline `--approval-ref`) | frozen manifest's `approval.approval_ref === cycle.id` | integration | dev-rehearsal.ts ("feature-cycle: freeze is a MINOR bump, approval_ref = cycle id, prior baseline superseded"); GAP at the extract-issues.test.ts unit level
- [extraction-gates.289] refuse-if-unchanged: an open cycle but the existing frozen baseline still verifies (nothing drafted) → throws "drafting cycle … is open but the canonical has not changed" | throws refuse-if-unchanged | integration | GAP
- [extraction-gates.290] freeze CLOSES the cycle: when `froze && openCycle`, `clearSpecCycle` + a `cycle_closed_by_freeze` info notification | state file deleted, notification emitted | integration | dev-rehearsal.ts ("feature-cycle: freeze CLOSED the cycle mechanically (state file gone)"); GAP at the unit level
- [extraction-gates.291] NEVER a reuse-close: a still-valid reused baseline with an open cycle does NOT clear the cycle (closure is gated on `froze`) — instead it takes the refuse-if-unchanged throw | cycle stays open on the reuse path | integration | GAP
- [extraction-gates.292] refreeze path closes the cycle: mid-loop `verifyFrozenManifest` fail → re-freeze with `resolveFreezeVersion` + `approvalRef=openCycle.id`; if still open afterward, `clearSpecCycle` + `cycle_closed_by_freeze` | cycle cleared on the refreeze branch too | integration | GAP
- [extraction-gates.293] `defaultRunFreeze` passes `approvalRef` through to `--approval-ref`, falling back to `vivicy-extract-<ISO>` when absent | correct arg on cycle and non-cycle freezes | unit | GAP

### factory/semantic-extraction-check.ts

- [extraction-gates.88] Placeholder mode: issues=[] and status=pending_llm_semantic_issue_generation exits 0, writes NO reports, summary "nothing to check yet" | exitCode 0, reportsWritten false | unit | semantic-extraction-check.test.ts ("fixture placeholder mode exits 0 and writes no reports")
- [extraction-gates.89] Placeholder mode against REAL repo-shaped artifacts (self-contained temp target) behaves identically | exitCode 0, placeholder true | unit | semantic-extraction-check.test.ts ("real repo artifacts: placeholder index passes without writing reports")
- [extraction-gates.90] --strict escalates placeholder mode into a hard failure | exitCode 1, error mentions "has not produced issues yet" | unit | semantic-extraction-check.test.ts ("--strict turns placeholder mode into a failure")
- [extraction-gates.91] CLI: exits 0 against the real placeholder artifacts, prints "nothing to check yet" | exit 0 | e2e-process | semantic-extraction-check.test.ts ("CLI exits 0 against the real placeholder artifacts")
- [extraction-gates.92] CLI: rejects unknown arguments with exit 2 and "Unknown argument" on stderr | exit 2 | e2e-process | semantic-extraction-check.test.ts ("CLI rejects unknown arguments")
- [extraction-gates.93] CLI: with no VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT configured, exits 2 with "no target project configured" | exit 2 | e2e-process | GAP (only the --bogus-arg CLI rejection and the happy VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT case are tested; the "no target configured" CLI branch is untested)
- [extraction-gates.94] Issue index fails schema validation (missing required field, wrong type) -> fail() with zod issue messages, no reports written | exitCode 1, reportsWritten false | unit | GAP (schema validation failure path for the index itself is not directly tested; only pin-mismatch and coverage failures are tested downstream of a valid schema)
- [extraction-gates.95] Issue index file is missing or unreadable -> caught error message "Unable to read issue index..." | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.96] Manifest file at index.manifest_path is missing or unreadable -> caught error "Unable to read baseline manifest..." | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.97] Manifest fails schema validation -> zod issue messages surfaced, fail() | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.98] Manifest status is not "frozen" -> pin mismatch error "expected frozen" | exitCode 1, error contains "expected \"frozen\"" | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.99] Any of the four pin fields (baseline_id, baseline_version vs manifest.version, manifest_hash, document_set_hash) mismatches the manifest -> fatal pin-mismatch error, fails even in placeholder mode | exitCode 1 | unit | semantic-extraction-check.test.ts ("index pin mismatch against the manifest fails even in placeholder mode") — covers manifest_hash; other 3 pin fields are GAP
- [extraction-gates.100] No manifest files[] match index.source_corpus glob -> "no manifest files[] match source_corpus" error | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.101] globToRegExp: `**/` matches nested path segments, bare `**` matches anything, `*` matches within a segment only | glob matcher behaves correctly for each pattern shape | unit | GAP (no direct unit test of globToRegExp; only exercised implicitly via the fixed source_corpus glob in fixtures)
- [extraction-gates.102] Exclusions file missing entirely -> fatal "exclusions file missing: create..." | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.103] Exclusions file fails schema validation (bad reason_class enum, missing note, non-integer start/end) -> zod messages, fail() | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP (schema-level exclusions validation untested; only semantic range/grammar failures on well-formed exclusion objects are tested)
- [extraction-gates.104] Exclusion references a file outside the canonical doc grammar pattern -> per-item error, does not crash the rest of the loop | exitCode 1, other exclusions still processed | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.105] Exclusion references a file not in the pinned manifest corpus -> per-item error | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.106] Exclusion has an inverted range (start > end) -> per-item error "inverted range" | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.107] Exclusion range is out of the document's line count -> per-item error "out of range" | exitCode 1 | unit | semantic-extraction-check.test.ts ("exclusion ref out of the doc's line range fails")
- [extraction-gates.108] Two overlapping exclusions on the same line: first exclusion wins (its reason_class is recorded), not the second | first-seen reason_class kept | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.109] Issue index has issues.length===0 but status !== placeholderStatus -> fatal "issue index has no issues but status is..." | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.110] Issue index has issues.length>0 but status is still placeholderStatus -> fatal "issue index carries N issue(s) but status is still..." | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.111] Duplicate issue id within index.issues -> "duplicate issue id in index" error | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.112] An issue's issue_path fails the issuePathPattern grammar -> per-issue error, loop continues to next issue | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.113] An issue's markdown file referenced by issue_path is missing on disk -> "issue file is missing" error | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.114] A source_line_ref fails requirementRefPattern grammar entirely (not just wrong directory) -> "does not match the source ref grammar" | exitCode 1 | unit | semantic-extraction-check.test.ts ("requirement ref outside the canonical grammar fails")
- [extraction-gates.115] A source_line_ref points at a file outside the pinned corpus (even if grammar-valid) -> "points to a file outside the pinned manifest corpus" | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP (only the fully-outside-grammar case and the beyond-line-count case are tested; the grammar-valid-but-not-in-corpus branch is untested)
- [extraction-gates.116] A source_line_ref has start > end (empty/inverted range) -> "empty or inverted range" | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.117] A source_line_ref end exceeds the file's line count -> "out of range" | exitCode 1 | unit | semantic-extraction-check.test.ts ("requirement ref beyond the file's line count fails")
- [extraction-gates.118] depends_on referencing an unknown issue id -> "depends_on references unknown issue id" | exitCode 1 | unit | semantic-extraction-check.test.ts ("depends_on referencing an unknown issue id fails")
- [extraction-gates.119] A depends_on cycle across 2+ issues -> "depends_on cycle detected" with the cycle path | exitCode 1 | unit | semantic-extraction-check.test.ts ("a depends_on cycle fails the gate")
- [extraction-gates.120] Boundary: a depends_on SELF-cycle (issue depends on itself) is detected as a length-1 cycle | exitCode 1, cycle path includes the id twice | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.121] Full coverage (every non-auto line covered or excluded) -> exitCode 0, all three reports written with correct totals | totals match exactly | unit | semantic-extraction-check.test.ts ("valid refs with full coverage pass and write the three reports")
- [extraction-gates.122] An uncovered canonical line (neither covered nor excluded, and not auto) fails the gate and is listed by exact range in coverage-report.json | exitCode 1, uncovered_ranges=["10"] | unit | semantic-extraction-check.test.ts ("an uncovered canonical line fails the gate")
- [extraction-gates.123] An exclusion covering exactly the previously-uncovered line turns the gate green | exitCode 0 | unit | semantic-extraction-check.test.ts ("an exclusion covering the same line turns the gate green")
- [extraction-gates.124] status llm_extraction_in_progress tolerates uncovered lines as WARNINGS (not errors), still writes full reports; --strict re-escalates them to fatal even mid-extraction | lenient exitCode 0 with warning; strict exitCode 1 | unit | semantic-extraction-check.test.ts ("llm_extraction_in_progress tolerates uncovered lines; --strict escalates")
- [extraction-gates.125] A line both covered AND excluded: covered wins, a warning is emitted (not an error); --strict escalates that specific warning to a failure | lenient exitCode 0 with "covered wins" warning; strict exitCode 1 with "escalated to failure" | unit | semantic-extraction-check.test.ts ("a line both covered and excluded warns, covered wins")
- [extraction-gates.126] Traceability block cross-check: any list field in the issue file's Traceability block that disagrees (as a set) with the index entry's corresponding field fails with "does not match index ..." | exitCode 1 | unit | semantic-extraction-check.test.ts ("traceability block disagreeing with the index entry fails") — covers source_line_refs; the other 5 list fields (depends_on/graph_refs/requirement_ids/spike_gates/verification_gate_ids) + the issue_id scalar mismatch are GAP
- [extraction-gates.127] Traceability block issue_id scalar disagrees with index id -> "traceability issue_id ... does not match index id" | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.128] parseTraceabilityBlock: missing "## Traceability" heading entirely -> "missing '## Traceability' section" | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP (traceability-block-missing is tested for spike-check.ts but not for semantic-extraction-check.ts's issue-file variant)
- [extraction-gates.129] parseTraceabilityBlock: a duplicate key inside the block -> "duplicate traceability key" | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.130] parseTraceabilityBlock: a scalar key given an empty value -> "must have a value" | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.131] parseTraceabilityBlock: a list key given an inline value instead of "- " items -> "must be a list of \"- \" items" | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.132] parseTraceabilityBlock: an unknown key inside the block -> "unknown traceability key" | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.133] parseTraceabilityBlock: an unparseable line (neither key: nor "- " item nor blank/fence) -> "unparseable traceability line" | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.134] parseTraceabilityBlock: a required key (any of the 6 scalar/list keys) entirely absent from the block -> "traceability block is missing ..." | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.135] parseTraceabilityBlock: source_line_refs present but an empty list -> "must list at least one ref" | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP (schema-level `.min(1)` on the index entry may catch this upstream, but the traceability-block-specific message is a separate check, untested directly)
- [extraction-gates.136] parseTraceabilityBlock: requirement_ids present but an empty list -> "must list at least one id" | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.137] C1: source-map.json's requirement_excerpts carries the TOOL-computed sha256 hash of the exact cited canonical lines (never authored) for a resolvable catalog requirement | hash matches createHash("sha256") over the exact lines | unit | semantic-extraction-check.test.ts ("C1: source-map carries the tool-computed per-requirement excerpt hash")
- [extraction-gates.138] computeRequirementExcerpts: a requirement whose sourceRefs are malformed or out-of-range is SKIPPED (not included in requirement_excerpts, and does not fail the gate) — that judgment belongs to traceability-check | requirement omitted from excerpts, gate still passes | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.139] computeRequirementExcerpts: catalog.json missing entirely -> requirement_excerpts is an empty array, no crash | empty array | unit | GAP (implicitly true whenever catalog.json is absent in other fixtures, but not directly asserted)
- [extraction-gates.140] computeRequirementExcerpts: catalog.json present but unparseable JSON -> empty array, no crash | empty array, no throw | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.141] computeRequirementExcerpts: a multi-range ref (e.g. `file.md:5-6`) concatenates the exact lines joined by "\n" before hashing | hash matches multi-line join | unit | GAP (only a single-line ref is exercised in the C1 test)
- [extraction-gates.142] coverage_summary is only rewritten into issue-index.json when at least one of its four numeric fields actually changed vs. the current value (avoids needless writes on an unchanged rerun) | no write when summary unchanged | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.143] Reports fail to write (e.g. permission error / invalid path) -> caught, "unable to write reports:" error, fail() | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.144] computeAutoExclusions: correctly classifies blank lines, code-fence delimiter lines (with a language tag), horizontal-rule lines (---, ***, ___), and exactly the FIRST H1 line (a second `# ` line is NOT auto-excluded) | auto set matches expectation | unit | semantic-extraction-check.test.ts (indirectly, via the SAMPLE_DOC totals auto_lines:7) — the "second H1 not auto-excluded" boundary is GAP
- [extraction-gates.145] compactClassifiedRanges / compactLineRanges: correctly merge contiguous same-classification (and same reason_class) lines into ranges, and do NOT merge across a classification or reason_class change | ranges match expected boundaries | unit | GAP (no direct unit test of these pure helpers; only observed indirectly through fixture-level totals)
- [extraction-gates.146] resolveRepoPath: throws "Path must be repository-relative" for an absolute path, and "Path must stay inside repository" for a path escaping repoRoot via `..` | throws in both cases | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.147] Boundary: a doc with 0 lines (empty file) — total_lines=0, no crash, no false uncovered | totals all zero | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.148] Boundary: a doc with exactly 1 line (only the H1, auto-excluded) — fully auto, zero coverage needed | totals: auto=1, everything else 0 | unit | GAP

### factory/traceability-check.ts

- [extraction-gates.149] Placeholder: issues.length===0 -> exitCode 0, placeholder true, "nothing to check yet" | exitCode 0 | unit | traceability-check.test.ts ("placeholder index: nothing to check yet")
- [extraction-gates.150] All MVP requirements covered and every ref resolves -> exitCode 0, no errors | exitCode 0 | unit | traceability-check.test.ts ("passes when MVP requirements are covered and all refs resolve")
- [extraction-gates.151] An issue references an unknown requirement_id -> "issue_requirement_resolves" error | exitCode 1 | unit | traceability-check.test.ts ("fails when an issue references an unknown requirement")
- [extraction-gates.152] An MVP must_implement requirement with NO covering issue (and no coveredByIssues) -> "requirement_covered" error | exitCode 1 | unit | traceability-check.test.ts ("fails when an MVP requirement is covered by no issue")
- [extraction-gates.153] An MVP requirement is covered ONLY via coveredByIssues (not referenced by any issue's requirement_ids) -> passes (the OR condition) | exitCode 0 | unit | GAP (the test suite always sets both; the "coveredByIssues alone, no issue references it" branch is untested)
- [extraction-gates.154] A non-MVP maturity or a non-MVP-disposition requirement with no covering issue does NOT fail (isMvp gate) | exitCode 0 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.155] Once issues exist, a missing catalog.json -> "catalog_required" error | exitCode 1 | unit | traceability-check.test.ts ("requires the catalog once issues exist")
- [extraction-gates.156] Once issues exist, a missing traceability-matrix.json (catalog present) -> "matrix_required" error | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP (only the catalog-missing branch is tested; the matrix-missing branch with a catalog present is untested)
- [extraction-gates.157] req.coveredByIssues references an issue id that does not exist in the index -> "covered_by_resolves" error | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.158] An issue's spike_gate resolving to a PENDING (not yet verified) spike passes — this gate is referential-only, not status-aware | exitCode 0 | unit | traceability-check.test.ts ("an issue spike_gate resolving to a pending spike passes")
