- [extraction-gates.159] An issue's spike_gate with no matching spike file on disk -> "issue_spike_resolves" error | exitCode 1 | unit | traceability-check.test.ts ("an issue spike_gate with no spike file fails")
- [extraction-gates.160] A malformed gate_id (spike file's slug != filename stem) makes readSpikes skip it entirely, so the referencing issue's spike_gate fails to resolve | exitCode 1, "issue_spike_resolves" | unit | traceability-check.test.ts ("a malformed gate_id (slug != filename) does not resolve")
- [extraction-gates.161] A spike still carrying the template placeholder requirement_ids ("pending-extraction...") -> "spike_requirement_backfilled" error | exitCode 1 | unit | traceability-check.test.ts ("a spike still carrying the placeholder requirement_ids fails")
- [extraction-gates.162] A spike's back-filled requirement_id is not in the catalog -> "spike_requirement_resolves" error | exitCode 1 | unit | traceability-check.test.ts ("a spike requirement_id that is not in the catalog fails")
- [extraction-gates.163] A spike whose requirement_ids lists MULTIPLE ids (space/comma separated) -> all are validated against the catalog individually | exitCode 1 if any one is missing | unit | GAP (only single-id spikes are exercised)
- [extraction-gates.164] A must_verify_with_spike requirement genuinely gated by a spike referencing it -> passes | exitCode 0 | unit | traceability-check.test.ts ("a must_verify_with_spike requirement gated by a spike passes")
- [extraction-gates.165] A must_verify_with_spike requirement with NO spike referencing it anywhere -> "spike_requirement_gated" error | exitCode 1 | unit | traceability-check.test.ts ("a must_verify_with_spike requirement with no spike to gate it fails")
- [extraction-gates.166] Multiple simultaneous failures across different rules all accumulate into errors[] in one run (not short-circuited after the first) | errors.length > 1, one run | unit | GAP (each test isolates a single failure type; no test asserts several different rule violations are all reported together in one run)
- [extraction-gates.167] resolveInside: throws on an absolute path or a path escaping root via `..` | throws | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.168] CLI entry: with no VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT, exits 2 with "no target project configured" | exit 2 | e2e-process | GAP
- [extraction-gates.169] CLI entry: runs runTraceabilityCheck() against VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT, prints errors to stderr (each multi-line Rule/Scope/Evidence/Expected/Required-fix block prefixed "error:\n"), prints summary to stdout, exits with result.exitCode | exit matches exitCode | e2e-process | GAP

### factory/reference-check.ts

- [extraction-gates.170] No entry docs at all (no AGENTS.md/README.md/CLAUDE.md, no .vivicy/canonical dir) -> exitCode 0, placeholder true | exitCode 0 | unit | reference-check.test.ts ("no entry docs -> nothing to check")
- [extraction-gates.171] All local markdown links across README/AGENTS/canonical resolve -> exitCode 0 | exitCode 0 | unit | reference-check.test.ts ("resolving links pass")
- [extraction-gates.172] A broken local .md link in an entry doc -> "reference_resolves" error | exitCode 1 | unit | reference-check.test.ts ("a broken local markdown link fails")
- [extraction-gates.173] External links (https:), pure #anchors, and non-.md targets (e.g. .png, .js) are all ignored | exitCode 0, linkCount excludes them | unit | reference-check.test.ts ("external links, anchors, and non-md targets are ignored")
- [extraction-gates.174] A canonical doc (not just README/AGENTS/CLAUDE) with a broken cross-link also fails | exitCode 1 | unit | reference-check.test.ts ("a canonical doc with a broken cross-link fails")
- [extraction-gates.175] A link escaping the project root (e.g. `../secrets.md`) -> "reference_inside_repo" error (never resolved to check existence) | exitCode 1 | unit | reference-check.test.ts ("a link escaping the project root fails")
- [extraction-gates.176] A broken link with a CommonMark title attribute (`"Spec"`) is still correctly parsed and caught | exitCode 1 | unit | reference-check.test.ts ("a broken link with a CommonMark title attribute is caught")
- [extraction-gates.177] A valid link WITH a title attribute passes (title correctly stripped, not mistaken for part of the path) | exitCode 0 | unit | reference-check.test.ts ("a valid link with a title attribute passes")
- [extraction-gates.178] A broken angle-bracket-wrapped link target (`<...>`) is unwrapped and still caught | exitCode 1 | unit | reference-check.test.ts ("a broken angle-bracket link is caught")
- [extraction-gates.179] A link resolving to a DIRECTORY (not a file) fails distinctly (still "reference_resolves") | exitCode 1 | unit | reference-check.test.ts ("a link resolving to a directory ... fails")
- [extraction-gates.180] A link inside a fenced code block is NOT resolved (illustrative, not real) | exitCode 0 | unit | reference-check.test.ts ("a link inside a fenced code block is not resolved")
- [extraction-gates.181] A percent-encoded link target (`%20`) resolves to its decoded path on disk | exitCode 0 | unit | reference-check.test.ts ("a percent-encoded link resolves to its decoded path")
- [extraction-gates.182] A malformed percent-escape in a link target is left as-is rather than throwing (decodeURIComponent catch branch) | no throw; treated as literal text | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.183] A link target that is a bare `#anchor` only (empty non-anchor part) is correctly excluded via isLocalMarkdown's `if (!target) return false` | not counted, not checked | unit | GAP (only the "anchor" case combined with other links is tested; a pure lone-anchor link is not isolated)
- [extraction-gates.184] A scheme link that is NOT http(s)/mailto (e.g. `ftp:`, `tel:`) is also correctly excluded by the generic scheme regex | not checked | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.185] Canonical dir entries are read in sorted order and only `.md` files (case-insensitive) are included as entry docs; non-.md files in .vivicy/canonical are ignored | only .md canonical files scanned | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.186] Only AGENTS.md/README.md/CLAUDE.md that actually exist AND are files (not directories) are treated as entry docs | directory named "README.md" is skipped, no crash | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.187] CLI entry: no VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT -> exit 2, "no target project configured" | exit 2 | e2e-process | GAP
- [extraction-gates.188] CLI entry: runs and exits with result.exitCode, printing errors to stderr and summary to stdout | exit matches exitCode | e2e-process | GAP

### factory/excerpt-drift.ts

- [extraction-gates.189] excerptMap: builds id->hash correctly from a well-formed requirement_excerpts array | map matches input | unit | excerpt-drift.test.ts ("excerptMap builds id -> hash from requirement_excerpts")
- [extraction-gates.190] excerptMap: tolerates a missing/empty requirement_excerpts array, and a null/undefined sourceMap entirely (empty map, no throw) | size 0 | unit | excerpt-drift.test.ts (same test, "tolerates a missing array")
- [extraction-gates.191] excerptMap: an entry with a non-string `id` is skipped (not added to the map) | skipped entry excluded | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.192] compareExcerpts: correctly classifies unchanged / changed / added / removed across prior vs new source-maps, each list sorted | four sorted arrays match expectation | unit | excerpt-drift.test.ts ("compareExcerpts classifies unchanged / changed / added / removed")
- [extraction-gates.193] compareExcerpts: an id present in both maps with source_excerpt_sha256 null in both is treated as unchanged (null === null) | classified unchanged | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.194] compareExcerpts: both prior and new source-maps are empty -> all four lists empty | {unchanged:[],changed:[],added:[],removed:[]} | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.195] formatExcerptDrift: renders "amended (excerpt changed)", "new", "removed" lines plus the unchanged count, only for non-empty categories | matches expected multi-line format | unit | excerpt-drift.test.ts ("formatExcerptDrift maps changed->amended...")
- [extraction-gates.196] formatExcerptDrift: reports "no requirement excerpts changed between the two baselines" plus unchanged count when nothing changed at all | matches expected text | unit | excerpt-drift.test.ts ("formatExcerptDrift reports cleanly when nothing changed")
- [extraction-gates.197] runExcerptDrift: both prior and new source-map files exist and parse -> returns the drift object and formatted report | drift populated | unit | GAP (no test file exercises runExcerptDrift's impure entry point at all — it is untested end-to-end)
- [extraction-gates.198] runExcerptDrift: prior source-map file is missing -> drift:null, report names which side is missing ("prior=false, new=true") | drift null, explanatory report | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.199] runExcerptDrift: new source-map file is missing -> drift:null, report names it | drift null | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.200] runExcerptDrift: a source-map file exists but is unparseable JSON -> treated the same as missing (caught, drift:null) | drift null, no throw | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.201] runExcerptDrift: repoRoot defaults to "." when not provided | resolves relative to cwd | unit | GAP

### factory/spike-check.ts

- [extraction-gates.202] No spikes directory at all -> exitCode 0, placeholder true, "nothing to check yet (no spikes directory)" | exitCode 0 | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("no spikes directory -> nothing to check")
- [extraction-gates.203] Spikes directory exists but is empty -> exitCode 0, placeholder true | exitCode 0 | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("empty spikes directory -> nothing to check")
- [extraction-gates.204] README.md and SPIKE-TEMPLATE.md inside the spikes dir are never treated as spikes (case-insensitive match against NON_SPIKE_FILES) | exitCode 0, placeholder true | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("the README and the template are not treated as spikes")
- [extraction-gates.205] A well-formed `pending` spike passes cleanly | exitCode 0 | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("a well-formed pending spike passes")
- [extraction-gates.206] A `verified` spike with full six-field evidence passes | exitCode 0 | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("a verified spike with full evidence passes")
- [extraction-gates.207] An out-of-enum status value -> "spike_status_enum" error | exitCode 1 | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("an out-of-enum status fails")
- [extraction-gates.208] gate_id fails the GATE_ID_PATTERN grammar entirely -> "spike_gate_id_grammar" error | exitCode 1 | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("a malformed gate id fails the grammar rule")
- [extraction-gates.209] gate_id matches the grammar but its slug disagrees with the filename stem -> "spike_gate_id_matches_file" error | exitCode 1 | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("a gate-id slug that disagrees with the filename fails")
- [extraction-gates.210] Any of the three required sections (Question / Must Verify / Evidence Required) missing -> "spike_section_required" error, one per missing section | exitCode 1 | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("a missing section fails") — only Question tested directly; Must Verify / Evidence Required omission is GAP by symmetry (same code path, low risk but untested)
- [extraction-gates.211] A `verified` spike missing one or more of the six completion evidence field labels -> "spike_completion_fields" error naming exactly the missing fields | exitCode 1, missing fields named | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("a VERIFIED spike missing a completion field fails")
- [extraction-gates.212] A `pending` spike with incomplete/placeholder evidence PASSES (completion enforced only at verified) | exitCode 0 | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("a PENDING spike with incomplete evidence passes")
- [extraction-gates.213] Two spike files describing the SAME dependency (stale rename left beside the original, e.g. `01-x.md` + `s01-x.md`) -> "spike_duplicate" error | exitCode 1 | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("a duplicate spike for the same dependency ... fails")
- [extraction-gates.214] Genuinely distinct spikes (different descriptive slugs) never false-positive the duplicate guard | exitCode 0 | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("the same-dependency guard does not false-positive")
- [extraction-gates.215] Boundary: a bare-number stem (e.g. "01.md", descriptive part empty after stripping the numeric prefix) is skipped by the duplicate-key guard (no crash, no false collision) | no spike_duplicate error from bare-number stems | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.216] readSpikes: indexes only well-formed spikes; a gate-id/filename mismatch causes the spike to be silently excluded from the index (not merely a check failure) | readSpikes result excludes it | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("readSpikes indexes only well-formed spikes")
- [extraction-gates.217] readSpikeGateStatuses: thin gate_id->status index built from readSpikes; a gate never present in readSpikes has() false | map matches expectation | unit | spike-check.test.ts (same test, asserts statuses.get/.has)
- [extraction-gates.218] A completely missing "## Traceability" heading -> "spike_traceability_block" error, and NO further per-field validation attempted (parseSpikeTraceability returns null) | exitCode 1 | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("a missing Traceability block fails")
- [extraction-gates.219] A Traceability block missing one of the three required scalar fields (requirement_ids/gate_id/status) -> "spike_traceability_field" error for exactly that field | exitCode 1 | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("a missing Traceability field (no status) fails") — only "status" tested; requirement_ids/gate_id omission is GAP by symmetry
- [extraction-gates.220] E2: gated_by referencing an unknown spike's gate_id -> "spike_gated_by_resolves" error | exitCode 1 | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("E2: gated_by referencing an unknown spike fails")
- [extraction-gates.221] E2: a valid gated_by chain across two well-formed spikes passes cleanly | exitCode 0 | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("E2: a valid gated_by chain passes")
- [extraction-gates.222] E2: `blocks` without the mirrored `gated_by` on the target -> "spike_blocks_consistency" error | exitCode 1 | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("E2: blocks without the mirrored gated_by fails")
- [extraction-gates.223] E2: `blocks` correctly mirrored by the target's `gated_by` -> passes | exitCode 0 | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("E2: blocks mirrored by the target's gated_by passes")
- [extraction-gates.224] E2: `blocks` referencing an unknown spike's gate_id -> "spike_blocks_resolves" error | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP (only the "resolves" rule for gated_by is directly tested; the symmetric blocks_resolves rule for an unresolvable blocks target is untested — the "blocks_consistency" test uses a resolvable-but-unmirrored target, a different rule)
- [extraction-gates.225] E2: a gated_by cycle across 2+ spikes -> "spike_gating_acyclic" error | exitCode 1 | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("E2: a gated_by cycle fails")
- [extraction-gates.226] E2: a `verified` spike whose gated_by chain is NOT fully verified -> "spike_verified_chain" error naming the unmet gate(s) | exitCode 1 | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("E2: a verified spike whose gated_by chain is NOT verified fails")
- [extraction-gates.227] E2: a `verified` spike with its WHOLE gated_by chain verified -> passes | exitCode 0 | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("E2: a verified spike with its whole gated_by chain verified passes")
- [extraction-gates.228] transitivelyVerifiedGates: counts a verified spike ONLY when every gate in its transitive gated_by chain is also verified; excludes a verified spike whose chain has a pending member, even transitively (multi-hop) | exact set matches expectation across a 4-node chain | unit | spike-check.test.ts ("E2: transitivelyVerifiedGates counts a verified spike only when its whole chain is verified")
- [extraction-gates.229] parseGateList: handles bracket-wrapped `[...]` syntax, comma/space separated lists, quoted entries, and the literal empty-list token `[]` | parsed array matches | unit | GAP (no direct unit test of parseGateList; only exercised indirectly via plain space-form gated_by/blocks strings in fixtures)
- [extraction-gates.230] findGateCycle (DFS three-colour): correctly finds a cycle in a graph with a non-cyclic branch alongside the cyclic one (does not stop at the first non-cyclic root and miss the cycle elsewhere) | cycle detected regardless of visit order | unit | GAP (only a simple 2-node mutual cycle is tested; a larger graph with a red herring branch is untested)
- [extraction-gates.231] Boundary: a spike self-referencing its own gate_id in `gated_by` (self-cycle) is caught by spike_gating_acyclic | exitCode 1 | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.232] CLI entry: no VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT -> exit 2, "no target project configured" | exit 2 | e2e-process | GAP
- [extraction-gates.233] CLI entry: runs runSpikeCheck() and exits with result.exitCode | exit matches exitCode | e2e-process | GAP

### factory/spike-prover.ts

- [extraction-gates.234] runSpikeProving throws when repoRoot is not provided | throws "No target project configured..." | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.235] Agree+verified: PROVER writes verified + full evidence, VERIFIER agrees -> flips the spike's status to "verified" IN the file (byte-preserving, no folder move); no CR drafted; the flipped spike passes runSpikeCheck for real | proved=[{gate_id,verdict:"verified"}]; readStatus verified; changeRequests=[] | integration | spike-prover.test.ts ("flips the spike's traceability status to verified IN the file")
- [extraction-gates.236] Ledger events: spike_proof_started then spike_proof_completed are emitted through recordEvent with the ledger-vocabulary underscored roles (spike_prover/spike_verifier), both declared in progressRoles/progressEventTypes | events=[started,completed] with correct role/type/gate_id/verdict | integration | spike-prover.test.ts ("emits spike_proof_started/completed through the injected recordEvent sink")
- [extraction-gates.237] recordEvent may be null (the extraction path passes null) without throwing | no throw, result still correct | unit | spike-prover.test.ts ("does not throw when recordEvent is null")
- [extraction-gates.238] Agree+failed: PROVER disproves the hypothesis, VERIFIER agrees it failed -> flips status to "failed", drafts a CR whose frontmatter (status:idea, source:agent, classification:major_product_change, affected_verification_gates:[gate_id]) passes the REAL change-control gate; CR body cites both the proof and verdict report paths | status failed; CR passes runChangeControlCheck; CR text cites both report paths | integration | spike-prover.test.ts ("flips the spike to failed and writes a CR whose frontmatter passes change-control")
- [extraction-gates.239] Two independently-failed spikes in the same run draft two CRs numbered sequentially (CR-0001, CR-0002), both passing change-control (gap-free numbering) | ids sorted = [CR-0001, CR-0002] | integration | spike-prover.test.ts ("numbers a second drafted CR sequentially")
- [extraction-gates.240] Disagreement: PROVER says verified, VERIFIER disagrees on attempt 1 -> retries ONCE with the disagreement fed back verbatim (matches /agree=false/) -> still disagrees on attempt 2 -> persistent disagreement flips status to "failed" and drafts a CR explaining the unresolved disagreement; the pair ran exactly twice, never more | proofCalls.length=2; proofCalls[1].disagreement matches /agree=false/; status failed; CR text matches /did not agree/i | integration | spike-prover.test.ts ("retries the pair once feeding the disagreement back")
- [extraction-gates.241] Disagreement RESOLVED on the retry (verifier disagrees attempt 1, agrees attempt 2) -> flips verified, drafts NO CR | proofCalls.length=2; status verified; changeRequests=[] | integration | spike-prover.test.ts ("a disagreement RESOLVED on the retry flips verified with no CR")
- [extraction-gates.242] Topological ordering: a spike gated_by another spike is proved AFTER its gate within the same run (live view of statuses updates as the run progresses, not only across separate invocations) | proof order = [gate, dependent]; both end verified; the verified chain passes runSpikeCheck | integration | spike-prover.test.ts ("proves a gate BEFORE the spike it gates")
- [extraction-gates.243] A spike whose gate FAILS this run is SKIPPED (never spawns its legs), with the skip reason naming the failed gate; the skipped spike keeps its pending status unchanged | proofCalls excludes the dependent; skipped=[dependent] with reason naming the gate; dependent's status stays "pending" | integration | spike-prover.test.ts ("skips a spike whose gate FAILED this run")
- [extraction-gates.244] A spike gated by an ALREADY-failed spike on disk (from a prior run, not this run) is also skipped, no legs spawned at all | proofCalls.length=0; skipped=[dependent] | integration | spike-prover.test.ts ("skips a spike gated by an already-failed spike ON DISK")
- [extraction-gates.245] An already-`verified` spike is left alone — only `pending` spikes are proof candidates; no legs spawned for it | proofCalls.length=0; proved=[]; failed=[]; status stays verified | integration | spike-prover.test.ts ("leaves an already-verified spike alone")
- [extraction-gates.246] A TIMED-OUT prover leg (writes no report, carries timedOut:true) collapses to an honest "no_report" -> failed proof + CR drafted, never a silent green | proved=[]; failed=[gate_id]; status failed; one CR passing change-control | integration | spike-prover.test.ts ("a TIMED-OUT prover leg ... is an honest failed proof + CR")
- [extraction-gates.247] A prover that returns a clean exit but writes literally NO verdict JSON is not trusted (no_report -> failed), even though it filled the evidence and exited 0 | proved=[]; status failed; one CR | integration | spike-prover.test.ts ("a prover that says verified but writes NO report is not trusted")
- [extraction-gates.248] Nothing to do: zero spikes on disk -> empty result object, zero legs spawned for either role | result deep-equals {proved:[],failed:[],skipped:[],changeRequests:[]} | unit | spike-prover.test.ts ("returns empty results and spawns no leg when there are no spikes")
- [extraction-gates.249] flipSpikeStatus: changes ONLY the `status:` line, every other line byte-identical (line count unchanged) | before/after diff isolated to the status line | unit | spike-prover.test.ts ("changes ONLY the status: line and leaves every other line byte-identical")
- [extraction-gates.250] flipSpikeStatus: preserves CRLF line endings on a Windows-authored spike file (never rewrites CRLF->LF) | output retains \r\n throughout, status line ends \r\n | unit | spike-prover.test.ts ("preserves CRLF line endings on a Windows-authored spike")
- [extraction-gates.251] flipSpikeStatus: throws (never silently no-ops) when the Traceability block has no `status:` line to flip | throws /no "status:" line/ | unit | spike-prover.test.ts ("throws (never silently no-ops) when there is no status line to flip")
- [extraction-gates.252] flipSpikeStatus: throws when the file has no "## Traceability" heading at all | throws "has no ... Traceability ... block" | unit | GAP (only the "heading present but no status: line" branch is tested; the "no Traceability heading at all" branch of flipSpikeStatus itself is untested — spike-check.ts's own equivalent rule is tested, but not this function's own throw)
- [extraction-gates.253] readProofReport: a proof.json with an off-enum verdict value (neither "verified" nor "failed") is treated as "no_report", not trusted | verdict "no_report" | unit | GAP (only "missing entirely" and "clean exit but not written" are exercised; an explicitly-off-enum verdict value written to the file is untested)
- [extraction-gates.254] readVerifierReport: an agree value that is truthy-but-not-exactly-true (e.g. "true" string, 1) is coerced to agree:false | agree false | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.255] readVerifierReport: problems present but not an array -> coerced to [] | problems=[] | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.256] disagreementFeedback: correctly stringifies non-string problem entries (objects) via JSON.stringify when building the feedback text | feedback text includes JSON-stringified problem | unit | GAP (only string problems are exercised; an object-shaped problem entry is untested)
- [extraction-gates.257] legFailureReason: a leg with a non-zero (but not timed-out) exit status -> "leg exited non-zero (status N)" distinct message | message reflects status code | unit | GAP (only the timedOut:true branch is exercised; a plain non-zero-exit-without-timeout leg is untested)
- [extraction-gates.258] Boundary: a spike graph with 3+ levels of gated_by chaining (A gated_by B gated_by C) proves in the correct full topological order across the whole chain in one run | proof order = [C, B, A] | unit | GAP (only a 2-level chain is directly tested for ordering; transitivelyVerifiedGates itself is tested at spike-check.ts level with 2 levels, not proving order at 3+ levels)
- [extraction-gates.259] writeChangeRequest / defaultWriteChangeRequest: the CR title differs correctly between "disproven" (kind!=="disagreement") and "unresolved" (kind==="disagreement") phrasing | title matches kind | unit | GAP (only reachable indirectly via the two integration tests; no isolated assertion of the title text branching itself)
- [extraction-gates.260] renderChangeRequest: formatRequirementIds falls back to "(recorded in the spike's Traceability block)" when spike.requirement_ids is falsy, and joins an array with ", " when it is an array | body text matches each branch | unit | GAP (readSpikes always yields a string requirement_ids in practice; the array-shaped branch of formatRequirementIds is dead-in-practice but untested either way)
- [extraction-gates.261] ensureReportsDir: creates the reports directory (recursive) when missing, and is a no-op when it already exists | dir exists after call in both cases | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.262] spikeStem: correctly derives the stem from a nested path (strips directory and .md extension, case-insensitively) | stem matches expectation | unit | GAP (only exercised implicitly via proofRel/verdictRel helper functions in the test file, not the exported behavior in isolation with edge-case input like a path with no directory or an uppercase .MD extension)
- [extraction-gates.263] clearFile: force-removing a non-existent proof/verdict file before each attempt never throws (idempotent) | no throw | unit | GAP (implicitly exercised every attempt but no dedicated assertion)

### factory/prompts/*.md (extractor.md, extraction-verifier.md, spike-prover.md, spike-verifier.md)

- [extraction-gates.264] extractor.md's documented architecture-map.yml schema (top-level keys, status_legend's exact six keys, layout_role enum, node/edge record shapes) matches EXACTLY what generate-viewer-data.ts's parseArchitectureMap actually accepts, so a corpus following the prompt literally always generates cleanly | a corpus authored to the letter of the prompt's schema section passes real map generation | integration | GAP (this is a prompt-vs-implementation consistency check; no test cross-validates the prompt's documented grammar against the actual parser beyond the fixture corpus incidentally exercising a subset)
- [extraction-gates.265] extraction-verifier.md's problem `kind` vocabulary (invented_requirement, dropped_obligation, scope_drift, bad_source_ref, requirement_id_mismatch, graph_ref_mismatch, map_mismatch, cross_document_contradiction, spike_evidence_gap) is treated as free text by defaultReadVerdict/formatVerdict — any string kind is accepted and rendered, so a verifier emitting an off-list kind does not break the orchestrator | any kind string renders correctly in formatVerdict | unit | GAP (formatVerdict's kind-agnostic rendering is implicitly proven by the "invented_requirement"/"scope_drift" fixtures, but no test uses an out-of-vocabulary kind to prove the orchestrator does not validate against the prompt's suggested enum)
- [extraction-gates.266] spike-prover.md's documented verdict JSON shape `{verdict, reason}` matches exactly what readProofReport parses (verdict must be exactly "verified"|"failed", nothing else accepted) | consistent with extraction-gates.253 | unit | GAP (documentation/implementation consistency, not independently tested)
- [extraction-gates.267] spike-verifier.md's documented agree JSON shape `{agree, problems}` matches exactly what readVerifierReport parses (agree must be exactly boolean true to count) | consistent with extraction-gates.254 | unit | GAP (documentation/implementation consistency, not independently tested)

### Area cross-notes

- **Fix-context wiring is the seam most exercised end-to-end**: extract-issues.test.ts proves formatCheckOutput/formatVerdict/formatMapError/formatFixContext both as pure units AND wired live through the fix loop (attempt N+1's `checkOutput` literally equals the formatted text of attempt N's failure). This is strong coverage; the only remaining gap (extraction-gates.70) is the simultaneous-triple-failure concatenation, which cannot happen in the real gate ORDER (deterministic gates short-circuit map-gen, which short-circuits fidelity) but IS reachable directly via the exported `formatFixContext` pure function and is untested there.
- **The five deterministic gates (semantic, traceability, spike, reference, change-control) are exercised as REAL implementations inside extract-issues.test.ts**, not mocked — so a regression in any gate's exit-code contract (e.g. semantic-extraction-check returning exitCode 0 with placeholder:true, which extract-issues additionally treats as non-green via `!semantic.placeholder`) would be caught by the orchestrator tests even without a matching unit test in the gate's own file. This means extraction-gates.50 is well covered at the integration seam even though semantic-extraction-check.ts's OWN test file has no test asserting that exact `!semantic.placeholder` orchestrator-side behavior (it only tests the checker's own exitCode:0/placeholder:true contract in isolation, correctly).
- **spike-check.ts and spike-prover.ts share the transitive-gated_by graph logic** (`transitiveGatedBy`, topological concerns) as two SEPARATE local implementations (spike-check.ts's own `transitiveGatedBy` + spike-prover.ts's own `transitiveGatedBy`) — a DRY violation the test suite does not flag, and a future edit to one chain-walk without the other risks the two modules disagreeing on what "transitively verified/provable" means. No test cross-validates that spike-check's `transitivelyVerifiedGates` and spike-prover's chain-gate check agree on the same corpus in every case (only that each independently behaves correctly on its own fixtures).
- **extract-issues.ts's spike-verification gate reads `transitivelyVerifiedGates` from spike-check.ts directly** (not from spike-prover's own per-run live view) — so a spike proved earlier IN THE SAME runSpikeProving call is correctly seen (spike-prover updates the file, transitivelyVerifiedGates re-reads from disk), but this cross-module hand-off (spike-prover flips file -> extract-issues re-reads via a fresh transitivelyVerifiedGates(repoRoot) call after proving completes) has no dedicated integration test proving the spike-verification check sees a spike VERIFIED DURING THIS RUN's proving stage (extraction-gates.21's "PROCEEDS to green" test seeds an already-verified spike rather than one flipped verified by the (stubbed) proving stage in the same call) — soft GAP.
- **excerpt-drift.ts's impure entry point `runExcerptDrift` is completely untested** (extraction-gates.197-201) despite being the one function in the file that touches the filesystem and is presumably wired into the Change-Control re-extraction flow — all four existing tests in excerpt-drift.test.ts exercise only the pure helpers (`excerptMap`, `compareExcerpts`, `formatExcerptDrift`). Given extract-issues.ts's own reopen path (extraction-gates.86) is also a GAP, this suggests the whole "Change-Control re-extraction reopens changed-excerpt issues" life-cycle is exercised nowhere in this area's test files, only presumably in a separate change-control.test.ts / reopen.test.ts outside this area's scope.
- **The four prompt files are pure LLM instructions** with no executable test surface of their own; the only meaningful "tests" for them are consistency checks against the deterministic parsers/checkers they promise to satisfy (generate-viewer-data.ts's YAML grammar, the verdict JSON shapes read by extract-issues.ts/spike-prover.ts). None of these consistency checks exist as automated tests today — they are currently guaranteed only by the fixture corpus (rehearsal/pocket-ledger) incidentally satisfying a subset of the schema, which is a much weaker guarantee than a schema-conformance test that walks the prompt's documented shape against the parser's accepted grammar systematically (e.g. fuzzing every documented enum value, every optional/required field).
- **CLI entry points across ALL SIX executable files** (extract-issues.ts, semantic-extraction-check.ts, traceability-check.ts, reference-check.ts, spike-check.ts) are almost entirely untested except semantic-extraction-check.ts (which has 2 CLI tests). The "no VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT configured -> exit 2" branch and the "exit code mirrors result.exitCode" branch are repeated, nearly-identical dead test debt across 5 files (extraction-gates.72-75, 93, 168-169, 187-188, 232-233) — a single shared e2e-process test helper parametrized over the 5 CLI entry files would close all of these gaps with minimal duplication.

### Adversarial additions

- [extraction-gates.268] `runExcerptDrift` (excerpt-drift.ts) has ZERO callers anywhere in the shipped factory code, not merely zero tests: `reopen.ts` imports `compareExcerpts`/`ExcerptDrift`/`SourceMap` directly from excerpt-drift.ts and never calls `runExcerptDrift`; `extract-issues.ts` reads both source-maps itself (`readJsonOrNull`) and calls `runReopen({ repoRoot, priorSourceMap, currentSourceMap })`, bypassing `runExcerptDrift` entirely | this sharpens extraction-gates.197-201 from "untested" to "unreachable in production" — either a dead export that should be deleted, or a wiring bug where the Change-Control flow was meant to go through this function's file-reading/error-reporting (missing/unparseable-file handling) but doesn't | unit/integration | GAP (confirmed via `grep -rn "runExcerptDrift" factory/*.ts` — only its own definition matches)
- [extraction-gates.269] extract-issues.test.ts has NO test at all touching `priorSourceMap`/`reopened`/reopen wiring (confirmed: zero matches for those identifiers in the whole test file) — extraction-gates.86/.87 are not soft/partial gaps, they are a completely blank describe block; the green path's `reopened` key, its conditional inclusion (`...(reopened.length ? { reopened } : {})`), and the "read currentSourceMap fresh after re-authoring, not the pre-run snapshot" ordering are all unexercised | a first extraction and a re-extraction over a changed baseline both need dedicated integration tests | integration | GAP
- [extraction-gates.270] `mapReview`'s legs' timeout/leg-death handling is completely untested: extract-issues.ts pushes `lensLeg.transcriptRel` for every entry in `review.legs` but never calls `legTimeoutReason` on a lens leg (unlike the extractor/verifier legs) — so a per-lens map-review leg that times out is silently swallowed as "no actionable findings" rather than surfacing a timeoutReason, and no test proves or disproves this asymmetry | a timed-out map-review lens leg produces no error, no retry-forcing signal, and no timeoutReason in the final result even though the extractor/verifier legs get this treatment | integration | GAP
- [extraction-gates.271] `extractorContext`'s two prompt-string branches (spikeMode "integrate" vs "extract", mapMode "reused" vs "authored") are exercised only through end-to-end fixtures that assert on OTHER observable effects (spike_mode/map_mode in the status); no test asserts the actual prompt text handed to the extractor differs correctly per branch (e.g. that "integrate" mode's context contains "NEVER rewrite/renumber/recreate" and "extract" mode's does not) | wrong branch text silently reaches the agent with no test catching it | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.272] `verifierContext`'s exact prompt text (the VERDICT_REL path and JSON shape instructions embedded in the string) has no dedicated unit test — a typo or path drift here (e.g. verifierContext citing the wrong VERDICT_REL) would only be caught indirectly if it happened to break an integration fixture, not directly | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.273] `legDepsForTarget`'s `cwdFilter: null` and `transcriptDirAbs` construction (`abs(\`${legCfg.transcriptsDir}/${issue.id}\`)`) is untested when `legCfg.transcriptsDir` is undefined/missing from cfg (would produce a path literally containing "undefined") | template-literal path corruption on a misconfigured cfg | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.274] `runGit` helper: no test exercises its behavior when `git` itself is not on PATH (`spawnSync` returns `status: null`, `stdout`/`stderr` undefined) — `ensureGitRepo`/`ensureLocalGitIdentity`/`defaultCommitCorpus`/`defaultCommitSpecSnapshot` all funnel through it and coerce `status ?? 1`, but the all-undefined-fields shape (no `error` property check) is unexercised | a missing git binary should fail closed (committed:false) not throw | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.275] Boundary: `options.maxRetries` set to a negative integer (e.g. -1) — `Number.isInteger(-1)` is true, so `maxAttempts = maxRetries + 1 = 0`, meaning the `for` loop body never executes at all and the function falls through directly to the terminal `extraction_blocked` with `attempts: 0` and `lastChecks`/`lastMap`/`lastVerdict` all still null (so `formatFixContext(null, null, null)` -> the honest empty-case string) — an unguarded negative input silently produces a "blocked after 0 attempts" result rather than validating the input | attempts:0, summary says "still not green after 0 attempt(s)" with the empty-context fallback text, extractor NEVER spawned once | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.276] Boundary: `options.maxRetries` set to `NaN` explicitly (not just `undefined`) — `Number.isInteger(NaN)` is false, so it correctly falls back to `DEFAULT_MAX_RETRIES`; only the implicit-undefined default path is asserted anywhere (per extraction-gates.3's own note), the explicit-NaN input is a distinct code path (the ternary condition itself) that is untested | falls back to 3 retries (4 attempts) | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.277] `resolveAgentLegs(process.env)` is called with the REAL `process.env` inside `extractIssues` (not injectable) — every integration test in extract-issues.test.ts therefore runs against whatever CLI env vars happen to be set in the test process; no test asserts what happens when the environment specifies the SAME CLI for both implementer and reviewer roles (the R12 invariant violation) reaching all the way into extractIssues — only resolveAgentLegs's own unit tests (presumably in dev-loop.test.ts) may cover the violation-detection itself, but not what extractIssues does when resolveAgentLegs throws (does the throw propagate uncaught, crashing the whole extraction with a raw stack trace instead of a structured extraction_blocked?) | uncaught throw crashes extractIssues with no structured result | integration | GAP
- [extraction-gates.278] `countIssues`'s own JSON.parse failure path silently returns 0 via its catch-all, which also swallows a genuinely corrupt (not just missing) issue-index.json on the GREEN path itself — the green summary would then read "extraction green after N attempt(s): 0 issue(s)" even though deterministic checks (which independently re-parse and validate the same file via runSemanticCheck) already proved the file well-formed with issues.length > 0; this specific "green but summary says 0 issues due to a redundant independent parse" scenario (e.g. a race where something truncates the file between the check and the summary render) is unexercised | cosmetic-but-misleading summary text on green | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.279] semantic-extraction-check.ts / traceability-check.ts / reference-check.ts / spike-check.ts: none of their CLI entries is tested for the case where `VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT` is set but points at a path that does not exist on disk at all (as distinct from "not configured") — `resolveTargetRoot()` presumably just returns the string unchanged since it only reads the env var, so every downstream `existsSync`/`readFileSync` call would need to independently degrade gracefully; no test drives any of the four checkers against a nonexistent repoRoot end-to-end | likely an uncaught ENOENT crash rather than a clean placeholder/error result | e2e-process | GAP
- [extraction-gates.280] excerpt-drift.ts's `compareExcerpts`: an id with `source_excerpt_sha256` explicitly `undefined` in the raw JSON (as opposed to `null`) is coerced by `excerptMap`'s `?? null` to `null` before comparison — untested that `undefined` and an explicit `null` in the SAME source-map (one requirement missing the field, another with it explicitly null) are both correctly treated as the same "no hash" value and compared as unchanged against each other | classified unchanged | unit | GAP
- [extraction-gates.281] `formatExcerptDrift`: no test exercises `unchanged.length === 0` combined with SOME other category populated (e.g. everything changed, nothing unchanged) — the fixed trailing `unchanged: 0` line's exact rendering in that combination (as opposed to the "nothing changed at all" fully-empty case, which IS tested) is unverified | "unchanged: 0" line still appears correctly alongside populated changed/added/removed lines | unit | GAP

## Area: map-ui-data-viewer

### components/map/architecture-map.tsx

- [map-ui-data-viewer.1] ArchitectureMap wraps ArchitectureMapInner in a ReactFlowProvider so useReactFlow/useViewport hooks resolve | provider present, no throw | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.2] Default render (editMode=false) produces read-only pan/select: nodes draggable=false, nodesDraggable=false, snapToGrid=false | nodes cannot be dragged | e2e-ui | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.3] edgeCounts/statesByRef/issuesByRef/activeRefs are memoized off data.edges/data.development — changing an unrelated prop (e.g. query) does not recompute them | referential stability preserved | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.4] visibleNodeIds: laneFilter="all" includes every lane; laneFilter=<specific> keeps only matching-lane nodes | correct node set | unit | GAP (component-level; lib-level covered by map-data.test.ts computeVisibleCounts)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.5] visibleNodeIds: statusFilter="all" bypasses status filtering entirely | all nodes pass regardless of status | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.6] visibleNodeIds: statusFilter=<specific> keeps nodes whose effectiveStatus matches OR whose id is an endpoint of a status-matching edge (statusMatchedEndpoints) | correct inclusion of edge-endpoint nodes even when their own status differs | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.7] visibleNodeIds: scopeFilter="all" vs scopeFilter=<specific> | correct scope-based inclusion/exclusion | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.8] visibleNodeIds: query filtering hides (not dims) non-matching nodes — nodeMatchesQuery("") matches all | node not in query match is entirely absent from flowNodes | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.9] visibleNodeIds: combination of all four filters (lane AND status AND scope AND query) — a node must pass every filter to be visible | correct intersection semantics | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.10] flowEdges: an edge is rendered only when BOTH endpoints are in visibleNodeIds — pruned edges never appear even if the edge itself would pass statusFilter | dangling-safe edge list | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.11] flowEdges: statusFilter="all" keeps every edge (subject to endpoint visibility); statusFilter=<specific> additionally requires statusMatchedEdgeIds.has(id) | correct edge subset | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.12] flowNodes color: view="target" uses kindColor(node.kind); view="progress" uses progressStatusColor(effectiveStatus(node)) | correct color token selection per view | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.13] effectiveStatus: prefers statesByRef.get(nodeGraphRef).status over node.status; falls back to node.status; falls back to "not_started" when neither present | correct 3-tier fallback | unit | GAP (mirrors resolveNodeStatus tested in lib but component wiring is untested)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.14] nodeGraphRef: uses node.graph_ref when present, else synthesizes `node:${id}` | correct ref key used for overlay/issue lookups | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.15] Node click calls onSelect({type:"node", item}) with the node resolved from data.nodes (not the possibly-stale React Flow node object), and stopPropagation prevents pane click from also firing clearSelection | correct selection payload; single selection event | e2e-ui | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.16] Node click for a node id no longer present in data.nodes (race with background refresh) — found is undefined, onSelect is not called | no crash, selection silently no-ops | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.17] Edge click calls onSelect({type:"edge", id, item}) resolved via edgeIndexFromId parsing the trailing "-<i>" suffix | correct edge selection payload | e2e-ui | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.18] Edge click for an out-of-range/unparseable id (edgeIndexFromId returns -1, data.edges[-1] is undefined) — onSelect not called | no crash | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.19] Pane click clears selection (onSelect(null)) UNLESS the click target matches SELECTABLE_TARGET (.react-flow__node, .react-flow__edge, .map-edge-label) | correct guard against clearing selection when clicking a node/edge/label | e2e-ui | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.20] Selection survives a background data refresh: selected is resolved by id against the current data.nodes/data.edges each render, not cached by object identity | stale selection object no longer breaks highlighting | integration | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.21] Edit-mode toggle button: click flips editMode; label/title/aria-pressed reflect state (t("editToolbar.editLabel") vs editingLabel) | toggle works, ARIA correct | e2e-ui | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.22] Turning editMode ON makes nodes draggable (draggable:true per node, nodesDraggable=true, snapToGrid=true with SNAP_GRID=[20,20]) | drag now possible | e2e-ui | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.23] Turning editMode OFF while layoutDirty is true still shows the Save button (showSave = editMode && (layoutDirty || saving || error)) is FALSE once editMode is false — Save button disappears even with unsaved dirty state | Save UI hidden outside edit mode | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.24] Dragging a node and dropping (onNodeDragStop) snaps the position to the grid via snapXY, updates the RF node position, and only calls markNodePositionDirty when the snapped position differs from the last recorded nodePositions entry (dedup) | no spurious dirty flag on no-op drag | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.25] commitNodePosition: dragging a node back to its exact original position (via markNodePositionDirty comparing to initialNodePositions) removes it from dirtyNodeIdsRef and can flip layoutDirty back to false if it was the only dirty item | dirty-tracking undo on revert | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.26] Dragging a cluster handle (ClusterBackdrops pointerdown) moves every member node by the same snapped delta (clusterMovedPositions), previews live during drag (commit=false), and commits final positions with markNodePositionDirty for every member on pointerup | correct rigid group translation + dirty tracking | e2e-ui | GAP (pure math covered by map-data.test.ts clusterMovedPositions; component wiring untested)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.27] moveCluster with zero visible members for a clusterId (memberIds.length===0) is a no-op — no dirty flag, no position write | guarded early return | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.28] moveCluster commit path: only calls markNodePositionDirty for members whose position actually changed vs. the last recorded nodePositions (changed check via samePosition) — a cluster with delta below one grid step commits nothing | avoids marking layout dirty for a no-op cluster drag | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.29] saveLayout with zero dirty nodes/edges: short-circuits — sets layoutDirty=false, saveStatus="saved", never calls fetch | no network call for a no-op save | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.30] saveLayout happy path: POSTs to /api/architecture-map/layout with nodes[] (id, layout_x/y rounded to 2dp via round2) and edgeLabels[] (index, from, to, relation, protocol, layout_label_ratio rounded to 4dp via round4/clampRatio); on 2xx clears both dirty refs, sets layoutDirty=false, saveStatus="saved" | correct payload shape and post-success state | integration | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.31] saveLayout: a dirty node id no longer present in nodesById (nodesById.get returns undefined) throws editToolbar.saveFailedUnknownNode and is caught, setting saveStatus="error" with that message, layoutDirty forced back true | user sees translated error, dirty flag preserved so retry is possible | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.32] saveLayout: a dirty edge label id no longer present in edgesById throws editToolbar.saveFailedUnknownEdge similarly | same error-preserving behavior for edges | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.33] saveLayout: fetch resolves non-ok (e.g. 403/404/400/422/500) — readErrorMessage parses the JSON body {error, code} and uses errorText(tErrors, `layoutSave.${code}`, error) when code present, else the raw error string, else falls back to editToolbar.saveFailedHttpStatus with the status | correct localized/prioritized error surfaced | integration | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.34] readErrorMessage: response body is not valid JSON — falls through to raw text; empty text falls back to the httpStatusFallback string | resilient error text extraction chain (JSON→text→status fallback) | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.35] saveLayout: fetch itself rejects (network error/thrown, not just non-ok) — caught by outer try/catch, saveStatus="error", saveError from error.message or the generic fallback | network failure handled distinctly from HTTP error | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.36] Save button disabled while saveStatus==="saving" or when !layoutDirty; label cycles Save→Saving…→(Save Failed on error) | correct button state machine across statuses | e2e-ui | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.37] "saved" status renders the map-save-status text only while editMode is true; toggling editMode off hides it even if saveStatus stays "saved" | transient success indicator scoped to edit mode | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.38] saveError paragraph renders only when saveStatus==="error" AND saveError is non-empty | no empty error paragraph | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.39] ArchitectureEdge: protocol label renders via EdgeLabelRenderer (floating, draggable) when isSelected or isConnected; otherwise via foreignObject fixed at labelRatio position, and only when protocol is non-empty (else nothing renders) | correct dual-render-path branching | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.40] ArchitectureEdge: labelRatio is clamped via clampRatio (MIN 0.08 / MAX 0.92); a ratio of exactly 0, 1, NaN, or Infinity all clamp to a safe value (NaN/Infinity → DEFAULT_LABEL_RATIO 0.5, others clamp to bounds) | no label rendered off-edge or NaN transform | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.41] Edge label pointerdown-drag (editable=true): drag threshold EDGE_LABEL_DRAG_THRESHOLD_PX=4 — movement below threshold does not call onMoveEdgeLabel; movement at/above threshold does, using projectedEdgeRatio of the pointer's flow-space projection onto the edge segment | correct drag-vs-click discrimination | e2e-ui | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.42] Edge label pointerdown when editable=false is a no-op (guarded by `if (!editable || !edgeData) return`) | label immovable outside edit mode | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.43] setPointerCapture throwing (jsdom/synthetic events without an active pointer) is caught and ignored, drag still proceeds via window listeners | no crash under test/synthetic dispatch | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.44] Double-click on an editable edge label resets its ratio to DEFAULT_LABEL_RATIO (0.5) via resetLabel; double-click when !editable is a no-op | reset-to-midpoint gesture works, guarded outside edit mode | e2e-ui | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.45] projectedEdgeRatio: a zero-length edge (source===target, lengthSquared===0) returns DEFAULT_LABEL_RATIO rather than dividing by zero | no NaN from a degenerate edge | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.46] markEdgeLabelDirty: dragging a label back to (almostEqual, epsilon 0.001) its initial ratio removes it from dirtyEdgeLabelIdsRef, potentially flipping layoutDirty to false | dirty-tracking undo mirrors node position revert | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.47] moveEdgeLabel: no-op guarded by almostEqual — calling with the ratio it already has does not call setEdgeLabelRatios/markEdgeLabelDirty (referential no-op) | avoids redundant state churn | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.48] Edge stroke styling: selected edge is always #0f172a/2.2px regardless of view; progress view with progressStatus!=="not_started" uses progressColor.border/1.9px; otherwise default #64748b/1.35px; isDimmed (unselected+unconnected) sets strokeOpacity 0.3 | correct visual precedence order | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.49] Edge zIndex ordering: selected=1000, connected=900, else 0 — selected edge always renders above a merely-connected edge | correct stacking under overlap | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.50] MiniMap nodeColor/nodeStrokeColor read `node.data.color` and fall back to UNKNOWN_KIND_COLOR when node.data is missing/malformed — the minimap never renders a colorless (invisible) node even for an untyped/legacy node object | nodeMinimapColor never throws, always returns a real ColorToken | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.51] ClusterBackdrops: groups computed per distinct node.data.cluster value ("ungrouped" default), bounding box padded by CLUSTER_PADDING_X/Y, sorted largest-area-first (z-order: bigger backdrops drawn first, smaller on top) | correct bbox math and paint order | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.52] ClusterBackdrops: cluster label only becomes a drag handle (button) when editMode; otherwise a plain non-interactive span | interaction affordance gated by edit mode | e2e-ui | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.53] Cluster tone assigned by `index % CLUSTER_TONES.length` (6 tones) — a 7th distinct cluster wraps back to tone 0 | tone cycling for >6 clusters | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.54] formatClusterLabel: "ungrouped" → "Ungrouped"; "core_services" → "Core Services" (underscore-split, each word capitalized) | correct label formatting including single-word and multi-word ids | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.55] edgeId format: `${from}->${to}-${index}` is stable and edgeIndexFromId round-trips the numeric suffix even when from/to themselves contain hyphens (regex only anchors the trailing `-\d+`) | id encode/decode consistency | unit | GAP (pure fn; partially exercised indirectly)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.56] round2/round4: rounding uses toFixed then Number() so trailing zeros are dropped (e.g. 12.10 → 12.1) and round4 additionally clamps via clampRatio before formatting | correct numeric payload formatting sent to the save API | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.57] Boundary: 0 nodes / 0 edges in data (post empty-map gate upstream) — component should not be reached in practice, but if given data.nodes=[] flowNodes/flowEdges are empty arrays without crashing | graceful empty render | unit | GAP

### components/map/map-node.tsx

- [map-ui-data-viewer.59] Renders id + translated edgeCount, kind · scope pill, label, tech (only if non-empty), and dataLine only if ownsData or linkedIssueCount produce non-empty content | correct conditional line rendering | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.60] dataLine composition: ownsData sliced to first 3 entries joined by ", "; when linkedIssueCount>0 appends " • N linked issue(s)" translated; when linkedIssueCount===0 no suffix; when ownsData is empty AND linkedIssueCount===0 dataLine is falsy and the whole <span> is omitted | correct truncation (max 3) and conditional suffix/omission | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.61] data-selected/data-future/data-active/data-dimmed attributes are present only when their boolean is true (using `|| undefined` pattern), never rendered as `="false"` | correct boolean-attribute idiom for CSS selectors | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.62] Inline style uses color.bg/border/text from node.color; the pill additionally uses color.pill/border | correct per-view/per-status coloring reaches the DOM | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.63] map-work-pulse indicator renders only when node.isActive is true, with a translated title (node.activeWorkTitle) | active-work affordance gated correctly | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.64] Target/source Handle elements always render regardless of node state (needed for React Flow edge connections even when nodesConnectable=false) | handles present for edge anchoring | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.65] Component is wrapped in React.memo — re-rendering the parent with referentially-identical `data` does not re-render MapNodeComponent | memoization prevents unnecessary re-render on unrelated parent state changes | unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.66] Boundary: ownsData with exactly 3, 4, and 0 entries — slice(0,3) boundary correctness at 3 (no truncation marker) vs 4 (silently drops the 4th+ with no "…" indicator) | verify no visual truncation cue is expected/missing | unit | GAP

- [map-ui-data-viewer.71] extracting=true: button label becomes the pending translation and is disabled; a click attempt does not invoke onExtract | covered | map-empty-state.test.tsx
- [map-ui-data-viewer.72] onExtract omitted (undefined): no Extract button rendered at all (showExtract requires onExtract truthy), but Import docs still offered for no_map/empty_map | covered | map-empty-state.test.tsx
- [map-ui-data-viewer.73] Import docs button opens the inline import Dialog hosting the shared `ImportDocsFlow` (role dialog with the "Import your docs" title/description) | covered | map-empty-state.test.tsx
- [map-ui-data-viewer.74] extractError with code="empty_canonical": message shown, Import button becomes variant="default" (highlighted/primary), Extract stays variant="outline" | covered | map-empty-state.test.tsx
- [map-ui-data-viewer.75] extractError with a different code (e.g. "spawn_failed"): message shown but Import stays variant="outline" (not highlighted) | covered | map-empty-state.test.tsx
- [map-ui-data-viewer.76] extractError=null (default): no error paragraph rendered at all | GAP (implicit in other tests but no dedicated assertion that the <p> is entirely absent)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.77] onImported callback: a successful import inside the inline dialog fires `ImportDocsFlow`'s `onImported(project)`, which closes the dialog (`setImportOpen(false)`) AND calls `onImported?.()` (map refresh), tolerant of `onImported` being undefined | dialog closes and the refresh callback fires; omitting it never throws | GAP (flow-internal success is the project area's; the host wiring itself is untested here)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.78] CardContent (button row) is omitted entirely when neither showExtract nor showImport apply (only reachable for no_target, where both are false) — confirmed no CardContent renders for no_target | GAP (test only asserts individual buttons absent, not that CardContent itself is unrendered)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.79] Icon per reason: FolderSearch for no_target, Workflow for no_map, MapPin for empty_map | GAP (icon identity is not asserted by any test — only text copy is)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.80] Boundary: extractError with an empty-string message (falsy-ish but object truthy) — still renders the alert row wrapper since extractError itself is truthy | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.278] reason="empty_canonical": renders ONE bare muted centered `<p>` (`data-empty-reason="empty_canonical"`, `text-muted-foreground`, `text-center`) split into TWO deliberate viewport-independent lines by a `<br>` — line 1 starts with the left arrow ← ("Talk to Vivi to get grilled"), line 2 is the capitalized consequence ("Your answers turn into the spec.", no em-dash) — and NO Card/border/icon/title/Extract/Import buttons — the passive twin of the auto-opened Vivi panel for a freshly scaffolded project | covered | map-empty-state.test.tsx

### components/map/status-dot.tsx

- [map-ui-data-viewer.81] Each of the 6 NodeStatus values maps to its correct bg-* token class, aria-hidden, rounded-full base | covered | status-dot.test.tsx
- [map-ui-data-viewer.82] null and undefined both fall back to bg-border (not_started) | covered | status-dot.test.tsx
- [map-ui-data-viewer.83] Distinct statuses produce mutually-exclusive classes (no bleed-through) | covered | status-dot.test.tsx
- [map-ui-data-viewer.84] Extra className merges via cn() alongside the status token rather than replacing it | covered | status-dot.test.tsx
- [map-ui-data-viewer.85] An invalid/unknown string status not in NodeStatus (TS would normally block this, but a runtime-untyped caller e.g. from JSON) — BG_BY_STATUS[status] lookup returns undefined, and `undefined` as a class in cn() is simply omitted, leaving NO background class at all (not even the not_started fallback, since `?? "not_started"` only guards null/undefined, not a garbage string) | GAP — this is a real edge case: an unrecognized status string silently produces an unstyled dot instead of falling back to bg-border

- [map-ui-data-viewer.94] asNodeStatus accepts each of the 6 known statuses and rejects unknown strings, undefined, and numbers | covered | map-data.test.ts

- [map-ui-data-viewer.96] resolveNodeStatus: progress view prefers overlay over node.status | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.97] resolveNodeStatus: progress view falls back to node.status absent an overlay entry | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.98] resolveNodeStatus: progress view returns null when neither overlay nor valid node.status exist | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.99] normalizeMapData: null/non-object/string input returns null | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.100] normalizeMapData: missing `name` or non-array `nodes` returns null | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.101] normalizeMapData: node missing id or graph_ref is silently dropped (flatMap → []); a non-object array entry ("garbage") is also dropped | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.102] normalizeMapData: defaults applied — label defaults to id, kind defaults to "unknown", layout_x/y default to 0 via toNumber, owns_data non-string entries filtered via toStringArray | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.103] normalizeMapData: edges referencing a missing `from`/`to` node id are dropped (no dangling edges); edge missing `to` entirely is dropped | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.104] normalizeMapData: development block preserved verbatim when a plain object | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.105] normalizeMapData: `development` that is an array (not a plain object) is treated as NOT an object per the `typeof === "object"` check — arrays pass typeof "object" so it WOULD be kept as-is, potentially violating the intended DevelopmentBlock shape | GAP — no test for development being an array or other non-record object shape
- [map-ui-data-viewer.106] normalizeMapData: `views` that is an array is explicitly excluded (`!Array.isArray(data.views)` check) — falls back to undefined | GAP — untested branch
- [map-ui-data-viewer.107] normalizeMapData: `statusLegend` uses isRecord guard — a non-record statusLegend (array, primitive) is dropped to undefined | GAP — untested branch
- [map-ui-data-viewer.108] normalizeMapData: `lanes` entries missing `id` are dropped; `label` defaults to `id` when absent | GAP — untested branch (only nodes/edges default-behavior is tested, not lanes)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.109] normalizeMapData: edge `layout_label_ratio` — non-finite (NaN, Infinity) or non-number values are dropped to undefined rather than propagated | GAP — untested branch
- [map-ui-data-viewer.110] normalizeMapData: edge `graph_ref` defaults to `edge:${from}->${to}` when absent/non-string (a DIFFERENT, simpler formula than `edgeGraphRef`'s slugged `edge:from->to:relation:protocol` used elsewhere) — potential inconsistency between the normalize-time default and the edgeGraphRef() helper used downstream for overlay lookups | GAP — untested; also a candidate correctness concern worth flagging (see cross-notes)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.111] normalizeMapData: `version`/`updated`/`purpose` type-coercion guards (wrong type → undefined) | GAP — untested branches
- [map-ui-data-viewer.112] applyLiveOverlay: builds graphRefs from every node (canonicalNodeGraphRef) and every edge (edge.graph_ref || canonicalEdgeGraphRef) — an edge with an explicit graph_ref uses it verbatim even if it wouldn't match the canonical slug formula | GAP — no direct unit test of applyLiveOverlay in map-data.test.ts (only indirectly via route.live-overlay.test.ts integration)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.113] applyLiveOverlay: never mutates its `data` input — returns a shallow copy with only `development.graph_item_states`/`active_items` replaced; static `issues`/`coverage_summary`/path pointers preserved | GAP — no explicit immutability assertion
- [map-ui-data-viewer.114] applyLiveOverlay: ledger null/undefined yields graph_item_states=[] and active_items=[] (delegates to deriveDevelopmentOverlay's own null-handling) | GAP — covered indirectly via route.live-overlay.test.ts "renders the static graph" case but not as a map-data-level unit test
- [map-ui-data-viewer.115] applyLiveOverlay: uses the tolerant verificationGateMatcher /.*/ and omits evidenceRefChecker — a verified item with a nonexistent evidence file does not throw | covered (integration) | route.live-overlay.test.ts (not unit-level in map-data.test.ts)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.116] applyLiveOverlay: a malformed ledger that deriveDevelopmentOverlay's validation would reject (e.g. bad graph_ref, duplicate state) — the thrown Error propagates uncaught out of applyLiveOverlay | GAP — no test proving the /api/map route would 500 (or otherwise handle) a ledger that fails the shared validation; readLedger's try/catch only guards JSON.parse failures, NOT validation errors thrown later inside applyLiveOverlay, so a syntactically-valid-but-semantically-invalid ledger WOULD 500 the route uncaught (see cross-notes)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.117] nodeMatchesQuery: empty/whitespace-only query matches everything | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.118] nodeMatchesQuery: matches label/tech/graph_ref case-insensitively | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.119] nodeMatchesQuery: no match returns false | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.120] nodeMatchesQuery: matches id/kind/lane/scope/status/owns_data | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.121] nodeMatchesQuery: source_refs field is also joined into the searchable string (per the doc comment) but NO test exercises matching against a source_ref | GAP — the comment claims source_refs is searchable; unverified
- [map-ui-data-viewer.122] snapXY/snapCoordinate: correctly snaps to the 20px grid, including negative coordinates and exact-boundary values (e.g. 10 → rounds to nearest: Math.round(10/20)*20 = 0 vs 20 tie-breaking) | covered (partially) | map-data.test.ts — boundary tie value (exactly half-grid, e.g. x=10) not explicitly asserted
- [map-ui-data-viewer.123] clusterMovedPositions: applies same snapped delta to every member, ignores non-members, preserves relative offsets, ignores missing-start ids, and a sub-grid delta becomes a no-op after snapping | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.124] MapFilters/computeVisibleCounts: no filters counts all nodes/edges | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.125] computeVisibleCounts: lane filter prunes edges whose endpoint became hidden | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.126] computeVisibleCounts: scope filter | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.127] computeVisibleCounts: query filter hides non-matching nodes and their edges | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.128] computeVisibleCounts: statusFilter=<specific> — status-matched endpoint nodes are counted even off-status, but the edge count additionally re-checks per-edge status match (edgeCount loop re-derives `s` independently of statusMatchedEndpoints used for node visibility) — a node kept alive only as a matched-edge endpoint does NOT inflate edge counts for other non-matching edges it participates in | GAP — no test isolates this specific double-check interaction (node visible via edge-endpoint exception, but that same node's OTHER edges of a different status correctly excluded from edgeCount)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.129] edgeGraphRef: explicit graph_ref returned verbatim | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.130] edgeGraphRef: derives slugged ref from endpoints/relation/protocol via canonicalEdgeGraphRef when graph_ref is empty string | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.131] edgeIndexFromId: parses trailing `-<i>`; returns -1 when absent | GAP — untested directly (only exercised via architecture-map.tsx's selectEdgeFromFlow, not as a focused unit test)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.132] buildEdgeCounts: counts incidence in both directions | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.133] buildEdgeCounts: boundary — a self-loop edge (from===to) counts twice for the same node id (once per get/set inside the loop, since both `edge.from` and `edge.to` resolve to the same id) | GAP — untested self-loop boundary
- [map-ui-data-viewer.134] buildIssuesByGraphRef: indexes issues by every graph_ref they touch, an issue with multiple refs appears in multiple buckets | covered | map-data.test.ts

- [map-ui-data-viewer.136] buildGraphStatesByRef: indexes by graph_ref, skips falsy graph_ref | GAP — the falsy-graph_ref skip branch (`if (state.graph_ref)`) is untested
- [map-ui-data-viewer.137] issueDisplayStatus: active item's live state wins over aggregate graph-item statuses | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.138] issueDisplayStatus: blocked wins over verified across graph items (priority: blocked > in_progress > reviewing > (all)verified > implemented > not_started) | covered | map-data.test.ts (blocked case; in_progress/reviewing priority ordering itself untested directly)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.139] issueDisplayStatus: verified only when ALL graph items verified | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.140] issueDisplayStatus: no graph_refs defaults to not_started | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.141] issueDisplayStatus: in_progress precedence over reviewing/implemented, and reviewing precedence over implemented (the exact priority chain: blocked > in_progress > reviewing > all-verified > implemented > not_started) | GAP — only 2 of the ~5 priority branches are exercised; in_progress-over-reviewing and reviewing-over-implemented and implemented-alone are all untested
- [map-ui-data-viewer.142] issueTranscriptRefs: aggregates refs from graph_item_states AND matching active_items, filters by `/${issue.id}/` path segment inclusion, dedupes via Set | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.143] issueTranscriptRefs: boundary — an issue id that is a PREFIX of another issue id (e.g. "ISS-1" vs "ISS-10") — the `/${issue.id}/` path-segment filter correctly disambiguates since it requires the exact segment bounded by slashes, not just a substring match | GAP — untested boundary; important because a naive `.includes(issue.id)` (without the surrounding slashes) would have false-matched ISS-1 against ISS-10's transcripts — confirm the slash-bounding actually prevents this
- [map-ui-data-viewer.144] formatLineCoverage: formats percentage to 1 decimal | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.145] formatLineCoverage: undefined summary or total_doc_lines=0 both return "0%" | covered | map-data.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.146] formatLineCoverage: boundary — issue_linked_doc_lines > total_doc_lines (data inconsistency) would produce >100% with no clamping | GAP — untested boundary/invariant violation

- [map-ui-data-viewer.148] toStringArray: non-array input returns []; array with mixed types filters to only strings | GAP — untested directly as a unit (only indirectly via owns_data normalization test)
