- [map-ui-data-viewer.156] patchArchitectureMapLayout (nodes): rewrites only the moved node's layout_x/y, preserves other fields byte-for-byte, preserves line count | covered | map-layout-save.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.157] patchArchitectureMapLayout (nodes): rounds coordinates to 2 decimals (formatNumber via toFixed) | covered | map-layout-save.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.158] patchArchitectureMapLayout (nodes): rejects an unknown node id | covered | map-layout-save.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.159] patchArchitectureMapLayout (nodes): rejects a duplicate node patch (same id twice in payload) | covered | map-layout-save.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.160] patchArchitectureMapLayout (nodes): a node record missing a layout_x/layout_y key entirely (replaceRecordValue's `keyLines.get(key) === undefined` branch) throws "missing {key} for record at line N" | GAP — untested; the source fixture always has both keys present
- [map-ui-data-viewer.161] patchArchitectureMapLayout (edges): inserts layout_label_ratio after protocol when absent | covered | map-layout-save.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.161b] patchArchitectureMapLayout (edges): removes layout_label_ratio key entirely when reset to the 0.5 default (within 0.0001 epsilon) | covered | map-layout-save.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.162] patchArchitectureMapLayout (edges): refuses to patch an edge whose from/to/relation/protocol identity doesn't match on-disk (assertSameEdge) | covered | map-layout-save.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.163] patchArchitectureMapLayout (edges): rejects out-of-range edge index | covered | map-layout-save.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.164] patchArchitectureMapLayout (edges): rejects a duplicate edge label patch index | GAP — untested (parallel to the duplicate-node-id test, but no duplicate-edge-index test exists)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.165] patchArchitectureMapLayout: multiple node patches AND multiple edge-label patches applied together in one call, in reverse-edge-order to keep earlier indices valid after any splice | GAP — every existing test patches a single node or a single edge; no test proves the reverse-iteration index-stability claim when BOTH an insert (splice, changing line count) and a later-index edge are patched together in the same call
- [map-ui-data-viewer.166] getYamlRecords/parseYamlRecord: a value using YAML double-quoted string containing a colon or escaped characters — unquoteYamlValue via JSON.parse | GAP — only plain unquoted/simple-quoted values are exercised in the fixture
- [map-ui-data-viewer.167] validateLayoutSavePayload: boundary — nodes:[] and edgeLabels:[] (empty payload, the `emptyPayload()` helper) is valid and produces a no-op patch (returns the source unchanged) | covered | map-layout-save.test.ts (applyLayoutSave "zero dirty" case is tested at the architecture-map.tsx level design intent, but patchArchitectureMapLayout with a fully-empty payload directly is only implicitly exercised via emptyPayload() helper in every other test, not asserted as its own "no-op" case)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.168] resolveMapPath: resolves the fixed relative path under an arbitrary target root | covered | map-layout-save.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.169] resolveMapPath: a target root chosen such that path.relative produces a ".." prefix (traversal) throws LayoutSaveError("map path escapes target root") — MAP_RELATIVE_PATH is a fixed in-repo constant so this can only be reached if targetRoot itself is pathologically constructed (e.g. empty string or relative weirdness) | GAP — untested; the traversal guard's throw branch has zero test coverage since MAP_RELATIVE_PATH never actually varies
- [map-ui-data-viewer.170] isLayoutWriteEnabled: unset env defaults to true | covered | map-layout-save.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.171] isLayoutWriteEnabled: every falsey spelling (0/false/no/off, case-insensitive, whitespace-trimmed) locks writes | covered | map-layout-save.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.172] isLayoutWriteEnabled: any other value (1/true/yes/on/empty string) stays enabled | covered | map-layout-save.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.173] applyLayoutSave: happy path patches source map, invokes regenerate(targetRoot), returns {ok:true, mapPath} | covered | map-layout-save.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.174] applyLayoutSave: refuses a target root that does not exist (no_target) | covered | map-layout-save.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.175] applyLayoutSave: refuses a target with no architecture map file (no_map) | covered | map-layout-save.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.176] applyLayoutSave: kill-switch off (read_only) never touches the file at all (byte-identical before/after) | covered | map-layout-save.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.177] applyLayoutSave: regeneration failure rolls the source map back to pre-save bytes (byte-identical) | covered | map-layout-save.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.178] applyLayoutSave: regenerate() rejecting with a non-Error thrown value (e.g. a string or plain object) — caught by `error instanceof LayoutSaveError ? error : new LayoutSaveError(error instanceof Error ? error.message : "viewer-data regeneration failed", "regen_failed")` — falls to the generic fallback message | GAP — only an Error("generator blew up") is tested; the non-Error-throw fallback message branch is untested
- [map-ui-data-viewer.179] applyLayoutSave: patchArchitectureMapLayout itself throwing (e.g. unknown node id) BEFORE any write occurs — file must remain untouched and the thrown LayoutSaveError propagates uncaught (not wrapped) since the write/regen try/catch only wraps the regenerate() call, not the earlier patch step | GAP — untested at the applyLayoutSave level (only tested at the pure patchArchitectureMapLayout level); need to confirm the file write (`writeFileSync(mapPath, next)`) never happens when patch validation throws
- [map-ui-data-viewer.180] regenerateViewerData (real, non-injected regenerate): script-not-found path (existsSync(script) false) throws regen_failed before ever calling execFile | GAP — completely untested; only the injected `regenerate` seam is exercised in tests, never the real regenerateViewerData implementation
- [map-ui-data-viewer.181] regenerateViewerData (real): execFile failure surfaces stderr (trimmed) or error.message as the regen_failed message, with VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT correctly set in the child env | GAP — untested; no test spawns the real generator subprocess (reasonable to keep mocked, but the wiring itself — env var passing, stderr capture — has zero coverage)

### app/api/map/route.ts

- [map-ui-data-viewer.182] GET returns 200 no_target empty state when isTargetResolved() is false; never reads any file | covered | route.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.183] GET returns 200 no_target when isTargetResolved() true but getArchitectureDataPath() returns null (defensive double-check) | GAP — untested; route.test.ts only covers the isTargetResolved=false path for no_target, not the "resolved but path still null" defensive branch

- [map-ui-data-viewer.185] GET returns 200 empty_map when the map parses/normalizes but nodes.length===0 | covered | route.test.ts

- [map-ui-data-viewer.187] GET returns 422 map_bad_shape when normalizeMapData returns null, with detail citing the file path | covered | route.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.188] GET returns 200 with the full normalized map (not an empty-state envelope) on the happy path | covered | route.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.189] GET overlays the live ledger via applyLiveOverlay(normalizedData, parsedLedger) and returns the overlaid body | covered | route.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.190] GET tolerates a missing/unreadable ledger — readLedger returns undefined (via its own try/catch around JSON.parse/readFile), applyLiveOverlay called with undefined, never 500s | covered | route.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.191] readLedger: getProgressLedgerPath() returning null (no target resolved for ledger path) — readLedger returns undefined without attempting a read | GAP — untested branch (getProgressLedgerPath null-path)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.192] readLedger: ledger file exists but contains invalid JSON — caught by the try/catch, returns undefined (treated as "no progress"), same as a missing file | GAP — route.test.ts's "missing/unreadable ledger" test uses a rejected readFile (ENOENT-style), not a resolved-but-invalid-JSON ledger; the JSON.parse-throws branch specifically is untested
- [map-ui-data-viewer.193] GET: applyLiveOverlay throwing (e.g. a syntactically valid but semantically invalid ledger — bad graph_ref, duplicate state, missing verification-gate ref) is NOT caught anywhere in the route — propagates as an unhandled rejection / Next.js default 500 with no typed error code, unlike every other failure mode in this route which is deliberately shaped as 200-empty or 422 | GAP — real production risk: a malformed-but-parseable progress ledger crashes the whole map endpoint with an opaque 500 instead of a graceful degrade; see cross-notes

- [map-ui-data-viewer.195] Boundary: architecture-data.json exists but is literally an empty file ("") — JSON.parse("") throws SyntaxError, correctly routed to the 422 map_not_json branch (same as any parse failure) | GAP — not distinguished from the generic invalid-JSON case, but worth a dedicated empty-file boundary case since it's the most likely "GAP" real-world file state right after target creation

### factory/map-review.ts

- [map-ui-data-viewer.262] MAP_REVIEW_LENSES: exactly 8 lenses, unique keys, every lens has a non-empty focus | covered | map-review.test.ts

- [map-ui-data-viewer.264] mapReviewLensContext: includes lens key, focus text, the map path, the per-lens output path, and the baseline id | covered | map-review.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.265] aggregateFindings: flattens per-lens findings tagging each with its lens key; a lens with result:null contributes zero findings (tolerant of a dead/silent leg) | covered | map-review.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.266] aggregateFindings: a lens whose `result.findings` is present but NOT an array (malformed leg output) — falls back to `[]` via `Array.isArray(result?.findings) ? result.findings : []` | GAP — untested branch (only `null` result is tested, not a malformed non-array `findings` field)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.267] actionableFindings: keeps everything except explicit real:false (default-real semantics — real:true and real-omitted both pass) | covered | map-review.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.268] formatMapReviewFix: empty findings array returns "" ; non-empty lists each finding as `[lens] target (source_ref): detail — fix: correction` | covered | map-review.test.ts
- [map-ui-data-viewer.269] formatMapReviewFix: a finding missing target/source_ref/correction (all optional per the interface) — falls back to "*" for target, omits the "(source_ref)" and "— fix:" segments entirely rather than rendering "undefined" | GAP — untested; only the fully-populated finding shape is tested, not the graceful-omission branches for missing optional fields
- [map-ui-data-viewer.270] runMapReview: clears any STALE report file left on disk from a prior run for each lens BEFORE fanning out (existsSync + rmSync) | GAP — untested; the test uses `repoRoot: "/nonexistent-temp-root"` specifically so existsSync is always false — the actual stale-file-deletion behavior (existsSync true → rmSync called) has zero coverage
- [map-ui-data-viewer.271] runMapReview: fans out one spawnLens call PER lens IN PARALLEL (Promise.all) — a slow/hanging lens does not block the others from completing (though the test only checks call order/results, not true concurrency) | covered (partially) | map-review.test.ts — parallelism itself (vs strictly sequential) is asserted only by implication (Promise.all in source), not by a timing-based test
- [map-ui-data-viewer.272] runMapReview: a lens whose spawnLens leg REJECTS (throws) — Promise.all would reject the WHOLE runMapReview call, contradicting the doc comment's claim "a lens whose leg dies or writes nothing simply contributes no findings (never blocks the others)" — Promise.all does NOT tolerate an individual rejection; a real dead leg would abort the entire review run | GAP — real correctness concern: the implementation uses `Promise.all`, which propagates a single rejection and cancels the aggregation, yet the header comment explicitly promises isolation from a leg's death; no test exercises a rejecting spawnLens to confirm/refute this claim (see cross-notes — likely should be Promise.allSettled)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.273] runMapReview: readFindings itself throwing (e.g. malformed JSON in a real file-reading implementation, since readFindings is an injected seam) — same aggregation question as a rejecting spawnLens (readFindings is called synchronously outside the Promise.all, so a throw here is NOT caught by Promise.all's rejection handling at all, and would propagate synchronously out of runMapReview) | GAP — untested
- [map-ui-data-viewer.274] runMapReview: with a custom (non-default) `lenses` array shorter than the full 8 — used correctly instead of MAP_REVIEW_LENSES | covered | map-review.test.ts

### factory/prompts/map-review.md

- [map-ui-data-viewer.275] Prompt template's `{{issue_id}}` placeholder is actually substituted by whatever orchestrator renders it (this file's own content has no test — it's static prompt text, verified only by the runtime legs' emitted JSON matching the documented schema) | GAP — no test verifies the prompt file's placeholder syntax, its documented output JSON schema stays in sync with `MapReviewFinding`'s TS interface, or that the file is even read/reachable from the expected path at runtime
- [map-ui-data-viewer.276] Schema drift risk: the prompt tells lenses to write `{ "findings": [{ target, source_ref, detail, correction, real }] }` — no automated check that a real lens's raw JSON output actually deserializes cleanly into `MapReviewFinding` (e.g. extra prose wrapper, invalid JSON, wrong field names) before reaching aggregateFindings, which silently defaults to `[]` on any shape mismatch (`Array.isArray` check only) | GAP — no fixture-based test feeds a "realistic bad LLM output" (extra prose, markdown code fence, wrong keys) through the real read-findings path to confirm graceful degradation rather than a wrong silent pass

### Area cross-notes

- **Ledger validation failure is unhandled in the read path (highest-severity GAP).** `/api/map`'s `readLedger()` only guards `readFile`/`JSON.parse` failures with try/catch (route.ts:96-104), but `applyLiveOverlay` → `deriveDevelopmentOverlay` throws plain `Error`s for a wide range of semantically-invalid-but-syntactically-valid ledgers (unknown graph_ref, duplicate graph_item_state, verified-without-gate-evidence, in_progress-without-heartbeat — all in development-overlay.ts). None of these throws are caught anywhere between `applyLiveOverlay` and the route's `Response.json`, so a single malformed ledger entry — plausible after a partial write, a bug in the dev-loop writer, or manual editing — takes down the ENTIRE map viewer with an opaque 500, contradicting the module's own "read path is tolerant... never 500s" doc comments (map-data.ts:214, 244). This applies to both the mocked unit test (route.test.ts) and the real-stack integration test (route.live-overlay.test.ts) — neither exercises an invalid ledger. See cases .116, .193, .199.
- **`Promise.all` vs. the "never blocks the others" claim in map-review.ts.** The module doc comment (map-review.ts:8) and `runMapReview`'s own inline comment ("A lens whose leg dies or writes nothing simply contributes no findings (never blocks the others)") describe fault-isolation semantics, but the implementation fans out via `Promise.all`, which aborts on the FIRST rejection rather than isolating it. If a real lens-spawning leg throws (agent process crash, spawn failure), the entire map review run fails instead of degrading to 7/8 lenses. Either the implementation needs `Promise.allSettled` + per-leg catch, or the comment overpromises; either way this is untested and worth a dedicated case. See .272.
- **Two independently-maintained copies of `slugGraphRefPart`/graph-ref formulas that must never drift**, called out explicitly in both files' own comments (development-overlay.ts:321-325, generate-viewer-data.ts implicit via `getNodeGraphRef`/`getEdgeGraphRef`). No test asserts the two produce identical output for the same input — a manual sync risk with zero automated backstop. Additionally, `normalizeMapData`'s OWN THIRD graph_ref default formula (`edge:${from}->${to}`, simpler, no relation/protocol) at map-data.ts:158-161 diverges from both — three formulas, one is a fallback used only when `edge.graph_ref` is absent from the parsed JSON (which in practice should never happen since the generator always emits it), but nothing prevents/tests the divergence if a hand-edited or third-party JSON omits it. See .110, .259.
- **`validateMap` (the ~300-line business-rule gate in generate-viewer-data.ts) has essentially zero direct test coverage** — the existing `generate-viewer-data.test.ts` file tests ONLY `parseArchitectureMap` and `reconcileLayout`, both pure syntax-level concerns. Every semantic invariant that keeps the architecture map internally consistent and honest (status must be not_started, no evidence_refs on the target map, canonical-file coverage, frozen-baseline pinning, kind/lane/status referential integrity, the anti-"fallback" banned-term check, high-risk line precision) is exercised by NO automated test in this repo area. This is the single largest coverage gap in the whole area and should be prioritized — a regression here would silently let an invalid architecture-map.yml reach production (or, conversely, block a valid one) with no test signal at all. See .229-.259.
- **`main()` — the actual CLI entrypoint — is completely untested**, including the `--reconcile-against` and `--write-map-metadata` flags, the layout-preservation assertions it wires together, and the final `writeFileSync` of the generated artifact. Every constituent pure function it calls is tested in isolation (except `validateMap`, see above) but the orchestration itself has zero coverage; a wiring bug (wrong argument order, skipped assertion call, wrong file path) would not be caught. See .223-.225.
- **Component-level (architecture-map.tsx) filter/selection/save logic is entirely untested** despite the underlying pure helpers in lib/map-data.ts and lib/map-layout-save.ts being well covered — there is no `architecture-map.test.tsx` in this area's file list at all. Every piece of UI wiring (filter combination → visible node/edge sets, click handlers, edit-mode drag/save state machine, edge-label drag math, ClusterBackdrops group computation/drag) is a GAP. This is the largest UI-side coverage gap; recommend at minimum a React Testing Library suite covering filter combinations, selection round-trips, and the save-error/dirty-tracking state machine (cases .1-.58).
- **`StatusDot` silently produces an unstyled (no background class) dot for a garbage status string** rather than falling back to `bg-border` — the `?? "not_started"` nullish-coalesce only catches `null`/`undefined`, not an arbitrary invalid string, and `BG_BY_STATUS[garbage]` is `undefined`, which `cn()` simply drops. Low blast radius (the type system should prevent this upstream) but worth one boundary test given how cheap it is to add. See .85.
- **`MapNodeData.ownsData` truncation to 3 entries has no visual "+N more" affordance** and no test confirms this is the intended (vs. accidental) UX — worth a design/product check alongside the test gap. See .66, .60.

### Adversarial additions

- [map-ui-data-viewer.X1] `applyLiveOverlay` at the `lib/map-data.ts` unit level: passing a ledger with an unknown graph_ref (or a duplicate graph_item_state, or `in_progress` without a matching active-item heartbeat, or `verified` missing a gate evidence_ref) THROWS synchronously out of `applyLiveOverlay` itself — there is no unit test in `map-data.test.ts` that calls `applyLiveOverlay` directly with an invalid ledger and asserts it throws (today's coverage only proves the valid-ledger path and only at the route-integration level per .116/.193/.199); this is the narrowest reproduction of the whole-area's #1 GAP and should be the FIRST fix (wrap the call in route.ts with a try/catch that degrades to the static graph, exactly like the missing-ledger case, then lock it here) | expected: either the route catches and degrades (200, `not_started` graph) once fixed, or — if the throw is intentionally preserved as a 500 — a route.test.ts case asserting `GET` rejects/  errors for this input, so the behavior is at least pinned instead of silently unexercised | kind: unit (map-data.ts) + integration (route.ts real-stack, mirroring route.live-overlay.test.ts) | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.X2] Concurrent POSTs to `/api/architecture-map/layout`: two overlapping `applyLayoutSave` calls both read the same pre-save `original` bytes, then each writes+regenerates independently — the second writer's `writeFileSync` silently clobbers whatever the first one wrote (last-write-wins), and if the SECOND call's regeneration fails, its rollback restores ITS OWN captured `original`, which is now stale relative to the first writer's already-committed change, discarding the first save entirely with no lock/version check anywhere in `applyLayoutSave` | expected (today, undocumented): silent lost update under concurrent drag+save from two browser tabs/sessions against the same target; no test exercises two interleaved `applyLayoutSave` calls against the same `root` | kind: integration | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.X3] `readLedger()` in `route.ts` reads the ledger file with a single `readFile` call while the dev-loop may be mid-`writeFileSync` of a new ledger version (no file lock, no atomic rename-based write visible in this route) — a request landing during a partial write sees truncated/incomplete JSON, `JSON.parse` throws, is caught, and `readLedger` returns `undefined` (degrading to "no progress", not a 500) — this specific interleaving (torn read of a live-written file) is never simulated in any test; worth a regression test writing a truncated-JSON ledger file directly and asserting `/api/map` still returns 200 with an empty overlay rather than assuming only "file absent" reproduces the catch branch | kind: integration | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.X4] `resolveMapPath`'s traversal guard (`rel.startsWith("..")`) is dead code under the current call graph: `MAP_RELATIVE_PATH` is a hardcoded module constant, never influenced by request input, so the guard can only ever fire if a FUTURE change threads a request-controlled path into `resolveMapPath` — no test proves the guard actually throws for a `targetRoot`/relative-path combination that would produce a `..`-prefixed result (e.g. by monkey-patching `MAP_RELATIVE_PATH` via a test-only export, or refactoring the function to accept the relative path as a parameter for testability) | expected: `LayoutSaveError` with code `patch_failed` and message containing "map path escapes target root" | kind: unit | GAP (already flagged as untested in .169, but note here it is effectively UNREACHABLE/untestable in the current signature — a design smell worth flagging to the area owner: either make the relative path an injectable parameter so the guard is exercisable, or drop the dead guard)
- [map-ui-data-viewer.X5] `main()` in `generate-viewer-data.ts`: exit code and stderr message when `VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT` is unset (`process.exit(2)` with the exact "error: no target project configured..." string) — this module-load-time guard runs BEFORE any exported function is reachable, so it can only be exercised via a child-process spawn test (`execFile`/`spawn` of the compiled script with the env var deleted), which does not exist anywhere in the test suite | expected: exit code 2, stderr matches the documented message | kind: e2e-process | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.X6] `validateMap`'s status/evidence_refs invariants interact with `deriveDevelopmentOverlay`'s OWN independent enforcement of the identical rule (status requires evidence, verified requires gate ref) — the STATIC map-level rule (`node.status must be not_started`, `node.evidence_refs must be absent`) and the LEDGER-level rule (deriveDevelopmentOverlay) are two separate gates guarding the same invariant from two different inputs (map YAML vs. ledger JSON) with zero test proving they cannot be bypassed by disagreeing with each other — e.g. a target map node hand-edited to `status: verified` (which `validateMap` DOES reject) versus a ledger entry claiming a graph_ref is verified for a node not present in the map at all (which graphRefs-set validation in `deriveDevelopmentOverlay` DOES reject) — both individually tested, but no integration test runs BOTH gates together against one deliberately-inconsistent target project ( architecture-map.yml disagreeing with progress-ledger.json in a way each gate alone wouldn't catch, e.g. a ledger graph_ref for a node that WAS in the map when the ledger entry was written but was since deleted from the map on a later extraction re-run) | expected: `/api/map`'s tolerant read-time overlay (which re-derives graphRefs from the CURRENT map) rejects the stale ledger entry as "references unknown graph item" — i.e. re-extraction silently orphans ledger entries for deleted nodes, which is exactly the uncaught-throw scenario in .116/.193/X1, not a distinct bug — but there is no test that arrives at this scenario via the realistic "node removed by re-extraction, stale ledger entry remains" story rather than a hand-crafted bad ledger | kind: integration | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.X7] `formatClusterLabel` / `ClusterBackdrops` grouping: a node with `layout_cluster` containing characters other than `_` (e.g. a hyphen, a space, or an empty string `""`) — `formatClusterLabel("")` returns `""` (empty label rendered in the cluster pill/handle with no visible text and no button title fallback), and a cluster id that collides with the literal string `"ungrouped"` for a node that ALSO has no `layout_cluster` set is visually indistinguishable from a real cluster explicitly named "ungrouped" | expected: either documented as intentional or given a display fallback (e.g. "(unnamed cluster)") | kind: unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.X8] `nodeMinimapColor` / `MiniMap` boundary: a node whose `data.color` is present but is not a valid `ColorToken` shape (e.g. a partial object missing `border`, from a version-skewed cached React Flow node during a hot-reload) — `nodeStrokeColor={(n) => nodeMinimapColor(n).border}` would pass `undefined` straight to React Flow's SVG `stroke` attribute since `nodeMinimapColor` only null-coalesces a MISSING `color` object, not a malformed one with individual missing fields | expected: never render an `undefined` stroke (SVG silently no-strokes, reintroducing the "empty minimap" bug the palette tests explicitly guard against for the KNOWN-kind case, but not for a malformed color object) | kind: unit | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.X9] `ArchitectureMap`'s `onNodesChange`/`onEdgesChange` (wired straight to React Flow's default reducers via `useNodesState`/`useEdgesState`) are never asserted to LEAVE the position/selection sync effects intact when React Flow internally fires a `remove` change (e.g. user presses Delete/Backspace while a node is focused, which React Flow supports by default since `nodesFocusable` is not disabled) — nothing in the code or tests proves a node cannot be deleted from the LOCAL `nodes` state via a stray keypress while `data.nodes` (the source of truth) still lists it, potentially desyncing the two on the next `flowNodes` recompute (the `useEffect` sync only PRESERVES a dragging position, it does not defend against a node having been removed from local `nodes` entirely — the effect would just re-add it from `flowNodes` on the next data-driven re-render, but between that recompute and the deletion there could be a render where the deleted node is simply gone from the DOM with no user-visible explanation) | expected: keyboard delete either is explicitly disabled (`nodesFocusable={false}` / `deleteKeyCode={null}`) or is proven harmless by a test; today neither the disabling prop nor a regression test exists | kind: e2e-ui | GAP
- [map-ui-data-viewer.X10] `saveLayout`'s fetch call has no `AbortController`/timeout and no guard against a user toggling `editMode` off (or navigating away / the target project changing via a background poll) WHILE a save is in flight — the `saving` -> `saved`/`error` state transition still fires against the (possibly stale/unmounted) component's closures (`setSaveStatus`, `setSaveError` reference the render's own setters, which in React are stable, but `nodesById`/`edgesById`/`nodePositions` are captured in the closure at call time, not re-read) — a target-project SWITCH mid-save (a real workflow: user drags a node, clicks Save, then immediately switches the open project via the top-left picker before the fetch resolves) would complete a layout write against the file paths resolved server-side for whatever `getTargetRoot()` returns AT THE TIME THE REQUEST ARRIVES, which could now be the NEW target — i.e. a save initiated against project A can land on project B if the switch completes before the in-flight POST is received | expected: either the request carries an explicit target identifier that the server validates against, or the client aborts in-flight saves on project switch; neither exists today, and there is no test simulating a target switch during a pending save | kind: integration | GAP

## Area: onboarding-project-scaffold

### components/project/folder-browser.tsx

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.1] | On `open` flipping true, the browser resets `newFolderOpen`/`newFolderName` and re-browses the default root (path=null) | `GET /api/fs/list` (no path) is called and listing renders from home | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.2] | `open` flips false then true again (dialog closed/reopened) | Re-browses from root again, discarding any prior navigated-into path | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.3] | `browse(path)` succeeds (200, ok:true) | `listing` state updates, `onListingChange` fires with the new listing | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.4] | `browse` receives non-ok HTTP status or `ok:false` body | Previous listing is preserved (not cleared), a toast error shows with mapped `code` via `errorText(tErrors, "fsBrowse.<code>", fallback)` or fallback text | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.5] | `browse` body has no `code` field on error | Toast falls back to raw `error` string or generic httpError text | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.6] | `fetch` rejects (network failure) during `browse` | Toast shows `error.message` or generic networkError text; listing untouched | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.7] | `res.json()` throws (malformed JSON) | Caught via `.catch(() => ({}))`, falls into `!res.ok || body.ok === false` branch with generic httpError fallback | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.8] | Breadcrumb row clicked mid-fetch (`busy`=true) | Breadcrumb buttons are `disabled` — click has no effect | e2e-ui | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.9] | Breadcrumb clicked for a non-root crumb | `browse(crumb.path)` called with that crumb's reconstructed absolute path | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.10] | Breadcrumb renders the server-provided `listing.crumbs` verbatim — no client-side path splitting, so segments are separator-correct on every OS | one `ghost xs` crumb button per segment, tight chevron separators (`px-1` crumbs, no inter-item gap) between them | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.11] | Click a non-root crumb | `browse(crumb.path)` navigates to that crumb's absolute path | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.12] | `allowCreate=false` (the scaffold form's parent picker AND the governed open-existing / change-project browser) | "New folder" button and inline form never render regardless of state | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.13] | `allowCreate=true` and `newFolderOpen=false` and no listing yet (`listing===null`) | "New folder" button renders but is `disabled` (no current dir to create into) | e2e-ui | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.14] | Click "New folder" button | `newFolderOpen=true`; inline form (name input, Create, X-cancel) renders, autofocused | e2e-ui | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.15] | Press Escape inside the new-folder name input | Form closes (`newFolderOpen=false`) and name resets to "" without submitting | e2e-ui | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.16] | Click the X/cancel button in the new-folder form | Same as Escape: closes form, clears name | e2e-ui | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.17] | Submit new-folder form with a blank/whitespace-only name | `createFolder` no-ops (guard `name.length === 0` after trim); no POST is sent | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.18] | Submit new-folder form with `listing === null` | `createFolder` no-ops (guard `!listing`) | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.19] | Submit button disabled state | Disabled while `creatingFolder` or when trimmed name is empty | e2e-ui | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.20] | `createFolder` succeeds (200, `ok:true`, `path` present) | Toast success with `body.path`; name/form reset; browser navigates into `body.path` (a second `browse` call) | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.21] | `createFolder` fails (non-ok, `ok:false`, or missing `path`) | Toast error with mapped `fsBrowse.<code>` or fallback `body.error`/httpError; form stays open, browser does not navigate | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.22] | `createFolder` fetch throws | Toast error with `error.message` or networkError fallback | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.23] | `createFolder` runs | `creatingFolder=true` during the request (name input + submit/X disabled), reset to false in `finally` regardless of outcome | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.24] | Parent passes `disabled=true` (e.g. the scaffold form mid-submit) while browser itself is idle | All rows/breadcrumbs disabled (`busy = disabled || ownBusy`) even though the browser's own `ownBusy` is false | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.25] | Browser's own busy state changes (loading or creatingFolder toggles) | `onBusyChange(ownBusy)` fires — NOT folding in the parent's `disabled` (per the code comment, avoiding a useless round-trip) | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.26] | `listing.parent` is non-null | A "parent directory" (CornerLeftUp) row renders above the subdirectory list, clicking it browses to `listing.parent` | e2e-ui | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.27] | `listing.parent` is null (filesystem root) | No parent-up row renders | e2e-ui | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.28] | Initial mount, `loading=true` and `listing=null` | Spinner + "loading" text shown in place of the list | e2e-ui | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.29] | Listing resolves with zero entries and not loading | Empty-state text (`t("empty")`) renders instead of any rows | e2e-ui | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.30] | Listing has N subdirectory entries | Each renders as a `FolderRow` with Folder icon, name truncated, keyed by absolute path | e2e-ui | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.31] | Click a subdirectory row | `browse(entry.path)` navigates into it | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.32] | Row `disabled` while `busy` | Row has `disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50`, click has no effect | e2e-ui | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.33] | Deep path breadcrumb overflow | Breadcrumb `<nav>` wraps within `min-w-0 flex-1` (multi-segment paths reflow onto new lines) so it never pushes the browser off-screen; separators are tightened so long paths stay legible and linear | e2e-ui | GAP

### components/project/import-docs-flow.tsx (docs-first, single-screen import; hosted by the Vivi panel's onboarding view AND the map empty state's inline dialog)

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.34] | The hosting surface (re)activates (`active` flips true) | `entries`, `listing`, `importing`, `dragActive` all reset — a reopened flow starts empty regardless of prior state | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.35] | User picks documents via the single "Choose files" input (multi-select) | each file becomes a `SelectedEntry` with `rel = webkitRelativePath || file.name` and `accepted` = client-allowlist match; `input.value` reset to "" after so the same file can be re-picked | integration | import-docs-flow.test.tsx ("an accepted document unlocks step 2 …")
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.38] | Selection contains an unsupported extension (via drag; the picker's `accept` filters most at the OS dialog) | the file is kept but flagged `accepted:false` and rendered struck-through with an "unsupported" badge — NOT a whole-selection rejection; a mixed batch is allowed and only the accepted count gates step 2 | unit | import-docs-flow.test.tsx ("a dropped unsupported file is marked skipped …")
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.39] | Picked file list is empty (`fileList.length === 0`) | `onFilesPicked` no-ops silently | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.44] | Drag enters the drop zone while not importing | `dragActive=true`, primary-tinted dashed highlight applied | e2e-ui | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.45] | Drag enters the drop zone while `importing` | `dragActive` stays false (guarded by `!importing`) | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.46] | Drag leaves the drop zone | `dragActive=false` | e2e-ui | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.47] | Drop while `importing` | `onDrop` returns early after `setDragActive(false)`; nothing added | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.48] | Drop with items/files present but `entriesFromDataTransfer` yields zero entries (folder drop, no File/Directory Entries API) | toast `folderReadErrorTitle`/`folderReadErrorDescription`; nothing added | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.49] | Drop with zero items and zero files (empty drag) | `hadItems=false`, skips the folder-read-error branch, `addRaw([])` no-ops | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.50] | Drop mixing accepted and unsupported files (and/or re-dropping the same rel) | all are accumulated and deduped by normalized rel (last wins), each classified accepted/rejected; the accepted subset drives the gate | unit | import-docs-flow.test.tsx ("a dropped unsupported file is marked skipped …")
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.51] | `entriesFromDataTransfer` with `webkitGetAsEntry` unavailable | Falls back to flat `dataTransfer.files`, each with `rel = file.name` | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.52] | `entriesFromDataTransfer` with entry API available, dropped items include null entries | Null entries filtered out before walking | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.53] | `walkEntry` on a file entry whose `.file()` callback rejects | Entry silently dropped (`.catch(() => null)`, `if (file)` guard) rather than throwing | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.54] | `walkEntry` on a directory entry with more than one `readEntries` batch | Reader is called repeatedly until an empty batch, all children collected across batches | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.55] | `walkEntry` where `readEntries` rejects | Caught, empty batch assumed, loop breaks (no infinite loop, no throw) | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.56] | `walkEntry` recurses nested subdirectories | Each child's `rel` is `${parentRel}/${child.name}`, preserving the folder-relative path sent to the server as `paths[]` | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.82] | `formatBytes` boundary values: 0, 1023, 1024, 1024*1024-1, 1024*1024, huge | 0/1023 -> "N B"; 1024..1MB-1 -> "X.X KB"; >=1MB -> "X.X MB" | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.83] | `isAcceptedFilename` case-insensitivity (`FOO.MD`, `bar.ZIP`) | Accepted regardless of case | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.84] | `isAcceptedFilename` with no extension or a partial match ("notmd", "readme.md.exe") | Rejected — only true suffix matches in the client allowlist count | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.348] | Two-step gating on one screen: step 2 (folder browser + submit) is disabled and shows the locked hint until ≥1 accepted document exists; the submit CTA is disabled | deterministic gate, no wizard navigation — both steps always visible | unit | import-docs-flow.test.tsx ("step 2 is locked and the import CTA disabled until a document is added")
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.349] | Step 1 offers ONE file picker (no separate folder/zip buttons); its `accept` is exactly the server allowlist plus `.zip` (drift-guard: rendered `accept`, split on comma, equals `[...SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS, ZIP_TRANSPORT_EXTENSION]`); folders arrive via drag or zip | exactly one `input[type=file]`, no `webkitdirectory`, and its accept-list set-equals the server allowlist + `.zip` transport | unit | import-docs-flow.test.tsx ("the single file picker's accept-list is exactly the server allowlist plus the .zip transport")
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.350] | Selected preview after adding files | "N documents ready · M file(s) skipped" summary; accepted rows plain, rejected rows struck-through with an "unsupported" badge; a "Clear" button empties the selection | unit | import-docs-flow.test.tsx ("a dropped unsupported file is marked skipped …")
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.351] | Step 2 embeds the shared `FolderBrowser` (`allowCreate`, New-folder legitimate here) with `open={active}`, disabled and dimmed until step 1 is ready; the browsed `listing.path` is the import target and is echoed in an "Imports into <path>" preview | one source for folder browsing across open/scaffold/import; target = current folder, not a selected row | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.352] | Submit builds `multipart/form-data` for `POST /api/project/import`: `targetDir=listing.path` plus index-aligned `files`/`paths` for the ACCEPTED entries only (client-rejected files are never uploaded) | request shape matches the import acquisition route's (`importDocuments`) contract; unsupported binaries are not sent | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.353] | The one submit CTA is the uniform flow primary: `variant=default` (green), default size, `w-full`; enabled only when step 1 has ≥1 accepted AND a folder is browsed AND not importing/browser-busy; shows a spinner + "Importing…" mid-request | matches the open/scaffold CTA trio | e2e-ui | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.354] | Import succeeds (200, `ok`, `project`+`accepted` present) | success toast: `{count} imported`, `Detected {language}` (ISO 639-3 → display name via `Intl.DisplayNames`, omitted for `und`/unresolved), and `{skipped}` = client-rejected + server `rejected[]`; `onImported(project)` fires so the host switches to the newly governed project | integration | import-docs-flow.test.tsx ("a successful import reports the acquired project and toasts count + language")
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.355] | Target is already governed → `409 already_governed` | error toast titled "Cannot import documents", body the `import.already_governed` copy (Open-flow voice, inverted meaning); the screen is kept (entries + folder intact); `onImported` never fires | integration | import-docs-flow.test.tsx ("a governed target is refused …")
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.356] | Other typed errors (`no_supported_files`, `zip_slip`, `zip_unreadable`, or ScaffoldError `not_absolute`/`not_a_directory`/`invalid_name`/`templates_missing`) | error toast mapped across the `["import","scaffold"]` families with a fallback; the screen is kept for another attempt | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.357] | The import `fetch` rejects (network failure) | toast with `error.message` or the generic network-error text; `importing` reset in `finally` | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.358] | `languageName` maps an ISO 639-3 code to a locale display name; `und`, `root`, or a code resolving to itself yields no language clause | the success toast never surfaces a raw `und`/code to the user | unit | GAP

### components/project/open-project-form.tsx (+ open-project-dialog.tsx, now a thin trigger-less Dialog wrapper the setup bar keeps mounted)

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.101] | The form (re)activates (`active` flips true — dialog open, or the panel's open-existing view mounting) | The `FolderBrowser` re-browses from its default root via its own `open` prop; the form holds no manual-path field of its own to reset | integration | GAP

### lib/project.ts

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.135] | `getCurrentProjectPath()` | Returns `<runtimeDir>/current-project.json` | unit | project.test.ts (indirectly, via getCurrentProjectPath assertions)
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.136] | `readCurrentProjectRoot()` when the file does not exist | Returns null | unit | project.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.137] | `readCurrentProjectRoot()` when the file exists with valid JSON `{root:"<path>"}` | Returns the trimmed root string | unit | project.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.138] | `readCurrentProjectRoot()` when the file is corrupt JSON | Returns null (never throws) | unit | project.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.139] | `readCurrentProjectRoot()` when JSON parses but `root` is not a string, or is a whitespace-only string | Returns null | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.140] | `describeProject` with a relative path | Throws `ProjectError` code `not_absolute` | unit | project.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.141] | `describeProject` with an absolute path that does not exist | Throws `ProjectError` code `not_found` | unit | project.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.142] | `describeProject` with an absolute path to a file (not directory) | Throws `ProjectError` code `not_a_directory` | unit | project.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.143] | `describeProject` with a valid directory, no `.vivicy/canonical` | Returns `{root, name: basename, hasCanonicalSpec:false}` | unit | project.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.144] | `describeProject` with a valid directory holding `.vivicy/canonical/` as a directory | `hasCanonicalSpec:true` | unit | project.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.145] | `describeProject` where `.vivicy/canonical` exists but is a FILE, not a directory | `hasCanonicalSpec:false` (statSync succeeds but `.isDirectory()` is false) | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.146] | `describeProject` trims surrounding whitespace from the candidate before validating | Root reflects the trimmed path | unit | project.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.332] | `describeProject` with a symlink-spelled root (e.g. macOS `/tmp/...` vs `/private/tmp/...`) | Root is canonicalized via `realpathSync` — ONE directory has ONE spelling, so the per-project runtime key (which hashes the root string) can never fork a project's namespace (run lock, notifications, sessions) across spellings | unit | project.test.ts ("canonicalizes a symlink-spelled root to ONE spelling")
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.147] | `setCurrentProject` with a valid candidate | Creates runtime dir if missing, writes `{root}\n` JSON (pretty-printed) to the store, returns the described record | unit | project.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.148] | `setCurrentProject` writes ONLY the `root` key — never any other field from the describing record or raw input | unit | project.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.149] | `setCurrentProject` with an invalid candidate | Throws before writing anything (no file created) | unit | project.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.150] | `getCurrentProject()` with nothing persisted | Returns null | unit | project.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.151] | `getCurrentProject()` with a persisted root that no longer resolves (deleted/moved) | Returns null (never throws) rather than surfacing the stale path | unit | project.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.152] | `getCurrentProject()` with a persisted root that DOES resolve | Returns the fresh describing record (re-validated each call, not cached) | unit | project.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.153] | `setCurrentProject` runtime dir mkdir uses `recursive:true` — nested runtime dir path that doesn't exist yet | Succeeds without a pre-existing parent | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.338] | `setCurrentProject(candidate, { requireGoverned: true })` on a folder with no `.vivicy` directory | Throws `ProjectError` code `not_governed` BEFORE any persist — the current project is never switched (no orphan runtime state) | unit | project.test.ts ("rejects a folder with no .vivicy directory (not_governed) without persisting")
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.339] | `setCurrentProject(candidate, { requireGoverned: true })` on a folder that holds a `.vivicy` directory | Persists and returns the described record (governance satisfied) | unit | project.test.ts ("accepts a folder that holds a .vivicy directory and persists it")
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.340] | `setCurrentProject(candidate)` with no `requireGoverned` option on an ungoverned folder | Persists as before — the `.vivicy` gate is opt-in per call (scaffold and import legitimately select ungoverned folders through the same store) | unit | project.test.ts ("persists an ungoverned folder when governance is not required")
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.341] | `ProjectError.code` union carries `not_governed` alongside `not_absolute`/`not_found`/`not_a_directory` | typed error code, never a raw throw | unit | project.test.ts (governance cases assert the code)

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.176] | `detectGateCommand` with neither signal (e.g. a bare `main.go`) | Returns null | unit | scaffold.test.ts

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.195] | `isGitRepo` on a target that is already a repo before scaffolding (from-scratch mode, pre-`git init`) | `initFromScratchRepo` skips init/commit entirely, returns `{initialized:false, committed:false}`, leaves owner's identity/history untouched | unit | scaffold.test.ts

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.202] | `SKELETON_DIRS` — every declared dir gets a `.gitkeep`, including nested paths like `.vivicy/development/issues` | Nested mkdir with `recursive:true` succeeds even though `.vivicy/development` itself isn't separately declared | unit | scaffold.test.ts

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.204] | `getTemplatesRoot()` reflects `VIVICY_FACTORY_ROOT` env override | Returns `<factoryRoot>/templates` | unit | GAP (implicitly exercised by every scaffold.test.ts test via the beforeEach env set, but the function itself isn't asserted in isolation)

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.205] | `getDefaultBrowseRoot()` | Returns `os.homedir()` | unit | GAP (used but not directly asserted for exact homedir() equality beyond "is absolute")
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.206] | `resolveBrowsePath` with empty/whitespace input | Returns the realpath'd default root (home) | unit | fs-browser.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.207] | `resolveBrowsePath` with a relative path | Throws `FsBrowseError` code `not_absolute` | unit | fs-browser.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.208] | `resolveBrowsePath` with an absolute path that does not exist | Throws `not_found` | unit | fs-browser.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.209] | `resolveBrowsePath` with an absolute path to a file | Throws `not_a_directory` | unit | fs-browser.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.210] | `resolveBrowsePath` collapses `..`/`.` traversal segments to a real, existing target | Resolved normally, not rejected | unit | fs-browser.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.211] | `resolveBrowsePath` traversal that resolves outside anything real | Throws `not_found` | unit | fs-browser.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.212] | `resolveBrowsePath` resolves a symlinked directory to its canonical realpath | Returns the real target, not the symlink path | unit | fs-browser.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.213] | `resolveBrowsePath` with a symlink pointing to a NON-existent target (dangling symlink) | `existsSync` on the normalized (still-symlink) path is false (existsSync follows links) -> throws `not_found` | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.214] | `resolveBrowsePath` with a symlink forming a cycle (A->B->A) | `realpathSync` throws (ELOOP) — uncaught, propagates as a generic Error (not FsBrowseError), route maps it to 500 | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.215] | `listDirectories` lists only subdirectories, sorted case-insensitively, omitting files and dotfiles | Confirmed order + filtering | unit | fs-browser.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.216] | `listDirectories` with a symlink entry pointing to a directory | Included (statSync resolves it as a directory) | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.217] | `listDirectories` with a symlink entry pointing to a FILE | Excluded (statSync says not a directory) | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.218] | `listDirectories` with a broken/dangling symlink entry | Excluded (statSync throws, caught, filtered out — never fatal) | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.219] | `listDirectories` with an unreadable child (permission error during stat) | Excluded, listing still succeeds for the rest | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.220] | `listDirectories` surfaces the canonical parent (via `browseParent`) for a non-root dir | `parent` = `path.dirname(dir)` | unit | fs-browser.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.221] | `listDirectories` at the filesystem root ("/") | `parent: null` (via `browseParent`) | unit | fs-browser.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.342] | `listDirectories` returns `crumbs` from the filesystem root down to the listed dir | `crumbs[0] = {label:"/", path:"/"}`, `crumbs.at(-1) = {label: basename, path: dir}` | unit | fs-browser.test.ts ("returns breadcrumb segments from the filesystem root down to the listed dir")
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.222] | `listDirectories` sorting with mixed-case names (e.g. "Beta" vs "alpha") | Sorted case-insensitively (`sensitivity:"base"`), not by raw char code | unit | GAP (test data is already lowercase, doesn't exercise case-insensitive comparator distinctly)
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.223] | `validateFolderName` accepts a clean single-segment name and trims whitespace | Accepted, trimmed | unit | fs-browser.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.224] | `validateFolderName` rejects: empty, whitespace-only, ".", "..", "../escape", "a/b", "/abs", ".hidden", "bad*name", "semi;colon", "new\nline" | All throw `invalid_name` | unit | fs-browser.test.ts (parameterized `it.each`)
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.225] | `validateFolderName` with a 255-char name (boundary: max length) | Accepted | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.226] | `validateFolderName` with a 256-char name (boundary: over max) | Rejected `invalid_name` | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.227] | `validateFolderName` with input not a string (undefined, number, object) | Coerces to "" -> rejected | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.228] | `createDirectory` creates a direct child of a validated parent, returns the absolute path, visible in the next listing | unit | fs-browser.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.229] | `createDirectory` rejects a name that already exists as a file OR directory | Throws `exists` code | unit | fs-browser.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.230] | `createDirectory` rejects an invalid name before touching disk | Throws `invalid_name`; nothing created anywhere, including NOT as a sibling of the parent | unit | fs-browser.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.231] | `createDirectory` rejects a non-existent parent | Throws `not_found` (via the shared `resolveBrowsePath` chokepoint) | unit | fs-browser.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.232] | `createDirectory` rejects a relative parent | Throws `not_absolute` | unit | fs-browser.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.233] | `createDirectory` canonicalizes a symlinked parent, writing into the REAL directory | Confirmed the created path is under the symlink's target, not the symlink itself | unit | fs-browser.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.234] | `createDirectory` defense-in-depth: `path.dirname(target) !== dir` assertion | Documented as theoretically unreachable for a validated single segment — no test exercises the `unsafe` code path directly (dead code / can't construct a failing input given validateFolderName's regex) | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.235] | `createDirectory` uses non-recursive `mkdirSync` (no `{recursive:true}`) | If somehow the target were nested (can't happen given validation) it would throw ENOENT rather than silently creating a chain — no direct test of this defense | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.343] | `pathCrumbs(dir, path.posix)` for a deep POSIX path and for the root | Deep: `/`, then one crumb per segment accumulating the absolute path; root "/": a single `{label:"/", path:"/"}` crumb | unit | fs-browser.test.ts ("builds POSIX crumbs from the root down" / "returns a single crumb at the POSIX root")
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.344] | `pathCrumbs(dir, path.win32)` for a Windows drive path and a bare drive root | Root crumb is the drive (`C:\`) and segments accumulate with backslash separators; a bare `C:\` yields a single crumb | unit | fs-browser.test.ts ("builds Windows crumbs from the drive root with backslash separators" / "returns a single crumb at a Windows drive root")
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.345] | `pathCrumbs("\\\\srv\\share\\proj", path.win32)` (UNC) | The whole `\\srv\share\` share is the root crumb; `proj` is the next segment | unit | fs-browser.test.ts ("keeps the UNC share as the root crumb")
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.346] | `browseParent(dir, p)` up-navigation stops at every OS root | POSIX: parent of `/x/y` is `/x`, of `/` is null; Windows: parent of `C:\Users` is `C:\`, of `C:\` is null; UNC: parent of a `\\srv\share` share root is null (cannot climb above the share) | unit | fs-browser.test.ts ("returns the POSIX parent and null at the root" / "returns the Windows parent and null at the drive root" / "returns null at a UNC share root")

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.238] | `getTargetRoot()` with neither persisted project nor env var | Returns null | unit | target.test.ts

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.241] | `getArchitectureDataPath()` with no target | Returns null | unit | target.test.ts

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.243] | `getProgressLedgerPath()` with no target | Returns null | unit | GAP

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.246] | `isTargetResolved()` when root exists and `.vivicy/canonical` is a real directory | True | unit | target.test.ts
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.247] | `isTargetResolved()` when `.vivicy/canonical` exists but is a FILE | False | unit | target.test.ts

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.361] | `canonicalHasSpecDoc(root)` when the canonical holds only the scaffold seed (`.gitkeep` + `README.md`) | False (README.md and non-.md files never count as authored spec) | unit | target.test.ts ("is false when the canonical holds only the scaffold seed (.gitkeep + README.md)") |
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.362] | `canonicalHasSpecDoc(root)` when the canonical directory cannot be read (readdir throws, e.g. ENOTDIR/EACCES/race ENOENT) | Returns False WITHOUT throwing — the predicate is total so its `/api/map` caller never 500s and the extract guard degrades to a loud actionable refusal, not a raw fs error | unit | target.test.ts ("is false without throwing when the canonical directory cannot be read") |
