### Area cross-notes

- **Import acquisition reports on success only — there is no deferred-report-on-close pattern.** The panel's import view hosts the self-contained `ImportDocsFlow`; the project is acquired the moment `POST /api/project/import` succeeds (the server sets the current project), and the flow calls `onImported(project)` → `onAcquired` exactly once at that point. Because the target is not persisted until submit, closing the panel or hitting Back before submit acquires nothing — there is no window where a half-acquired target must be remembered, so the old "stash the target on pick, report on dialog close" ordering is gone. No automated test drives the panel's import-to-chat transition end-to-end (the component's success wiring is covered in `import-docs-flow.test.tsx`).
- **FolderBrowser is reused by four contexts with different `disabled`/`allowCreate` wiring** (governed open-existing + setup-bar change-project: no `allowCreate`, `disabled=selecting`; import target-pick: `allowCreate` + `requireGoverned={false}`; ScaffoldForm: no `allowCreate`, `disabled=scaffolding`) — no test asserts that only the import target-pick shows the New-folder affordance while the governed-open and scaffold browsers omit it, which is the entire reason the contexts share one component instead of forking it.
- **Scaffold-then-select-current-project chain**: `scaffoldProject` calls `setCurrentProject` internally (lib/scaffold.ts step 5), coupling scaffold.ts to project.ts. No test in scaffold.test.ts mocks `setCurrentProject` to fail (e.g. a describeProject race where the just-created dir vanished between steps) — an unlikely but real TOCTOU gap between scaffold writing files and `setCurrentProject` re-validating the same path.
- **fs-browser.ts and project.ts both implement "must be absolute, existing, real directory" validation independently** (`resolveBrowsePath` vs `describeProject`) with different error-code enums (`FsBrowseError` vs `ProjectError`) — not literally duplicated logic (different concerns: browse-anywhere vs is-a-project-root) but no cross-test proves the two never diverge in what "a directory" means (e.g. one resolving symlinks and the other not: `describeProject` does NOT realpath/resolve symlinks the way `resolveBrowsePath` does — a symlinked project root would describe/persist the SYMLINK path, not the canonical one, which is inconsistent with the browser's canonicalization guarantee). This is a real, non-obvious asymmetry — flagged as a GAP.
- **The scaffold API route trusts the lib entirely for validation ordering** (name validated before target dir, per `scaffoldProject` source order) — the route-level test only proves the codes map correctly, not the actual precedence, which lives untested at the route/integration boundary (only implicitly at the lib level, and not even explicitly there either — see GAP list).
- **No End-to-end (e2e-ui / Playwright) tests were found for this entire onboarding surface** in the read files (only component-level `setup-bar.test.tsx` exists as a Testing-Library integration test). Given the task mentions `e2e/*.spec.ts` exists in the repo, this area's actual E2E coverage should be checked outside this file list — everything marked `e2e-ui` above is a GAP relative to the files in scope, but may be covered by files outside this area (e.g. `e2e/onboarding.spec.ts`) not included in the read set.
- **The import flow and the scaffold/open-project forms share the "server returns non-2xx with a body that still carries domain data" pattern** (verify's red-verdict-is-200 vs apply's would_overwrite-is-409-with-collisions) — these two response shapes are handled by bespoke inline logic in `import-docs-flow.tsx` rather than a shared helper, which is consistent with them being genuinely different contracts, not accidental duplication.

### Adversarial additions

**Correction to the base matrix first**: `e2e/onboarding.spec.ts` DOES exist and DOES cover part of this area — the base matrix's own cross-note flagged this as unverified but left every `e2e-ui` row marked GAP regardless. The corrections are folded into the base rows above ([.128] cites its covering specs; [.182] was already covered by scaffold.test.ts).
- Every OTHER e2e-ui row in this matrix (the open-existing and import choices individually, folder-browser interactions, the import flow, scaffold validation states) is a genuine GAP — only the three-choices-visible assertion and the scaffold happy path have e2e coverage; open-existing, import-documents, and every error/validation/loading state in every flow remain untested at the e2e layer.

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.287] | `onAcquired` throws inside the consumer (e.g. the panel's `onActivity` → `page.tsx` handler throws synchronously) | `ViviOnboarding` provides no try/catch around the callback — an uncaught throw would propagate up through the acquiring flow's success handler during a React state update, potentially in an inconsistent render | unit | GAP

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.288] | `components/project/folder-browser.tsx`: `browse()` called twice in rapid succession (e.g. user double-clicks two different rows before the first `fetch` resolves) | No request cancellation/AbortController — both fetches race; whichever resolves LAST wins and overwrites `listing`, which may not match the last row the user actually clicked | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.289] | `createFolder()` succeeds and navigates into the new folder (`browse(body.path)`), but that second `browse` call itself fails (e.g. permission revoked between mkdir and list) | Toast success for creation already fired; then `browse`'s own error toast fires separately — user sees a success toast immediately followed by an error toast, and the browser is left showing the PRE-creation listing (stale), not the new folder, since `browse` preserves the previous listing on failure | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.290] | `FolderBrowser`'s `open` prop flips true while a PRIOR `browse()` call from before the close is still in flight (unmount/remount race, or open→close→open faster than the fetch resolves) | The effect has no cleanup/cancellation flag; the stale in-flight `browse` can resolve after the new one starts and clobber `listing` with outdated data (last-write-wins, not last-request-wins) | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.291] | `toCrumbs` on a path containing a literal empty segment from a double slash (e.g. `/a//b`, which `path.normalize` would collapse before this ever runs, but the function itself has no input-sanitizing guard) | `.filter((p) => p.length > 0)` already strips empty segments defensively — should be asserted directly since it is the ONLY defense against a double-slash producing a `path:""` breadcrumb | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.292] | `newFolderName` containing only whitespace after some non-whitespace was typed and deleted (e.g. type "abc" then backspace to "   ") | Submit button `disabled={... || newFolderName.trim().length === 0}` correctly disables, but pressing Enter directly (form submit) still fires `createFolder`, which independently re-checks `name.length===0` after trim and no-ops — two independent guards for the same condition, neither tested together | unit | GAP

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.293] | `components/project/import-docs-flow.tsx`: `walkEntry` encounters a FileSystemEntry that is neither `isFile` nor `isDirectory` true (a theoretically-impossible-per-spec but defensively-unhandled third state) | Neither branch executes; the entry is silently dropped with no push to `out` and no error — matches "skip, never fatal" pattern elsewhere but is unverified for this specific entry shape | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.294] | Apply's `would_overwrite` response arrives but `collisions` is present and non-empty while `verdict` on the reverted state was actually never green (defensive: what if body.collisions is a non-array truthy value, e.g. a string) | `applyCollisions: body.code === "would_overwrite" ? body.collisions : undefined` — no runtime type-check that `body.collisions` is actually `string[]`; a malformed/compromised server response would render `.map` over non-array data and crash the panel | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.295] | User selects the SAME zip file twice in a row via the "Choose zip" input (same filename, same bytes) | `event.target.value = ""` reset after each pick means the `onChange` fires again even for the identical File object — re-stages it as a fresh `stagingId`, no de-dup, no warning that this duplicates the prior staged set | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.296] | Drag-and-drop of a MIX of loose files and a folder in the same drop event | `entriesFromDataTransfer` walks all entries (files flatten to `rel:name`, folders recurse to `rel:folder/child`) uniformly in one `Promise.all` — no test exercises a combined heterogeneous drop, only pure-folder or (implicitly) pure-file cases | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.297] | Extremely large file count in one drop/pick (e.g. 5,000 files in a nested folder) | No client-side cap on `entries.length` before building the multipart `FormData` — a huge selection is sent as one `POST /api/upload` with thousands of `files`/`paths` field pairs; no test establishes behavior at this boundary (timeout, memory, or server-side rejection) | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.298] | Verify's `normalized` array is populated (green verdict) but never rendered anywhere in `VerifiedPanel`'s JSX | Dead data the component receives and stores in state but never displays — confirms `normalized` is currently write-only from the UI's perspective (may be intentional but is untested/undocumented as such) | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.299] | `StagedList`/`PlacedList` render when `staged`/`placed` arrays are extremely large (hundreds of entries) | `ScrollArea` is fixed-height (`h-48`); no virtualization — a huge list still renders every row into the DOM at once, untested for performance/jank at scale | e2e-ui | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.300] | Two files staged with IDENTICAL `rel` paths (e.g. case-differing filenames on a case-insensitive OS, or a client bug producing a duplicate) | `StagedList`'s `key={file.rel}` would collide — React would warn/misrender duplicate keys; no test constructs this input | unit | GAP

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.301] | `lib/project.ts` `describeProject`: candidate path that IS a symlink to a valid directory | Unlike `resolveBrowsePath` (`lib/fs-browser.ts`), `describeProject` never calls `realpathSync` — `statSync` follows the link for the existence/directory checks but the RETURNED/PERSISTED `root` is the raw symlink path, not its canonical target. A project opened via a symlinked path and later accessed via its canonical path (or vice versa) would be treated as two different projects by `getCurrentProject`/`readCurrentProjectRoot` string equality. No test constructs a symlinked project root. | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.302] | `setCurrentProject` called concurrently from two requests with different roots (e.g. two browser tabs each picking a different project at nearly the same time) | No lock/mutex around the write; `writeFileSync` is not atomic across concurrent Node event-loop turns handling two API requests — last `POST /api/project` to complete its `writeFileSync` wins, with no version check or conflict signal to the "losing" tab | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.303] | `getCurrentProjectPath()` / `getRuntimeDir()` when the runtime dir path itself is unwritable (permission denied) | `setCurrentProject`'s `mkdirSync(getRuntimeDir(), {recursive:true})` would throw a raw `EACCES` Error (not a `ProjectError`), which the API route maps generically to 500 — never asserted | unit | GAP

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.304] | `lib/scaffold.ts` `scaffoldProject`: `targetDir` resolves to a path where the OWNER'S filesystem denies write permission mid-way through (e.g. `mkdirSync` for one `SKELETON_DIRS` entry succeeds, a later one throws EACCES) | Partial scaffold: some skeleton dirs/files written, then an uncaught throw aborts the rest — `written` is never returned to the caller (the function throws, not returns a partial result), and `setCurrentProject` (step 5) never runs, so the project is NOT set as current despite files existing on disk. No test verifies this partial-failure / no-rollback state. | integration | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.305] | `scaffoldProject` existing-project mode where the pre-existing repo has a `package.json` with `scripts.test` that is an EMPTY string (`""`) | `typeof pkg.scripts.test === "string"` is true even for `""` — `detectGateCommand` would return `"npm test"` even though the actual test script does nothing; no test distinguishes a present-but-empty test script from a real one | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.306] | `scaffoldProject` existing-project mode where BOTH a `package.json` with `scripts.test` AND a `Makefile` with a `test:` target exist | `detectGateCommand` checks `package.json` first and returns immediately — the Makefile is never consulted; no test proves this precedence order (npm wins over make) | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.307] | `renderTemplate`: a replacement VALUE itself contains another token string (e.g. `projectName` literally equal to `"{{GATE_COMMAND}}"`, a pathological but user-controlled input since `validateProjectName` allows dots/spaces/hyphens but the regex doesn't forbid the literal substring `{{...}}` if composed from allowed chars — actually `{` and `}` are NOT in `PROJECT_NAME_RE`'s allowed charset, so this is unreachable for `PROJECT_NAME_TOKEN`, but confirms the regex is the ONLY thing preventing token-injection via project name) | Blocked entirely by `PROJECT_NAME_RE` rejecting `{`/`}` — worth an explicit regression test asserting a name containing braces is rejected as `invalid_name`, since the current tests never try `{` or `}` specifically | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.308] | `vivicyConfig()` / the written `vivicy.json` when `gateCommand` (from `detectGateCommand` or the default) contains characters that would break JSON encoding (e.g. embedded quotes from a hypothetically malicious `package.json` script name) | `JSON.stringify` handles escaping correctly by construction, but no test feeds a `gateCommand`-detection path a pathological script value (e.g. containing `"`, backslash, or newline) to confirm the round-trip stays valid JSON | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.309] | `scaffoldProject` where `target` (from `resolveTargetDir`) is a path exactly at the OS max path length boundary, or a project name that renders into a heading exceeding typical line-length assumptions in the template | Untested boundary; `mkdirSync`/`writeFileSync` could throw `ENAMETOOLONG` uncaught, surfacing as a generic 500 | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.310] | `initFromScratchRepo`: `git init` succeeds, `ensureLocalGitIdentity` runs, `git add -A` succeeds, but the working tree has ZERO files staged after `.gitignore` filtering (a pathological from-scratch scaffold where every written file happened to match an ignore pattern — not reachable today, but the commit step's behavior when `git commit` runs with nothing staged is unverified) | `git commit` would fail (exit non-zero, "nothing to commit") -> `commit.status === 0` is false -> returns `{initialized:true, committed:false}`, silently leaving an initialized-but-uncommitted repo; no test constructs this | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.311] | `scaffoldProject`'s `SKELETON_DIRS` loop and `templateFiles`/`generatedFiles` loops execute in FIXED source order (skeleton dirs, then AGENTS/CLAUDE/README, then vivicy.json/.gitignore) — a test asserting `written` sort-order only checks the FINAL sorted array, not the intermediate write sequence | If a later step depended on an earlier one's side effect beyond file existence (none currently do, but the coupling is implicit/undocumented), a reordering refactor could silently break something with no test catching the sequence change itself | unit | GAP

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.312] | `lib/fs-browser.ts` `resolveBrowsePath`: the resolved `real` (realpath) directory has zero read permission for the current process (`readdirSync` would throw inside `listDirectories`, not `resolveBrowsePath` itself) | `listDirectories` has no try/catch around its own top-level `readdirSync(dir, ...)` call (only around per-entry `statSync` for symlink resolution) — an unreadable target directory throws an uncaught `EACCES`, surfacing as a generic 500 from the route rather than a typed `FsBrowseError` | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.313] | `createDirectory`: `parent` and the newly-created child directory both resolve, but a file/directory with the exact target name is created by ANOTHER process between the `existsSync(target)` check and the `mkdirSync(target)` call (TOCTOU race) | `mkdirSync` (non-recursive, no `{force:true}`) would throw `EEXIST` synchronously, propagating as an uncaught Error rather than the typed `exists` `FsBrowseError` — the check-then-act is not atomic | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.314] | `listDirectories` `.sort()` comparator with names that are pure whitespace or contain unicode combining characters/emoji (extreme non-ASCII folder names) | `localeCompare` with `sensitivity:"base"` behavior at these edges is unverified — worth at least one non-Latin-charset case (e.g. "日本語", "café", "🚀folder") to confirm stable, sensible ordering | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.315] | `FOLDER_NAME_RE` boundary: a name that is exactly the regex's max length (255) but with a MULTI-BYTE unicode character counted by `.length` (JS string length counts UTF-16 code units, not bytes or graphemes) | A name near the 255-char limit built from surrogate-pair characters (e.g. emoji) could have very different actual byte-length on disk than the character count implies — filesystem-level name-length limits (typically 255 BYTES on most filesystems) could reject a name this regex accepts | unit | GAP

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.316] | `lib/target.ts` `getTargetRoot`: BOTH a persisted current-project AND `VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT` are set simultaneously | Confirmed as GAP already in the base matrix (#236) — adding: the precedence is asserted nowhere across ANY test file in this area, not even indirectly; this is a one-line test (`setCurrentProject` + set env var + assert `getTargetRoot()` returns the persisted one) that is trivially addable and currently completely absent | unit | GAP (duplicate-flagging for emphasis — base matrix under-weighted this as just another row rather than calling it out as critical)
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.317] | `getProgressLedgerPath()` is entirely untested (zero references in target.test.ts) despite being a public exported function alongside `getArchitectureDataPath` which IS tested identically | Both target-set and no-target-set cases are GAP; this is a straightforward parity gap (one function tested, its structural twin is not) | unit | GAP

- [onboarding-project-scaffold.318] | `app/api/project/route.ts` `POST`: body's `root` field present as a string but containing only a Unicode zero-width space or other whitespace-like-but-not-`\s`-matched character | `.trim()` in JS trims standard whitespace per spec (includes most Unicode space separators via `String.prototype.trim`), but no test confirms behavior with something like `​` (zero-width space, NOT trimmed by `.trim()`) — could pass the `.trim().length===0` guard as non-empty and reach `setCurrentProject` with a garbage "path" | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.319] | `app/api/project/scaffold/route.ts` and `app/api/project/route.ts`: neither route checks `Content-Type` header or method — a GET-only or wrong-content-type POST is not exercised | Next.js route handlers dispatch purely by exported function name (`POST`), so a non-JSON `Content-Type` POST would still hit `request.json()` and likely throw, caught by `.catch(()=>null)` — behaves like a malformed body, but no test sends a non-JSON content type explicitly | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.320] | `app/api/fs/list/route.ts` `GET`: `?path=` supplied multiple times (e.g. `?path=/a&path=/b`, a malformed/duplicate query) | `URLSearchParams.get` returns only the FIRST value silently — no test confirms this is the actual behavior relied upon (vs. `getAll` or last-wins in some parsers) | unit | GAP
- [onboarding-project-scaffold.321] scaffold-dialog `targetPreview` next-intl rich-text: the catalog message's `<target>{targetDir}</target>` tag must match the `t.rich` handler names (tag → chunk handler, `targetDir` → plain value) | the preview renders the styled span with the path, zero React console errors (regression 2026-07-05: a plain `{targetDir}` argument fed a function value rendered a function as React child — caught only by the browser-issues gate during the Mode B onboarding spec, invisible to the passing tests) | unit | GAP (indirectly guarded by the e2e browser-issue gate on onboarding.spec.ts; no unit test pins the tag/handler contract — the known next-intl rich-text tag/handler-mismatch footgun class)

### Area cross-notes (additional)

- **The base matrix significantly under-verified its own e2e-ui GAP claims.** Every row labeled `e2e-ui | GAP` was asserted without checking whether `e2e/*.spec.ts` files actually exist for this area, despite the task's own file list implying they should be checked. `e2e/onboarding.spec.ts` exists and does cover the four-cards-visible assertion and the full Mode-B (scaffold) happy-path flow end to end — those two rows are corrected above from GAP to COVERED. All other e2e-ui rows remain genuine gaps (no e2e coverage found for Mode A/import/Vivi-chat paths, folder-browser interactions, or any dialog's error/validation states).
- **No test anywhere in this area exercises TWO concurrent requests to the same mutating endpoint** (`POST /api/project`, `POST /api/project/scaffold`, `POST /api/fs/mkdir`) — every mutation (current-project write, scaffold write, mkdir) is check-then-act without locking, and the whole area assumes a single interactive local user, but nothing documents or tests that assumption's boundary (e.g. two browser tabs of the same Vivicy instance).
- **`describeProject`'s symlink asymmetry (finding #302/#301 above) is the single most concrete, easily-reproducible gap in the whole area** — unlike most "theoretical" TOCTOU/race gaps, this one is deterministic and trivial to write a test for (symlink a temp dir, call `describeProject` on the link, assert `root` against `realpathSync`), and it directly touches the persisted `current-project.json` contract that every other route/component in this area depends on.
- **Every `detectGateCommand`/scaffold precedence rule (package.json before Makefile, empty-string test script counting as detected) is fully unverified** — the function has real decision branches (`return` early on package.json even if it's a hollow signal) that no test distinguishes from "detected a real thing."

---

**Total case count: 320 (282 base + 38 adversarial additions)**
**GAP count: 216 (196 base, minus 2 corrected to COVERED, plus 22 net new adversarial GAP rows — note items 283–320 are 38 new rows, 2 of which are duplicate-emphasis call-outs on existing base GAPs rather than net-new distinct cases, so distinct new GAP rows = 36; 196 − 2 + 36 = 230; see recount note below)**

**Recount note**: precise arithmetic — base GAP count 196, minus 2 rows corrected from GAP to COVERED (#86, #128/182) = 194, plus 36 net-new distinct adversarial GAP rows (#283–320 excluding the two explicitly marked "duplicate-flagging for emphasis" at #316) = **230 GAP rows out of 320 total cases**.

## Area: pipeline-notifications-agents-ui

### components/pipeline/pipeline-stages.ts

- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.1] PIPELINE_STAGES contains exactly the 14 stages S0..S12 with SK inserted between S7 and S8, in that order | array equals the fixed id sequence | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.2] The non_loop/dev_loop boundary sits exactly between S1 and S2 | S0,S1 side=non_loop; S2..S12 side=dev_loop | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.3] Only S6, SK, S9 carry a `retryStage` (extract/skills/dev respectively), nothing else | retryable stage id list equals ["S6","SK","S9"] | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.4] No stage object carries a `label` property (labels live in i18n catalog, not this module) | `"label" in stage` is false for every stage | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.5] Each stage's `marker` matches the stage typing table exactly (user/agent/mixed per stage) | full marker map equals expected dict | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.6] MARKER_GLYPH has an entry for every marker kind (user 🖥️, agent 🤖, mixed 🖥️🤖) | glyph lookup never undefined for a valid marker | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.7] deriveStageStates(null, null) with no skills arg returns every stage "pending" | all 14 values === "pending" | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.8] deriveStageStates(null, null, null) explicit-null skills also returns all pending (no crash on null third arg) | no throw, SK "pending" | unit | GAP (only the 2-arg null/null case and the explicit-null-third-arg-with-non-null-extraction case are tested, not null/null/null together)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.9] reachedDevLoop is true and S0/S1 flip green when extraction is non-null even with phase absent (e.g. `{}`) | S0/S1 green even if `.phase` is undefined | unit | GAP (test only exercises `{ phase: "green" }`, not an extraction object with no phase key)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.10] reachedDevLoop is true when extraction is null but status.issues_total > 0 (dev status outlived extraction file) | S0/S1 green from status alone | unit | GAP (only extraction-driven branch of `reachedDevLoop` is tested; the `status?.issues_total ?? 0) > 0` OR-branch has no direct test)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.11] status.issues_total exactly 0 with extraction null keeps S0/S1 pending (boundary at 0) | S0/S1 stay "pending" | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.12] extraction.phase === "green" marks S2..S6 all green | S2,S3,S4,S5,S6 === "green" | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.13] extraction.phase === "extraction_blocked" marks S2-S5 green, S6 red | exact set | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.14] extraction.phase === "blocked_on_unverified_spikes" marks S2 green, S3 red, S4-S6 left pending (proving-before-freeze order) | exact set | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.15] Each EXTRACTION_RUNNING_PHASES phase individually (authoring, fixing, refreezing, validating, mapping, verifying, map-review) resolves to the correct running stage via phaseToRunningStage | authoring/fixing/validating/verifying→S6 running; refreezing→S4 running; mapping/map-review→S5 running | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts (authoring, refreezing, mapping, map-review covered explicitly; fixing, validating, verifying NOT explicitly asserted)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.16] "fixing" phase specifically pulses S6 running (not just "authoring") | S6 === "running" | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.17] "validating" phase specifically pulses S6 running | S6 === "running" | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.18] "verifying" phase specifically pulses S6 running | S6 === "running" | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.19] A running phase mapped by phaseToRunningStage does NOT overwrite itself as green in the "earlier stages" backfill loop (id !== runningStage guard) | the running stage itself stays "running", not clobbered to "green" | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts (implicitly, via S6 running assertion)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.20] An unrecognized/unknown extraction phase string (not in EXTRACTION_RUNNING_PHASES, not green/extraction_blocked/blocked_on_unverified_spikes) leaves all extraction-derived stages untouched (pending, unless already set by reachedDevLoop) | S2..S6 stay "pending" | unit | GAP (only the skills-side has an "unknown phase" test; extraction-side has none)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.21] applyExtractionStates is a no-op (early return) when extraction is null or extraction.phase is falsy/empty string | states unchanged | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts (covered via the "everything pending" and null-argument cases)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.22] SK stays "pending" with no skills report (third arg omitted or explicit null), even when extraction is green | SK === "pending" | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.23] SK pulses "running" for each of the 3 SKILLS_RUNNING_PHASES individually (selecting, auditing, installing) | SK === "running" for all three | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts (loop covers all three)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.24] SK is "green" on skills.phase === "green" | SK === "green" | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.25] SK is "green" on skills.phase === "skipped" (honest "nothing to install", not a failure) | SK === "green" | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.26] SK is "red" on skills.phase === "failed" | SK === "red" | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.27] An unrecognized skills.phase string leaves SK "pending" rather than guessing | SK === "pending" | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.28] applyDevStates is a no-op when status is null | no crash, dev-derived stages stay whatever the other two functions set | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts (implicit in "everything pending" test)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.29] status.issues_total === 0 (hasIssues false) short-circuits applyDevStates entirely, even if run_active is true | S7..S12 unaffected/stay pending | unit | GAP (boundary: issues_total=0 with run_active=true is not tested — only issues_total=0 as part of "no status" cases)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.30] hasIssues true (issues_total=1, the minimal boundary) with extraction never reporting (extraction null) marks S7 green defensively | S7 === "green" even though extraction argument is null | unit | GAP (test always pairs status with `{phase:"green"}`; the "extraction never reported" defensive branch for S7 is never isolated)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.31] If S7 is already non-pending (e.g. green from extraction phase=green) applyDevStates does NOT downgrade or re-touch it | S7 stays whatever extraction set (idempotent guard `if (states.S7 === "pending")`) | unit | GAP (no test verifies the guard actually prevents overwrite — current tests never conflict on S7)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.32] status.run_active true pulses S8 and S9 running regardless of done/total ratio (0 done, partial done, or fully done-but-still-marked-active) | S8/S9 === "running" | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts (only the partial-done case is tested; done===0 and done===total-while-active are GAPs)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.33] run_active true with issues_done === 0 marks S10 "pending" (not green) — done>0 boundary | S10 === "pending" | unit | GAP (code branch `done > 0 ? "green" : "pending"` only tested at done=2; done=0 boundary untested)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.34] run_active true with issues_done > 0 marks S10 "green" | S10 === "green" | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts (done:2 case asserts S8/S9 running but does not explicitly assert S10 green in that test — verify)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.35] Not running, gates.fail > 0, and done < total marks S9 "red" (S8/S10 untouched/pending) | S9==="red" | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.36] Not running, gates.fail > 0, but done >= total does NOT mark S9 red (the "done < total" guard) — falls through to the done>=total green branch instead | S9 === "green", not "red" | unit | GAP (boundary interaction between the two mutually-exclusive-looking conditions is untested)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.37] Not running, no failing gate, done >= total (total>0) marks S8,S9,S10 green and S12 green | exact set | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.38] Partial completion, not running, no failing gate (0 < done < total) leaves S8,S9,S10,S12 all pending — never fabricated green (P1, "idle" state) | all pending | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.39] S11 (CRs) is NEVER set by deriveStageStates under any input combination — it has no observable signal in this derivation | S11 always "pending" regardless of status/extraction/skills | unit | pipeline-stages.test.ts (asserted for the done>=total case; not asserted across other branches, e.g. red/running paths — minor gap)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.40] done === total === 0 (total is 0, so hasIssues is false) never reaches the S12 green branch (`total > 0` guard) | S12 stays "pending" | unit | GAP (explicit 0/0 boundary for the S12 guard is untested; only exercised implicitly via "no status" cases)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.41] Huge issue counts (e.g. issues_total: 100000, issues_done: 100000) still resolve done>=total correctly (no overflow/precision issue) | S8,S9,S10,S12 green | unit | GAP

### components/pipeline/pipeline-widget.tsx

- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.43] Widget renders all 14 stage nodes (`[data-stage="…"]`) including SK when `open` | all 14 `data-stage` nodes present | e2e-ui | pipeline-widget.test.tsx
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.44] The non_loop/dev_loop boundary marker (`[data-boundary]`) renders exactly between the S1 and S2 nodes in DOM order | order[boundaryIndex-1]==="S1", order[boundaryIndex+1]==="S2" | e2e-ui | pipeline-widget.test.tsx
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.236] The opened strip is clamped to the card (`max-w-full` on the inner content div): the strip's `shrink-0` chips have a ~1100px min-content width which would otherwise size the item PAST the outer `max-w` (intrinsic sizing ignores descendants' max-width) — on a phone that overflow makes mobile Chromium ENLARGE the layout viewport (innerWidth 412 → 768) and flip `useIsMobile()`/`md:` to desktop mid-session | opened strip scrolls inside `overflow-x-auto`; `window.innerWidth` stays at device width | e2e-ui | GAP (the widget is trigger-less/dormant in the app so this real-browser viewport guard is no longer reachable in-app; the clamp + its invariant comment survive for a future host that mounts the widget `open`, and must be re-covered by an e2e then)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.45] No boundary marker renders anywhere else in the strip (only one dashed separator, between S1/S2) | exactly one `[data-boundary]` node | e2e-ui | GAP (test checks position of the one boundary found, not that there is exactly one / none elsewhere)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.46] ChevronRight separator renders between consecutive non-boundary stages (index>0 && !boundary) | chevrons present between stage pairs other than the boundary pair | e2e-ui | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.241] Trigger-less/controlled: with no `open` (or `open={false}`) the widget renders nothing (`[data-pipeline-widget]` absent) and opens no status stream (no EventSource, no report fetch) — dormant until a host drives `open` | container absent; FakeEventSource never constructed | e2e-ui | pipeline-widget.test.tsx ("trigger-less: renders nothing and opens no status stream when not open")
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.52] `[data-stage-state]` reflects "running" for S6 during an authoring extraction phase | attribute === "running" | e2e-ui | pipeline-widget.test.tsx
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.53] `[data-stage-state]` reflects "red" for S6 on extraction_blocked | attribute === "red" | e2e-ui | pipeline-widget.test.tsx

- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.55] S9 shows red on a failing gate mid-run (not running, done<total, gates.fail>0) | attribute === "red" | e2e-ui | pipeline-widget.test.tsx
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.56] While `open`, the widget fires fetchReports for BOTH /api/control/extract and /api/control/skills as soon as the effect engages, before any SSE frame arrives | both endpoints hit at least once once open | integration | GAP (fetch calls are stubbed/asserted only for retry-stage POSTs and only indirectly for the report fetches; no test explicitly counts the initial fetchReports calls)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.57] While `open`, fetchReports is polled every POLL_INTERVAL_MS (10s) via setInterval, independent of SSE activity | fetch call count increases over fake-timer advances | integration | GAP (no fake-timer test advances 10s+ to prove the poll interval fires)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.58] While `open`, every incoming SSE message also triggers an extra fetchReports call (`void fetchReports()` inside `onmessage`) | fetch(extract)/fetch(skills) called again after each `emit` | integration | GAP (widget test emits many times but never asserts fetch call *counts* increase per emit — only end-state attributes are asserted)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.59] A non-ok response or `body.ok === false` from /api/control/extract leaves the previous `extraction` state untouched (best-effort fetch failure) | stale/last-known extraction retained, no crash | integration | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.60] A network-level throw from fetch (extract or skills) is swallowed by the try/catch, leaving prior state | no unhandled rejection, no crash | integration | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.61] A malformed (non-JSON) SSE `event.data` payload is caught and the widget keeps its last known `status` (try/catch around JSON.parse) | no crash, prior status retained | integration | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.62] An SSE frame carrying `{error: "..."}` is ignored (early return before setStatus) | status unchanged, no state flip on an error frame | integration | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.63] EventSource is closed (source.close()) on unmount (cleanup return in the SSE effect) | `FakeEventSource.last.closed === true` after unmount | integration | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.64] setInterval poll timer is cleared on unmount (no leaked timer double-fetching after the component unmounts) | clearInterval called | integration | GAP

- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.66] Clicking Retry on SK opens confirm dialog and POSTs `{stage:"skills"}` | POST body === `{stage:"skills"}` | e2e-ui | pipeline-widget.test.tsx

### components/chat/vivi-notifications.tsx (useNotificationsFeed + NotificationsFeed — the bell/center are retired; the panel's Notifications tab is the ONLY notification surface)

- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.215] `useNotificationsFeed()` runs ALWAYS (panel open OR closed, so the launcher can carry a closed-panel attention badge): initial GET /api/control/notifications + GET /api/control/crs in parallel, a 10s poll (the fallback for app-side emissions the dev-status frame cannot reflect), and an SSE-signature-triggered refetch — pending CRs ride the SAME triggers (folded in from the retired per-CrReviewCards fetch, so the CR cards + their count never go stale) | unconditional fetch/poll; notifications + CRs share reload triggers | integration | GAP (no fake-timer advance test for the 10s poll loop; the always-on fetch is exercised via every panel test and the closed-panel badge test)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.216] First SSE frame after (re)subscribing only records the signature baseline — no refetch; a CHANGED signature (issues done/total, gates.fail, run_active) triggers an immediate refetch (P9) | GET count increments only on a changed later frame | integration | vivi-panel.test.tsx ("a dev-status signature change on the SSE stream refetches the feed")
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.217] An identical signature repeated across frames causes no additional refetch; a malformed frame or one carrying `{error}` is ignored without breaking the stream handler | no churn, no throw | integration | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.218] Re-subscription on reopen resets `lastSignatureRef` to null first, so a reopen never refetches off a stale signature from the previous stream; the EventSource is closed and the poll cleared on deactivation/unmount | fresh baseline per subscription; `source.close()` called | integration | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.219] `visibleNotifications` filters `dismissed`, returns newest-first without mutating the input (`.slice().reverse()`) — the sole visible-list rule shared by the tab badge and the feed | order + filter | unit | vivi-panel.test.tsx ("the tab shows the undismissed count and the feed lists newest first")
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.220] The Notifications tab trigger carries a destructive Badge with the ATTENTION count (undismissed notifications + pending CRs, so it can never disagree with the launcher badge) and the plural-aware `unreadAriaLabel`; zero renders no badge; > 99 renders `unreadOverflow` | badge presence/text per combined count | unit | vivi-panel.test.tsx (count + aria label; the closed-panel badge test asserts the notifications+CRs sum on both surfaces) — the >99 overflow flavor is GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.221] Per-item dismiss POSTs `{id}` to /api/control/notifications, then `onReload` re-fetches — the row leaves the feed and the badge decrements (single source of truth: the server log) | exact POST body; row + badge update | e2e-ui | vivi-panel.test.tsx ("dismissing one notification POSTs its id and drops it from feed + badge")
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.222] "Clear all" behind the AlertDialog confirm POSTs `{all:true}` and the feed collapses to the empty state | exact POST body; empty copy renders | e2e-ui | vivi-panel.test.tsx ("clear all confirms, POSTs { all: true }, and empties the feed")
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.223] LEVEL_ICON per level: error→destructive CircleAlert, warning/warn→TriangleAlert styled `text-warning`, success→primary CircleCheck, info→muted Info, unknown/missing level→info fallback; error rows additionally get the destructive border | icon + styling per level | unit | GAP (the `--warning`/`--color-warning` token pair is defined in `app/globals.css` and resolves — verified live)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.224] `relativeTime` buckets (just now / Nm / Nh / Nd), raw-ts fallback on an unparsable string, and the em-dash for a missing ts (ported verbatim from the retired center) | exact strings per bucket | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.225] Each row's compact "Ask Vivi" ghost button hands the DISPLAYED (translated-or-fallback) text to `onAskVivi` — the panel pre-fills the composer from the i18n template and the USER presses send (no auto-send) | composer value = template with the row text; zero /api/vivi POSTs | e2e-ui | vivi-panel.test.tsx ("Ask Vivi lands on the Chat tab with the composer pre-filled — the user presses send")
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.226] A row with neither `id` nor `ts` (malformed legacy line) renders keyed by index WITHOUT a dismiss button (`dismissRef` undefined) | no crash, no ghost control | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.227] Row text resolves through `notificationText(t, stage, event, message)` — a mapped `events.<stage>.<event>` key renders the catalog string with the BARE `{id}` interpolated (the extraction regex is LAZY, so "ISS-0004: gate red" never renders a double colon), an unmapped pair falls back to the raw stored message | both flavors | unit | vivi-panel.test.tsx ("a mapped stage/event renders the catalog text with the bare id — never a double colon" + the unmapped fixtures in every other feed test)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.228] Dismiss/clear pending state: the in-flight row's X (or the Clear-all button) disables while `pending` matches; other rows stay operable | per-item pending, not a global lock | e2e-ui | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.237] Dismiss/clear-all failure path: a non-ok response OR a thrown fetch from the shared `post()` helper sets an inline `t("updateFailed")` error rendered as a `role="status"` destructive Marker above the list (never a silent no-op — a row that fails to dismiss would otherwise reappear on the next poll with no explanation) | inline role=status error on failure; `pending` still clears in `finally` | unit | GAP — no test drives a failing dismiss/clear-all POST
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.238] `fetchNotifications`/`fetchCrs` each fail independently (try/catch → null, `Array.isArray`/`body.ok` guards) inside the shared feed hook — a thrown fetch, malformed JSON, or a non-array payload on ONE endpoint leaves its own state at the last-known value without blanking the OTHER endpoint's data (the CR list and the notification list can never take each other down) | one endpoint's outage never blanks the other's last-known list | unit | GAP — no test isolates a single-endpoint failure while the other succeeds

### components/agents/agents-gate.tsx (+ agent-status.tsx — prerequisite gate)

- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.229] `agentsGateBlocked`: true when EITHER (or both) CLI binary is `present:false`; false when both present regardless of auth (present-but-unauthenticated and auth-unknown are NOT blocking) | exact blocking rule | unit | agents-gate.test.tsx ("blocks when either (or both) CLI binary is missing" + "present-but-unauthenticated (or auth unknown) is NOT blocking")
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.230] Both missing: the full-screen gate renders the brand illustration (the shared `InsiemeIllustration` atom — `next/image` of `/brand/3.small/insieme_sm.png`, intrinsic 500×313 rendered ~224px, alt from `common.insiemeAlt` = "La Nonna and il Nonno") above the explanation, then one Card per agent laid out table-like — the CLI name left and its status badge on the SAME row far right (shadcn `CardAction`; header grid switches to `1fr auto`) — with the harmonized guidance line + labeled external install-docs link (`AGENT_GUIDANCE.docsUrl`, `target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"`, sr-only "(opens in new tab)" cue) centered in the card; NO install command and NO copy button anywhere on the page (install commands change too often to pin; the docs are authoritative) | illustration present (alt); each name⟷badge on one row inside `[data-slot=card-action]`; both docs links (exact href + new-tab attrs), centered; no command, no copy button | unit | agents-gate.test.tsx ("both missing: per-agent cards link to each CLI's install docs in a new tab — no command, no copy button")
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.231] One missing: the present agent shows its version suffix + Installed/auth badges on the name row (`CardAction`) and NO docs link; only the missing agent gets the centered guidance line + install-docs link | selective guidance; present-agent badges on the header row | unit | agents-gate.test.tsx ("one missing: the present agent shows its version/auth rows and no docs link; only the missing one links to its install docs")
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.232] "Check again" fetches `/api/agents/health?fresh=1` (bypassing the route memo) and hands the fresh snapshot up via `onHealth`; the button shows the checking spinner and disables while in flight | exact URL + onHealth payload | unit | agents-gate.test.tsx ("Check again re-probes with ?fresh=1 and hands the fresh snapshot up")
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.233] A failed/thrown re-probe keeps the last snapshot (best-effort) and re-enables the button in `finally` | no crash, retryable | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.234] `AgentsAuthBanner`: one line per agent that is present AND `authenticated === false`, each with its exact auth command in a `<code>`; the Alert/icon carry `border-warning/50`/`text-warning` classes (same intended token as vivi-notifications.tsx's LEVEL_ICON warning color); the X dismisses the banner (local state); nothing renders when no agent is verifiably signed out — auth-unknown (`null`) never triggers the banner (honesty: never fabricate "not signed in") | per-agent lines, dismiss, null-excluded | unit | agents-gate.test.tsx ("lists each signed-out agent with its auth command and dismisses on X" + "renders nothing when every present agent is authenticated (or auth is unknown)")
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.235] `agent-status.tsx` holds the SHARED atoms, one source of truth each: `AgentStatusBadge` (badge three-way ok/bad/unknown) rendered by both the gate and the setup dialog; `InstallDocsLink` (guidance line + labeled external docs link — `target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"`, `ExternalLink` icon aria-hidden + sr-only "(opens in new tab)", shadcn `Button variant="link"` focus-visible ring; optional `className` passthrough lets each surface set alignment without forking — the gate centers it via `items-center text-center`, the dialog keeps default left alignment) rendered by both surfaces for a not-installed agent; `CopyableCommand` (copy-to-clipboard block with success/failure toasts, keyboard-focusable scrollable `<code>` via `tabIndex={0}` + `commandScrollAriaLabel` + focus ring) now rendered ONLY by the dialog's sign-in branch — the install command was removed from every surface | shared rendering, no divergence; docs link is the only install affordance; copy block is sign-in-only | unit | GAP (InstallDocsLink's href/new-tab attrs are asserted in agents-gate.test.tsx; the CopyableCommand copy click + command focusability remain without a dedicated assertion — see .135/.136)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.239] `AgentsGate`'s outer scroller uses `items-start` + `m-auto` (not `items-center`): centering would clip the TOP of gate content taller than the viewport unreachably (a short window or 200% zoom), while auto margins only center when there is room to spare | the top of tall gate content stays scrollable into view rather than clipped | unit | GAP — layout-only, no viewport-constrained render test exists

### components/agents/agents-health-dialog.tsx

- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.240] AgentsHealthDialog renders NO internal trigger — the setup-bar "Agents" chip and its `overallState` status dot were removed by owner order; the dialog is now controlled via optional `open`/`onOpenChange` props (defaulting closed), while the mount-effect still probes `/api/agents/health` and fires `onWarning` even while the dialog stays closed, so the health side-effect and the "never fabricate green" honesty (auth===null surfaces a warning, never a ready state) survive the removed trigger | no chip / `data-agents-state` element in the DOM; passing `open` renders the dialog content; a not-ready health payload invokes `onWarning` on mount | unit | agents-health-dialog.test.tsx (opens the dialog via the `open` prop) + setup-bar.test.tsx ("still probes agent health on mount and surfaces a warning")

### components/transcript/transcript-modal.tsx

- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.154] useTranscript() throws a clear Error when called outside a TranscriptProvider | throws "useTranscript must be used within a TranscriptProvider" | unit | GAP (no test file exists for this component at all)
