- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.156] TranscriptProvider.open(ref) with no title AND ref has no "/" falls back to the full ref itself as the title | title === ref | unit | GAP (boundary: `"".slice(-1)[0]` when ref has no slash — split returns single-element array, `?? ref` guard covers a theoretical empty-string edge)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.157] TranscriptProvider.open(ref, title) with an explicit title uses that title verbatim, ignoring path derivation | title === explicit param | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.158] Dialog is closed (`target === null`) initially, and the closed state renders a placeholder header ("Transcript" / "—") instead of the TranscriptBody | placeholder header content | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.159] Calling open() sets the dialog open with `target !== null`, rendering TranscriptBody keyed by `target.ref` | dialog opens, body mounts | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.160] Closing the dialog (onOpenChange(false)) resets `target` to null | subsequent render shows the placeholder again | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.161] Opening a NEW ref while a transcript is already open remounts TranscriptBody fresh (the `key={target.ref}` forces remount) — the loading state resets to "loading" rather than showing stale entries momentarily | loading state shown fresh, not previous entries | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.162] TranscriptBody starts in "loading" status and shows "Loading transcript…" before the fetch resolves | loading text renders first | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.163] A successful fetch + non-empty parseTranscript result shows "ok" status with `"{format} · {N} entries"` meta text and renders each entry | meta text + entry count match | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.164] A successful fetch that parses to a `format === ""` (empty format) omits the "format · " prefix from the meta text, showing only "{N} entries" | meta string has no leading "· " artifact | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.165] A successful fetch with ZERO parsed entries shows "No renderable entries." rather than an empty list with no explanation | empty-entries message renders | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.166] A non-ok HTTP response (`!r.ok`) throws `Error("HTTP {status}")`, caught into "error" status showing "Could not load transcript: {error}" | error text includes the HTTP status | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.167] A network-level fetch rejection is caught the same way, landing in "error" status with the thrown error's string representation | error text present | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.168] Unmounting (or switching `target.ref`) before the fetch resolves does not call `setState` on the stale request — the `alive` flag guard prevents a state update racing a newer ref's load | no crossed-wires state update from an in-flight stale fetch | integration | GAP (this is a real race-condition safeguard in the code with zero test coverage)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.169] RichText splits fenced code blocks (```lang\n...```) from surrounding prose, rendering each code block as a collapsed `<details>` with a summary showing `code · {lang} · {N} lines`, and lang omitted from the summary when no language tag is present | correct split + summary text per fence | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.170] RichText with NO fenced code at all renders the whole text as a single prose `<p>`, trimmed | single paragraph, trimmed content | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.171] RichText with a fence that never closes (unterminated ` ``` `) — the regex requires a closing fence, so an unterminated block is NOT treated as code and stays as literal prose text | text renders as-is, not a broken/dangling <details> | unit | GAP (boundary: malformed markdown input)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.172] RichText with MULTIPLE code fences in one text interleaves prose paragraphs and code `<details>` blocks in original order | correct ordering of mixed segments | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.173] EntryView "thinking" kind renders a collapsed `<details>` with a "Thinking" badge and a char-count of `entry.text.length` | badge + count text | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.174] EntryView "tool" kind renders name, Input (raw `entry.input`), and Output ONLY when `entry.output !== undefined` (absent output shows no Output section at all — distinct from an empty-string output, which DOES render) | Output section presence tied to `!== undefined`, not truthiness | unit | GAP (subtle boundary: empty string output vs undefined output)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.175] EntryView "tool" output longer than 4000 chars is truncated to the first 4000 chars plus a "… (truncated)" suffix; exactly 4000 chars shows no truncation suffix (boundary) | 4000-char boundary honored exactly | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.176] EntryView "user" kind renders a left-bordered block with a "User" label and RichText body | correct structure | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.177] EntryView assistant kind (not thinking/tool/user) with `entry.final === true` gets primary-bordered styling and "Final response" label; `entry.final` falsy gets default styling and "Assistant" label | conditional class + label pair | unit | GAP

### factory/notify.ts

- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.178] notify() with no VIVICY_RUNTIME_DIR set and no options.runtimeDir returns false and writes nothing (strict no-op contract) | returns false, no file created | unit | notify.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.179] notify() with a runtimeDir but missing `stage` returns false (guard `!stage`) | returns false | unit | GAP (only the "no runtime dir" no-op is tested; missing-stage and missing-event guards are untested individually)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.180] notify() with a runtimeDir but missing `event` returns false (guard `!event`) | returns false | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.181] notify() with runtimeDir, stage, and event all present but `message` omitted falls back to using `event` as the message (`String(message ?? event)`) | written line's `message` field equals the `event` string | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.182] notify() with `level` omitted defaults to `"info"` | written line's `level` === "info" | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.183] notify() creates the runtime directory recursively if it does not yet exist (`mkdirSync(..., {recursive:true})`) | directory created, line appended | unit | notify.test.ts (implicitly, since mkdtempSync already creates the dir — the recursive-create-if-missing branch itself is not explicitly exercised with a NON-existent nested dir)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.184] Two notify() calls append two distinct lines to notifications.jsonl (one per line, valid JSON each) with all contract fields present (id, ts, level, stage, event, message) | 2 parsed lines, all fields truthy | unit | notify.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.185] Two notify() calls in immediate succession produce different `id`s even at the same millisecond (the `counter` module-level tie-breaker) | ids differ | unit | notify.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.186] stampId format is `{pid-base36}-{ms-base36}-{counter-base36}` — different processes (different pid) would never collide even at identical ms+counter (not directly testable in-process, but the format itself is inspectable) | id matches the 3-part base36 pattern | unit | GAP (format itself, e.g. via regex match on a produced id, is never asserted — only inequality between two ids)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.187] notify() never throws on an unwritable directory path (e.g. `/dev/null/nope`) and returns false | returns false, no throw | unit | notify.test.ts
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.188] notify()'s custom `now` option controls the `ts` field (ISO string derived from the injected epoch-ms), enabling deterministic tests | `ts` matches `new Date(injectedMs).toISOString()` | unit | GAP (the test passes `now: () => Date.now()` — a real clock — never a fixed injected value, so the ts-derivation-from-now is not actually pinned/verified)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.189] The appended line ends with exactly one `\n` per entry (no double-newlines, no missing trailing newline breaking the next append) | `readFileSync(...).split("\n")` yields exactly N non-empty lines for N calls | unit | notify.test.ts (implicit via `.trim().split("\n")`)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.190] `options.runtimeDir` takes precedence over `process.env.VIVICY_RUNTIME_DIR` when both are set | writes to the explicit option's dir, not the env var's | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.191] Empty-string `stage` or `event` (falsy but not undefined) is treated the same as absent — still a no-op (the `!stage`/`!event` checks catch `""` too) | returns false for `stage: ""` | unit | GAP (boundary: empty string vs undefined for the guard clauses)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.192] `existsSync` check avoids a redundant `mkdirSync` call when the runtime dir already exists (no throw from mkdir-on-existing-dir, though `recursive:true` would tolerate it anyway) | no error even across repeated notify() calls into the same dir | unit | notify.test.ts (implicit, since the 2-call test reuses the same mkdtemp'd dir)

### factory/notify.test.ts

- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.193] Existing suite structure: confirms the no-runtime-dir no-op, the 2-line append-with-unique-ids happy path, and the never-throws-on-unwritable-dir guarantee | test file exits green | unit | n/a

### Area cross-notes

- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.194] End-to-end chain: `factory/notify.ts` writes a line to the per-project notifications.jsonl → the app's read contract (`lib/notifications.ts`, outside this area) surfaces it via `/api/control/notifications` → `useNotificationsFeed` polls/SSE-triggers a refetch → the panel's Notifications tab renders it newest-first and dismiss round-trips back to the same file. No test in this area exercises the full chain from an actual `notify()` call through to a rendered tab badge — the feed tests stub the HTTP layer entirely, and notify.test.ts never touches the HTTP/React side | GAP (integration test spanning factory → API → UI does not exist in this area)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.195] `PipelineWidget` and the panel's `useNotificationsFeed` both independently open their own `EventSource("/api/status/stream")`, but only the feed applies the "skip first frame" / signature-debounce discipline (tested); the widget's analogous first-SSE-frame behavior (it always calls `fetchReports()` on every message, no skip-first logic) is architecturally different and that asymmetry itself is undocumented/untested — worth flagging: is the widget's "fetch on every SSE frame" intentional given the feed deliberately debounces via signature comparison? | GAP (architectural inconsistency between two SSE-consuming components in the same area, no test locks in the intended behavior for either)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.196] Both `PipelineWidget`'s retry-stage flow and `AgentsHealthDialog`'s update flow follow the same allowlisted-server-side-exec pattern (POST to a fixed route, server execs a fixed command) — no test in this area verifies the CLIENT never sends attacker-controllable stage/agent values beyond the fixed enum (e.g. an arbitrary string for `stage` or `agent`); client-side guards rely entirely on the retryStage/agentKey TypeScript union types with no runtime validation before the fetch body is constructed | GAP (type-only enforcement, no runtime allowlist check client-side — server-side enforcement is out of this area's file list but worth a security-review cross-check)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.197] `TranscriptProvider`/`useTranscript` and `CrReviewCards` (mounted inside the panel's Notifications tab) are both "always-mounted" providers/singletons in the app shell, yet neither has any test in this area verifying they coexist without ref/context collisions (e.g. opening a transcript modal while the panel's notifications tab with CR review is also open) | GAP (no cross-component concurrent-modal test)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.198] `AgentsHealthDialog`'s `onWarning` callback feeds a parent-level toast; the call site that passes this prop is `SetupBar` (setup-bar.test.tsx now asserts a not-ready payload invokes the callback on mount), but the final toast render lives in `app/page.tsx`'s `onAgentsWarning` handler outside this area — so the producer→callback hop is proven, the callback→toast hop is not | GAP (cross-area contract: callback consumption into an actual on-screen toast still unasserted end-to-end)

### Adversarial additions

- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.200] The three real `notify()` call sites (`factory/dev-loop.ts` emit(), `factory/extract-issues.ts` line 953, `factory/install-skills.ts` line 458) are entirely untested in this area — `notify.test.ts` only unit-tests the generic `notify()` function with hand-built payloads, never the `NOTIFY_BY_EVENT` mapping table itself (e.g. that `gate_passed` really maps to `{level:"success", stage:"S9"}`, or that the message is built as `"${issue_id}: ${label}"`) | each `EmitEvent.event_type` in `NOTIFY_BY_EVENT` produces the documented level/stage/message triple | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.201] `extract-issues.ts`'s `if (mapped) notify({ ...mapped, event: `extraction_${status.phase}` })` and `install-skills.ts`'s analogous call both imply a phase-to-notify mapping function/table (`mapped`) that determines which extraction/skills phases actually emit a notification (vs. stay silent) — this mapping is completely unread/untested from this area's perspective; the `messages/en/notifications.json` `events.S6`/`events.S3`/`events.SK` catalog entries suggest only SOME phases map to a notification, but nothing here confirms which, or that a catalog key exists for every phase that CAN notify | every phase that calls `notify()` from extract-issues.ts/install-skills.ts has a matching `events.<stage>.<event>` key in notifications.json (no silent-fallback-to-raw-message for a phase that was expected to have friendly text) | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.203] `notificationText()`'s id-regex `/^(\S+?)[: ]/` has an unexercised failure mode: a message with no leading whitespace-delimited token before `:`/` ` (e.g. a message that's a single word with no colon, or starts with a space) yields `id undefined`, so `t(key, undefined)` is called — if the catalog string requires `{id}` this throws or renders "undefined" depending on next-intl's ICU handling; no test drives a malformed/id-less message through a stage/event pair that HAS a catalog entry | a mapped stage/event with a message lacking the expected `<id>: ` prefix does not crash and does not render literal "undefined" | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.204] `lib/notifications.ts` `dismissNotifications()` — the actual server-side implementation the panel feed's "Dismiss"/"Clear all" POST ultimately calls (via the `/api/control/notifications` route, outside this area) — has real branches untested from THIS area's perspective since vivi-panel.test.tsx stubs the HTTP layer entirely: dismissing an already-dismissed row is idempotent (`if (row.dismissed) return row` short-circuits before incrementing `changed`), and a `refs` array containing an id that matches nothing leaves `changed === 0` and skips the `writeFileSync` entirely | double-dismiss of the same id is a no-op returning `changed:0` the second time; an unmatched ref writes nothing | unit | GAP (out of this area's file list for the implementation, but the feed's dismiss UX assumes this contract and never integration-tests it end-to-end)
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.206] `AgentsHealthDialog`'s two load-triggering effects (mount effect + `[open, load]` effect) both call `load()` independently; on initial mount when `open` is still `false`, does the second effect's `if (!open) return` guard actually prevent a REDUNDANT third fetch beyond the mount-effect's one call? No test counts fetch calls at initial mount to confirm exactly one health fetch occurs, not two | fetch("/api/agents/health") called exactly once during initial mount (before any dialog open) | integration | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.207] `warningFor` is called inside `load()` synchronously after `setHealth`, and `onWarning` fires on EVERY load (mount AND every reopen) per the code — but the component doc says "Called once per load", which is ambiguous: does the parent consumer risk a warning toast firing repeatedly every time the user reopens the dialog even when nothing changed (e.g. still not signed in)? No test drives two consecutive reopens with an unchanged unhealthy snapshot to check whether `onWarning` fires twice with an identical message (which could double-toast in the real consumer) | reopening the dialog twice with unchanged unhealthy health calls onWarning twice with the same string (confirms current behavior either way) | integration | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.208] `MethodBadge`'s `data-auth-method={authMethod}` attribute is set on the DOM but no test in agents-health-dialog.test.tsx queries or asserts on `[data-auth-method]` directly — coverage is only via the note-text side effect, so a regression that renders the WRONG badge (e.g. api_key badge on a subscription account) while still showing the right cost-note text would go undetected | `[data-auth-method="subscription"]` vs `[data-auth-method="api_key"]` exactly matches each agent's actual authMethod | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.209] `AgentStatusBadge`'s presence check hardcodes `unknown={false}` (agents-health-dialog.tsx / agents-gate.tsx) — meaning "not installed" can NEVER render as unknown, only present/absent, per item 130's note, but there's no test that actively asserts an absent-CLI-detection-error still resolves `present` to a definite boolean rather than propagating some other tri-state; i.e. no test proves `present` itself is never null upstream in `AgentHealth` (type-level assumption, unverified at the boundary) | `present` is always strictly boolean even when the underlying detection had an error (defensive boundary check) | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.210] `RichText`'s fenced-code regex `/```([\w-]*)\n?([\s\S]*?)```/g` is non-greedy and lazy across `[\s\S]*?` — a text containing THREE or more backtick-fences where an inner fence's language tag itself contains something regex-special is unexercised; more importantly, adjacent fences with NO prose between them (` ```a``` ` immediately followed by ` ```b``` `, zero characters apart) is a boundary the "MULTIPLE code fences" gap (item 172) doesn't explicitly call out — the `if (m.index > last)` guard must correctly skip emitting an empty prose paragraph between them | zero adjacent-fence gap produces exactly two code `<details>` and no empty `<p>` in between | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.211] `EntryView`'s "tool" kind renders `entry.input` in a raw, untruncated `<pre>` (unlike `entry.output`, which is capped at 4000 chars) — a tool call with a multi-megabyte input (e.g. a huge file write) has no truncation guard at all, unlike output; no test exercises a huge `entry.input` to confirm this asymmetry is intentional (DOM/perf risk) rather than an oversight | a very large `entry.input` (e.g. 1MB string) still renders without truncation — confirms the asymmetric-by-design behavior, or flags it as a likely oversight | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.212] `TranscriptBody`'s fetch effect keys on `target.ref` only (`[target.ref]` dependency array) — if `useTranscript().open()` is called twice with the SAME ref but a different explicit `title` (e.g. re-opening the same transcript from a different UI affordance with a friendlier label), the `key={target.ref}` on the parent does NOT force a remount (same key), so does the displayed title actually update to the new title, or does stale JSX linger from the first open? No test opens the same ref twice with different titles | second `open(sameRef, "New Title")` updates the visible dialog title to "New Title" even without a remount | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.213] `notify.ts`'s `stampId` counter is module-level (`let counter = 0`, never reset) — across a long-lived factory process emitting thousands of notifications, `counter.toString(36)` grows unboundedly; not a correctness bug but an unbounded-growth boundary (huge counter values still base36-encode fine, but no test drives the counter past a few calls to confirm no overflow/truncation at very large values, e.g. counter > Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER in a pathological case) | counter remains a valid base36 string at very large call counts, ids stay unique | unit | GAP
- [pipeline-notifications-agents-ui.214] Cross-file: `factory/notify.ts`'s `NotificationLevel` type (`"info" | "success" | "warning" | "error"`) and `lib/notifications.ts`'s `NotificationInput.level` (typed as bare `string`, no union at all) diverge — the reader/UI side has NO compile-time guarantee that `level` is ever one of the four factory-side values, so a typo'd level string from any future writer would silently pass through both the file format and `readNotifications()` with no validation, landing in the UI's untyped `LEVEL_ICON` fallback | a notification with an arbitrary/malformed `level` string (e.g. `"critical"`) is written, read back, and rendered without crashing, falling back to the info icon | unit | GAP (contract-mismatch: no shared level enum between writer and reader/UI layers)

## Area: upload-vivi-chat

Scope: `lib/import-docs.ts`, `lib/import-docs.test.ts`, `app/api/project/import/route.ts`, `lib/vivi.ts`, `lib/vivi.test.ts`, `app/api/vivi/route.ts`, `app/api/vivi/sessions/route.ts`, `app/api/vivi/sessions/[sessionId]/route.ts`, `app/api/vivi/card/route.ts`, `factory/vivi-turn.ts`, `factory/prompts/vivi.md`, `components/chat/vivi-panel.tsx`, `components/chat/vivi-panel-context.tsx`, `components/chat/decision-card.tsx`, `components/chat/vivi-panel.test.tsx`, `components/chat/decision-card.test.tsx`, `components/chat/message-bubble.tsx`. The import half is a dumb-fast acquisition: `POST /api/project/import` receives a multipart batch, refuses an already-governed target, lays the governance scaffold (reusing `lib/scaffold.ts`), explodes zips, filters by the server type allowlist, writes `.vivicy/uploads/<batch-id>/` + a per-batch `manifest.json` (hashes + dominant language), and emits an append-only notification — there is NO verification leg at import time (the pipeline consumes the latest batch later). The launcher bubble + left overlay panel host Vivi chat; the onboarding view is inventoried in the onboarding area, the notifications feed in the pipeline-notifications area.

### lib/import-docs.ts

- [upload-vivi-chat.413] `importDocuments` refuses an already-governed target (`.vivicy` present) with `ImportError` code `already_governed`, BEFORE any write — the existing `.vivicy` bytes stay untouched and no `uploads/` dir appears | throws already_governed, target unchanged | unit | covered-today: lib/import-docs.test.ts ("refuses a target that is already governed, before any write")
- [upload-vivi-chat.414] `importDocuments` into a brand-new (non-existent) dir governs it (`from_scratch`): skeleton + `vivicy.json` laid, git init+commit, current project set, batch written | mode from_scratch, governed, batch present | unit | covered-today: lib/import-docs.test.ts ("governs and imports into a brand-new (non-existent) directory")
- [upload-vivi-chat.415] `importDocuments` into an existing NON-governed dir governs it (`existing_project`) without clobbering the dir's own files | mode existing_project, README preserved, governed | unit | covered-today: lib/import-docs.test.ts ("governs and imports into an existing non-governed directory without clobbering its files")
- [upload-vivi-chat.416] governance scaffold laid exactly once — a second import on the same (now-governed) target is refused `already_governed` | second import throws already_governed | unit | covered-today: lib/import-docs.test.ts ("lays the skeleton exactly once — a second import is refused as already_governed")
- [upload-vivi-chat.417] `importDocuments` with zero entries throws `ImportError` `no_files` before resolving the target | throws no_files | unit | covered-today: lib/import-docs.test.ts ("refuses an empty upload with no_files")
- [upload-vivi-chat.418] `importDocuments` where every entry is an unsupported type throws `no_supported_files` before governing — the target dir is never created | throws no_supported_files, target absent | unit | covered-today: lib/import-docs.test.ts ("refuses the whole import when no file is a supported type, before governing")
- [upload-vivi-chat.419] per-file type filtering: supported extensions accepted, unknown ones rejected per-file with `{path, code:"unsupported_type"}` — the batch is not failed for a skipped file | accepted+rejected split | unit | covered-today: lib/import-docs.test.ts ("accepts supported types and rejects unknown ones per-file, not the whole batch")
- [upload-vivi-chat.420] batch written under `.vivicy/uploads/<batch-id>/` preserving nested relative structure (folders from drag) | files land at exact nested paths | unit | covered-today: lib/import-docs.test.ts ("writes the batch under .vivicy/uploads/<id>/ preserving nested structure")
- [upload-vivi-chat.421] `mintBatchId` derives a filesystem-safe id from ISO time (`:`/`.` -> `-`) and is collision-safe: a same-instant second call gets a `-<n>` suffix, atomic via `mkdir` EEXIST retry; ids sort lexicographically = chronologically | distinct ids, second suffixed | unit | covered-today: lib/import-docs.test.ts ("mints collision-safe ids: two batches in the same instant get distinct dirs")
- [upload-vivi-chat.422] zip explosion: a `.zip` entry is exploded server-side into the batch preserving its internal structure (relative to the zip's parent); the zip itself is not stored; unsupported inner files are rejected per-file | inner files placed, zip gone, inner unknown rejected | unit | covered-today: lib/import-docs.test.ts ("explodes a zip into the batch preserving its internal structure")
- [upload-vivi-chat.423] zip-slip: a zip inner path containing `..` or an absolute/drive prefix is rejected with `ImportError` `zip_slip` (security boundary) before governing | throws zip_slip, target absent | unit | covered-today: lib/import-docs.test.ts ("rejects a zip-slip (../) entry as a security boundary")
- [upload-vivi-chat.424] a corrupt/unreadable archive throws `ImportError` `zip_unreadable` | throws zip_unreadable | unit | covered-today: lib/import-docs.test.ts ("throws zip_unreadable on a corrupt archive")
- [upload-vivi-chat.425] nested zips: one level of nesting (uploaded zip -> inner zip) is exploded; a zip nested deeper (explosion depth >= MAX_ZIP_DEPTH=2) is rejected per-file as `unsupported_type` | one level exploded, deeper rejected | unit | covered-today: lib/import-docs.test.ts ("explodes one level of nesting but rejects a zip nested deeper")
- [upload-vivi-chat.426] `manifest.json` records `{path, size, sha256}` per batch file (from disk — the single source), sorted by path, plus `batchId` + `createdAt`; the response `accepted` equals `manifest.files` | manifest correct and equals accepted | unit | covered-today: lib/import-docs.test.ts ("records relative path, byte size, and sha256 for every batch file, sorted")
- [upload-vivi-chat.427] dominant language by byte weight: a batch dominated by English text yields `eng`; a batch with no scannable text file (only binary formats) yields `und` — stated, no warning, no arbitration | language eng / und | unit | covered-today: lib/import-docs.test.ts ("sets the dominant language by byte weight and states 'und' when nothing is scannable")
- [upload-vivi-chat.428] `dominantLanguage` weights by byte count and breaks ties lexicographically (equal weights -> smallest ISO 639-3 code); empty weights -> `und` | weighted argmax, lex tie-break | unit | covered-today: lib/import-docs.test.ts ("dominantLanguage weights by byte count and breaks ties lexicographically")
- [upload-vivi-chat.429] `manifest.json` is the LAST write of an import (batch-complete marker) — a batch dir lacking it is an interrupted, non-consumable batch the pipeline must skip | invariant: manifest written last | unit | GAP (invariant enforced by write ordering; no crash-mid-write test)
- [upload-vivi-chat.430] `safeRel` strips `..`/leading-`/` from a direct (non-zip) upload entry's rel path so it can never escape the batch (zip inner paths take the harder `zip_slip` reject path instead) | direct traversal neutralized | unit | GAP (covered indirectly via nested-structure; no direct `../` payload on a plain entry)
- [upload-vivi-chat.431] language detection reads ONLY the plain-text extension subset; binary document formats (.pdf/.docx/.odt/.rtf) are accepted into the batch but never parsed or modified (uploads are never touched) | binaries stored, not scanned | unit | covered-today: lib/import-docs.test.ts (the und/binary case exercises the not-scanned path)

### app/api/vivi/card/import/route.ts (POST /api/vivi/card/import)

- [upload-vivi-chat.461] `POST /api/vivi/card/import` (multipart): parses `sessionId`/`cardId`/`actionId` (400 if missing/non-string) + index-aligned `files`/`paths` into `RawEntry[]`, calls `decideCardImport`, returns the `CardImportResult` (200 when ok, 422 when not); maps an `ImportError` to its status (`no_files`/`no_supported_files`/`zip_slip`/`zip_unreadable` → 400, `not_governed` → 409) with `code` + details, a `ControlError` → 422, anything else → 500 | typed status mapping; the card-import glue for the client picker | integration | GAP (curl-proven live; no route-level test, matching the sibling `/api/vivi/card` + `/api/project/import` routes' coverage pattern)
- [upload-vivi-chat.462] `lib/supported-extensions.ts` is the single CLIENT-SAFE source of the document type list: `SUPPORTED_DOC_EXTENSIONS` (+ `.zip` transport → `IMPORT_ACCEPT_EXTENSIONS`/`IMPORT_ACCEPT_ATTR`) feeds `lib/import-docs.ts`'s server `SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS` set, the DecisionCard picker's accept attr, AND the ImportDocsFlow screen (accept attr + accepted-formats hint + client-side filename filter), so the server authority and the client surfaces never drift and no `node:fs` module is pulled into the client bundle | one list, three consumers, no client-side node import | unit | discriminating drift-guard in import-docs-flow.test.tsx (rendered accept === server `SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS` + `.zip`); decision-card + import-docs suites assert the same extensions
- [upload-vivi-chat.463] `lib/language.ts` `languageDisplayName(code, locale="en")` resolves an ISO 639-3 code to a human name via `Intl.DisplayNames`, returning null for `und`/unresolvable/`root` so callers omit the language clause; deterministic, no i18n runtime dependency | eng → "English"; und/unknown → null | unit | covered indirectly via decideCardImport ack/summary tests (eng→English clause present, und→clause omitted)

- [upload-vivi-chat.450] Streaming scroll behavior (the shadcn message-scroller contract): `autoScroll` pins the view to the live edge only WHILE the reader is at the bottom, releases the pin on scroll-up, the `MessageScrollerButton` becomes active (`data-active=true`) when unpinned and returns to the bottom on click (then `data-active=false`), and sending a turn never jumps the thread to the absolute top | pinned through a streamed reply; scroll-up releases + affordance appears; jump re-pins; no top-jump on send, desktop + mobile | e2e-ui | GAP (proven live against an isolated fake-spawn dev server; jsdom can't drive real scroll/IntersectionObserver, so no automated unit test)

Still no test for: pending marker mid-flight, multi-turn session continuity in the UI layer, or the re-fetch-failure reply fallback | — | e2e-ui | GAP (see individual line items; this is a summary note of the file's actual coverage ceiling)
