### Area cross-notes

- [upload-vivi-chat.239] **Asymmetric agent trust model.** `runViviTurn` (lib/vivi.ts) enforces a full structural allowlist with snapshot/diff/restore around the agent leg's filesystem writes; `runUploadVerify` / `verifyUpload` (control.ts + factory/verify-upload.ts) has NO equivalent enforcement — the upload-verifier prompt says "read-only, never edit" but nothing in code snapshots/diffs/restores the normalized corpus or the target's `.vivicy/canonical` around that leg's execution. A rogue or buggy verifier leg could silently mutate files and no test (or code path) would catch it. This is the single most consequential architectural GAP found across the whole area.
- [upload-vivi-chat.240] **Both the upload and vivi route layers reuse the exact same `uploadErrorResponse`/`ControlError` status-mapping conventions.** The prior ambiguity here is FIXED: `/api/upload/verify` used to collapse "no target root selected" and "unknown staging id" onto the same `missing_target` code and the same HTTP 422 (see upload-vivi-chat.79's history); `runUploadVerify` now throws the distinct `unknown_staging` code for a bad staging id, which the route maps to 400 instead — a client can now tell "pick a project" (422) apart from "your staging id is stale/typo'd" (400) by status code alone. No test exercises either status code at the route level though (route.ts is still 0%-covered, per upload-vivi-chat.242).
- [upload-vivi-chat.241] **Deletion blind spot shared by both flows.** `diffVivicy` (Vivi) only detects additions/hash-changes, never deletions; `applyUpload`'s collision check only prevents overwriting existing files, it says nothing about a normalized file that would need to DELETE a stale target file (not applicable today since apply only adds, but worth flagging as the same "additive-only" philosophy running through both the upload and vivi flows — a future one-way sync assumption that should be tested if either flow ever grows a delete/prune semantic).
- [upload-vivi-chat.242] **No end-to-end (e2e-ui or e2e-process) test exists that drives an upload through ALL FOUR steps** (stage -> verify -> apply) via the actual HTTP routes; the only coverage is at the `lib/upload.ts` unit level (which calls `stageUpload`/`normalizeStaging`/`applyUpload`/`runUploadVerify` directly, bypassing the Next.js route handlers, form-data parsing, and notification side effects entirely). Same story for Vivi: no route-level or e2e test drives `POST /api/vivi` end-to-end; `components/chat/vivi-panel.test.tsx` / `decision-card.test.tsx` stub `fetch` directly rather than hitting real (or route-level-tested) handlers. The route handlers (`app/api/upload/route.ts`, `.../apply/route.ts`, `.../verify/route.ts`, `app/api/vivi/route.ts`, `app/api/vivi/sessions/route.ts`, `app/api/vivi/sessions/[sessionId]/route.ts`, `app/api/vivi/card/route.ts`) are effectively 0%-covered by any test file in the repository as searched.
- [upload-vivi-chat.243] **Idempotence / re-run behavior is unexplored everywhere in this area**: re-staging identical content (new stagingId each time, no dedupe), re-verifying the same stagingId twice (does a stale report.json linger correctly), re-applying an already-applied stagingId (should hit `would_overwrite` naturally on the second attempt — this IS implicitly testable but not tested), and re-sending an identical Vivi message on the same session (no de-dup, transcript just grows) are all GAPs.

### Adversarial additions

- [upload-vivi-chat.244] **`runUploadVerify` actually lives in `lib/control.ts`, not `lib/upload.ts`** — it does its OWN `existsSync(targetRoot)` check (throws `missing_target`) THEN `existsSync(stagingDir)` check (throws the now-distinct `unknown_staging`) BEFORE calling `normalizeStaging`. This means: (a) a bad/nonexistent stagingId passed to `/api/upload/verify` throws `unknown_staging` (400) from control.ts's own check, NEVER reaching `normalizeStaging`'s `assertStaging` (`bad_staging`) at all — so upload-vivi-chat.80's premise ("the underlying UploadError... bad_staging... falls through") is actually UNREACHABLE from this call path since control.ts's staging-dir check runs first and throws ControlError, not UploadError. This should correct/replace item 80's stated GAP scenario. | GAP — control.ts's own resolveContext/existsSync ordering vs. normalizeStaging's assertStaging is untested, and no test proves the route's 400 mapping actually flows from THIS check | unit | GAP
- [upload-vivi-chat.245] `resolveContext()` (called by `runUploadVerify`) is itself untested in this area — what does it do when `VIVICY_FACTORY_ROOT`/`VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT` env vars are unset AND no persisted target/factory root exists on disk (fresh install, no project ever selected)? The control-plane describe block in lib/upload.test.ts always sets both env vars in `beforeEach`, so the "truly nothing configured" boundary is never exercised for the upload-verify path specifically | GAP
- [upload-vivi-chat.246] **`uploadStatus()`'s default branch silently maps ANY unrecognized future `UploadError["code"]` to 400** — the switch has no exhaustiveness check (no `never` assertion in the default arm), so adding a new UploadError code without updating `uploadStatus` fails silently into 400 with no compiler error and no test catching the drift | unit | GAP
- [upload-vivi-chat.247] `/api/upload/apply`'s catch-all calls `uploadErrorResponse(error, ...)` even for a thrown value that is neither `UploadError` nor `Error` (e.g. a thrown string or thrown plain object) — falls into the `error instanceof Error ? error.message : fallback` branch correctly, but the PRECEDING `appendNotification` call on the same line already did its own separate `instanceof Error` ternary duplicating this exact same dead-value-shape logic in two places with no shared helper; a future edit to one message-extraction branch and not the other silently diverges the notification text from the HTTP response text | unit | GAP (code-quality/DRY observation, not just a test gap)
- [upload-vivi-chat.248] **CRITICAL — cross-family error-code collision renders the WRONG message to the user.** In `lib/vivi.ts`, FOUR semantically distinct failures all throw `ControlError` with the identical code `"missing_target"`: (1) no project selected (line 464), (2) target root doesn't exist on disk (line 470), (3) empty/whitespace-only message (line 502), (4) malformed/invalid session id (line 127). The UI (`vivi-panel.tsx`) calls `errorText(tErrors, \`control.${body.code}\`, fallback)` whenever `body.code` is present, and `messages/en/errors.json`'s `control.missing_target` key is the single fixed string "no project selected — choose a target project first". So when a user submits an EMPTY message, or the app carries a corrupted/forged sessionId, the inline error line shown is "no project selected — choose a target project first" — actively wrong and misleading, not just a generic fallback. This is more severe than the already-noted upload/verify missing_target ambiguity (item 240) because there the two meanings at least produce a semantically-plausible (if ambiguous) message; here the rendered message is affirmatively FALSE for cases 3 and 4. Confirmed by reading messages/en/errors.json's `control` block (only one `missing_target` entry) and lib/i18n-errors.ts's `errorText` (`t.has(key) ? t(key,...) : fallbackMessage` — since the catalog DOES have `control.missing_target`, the translated text wins over the more-accurate raw `error.message` fallback). | e2e-ui / integration | GAP — vivi-panel.test.tsx now asserts the `control.missing_target` line renders for a coded POST failure (the mapping itself), but no test exercises the empty-message or malformed-sessionId FLAVORS where that rendered text is affirmatively false; the defect stands
- [upload-vivi-chat.249] **`getSpawner()` / `VIVICY_FAKE_SPAWN=1` dry-mode path (`lib/spawner.ts`) is entirely absent from the matrix and untested for this area.** `fakeSpawner.run` special-cases `vivi-turn.ts` by name (`writeFakeViviReply`) to write a canned "Vivi is running in dry mode…" reply to `--reply-file` and touch nothing under `.vivicy` (so the allowlist diff stays clean) — but it does NOT special-case `verify-upload.ts` or `change-control.ts` at all, meaning: (a) in dry-mode, an upload verify would fall through to the generic `{name}: OK (fake spawn)` success line with NO report.json ever written by the fake, so `readReport` returns null and `runUploadVerify` correctly goes red (fail-closed) — untested; (b) in dry-mode, a POST-freeze Vivi turn's change-control validation spawn ALSO falls through to the same generic "OK" stub (code 0), meaning change-control validation ALWAYS silently passes in dry mode regardless of what the fake "wrote" — since the fake never writes a CR at all this is currently inert, but it is an untested, undocumented assumption baked into the dry-mode seam | e2e-process | GAP — no test exercises `getSpawner()`'s env-switch, nor `fakeSpawner`'s behavior against `verify-upload.ts` or `change-control.ts` script names specifically
- [upload-vivi-chat.250] `writeFakeViviReply` locates `--reply-file` via `args.indexOf("--reply-file")`; if that flag is absent (`flag === -1`), `args[flag+1]` is `args[0]` — silently writing the fake reply to the WRONG position's value (whatever args[0] happens to be) instead of skipping, UNLESS `flag >= 0` guards it (it does: `const replyFile = flag >= 0 ? args[flag + 1] : undefined; if (!replyFile) return`) — so this is actually safe, but the guard's correctness is itself untested (no test calls the fake spawner with args lacking `--reply-file`) | boundary | GAP
- [upload-vivi-chat.251] **`normalizeStaging` re-derives `scanRaw(stagingId)` from scratch every call** (re-walks + re-classifies the whole `raw/` tree via `classify()`, which re-reads file content for every `.yml`/`.yaml` again) rather than reusing `stageUpload`'s already-computed `staged` array — meaning classification runs TWICE per file for any upload containing map candidates (once in `stageUpload`, once in `normalizeStaging`). Not a correctness bug (classify is pure/deterministic) but an untested assumption that the two classifications never diverge between the two calls (e.g. if the raw file were mutated on disk between stage and normalize/verify, which nothing prevents) | integration | GAP — no test mutates a raw/ file between `stageUpload` and `normalizeStaging`/`applyUpload` to confirm classification re-reads live disk state rather than trusting the original `staged` result
- [upload-vivi-chat.252] `applyUpload`'s classification-at-placement-time re-reads `normalized/` files via `classify(rel, () => readFileSync(abs, "utf8"))` a THIRD time (after stage + normalize) — for a `.yml`/`.yaml` map file this means the placement decision is based on re-classifying the NORMALIZED copy's content, not the original raw content; if `normalizeStaging` ever altered map content in a way that changed its classification-relevant markers (it copies verbatim today, so currently safe), `applyUpload` would place it differently than `stageUpload` reported. No test guards this invariant (that verbatim map copying never changes `nodes:`/`kind_taxonomy` detection) | unit | GAP
- [upload-vivi-chat.253] `destinationFor` uses `path.basename(normalizedRel)` for canonical/spike placement, meaning **two canonical files with the same basename in different subdirs under `normalized/`** collide at the SAME destination — this is exactly the "staged twice" collision case already tested for spikes (upload-vivi-chat.50), but no test exercises the CANONICAL-kind flavor of the same collision (only spike-vs-spike is tested; canonical-vs-canonical with different source subdirs is unexercised, though the code path is identical) | unit | GAP
- [upload-vivi-chat.254] **`walkFiles` (used by `scanRaw`, `applyUpload`'s plan-building, and `lib/vivi.ts`'s snapshot/diff) does not follow symlinks specially** — `entry.isDirectory()`/`entry.isFile()` from `withFileTypes` reports symlink targets' type per Node's dirent semantics on most platforms, but a symlink loop (a raw/ entry symlinked to an ancestor dir) would cause unbounded stack growth / infinite traversal. No test constructs a symlinked raw upload entry or a symlink loop inside `.vivicy` for the vivi.ts snapshot walker | security/boundary | GAP
- [upload-vivi-chat.255] **`isAcceptedFilename`/`stageUpload`'s upfront validation loop uses `entry.rel || entry.name`** — if a client sends a `rel` that is a non-empty string but points to a DIFFERENT extension than `entry.name` (e.g. `rel: "notes.md"`, `name: "malware.exe"`), only `rel`'s extension is checked, yet `safeRel` later falls back to `name` when `rel` is empty — but here `rel` is non-empty so `safeRel` uses `rel`, meaning the extension actually checked matches the extension actually staged (self-consistent) — HOWEVER the reverse is unexercised: what if `rel` is whitespace-only (not empty per JS truthiness, `" "` is truthy) — then `entry.rel || entry.name` picks the whitespace `rel`, `isAcceptedFilename(" ")` almost certainly returns false (no matching extname) so it correctly rejects as unsupported_type, but no test confirms a whitespace-only (not literally `""`) rel is handled | boundary | GAP
- [upload-vivi-chat.256] **`safeRel`'s `..`-and-`.`-segment filter also silently drops a segment that is EMPTY due to a double slash** (e.g. `rel: "a//b/../c.md"` — normalize collapses this, but a raw client-supplied rel with `\0` NUL bytes or other exotic path characters is never tested) — Node's `path.normalize`/`writeFileSync` behavior on a rel containing a NUL byte (`"a\0.md"`) is platform-dependent and could throw a low-level `ERR_INVALID_ARG_VALUE` that propagates as an unhandled 500 rather than a clean `UploadError`; untested | security/boundary | GAP
- [upload-vivi-chat.257] **`normalizeStaging`'s per-file loop has no total-file-count or total-size cap**, mirroring the already-noted per-file huge-file gap (item 26) but at the AGGREGATE level: hundreds of thousands of tiny files (e.g. a maliciously crafted zip bomb of empty files) would make `walkFiles`'s stack-based DFS and `scanRaw`'s per-file `statSync`/`readFileSync` calls scale linearly with no upper bound anywhere in the stage/normalize/verify/apply pipeline — a classic zip-bomb-by-file-count (as opposed to by-bytes) DoS vector via the `.zip` upload path, entirely untested | security/boundary | GAP
- [upload-vivi-chat.258] `runViviTurn`'s snapshots (`bytesBefore` once before round 1; per-round `roundBase`) cover `targetRoot/.vivicy`, while the prompt scratch file is written under the per-project `projects/<key>/vivi/` (outside `.vivicy`), so it never pollutes the snapshot — but the full recursive `.vivicy` hash is recomputed EVERY round with no caching, an O(n) hash-everything cost per turn, unbenchmarked for a large frozen project | boundary | GAP
- [upload-vivi-chat.259] if `spawner.run` throws inside the round loop, the pre-round snapshots (`bytesBefore`/`roundBase`) AND the git witness (`preDirty`/`preDirtySnapshot`) are discarded in memory with NO rollback (enforcement runs only AFTER a completed round) — a leg that crashes mid-write leaves partial writes on disk permanently, worse than a normal violation rejection which restores/removes | unit/security | GAP
- [upload-vivi-chat.260] `hasFrozenBaseline`/`isSpecCycleOpen` are re-read every round (a directory scan + JSON parse of every baseline manifest / the spec-cycle state file); no test seeds MULTIPLE baseline manifest files (an old superseded one plus a new frozen one) to confirm correctness/perf with a realistic multi-baseline history | integration | GAP
- [upload-vivi-chat.261] **`vivi-panel.tsx`'s `send()` builds the user-visible optimistic bubble from `draft.trim()` but the POST body sends the SAME trimmed `message`** — consistent — however if `onKeyDown`'s Enter-without-Shift fires while an IME (Input Method Editor, e.g. composing Japanese/Chinese text) composition is in progress, standard chat UIs guard with `event.nativeEvent.isComposing` to avoid sending a message mid-composition on the IME's own confirm-Enter keystroke; no such guard exists here (`onKeyDown` only checks `event.key === "Enter" && !event.shiftKey`), so an IME user confirming kanji conversion with Enter would prematurely SEND the in-progress draft. Untested and unguarded — a real i18n defect for CJK users | e2e-ui | GAP
- [upload-vivi-chat.262] **`MessageScrollerItem scrollAnchor` is applied to the `PendingMarker` item** (the last item while sending) and also to the inline send-error item (the two never coexist) — no test verifies the auto-scroll-to-latest behavior actually anchors correctly across the transition from "last message" to "pending marker appears" to "pending marker replaced by the re-fetched turns" (three consecutive DOM structure changes); a naive anchor-key change could cause a visible scroll-jump or a lost anchor, untested | e2e-ui | GAP
- [upload-vivi-chat.264] **`components/chat/vivi-panel.test.tsx`'s POST stub returns one canned body per test** — meaning no test ever exercises TWO turns in sequence at the UI layer (session continuity from the UI's perspective: does `sessionId` state get threaded into the SECOND POST body correctly after being set from the first response?) — this is a real UI-layer GAP distinct from the already-noted lib-level session-continuity tests (which don't go through the component) | e2e-ui | GAP
- [upload-vivi-chat.265] **No test anywhere in this area confirms `settingsToEnv(readSettings())`'s output does not COLLIDE with or get overridden by the explicit `VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT`/`VIVICY_SPEC_FROZEN` keys** set immediately before it in the same object spread (`{...process.env, VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT, VIVICY_SPEC_FROZEN, ...settingsToEnv(...)}` — object spread ORDER means if `settingsToEnv` ever emitted a `VIVICY_TARGET_ROOT` or `VIVICY_SPEC_FROZEN` key itself, it would silently OVERWRITE the two explicit, load-bearing values placed earlier in the same literal since it is spread LAST. Confirmed by re-reading lines 553-558: `settingsToEnv` is spread AFTER the two explicit keys, so this is a live footgun if `settingsToEnv`'s key set ever grows to include either name — untested invariant, no test asserts `settingsToEnv`'s key set is disjoint from the two explicit ones | unit | GAP

### Persistent panel + decision cards

- [upload-vivi-chat.266] Launcher bubble: fixed bottom-left, `overflow-hidden rounded-full` primary Button whose brand-green disc insets the shared `ViviAvatar` (upload-vivi-chat.411) with an even ring (`p-1.5` on the button, `size-full` avatar) so Vivi's Nonna face sits centered with visible green breathing room on every side, never touching the disc edge; no monoline glyph; the `ViviPanel` component renders it whenever `hasTarget !== false`, while the page mounts `ViviPanel` only once the CLI gate is passed (`agentsHealth !== undefined && !gateBlocked` — the launcher gating lives in app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.340/.342/.353); click toggles the panel | bubble present with `aria-label` "Open Vivi" whenever the component is mounted and not on the no_target screen (`hasTarget === false` suppression: upload-vivi-chat.410) | e2e-ui | vivi-panel.test.tsx ("renders the launcher bubble when not on the no_target screen...") — per-load-state render (loading/error/empty/ready) GAP at the page level
- [upload-vivi-chat.410] Launcher-bubble suppression on the no_target onboarding screen: `ViviPanel` receives `hasTarget` and renders the bottom-left bubble only when `hasTarget !== false`; on `hasTarget === false` (the no_target empty state — one action, the "Talk to Vivi" CTA) the bubble is not rendered, while the panel machinery stays fully functional (the CTA's `openPanel` opens it, close/reopen work, a persisted-open state still renders it). Every other loaded state keeps the bubble; the CLI-gate condition (bubble absent when a required agent CLI is missing, fail-open on null health — app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.340/.342) is unchanged. Single source: the same `hasTarget` value that renders the empty state (app/page.tsx). | no bubble on `hasTarget === false` and the panel still opens via the CTA; bubble present for `hasTarget` true/undefined | unit | vivi-panel.test.tsx ("hasTarget=false hides the launcher bubble; the CTA opens the panel, which still closes and reopens" + "hasTarget=true keeps the launcher bubble present")
- [upload-vivi-chat.267] While the panel is open the launcher goes `aria-hidden` + `inert` + invisible (the full-height panel would otherwise sit under it); the header X is the close affordance and focus returns to the launcher on close | no duplicate "Close Vivi" targets in the a11y tree; focus lands back on the bubble | e2e-ui | GAP (the header X close-and-reopen cycle and the focus handoff are unasserted; the single-close-target invariant is implicitly pinned by the open-panel test's `getByRole` queries)
- [upload-vivi-chat.268] Panel shell semantics: non-modal left overlay — `role="complementary"` labelled by the "Vivi" heading, no backdrop (the map stays interactive), off-canvas via `-translate-x-full` when closed so `body.scrollWidth` never grows | role appears only when open; e2e overflow spec stays green with the panel mounted | e2e-ui | vivi-panel.test.tsx (role visibility open vs closed) + e2e/overflow.spec.ts (page-level scrollWidth guard; panel-OPEN overflow GAP)
- [upload-vivi-chat.411] `ViviAvatar` (components/brand/vivi-avatar.tsx) is the shared Vivi face atom: a `next/image` of `/brand/3.small/la_nonna_on_sm.png` (intrinsic 256×256), decorative (`alt=""` — the surrounding control always supplies the label), sized by the caller's `className` with `object-contain`; it is the single source for Vivi's face in the panel header (`size-6`) and the launcher bubble (upload-vivi-chat.266), so both chrome spots move together and no monoline `NonnaIcon` glyph remains in either | crisp face at the rendered size; no `NonnaIcon` in the panel header or launcher | unit | GAP (presentational brand atom; rendered transitively by vivi-panel.test.tsx, not asserted on its own — mirrors the NonnaIcon atom's app-shell-sidebar-ui-kit.351)
- [upload-vivi-chat.412] Panel header composition: `ViviAvatar` + the "Vivi" heading + close, and NOTHING else — the "New conversation" (Plus) button is removed everywhere (the new-session capability stays: the server mints a fresh session for any sessionId-less POST, and the panel resets to a fresh session on project switch) — and NO engine/model badge anywhere in the panel chrome (the implementer engine is configurable and is surfaced only in the settings dialog and agent-health surfaces, never re-stated here); the GET /api/vivi engine endpoint is gone with it | no `claude-opus-4-8`/provider text and no "New conversation" button in the open panel | unit | vivi-panel.test.tsx ("clicking the bubble opens the panel with header controls and tabs, no engine badge, no new-conversation button")
- [upload-vivi-chat.269] REHYDRATION on first open with no sessionId: GET /api/vivi/sessions, pick the newest, GET /api/vivi/sessions/<id>, thread restored from its turns — runs ONCE (hydratedRef), never re-runs on reopen | user/vivi bubbles incl. wrote chips + rejected marker restored | e2e-ui | vivi-panel.test.tsx ("first open resumes the newest session...") — the run-once-on-reopen half GAP
- [upload-vivi-chat.270] Rehydrated role "action" turn renders as the compact muted block: MessageHeader titled from i18n ("Actions") + mono whitespace-pre-wrap `turn.text` (✓/✗ lines) | titled mono block visible | unit | vivi-panel.test.tsx (asserts the "Actions" title + the ✓ line)
- [upload-vivi-chat.271] Rehydrated role "card" turn renders a `DecisionCard`; a card turn present while `sessionId` is somehow unset renders nothing (defensive guard, unreachable from real flows) | card title/body/buttons visible; no crash on the guard | unit | vivi-panel.test.tsx (card render via rehydration) — the no-sessionId guard GAP
- [upload-vivi-chat.272] After a successful POST /api/vivi the panel RE-FETCHES GET /api/vivi/sessions/<id> and replaces the thread from the transcript (the server may have appended intermediate vivi/action turns during multi-round action execution) instead of trusting the returned reply | the server-appended action turn renders | e2e-ui | vivi-panel.test.tsx ("sending POSTs the message, then re-fetches the session so server-appended turns render")
- [upload-vivi-chat.273] Post-send re-fetch FAILS (network/shape): the returned reply is appended locally as a fallback vivi turn so the turn is never invisible | fallback bubble with wrote/rejected from the POST body | boundary | GAP
- [upload-vivi-chat.275] A decided card bubbles up: the panel re-fetches the session turns (decided stamp + appended action/vivi turns) and calls `onActivity` unless the clicked action kind is "dismiss" | thread re-synced; map refresh triggered for non-dismiss kinds only | integration | GAP (DecisionCard's `onDecided` is unit-covered; the panel-side wiring is not)
- [upload-vivi-chat.276] DecisionCard renders title, optional whitespace-pre-wrap body, and one Button per card action with the variant mapping ("default"/"destructive"/"outline") | all buttons enabled pre-decision | unit | decision-card.test.tsx ("renders the title, body, and one live button per action")
- [upload-vivi-chat.277] DecisionCard click POSTs `{sessionId, cardId, actionId}` to /api/vivi/card; every action button is `aria-disabled` (never native `disabled` — a natively-disabled button would drop keyboard focus to `<body>` on click) while pending and FOREVER once decided, then a `role="status"` Marker (Check icon + chosen label + summary) announces the outcome and `onDecided(action)` fires | exact payload; `aria-disabled="true"` on both buttons; summary line announced | unit | decision-card.test.tsx ("clicking POSTs the exact payload, then disables all buttons forever and shows the summary" — asserts `aria-disabled` on both buttons)
- [upload-vivi-chat.278] DecisionCard given a pre-decided turn (`decided` prop from rehydration) renders every button `aria-disabled="true"` with the recorded outcome and never fetches | `aria-disabled` buttons + recorded line, zero network | unit | decision-card.test.tsx ("a turn rehydrated with `decided` renders disabled with the recorded outcome — no fetch" — asserts `aria-disabled` on both buttons)
- [upload-vivi-chat.279] DecisionCard failed POST under the EXTENDED contract (POST /api/vivi/card now ALWAYS returns `decided` once the card is decided — already-decided OR executed-but-failed): a failure CARRYING `decided` calls `setLocalDecision(body.decided)` so the permanent decided line renders alongside the destructive error line, buttons locked FOREVER (no re-click the server would refuse); a decision-LESS failure (a validation error that recorded nothing) shows only the error line and re-enables the buttons for a retry — `onDecided(action)` fires whenever `decided` is present | decided-carrying failure locks + both markers; decision-less failure retries | unit | decision-card.test.tsx ("a 422 carrying `decided` (already-decided / executed-but-failed) locks the buttons and shows both markers" + "a decision-less failure (no `decided`) shows the error and re-enables for a retry")
- [upload-vivi-chat.459] DecisionCard for an `import_docs` action renders one primary button + a HIDDEN `multiple` `<input type=file>` whose `accept` mirrors the server allowlist + `.zip` (`IMPORT_ACCEPT_ATTR` from `lib/supported-extensions.ts`), NOT a drop zone; the click opens the native picker (`importActionRef` + `fileInputRef.click()`) — the upload, not the click, is the decision — so cancelling the picker (no files chosen) leaves the card undecided and fires no request | hidden `.zip`/`.md`-accepting multi-input present; cancel = no fetch, button still enabled | unit | decision-card.test.tsx ("offers a hidden multi-file picker accepting documents and .zip, not a drop zone" + "cancelling the picker (no files) leaves the card undecided — no upload")
- [upload-vivi-chat.460] DecisionCard import upload: choosing files POSTs a multipart batch (`sessionId`/`cardId`/`actionId` + index-aligned `files`/`paths` from `webkitRelativePath`||`name`) to `/api/vivi/card/import` through the SHARED outcome handler (`record`, reused by the JSON `decide` path); success locks the button `aria-disabled`, stamps the "Chose … — N documents imported · <Lang>" decided marker, and calls `onDecided`; a server refusal shows the error and keeps the picker live for a retry (no `decided`, `onDecided` not called) | exact multipart payload; success locks + decided marker + onDecided; refusal shows error + stays live | unit | decision-card.test.tsx ("choosing files uploads a multipart batch, decides the card, and re-syncs" + "a server-refused upload surfaces the error and keeps the picker live for a retry")
- [upload-vivi-chat.280] DecisionCard whose `decided.actionId` no longer matches any card action (corrupt/rewritten transcript) falls back to rendering the raw actionId as the label | no crash, honest label | boundary | GAP
- [upload-vivi-chat.281] `useViviPanel()` outside a `ViviPanelProvider` throws the explicit error (never a silent no-op); openPanel/closePanel/togglePanel drive one shared `open` state | thrown message names the provider | unit | GAP (indirectly exercised: every panel test renders through the provider)

### Turn resilience — epoch guard, rehydration latch, mid-turn resume, attention badge

- [upload-vivi-chat.398] Epoch guard on the send response: `send()` captures `epochRef.current` before its awaits; if a project switch bumps the epoch while the POST/re-fetch is in flight, every write in the response handler (error branch, success branch, the post-fetch `fetchSessionTurns`, and the `finally`'s `setSending(false)`) is skipped once `epoch !== epochRef.current` | a stale reply never blanks the new thread or leaks a foreign sessionId into it; the new project's `sending` is not left stuck by the retired send | unit | vivi-panel.test.tsx ("a project switch mid-send discards the stale reply instead of writing it into the new project")
- [upload-vivi-chat.399] Epoch guard on a card resync: `onCardDecided` captures the epoch before its `fetchSessionTurns` await; if a project switch bumps the epoch — or changes the live `sessionId` away from the card's own session — before the resync resolves, `setTurns(restored)` is skipped | a late card resync cannot resurrect a thread the owner just cleared | unit | vivi-panel.test.tsx ("a card resync landing after a project switch does not resurrect the cleared thread")
- [upload-vivi-chat.400] Rehydration latch set ONLY on a successful, non-cancelled restore: a failed index fetch (`!res.ok`/`body.ok===false`) or an effect cancelled mid-flight (a dependency flip while the fetch is outstanding) leaves `hydratedRef.current` false, so the next effect run retries instead of permanently latching on a transient miss | a cancelled/failed rehydration attempt is retried, not abandoned | unit | vivi-panel.test.tsx ("a rehydration cancelled by a dep flip retries instead of latching hydrated")
- [upload-vivi-chat.401] Mid-turn resume poll: on load, if the rehydrated thread's last turn is the user's with no reply yet (`!sending && sessionId && lastTurn.role==="user"`), the pending marker shows immediately and a `setInterval` polls `GET /api/vivi/sessions/<id>` every `RESUME_POLL_MS` (5s) — epoch-guarded like every other post-await write — until a non-user last turn lands (`setTurns(restored)`, which also ends `awaitingReply`); the poll self-clears after `RESUME_POLL_MAX` (120) ticks (~10 minutes) with no error state, so a turn the server silently dropped just stops polling | pending marker shows on load without a live send; polling stops once the reply lands or the bound is hit | integration | vivi-panel.test.tsx ("a reload whose last turn is the user's shows the pending marker and polls until the reply lands")
- [upload-vivi-chat.402] Closed-panel launcher attention Badge: rendered only while `!open && attentionCount > 0` (the same `attentionCount` — undismissed notifications + pending CRs — the in-panel tab badge shows, so the two can never disagree), capped at "9+" past 9 (a LOWER cap than the tab badge's 99, since the launcher's circular badge has far less room), with a plural-aware `launcherBadgeAriaLabel` | badge hidden at zero or while open; count/aria-label match the tab's total | unit | vivi-panel.test.tsx ("the launcher badge sums undismissed notifications + pending CRs, hidden while open")
- [upload-vivi-chat.403] Focus enters the panel on open even when no composer is mounted: `(textareaRef.current ?? closeRef.current)?.focus()` falls back to the header close button in the onboarding state (no textarea to focus); on close, focus returns to the launcher bubble | keyboard focus always lands inside the panel on open, never stranded at `<body>` | unit | GAP — no test opens the panel from the onboarding state and asserts focus lands on the close button
- [upload-vivi-chat.404] `appendPendingCrCards` after a `crs.list` action result: one `role:"card"` turn per PENDING CR (status idea/under_review), card id `cr-<CR-ID>`, actions approve (`cr_decide` approved) / reject (`cr_decide` rejected, destructive) / decide-later (`dismiss`) — the production PRODUCER of the generic card mechanism; capped at 3 per turn | one card per pending CR with the three owner-click actions | unit | vivi.test.ts ("appends one card per pending CR after a crs.list action, idempotently")
- [upload-vivi-chat.405] `appendPendingCrCards` idempotence: a CR already carded in this session (decided or not, matched on `cr-` id prefix) is never carded again by a later `crs.list`; a failed or absent `crs.list` result appends nothing | second listing appends zero cards | unit | vivi.test.ts ("appends one card per pending CR after a crs.list action, idempotently")

---

# Cross-matrices

## Cross: cross-journeys

Scope: greenfield AND brownfield (non-empty repo) journeys through Vivicy's real UI, grounded in the actual modules (`lib/control.ts`, `lib/vivi.ts`, `lib/upload.ts`, `factory/dev-loop.ts`, `factory/dev-loop-supervised.ts`, `factory/install-skills.ts`, `factory/cr-apply.ts`, the sidebar/project/chat/crs/notifications/pipeline components, and `e2e/**`). Every case cites the file(s) whose behavior it exercises.

Format: `[id] | scenario | expected outcome | kind | covered-today`

---

### 1. Onboarding — panel-hosted (was the four-card chooser)

- [cross.journeys.1] | Fresh target with no `.vivicy/` at all: app boots into the calm `no_target` empty state with the Vivi panel CLOSED; the user opens it via the empty-state "Talk to Vivi" CTA — the sole opener on this screen, since the bottom-left launcher bubble is hidden here — to reach the onboarding view (`components/chat/vivi-onboarding.tsx`) with the three acquisition choices (Open existing / Start new / Import documents). | Empty-state sentence visible with no panel heading and no launcher bubble until the click; then panel heading + all 3 choice buttons visible; no chat composer. | e2e-ui | `e2e/onboarding.spec.ts` ("no_target keeps the Vivi panel closed until the empty-state CTA opens the start choices")
- [cross.journeys.2] | "Open an existing project": expands the shared `OpenProjectForm` in-panel — the SAME form the setup bar's now trigger-less `OpenProjectDialog` wraps. | Selecting a folder persists `current-project.json` and reports up via `onAcquired`. | e2e-ui | open-project-form.test.tsx (folder listing → `POST /api/project` with the persist payload → `onChanged(project)`, plus the `not_governed` refusal branch) — the select→persist→report-up contract behind this journey; the panel-hosted end-to-end (via `vivi-onboarding`) is the remaining e2e gap tracked at e2e-test-infra-rehearsal.55
- [cross.journeys.3] | "Start a new project": the in-panel scaffold form collects a project name + absolute target path, POSTs scaffold, and on success the app lands on the freshly scaffolded project's `empty_canonical` state (canonical holds only the scaffold seed) while the panel auto-opens to chat with Vivi's seeded, transcript-backed welcome greeting (persisting across a reload). | Toast "Project scaffolded", `[data-empty-reason="empty_canonical"]` bare sentence ("Talk to Vivi to get grilled") renders, composer visible, the welcome ("what do you want to build?") visible before and after reload, 0 map nodes, no page errors. | e2e-ui | `e2e/onboarding.spec.ts` ("the scaffold choice creates a new project and lands on the empty-canonical map state")
- [cross.journeys.4] | "Import documents": the panel's import view hosts the self-contained `ImportDocsFlow` — documents first, then the working folder, on one screen. A successful `POST /api/project/import` scaffolds+governs the chosen folder, writes the batch, switches the current project, and (via `onImported`→`onAcquired`) the page re-fetch flips the panel to chat (`vivi-onboarding.tsx`). | one screen with the two ordered steps; on submit the app lands on the newly governed project's `no_map` state with the composer visible. | e2e-ui | GAP — no e2e drives the panel import; the component happy path is covered in import-docs-flow.test.tsx
- [cross.journeys.5] | Import leg: user opens the import flow then BACKS OUT (or closes the panel) before submitting. | Nothing was acquired — the flow reports only on a successful import — so Back just returns to the choices view; no project switch, no re-report. | e2e-ui | GAP
- [cross.journeys.6] | Chat-first intake: ANY successful acquisition flips the panel to chat mode with the composer focused and the empty-thread hint — the user's first message to Vivi IS the intake. | The panel lands on chat over the newly-acquired empty target; the target is reported up exactly once. | e2e-ui | vivi-panel.test.tsx ("acquisition flips the panel to chat mode with the composer focused") + e2e/onboarding.spec.ts (composer visible after the scaffold)
- [cross.journeys.7] | Onboarding back-out: user expands a choice and hits Back WITHOUT ever acquiring. | The choices view re-renders; no target is reported; the onboarding view stays hosted (no-op). | e2e-ui | GAP
- [cross.journeys.8] | Brownfield: pointing the app at a NON-empty repo that already has `.vivicy/canonical/` but no generated map (a project mid-spec, pre-extraction). | `no_map` onboarding card renders (not the panel onboarding view): "No issues extracted yet", "authors the full plan" copy, an "Extract from docs" button, zero react-flow nodes, no raw error text (`architecture map not found` must be absent). | e2e-ui | `e2e/empty-state.spec.ts`
- [cross.journeys.9] | Brownfield: pointing at a fully populated target (`.vivicy/` with generated architecture-data.json + issues, the demo fixture). | Map renders with nodes immediately; the no-target empty state and no-map card are both absent. | e2e-ui | `e2e/control.spec.ts` (asserts `.react-flow__node` renders from the demo target)

### 2. Import corpus journeys (S1-import) — `components/project/import-docs-flow.tsx` + `POST /api/project/import`

- [cross.journeys.10] | Good corpus: user adds a few clean `.md`/`.txt` docs (and/or a `.zip`), browses to an empty or non-governed folder, and hits the single "Import documents" submit (`POST /api/project/import`). | The folder is scaffolded+governed, the batch is written under `.vivicy/uploads/<id>/`, the current project switches to it, a success toast reports the count + detected language, and `onImported(project)` drives the page re-fetch → panel flips to chat, map shows `no_map`. | e2e-ui + integration | GAP — no e2e drives the panel import; the component happy path is covered in import-docs-flow.test.tsx
- [cross.journeys.13] | Unsupported file types among the selection (e.g. a `.png`/`.exe` dropped with the docs). | The client marks them `accepted:false` (struck-through, "unsupported" badge) and never uploads them; the server independently re-filters and returns per-file `rejected[]` for anything that slips through (e.g. inside a zip). The success toast reconciles both into one "skipped" count. | unit + e2e-ui | GAP (e2e); the per-file marking is covered in import-docs-flow.test.tsx
- [cross.journeys.14] | Zip upload: user adds a single `.zip`. It is sent as one transport file part; the server explodes it (fflate), preserving internal folder structure, and returns the exploded files as `accepted[]`. | The client preview shows the `.zip` as one accepted entry; after import the batch on disk holds the exploded files and the manifest lists them; the toast counts the exploded documents. | integration | GAP
- [cross.journeys.15] | Zip that cannot be read (corrupt archive) or whose inner path escapes the batch (zip-slip). | The server returns `400 zip_unreadable` / `400 zip_slip` (nothing written, target not created); the flow surfaces the mapped error toast and keeps the screen for a retry. | integration | GAP
- [cross.journeys.16] | Folder dropped onto the drop zone (recursed via `webkitGetAsEntry`). | Every leaf file is added preserving its folder-relative `rel`, sent to the server as the index-aligned `paths[]`, and written into the batch under that relative path. | e2e-ui | GAP
- [cross.journeys.17] | Folder DROPPED on a browser with no File/Directory Entries API (`entriesFromDataTransfer` falls back to flat `dataTransfer.files`, which is NEVER populated for a directory). | `hadItems && entries.length === 0` is detected (`import-docs-flow.tsx`): an honest "folder read error" toast is shown instead of a silent no-op with an unchanged drop zone. | e2e-ui | GAP — this exact browser-capability edge case has no test at all
- [cross.journeys.88] | Import into an ALREADY-governed folder: user browses the working-folder step to a directory that already holds `.vivicy/`. | `POST /api/project/import` returns `409 already_governed`; the flow shows the "Cannot import documents — already governed … would overwrite it" error toast (top-right, destructive), writes nothing, and keeps the selection so the user can pick a different folder. | e2e-ui | GAP — the refusal is covered at the component level in import-docs-flow.test.tsx
- [cross.journeys.89] | Every added file is unsupported (or a zip that explodes to nothing supported): submit is still allowed because a `.zip` counts as a provisional accepted, but the server refuses the whole batch. | `POST /api/project/import` returns `400 no_supported_files` (nothing written); the flow shows the mapped error toast and keeps the screen. | integration | GAP

### 3. Vivi chat journeys (S1-chat) — `lib/vivi.ts` + `components/chat/vivi-panel.tsx`

- [cross.journeys.23] | Vivi from empty (no canonical yet, no frozen baseline): first turn. `hasFrozenBaseline` is false (`lib/vivi.ts` lines 241-263), so the PRE-freeze allowlist applies (`CANONICAL_DIRS` — canonical docs + spikes). Vivi's reply legitimately writes one or more `.md` files under `.vivicy/canonical/` or `.vivicy/development/spikes/`. | `wrote` array is non-empty; each bubble shows Attachment chips for the written files (`MessageBubble`); `onActivity` fires so the caller can refresh map/project state; no `rejected` field. | e2e-ui + integration | GAP — `vivi-panel.tsx` has 6 unit tests (`vivi-panel.test.tsx`) covering the send/rehydration mechanics; no e2e drives a real multi-turn Vivi session
- [cross.journeys.24] | Vivi continues past the first turn — the transcript is rendered BOUNDED for the prompt (`renderTranscript`, `lib/vivi.ts` lines 168-181): every turn gets one clipped `firstLine(200)` EXCEPT the single most recent prior turn, kept in full. | A long-running session's prompt never grows unbounded, but the immediate last exchange is never truncated — verifiable by asserting the composed prompt content at a chosen turn count. | unit | GAP — no test found exercising `renderTranscript`/`composePrompt` end-to-end with a multi-turn transcript
- [cross.journeys.25] | Vivi writes a file OUTSIDE the allowlist (structural violation — a misbehaving/malicious agent leg edits, say, `package.json` or a canonical doc post-freeze). | `diffVivicy` flags it as a violation (`lib/vivi.ts` lines 421-433); the WHOLE turn is rejected and rolled back byte-for-byte via `restoreSnapshot` (lines 443-458) — both the violating write AND any legitimately-allowed write in the same turn are discarded; the transcript records `rejected: "…wrote outside its allowlist…"`; the user sees the rejected marker on the bubble (inline, no toast). | integration | GAP — no test exercises a real allowlist violation end-to-end (only unit coverage of the pure diff/snapshot helpers would plausibly exist, not confirmed)
- [cross.journeys.26] | Vivi post-freeze, CR-only (B8.1): a frozen baseline exists (`hasFrozenBaseline` true). User asks for a product change mid-run. | The allowlist narrows to `POST_FREEZE_DIRS` (only `.vivicy/change-requests/**.md`); Vivi is instructed to draft exactly one CR file named with the deterministic `nextCrId()` (`lib/vivi.ts` lines 287-299, gap-free sequential numbering even from a partially-written registry); any attempt to touch canonical/spikes post-freeze is a violation and rolled back (case 25's same mechanism). | e2e-ui + integration | GAP
- [cross.journeys.27] | Vivi post-freeze, the written CR FAILS `change-control.ts`'s validation (malformed frontmatter, bad enum, wrong id sequence). | `validateChangeControlSafely` runs the checker via the SAME injected Spawner (`lib/vivi.ts` lines 692-711); a non-zero exit (or the script missing, or a throw) is FAIL-CLOSED — treated as invalid — and the turn is rejected/rolled back exactly like an allowlist violation (never trusted through). | integration | GAP
- [cross.journeys.28] | Vivi post-freeze, a message that needs NO product change (a clarifying question, small talk). | Vivi is instructed to "just answer it and write nothing" (`composePrompt`'s frozen task text, `lib/vivi.ts` lines 332-341); `wrote` is empty, no CR file appears, no violation, no rejection — a legitimate zero-write turn. | integration | GAP
- [cross.journeys.29] | Vivi skills request — SAFE skill: user explicitly asks "install owner/repo@skill" (or pastes a `https://skills.sh/...` URL). Vivi's reply ends with the strict `vivicy-skills` fenced JSON block (`{"install": ["owner/repo@skill"]}`); `parseSkillsDirective` accepts it (`lib/vivi.ts` lines 622-643); `applySkillsDirective` starts an EXPLICIT-mode install (`startSkillsInstall(spawner, {ids})`, line 659) and appends "→ skills install started (explicit mode); check the Skills section." to the reply. | Chat bubble shows the appended status line; the sidebar Skills section (`SectionSkills`) will show the skill once the (real) install completes, with its official/community badge. | e2e-ui + integration | GAP — no test drives the fenced-block → `startSkillsInstall` path end to end (only unit coverage of `parseSkillsDirective` as a pure function is plausible)
- [cross.journeys.30] | Vivi skills request — the skill later fails the RED audit (installer-side): `auditVerdict` returns `red_audit`/`too_many_warnings`/`unaudited` (`install-skills.ts` lines 151-158) and `VIVICY_ALLOW_UNSAFE_SKILLS` is NOT set. | The candidate is REJECTED (`report.rejected.push`, lines 271-273) with the machine reason; it is NEVER installed; the Skills section shows it under the collapsed "Rejected" list with its reason + detail (`RejectedList`, `section-skills.tsx` lines 178-204); the chat's own directive-start message does not reflect this (the audit runs asynchronously in the detached installer, not in the synchronous `applySkillsDirective` call). | e2e-ui + integration | GAP — this is the case explicitly named in the task ("red-audit skill … without the allow-risky setting")
- [cross.journeys.31] | Vivi skills request — red-audit skill WITH `allowUnsafeSkills` turned on in Settings (`settings-dialog.tsx` "Allow risky skills" toggle → `VIVICY_ALLOW_UNSAFE_SKILLS=1` via `settingsToEnv`). | The skill installs anyway, `security_waived: true`, and its `reason` is stamped with the waived verdict (`install-skills.ts` lines 269-270, 293); the sidebar `SkillCard` shows the `ShieldAlert` "security waived" badge (`section-skills.tsx` lines 168-173). | e2e-ui + integration | GAP — this is the second half of the task's explicit ask ("… and with the allow-risky setting")
- [cross.journeys.32] | Vivi skills request — a MALFORMED fenced block (invalid JSON, or `install` missing/empty/non-string entries). | `parseSkillsDirective` returns `{malformed: "…"}` (three distinct honest reasons, `lib/vivi.ts` lines 629-641); `applySkillsDirective` appends "→ skills install NOT started: <reason>." to the reply WITHOUT rejecting the whole turn (a malformed directive is not an allowlist violation — the canonical/CR write, if any, still stands). | unit | GAP
- [cross.journeys.33] | Vivi skills request — the install-skills control call itself refuses (e.g. `already_running`, or `missing_target`). | `applySkillsDirective`'s catch surfaces the `ControlError`'s message the same way as the malformed case ("→ skills install NOT started: <message>."), never silently swallowed, never crashing the turn. | unit | GAP

### 4. Freeze → extract → skills auto → run to Done (the whole S2–S12 spine)

- [cross.journeys.34] | Freeze then Extract: user clicks "Extract" on the control bar (`ProcessControlBar`, `components/sidebar/process-control-bar.tsx` lines 200-232) against a target with a real canonical corpus. `assertRealCanonical` passes (`control.ts` lines 561-586); `runExtract` drives `extract-issues.ts` (freeze → author → verify → map). | Green: `ExtractResult.ok` true, `status: "green"`; toast "Extraction succeeded" listing the steps; `onMapRefresh` fires; the pipeline widget's S2–S6 all flip green (`deriveStageStates`, `pipeline-stages.ts` lines 126-129). | e2e-ui (control bar exists+gated) + integration | `e2e/control.spec.ts` asserts the ALREADY-extracted gated state (button greyed with tooltip) but never drives a FRESH extraction from an unextracted target to green
- [cross.journeys.35] | Extract from an EMPTY canonical (only the scaffold README, no real docs — the exact post-scaffold state from case 3). | `assertRealCanonical` throws `ControlError "empty_canonical"` BEFORE any script spawns (`control.ts` lines 561-586) — "canonical is empty (only the scaffold README)"; the control bar's Extract click surfaces this as an error toast; no agent leg is ever launched into an empty spec (the guard's whole stated purpose, comment lines 554-560). | integration | GAP — no test drives Extract against a freshly-scaffolded (empty-canonical) target through the UI
- [cross.journeys.36] | Extraction reaches `extraction_blocked` (deterministic checks stayed red after bounded retries). | `runExtract` returns `{ok: false, blocked: true, status: "extraction_blocked", summary: <failing checks>}` (`control.ts` lines 540-551); pipeline widget shows S6 red (`pipeline-stages.ts` lines 130-135); a human must look — the summary surfaces the exact failing checks, never a generic failure. | integration | GAP — no test drives extraction all the way to a genuine block state through the app (only the gated-button gate is e2e-tested)
- [cross.journeys.37] | Extraction blocked on unverified spikes: `blocked_on_unverified_spikes` phase. | Pipeline widget shows S2 green, S3 red specifically (not S6) — `applyExtractionStates`'s dedicated branch (`pipeline-stages.ts` lines 137-144), since spike proving (S3) is the actual blocker and S4-S6 never got a chance to run. | integration | GAP
- [cross.journeys.38] | Skills auto stage — 0 skills selected: the scout leg proposes an empty `{"skills": []}` array (a legitimate outcome, `install-skills.ts` line 342 comment "0-6 entries"). | `report.phase` reaches "green" with `installed: []`, `rejected: []`; summary explicitly notes "(zero skills is a legitimate outcome)" (lines 305-306); Skills section shows the empty-state copy (`section-skills.tsx` "emptyState" translation), not an error. | integration | GAP
- [cross.journeys.39] | Skills auto stage — 6 skills selected (the exact cap): scout proposes 6 valid candidates, all pass audit, all install. | `MAX_PROJECT_SKILLS = 6` is exactly met (`installedSkillIds` union is empty at project start, `slots = 6`); all 6 accepted, none rejected for `cap_exceeded`; AGENTS.md managed block + `vivicy.json requiredSkills` both list all 6 (`updateAgentsMd`/`mergeRequiredSkills`, lines 297-299, 501-526). | integration | GAP
- [cross.journeys.40] | Skills auto stage — cap OVERFLOW: scout proposes more candidates than remaining slots (e.g. 8 candidates when 0 are pre-installed, cap 6; or any candidates when the project already has skills near the cap). | Official-vendor candidates are kept FIRST when the selection overflows (stable official-first ordering, `install-skills.ts` lines 250-253); the excess candidates beyond `slots` are rejected with `reason: "cap_exceeded"` and an honest detail naming the current count and the cap (lines 256-258); already-installed candidates are silently dropped as a no-op (never counted as a rejection, lines 247-248). | integration | GAP
- [cross.journeys.41] | Skills — official-vs-community labels: an installed skill's owner is in `OFFICIAL_VENDOR_OWNERS` (e.g. `vercel`, `anthropics`, `stripe`) vs. an arbitrary community owner. | `SkillCard` renders the "official" badge (`bg-status-verified`) vs. "community" badge (`bg-status-implemented`) — `official: OFFICIAL_VENDOR_OWNERS.has(ref.owner)` is computed once at candidate-build time and threads through unchanged to the installed entry and the AGENTS.md block (`install-skills.ts` lines 359, 476-487). | e2e-ui | `e2e/control.spec.ts` does not touch skills; `section-skills.test.tsx` presumably unit-covers the badge rendering (not confirmed read) — GAP for driving this from a live scout run
- [cross.journeys.42] | Skills stage idempotence: re-running `installSkills` in AUTO mode when the prior report is already `"green"`/`"skipped"` for the SAME baseline id. | Immediately returns `phase: "skipped"` without spawning any scout leg or re-auditing anything (`install-skills.ts` lines 205-211) — the stage is genuinely a no-op, verifiable by asserting no leg spawn occurs on a second call. | unit | GAP (through the UI's "Find skills" button, which explicitly confirms before starting — case below)
- [cross.journeys.43] | Skills stage re-runs after a NEW frozen baseline (a CR applied and re-froze): prior report's `baseline_id` differs from the current one. | `skillsStageNeeded` (used by the supervisor, `dev-loop-supervised.ts` lines 65-73) returns true even though the prior phase was green — a changed baseline always re-triggers the stage. | unit | GAP
- [cross.journeys.44] | User manually clicks "Find skills" in the sidebar (`SectionSkills`, `section-skills.tsx` lines 108-127) — a CONFIRMED sensitive action (spawns agent legs). | AlertDialog gates the click; confirming POSTs `/api/control/skills` (auto mode); success toast + report starts polling; the button becomes disabled while `starting || running` (in-flight phase). | e2e-ui | GAP — no e2e drives this confirm-dialog + start flow
- [cross.journeys.45] | Full run to Done: sequential loop (`maxParallel = 1`) processes every ready issue via `runIssueCycle` (implement → review → gate) until none remain; `runLoop` (`dev-loop.ts` lines 3023-3106) returns when `pickNextIssue` yields null. | All issues move to `done/`; the ledger reflects verified for every graph ref; `S9`→`S12` in `deriveStageStates` reach green once `done >= total` (`pipeline-stages.ts` lines 209-230); the control bar's progress bar reads `N/N` and phase flips to "done" (`resolveRunPhase`). | e2e-process (via the fake spawner) + unit | `e2e/control.spec.ts` exercises Run/Stop and reads the demo ledger's pre-existing state, but does not drive a run all the way from 0 issues to done with the fake spawner
- [cross.journeys.46] | Full run to Done: PARALLEL loop (`maxParallel > 1`, up to 12 worktrees, `runLoopParallel`, `dev-loop.ts` lines 3127+). Multiple independent issues run concurrently in isolated git worktrees, spread across the architecture map (max-spread `selectIndependentBatch`); each settles (merge onto integration branch under a lock, post-merge re-gate) independently. | Every worktree issue that reaches a clean post-merge gate is marked verified and moved to `done/`; a merge conflict or post-merge-red gate blocks ONLY that one issue (never the others); the scheduler keeps refilling slots from the ready set until nothing is schedulable. | e2e-process + unit | GAP at the UI/e2e level — the settings e2e sets `maxParallel` to 3/6/12 and reads it back (`e2e/settings.spec.ts`), but no e2e/process test actually RUNS a parallel dev-loop end to end and observes the UI reflect multiple concurrent worktrees
- [cross.journeys.47] | Supervisor kill-resume (detached process): `dev-loop-supervised.ts` relaunches the child loop whenever it dies for any reason, and resumes from `done/` + the ledger (never re-doing completed work). | A killed loop process (simulated crash) leads to a relaunch (`nextSupervisorAction` returns `"relaunch"`, `dev-loop-supervised.ts` lines 48-59) that picks up exactly where it left off; `done`/`total`/`blocked` counts are read fresh from disk each iteration, never cached across the crash. | unit (pure `nextSupervisorAction`) + e2e-process | GAP at e2e-process — no test actually kills a real supervised child and observes relaunch+resume through the app's status endpoint
- [cross.journeys.48] | Supervisor terminal states: `"done"` (all issues done), `"blocked"` (a `-blocked.json` report exists), `"stalled"` (no new `done/` entries across `STALL_LIMIT` consecutive relaunches), `"max_relaunches"` (attempt cap hit). | Each terminal state writes the heartbeat state file (`writeState`) and exits with the right code (`ok` only for `"done"`); the app's status/UI must reflect a stalled or maxed-out run distinctly from a clean done (e.g. the pipeline widget / control bar phase). | unit (`nextSupervisorAction` pure logic) + e2e-ui | GAP — no UI-level test asserts how "stalled" or "max_relaunches" renders in the control bar/pipeline widget (only `resolveRunPhase`'s idle/running/done/blocked/stalled enum exists per `process-control-bar.tsx` — "stalled" IS a rendered phase, but there's no scenario driving the app into it)

### 5. CR decision from the panel's Notifications tab, mid-run

- [cross.journeys.49] | A CR reaches `idea`/`under_review` status (agent-submitted, e.g. from a Vivi post-freeze turn per case 26, or from the dev-loop's own S8 readiness `needs_cr` path). It appears in `CrReviewCards`, which lives INSIDE the always-available Vivi panel's Notifications tab (reachable in ANY app state, not gated behind the map/sidebar). | The tab's unread badge and the CR section both surface it; `PENDING_STATUSES = {"idea","under_review"}` is the only filter (`vivi-notifications.tsx`). | e2e-ui + component | vivi-panel.test.tsx (CR card render + decide, not e2e) — GAP for driving this live from the actual panel mid-run
- [cross.journeys.50] | Approve a CR: clicking "Approve" opens a CONFIRM AlertDialog (`ConfirmDecision`, `vivi-notifications.tsx`) before anything happens — sensitive action pattern. Confirming POSTs `/api/control/crs/decide {id, decision: "approved"}`, which drives `decideCr` (`control.ts` lines 968-1027): DECIDE (deterministic, no agent) then, for an approval, the FULL apply chain (`cr-apply.ts`: apply → verify → freeze → stamp → retire spikes → re-extract, reopening impacted issues). | On green: the inline outcome marker with the chain's summary (e.g. "…re-frozen … re-extracted green (reopened N impacted issue(s))"); the CR leaves the pending set (its decided card stays visible this mount); `onDecided` fires a reload + map refresh. | e2e-ui + integration | GAP — no e2e/integration test drives an approval through the REAL apply chain (mocked `cr-apply.ts` legs would be needed); unit tests presumably exist per-step (`cr-apply.ts`'s own test suite, not read in this pass)
- [cross.journeys.51] | Approve a CR whose apply chain gets BLOCKED at some phase (e.g. reference-check stays red after `DEFAULT_APPLY_ATTEMPTS`, or re-extraction doesn't reach green). | `decideCr`'s `applied.ok` is false, `applied.blocked` may be true; the route/UI treats this as an HONEST non-2xx-equivalent failure (`vivi-notifications.tsx`: "An approval whose apply chain stayed red is honest news, not a silent pass" — the blocked outcome renders inline on the card with "approveBlockedToastTitle"); the CR itself stays `accepted_current_build` (never falsely stamped `docs_applied`, `cr-apply.ts` lines 215-220, 300-305), so it is retryable. | integration | GAP
- [cross.journeys.52] | Reject a CR: confirm dialog, then POST decision `"rejected"`. | `decideCr` records the decision ONLY — no apply chain runs at all (`control.ts` lines 998-1001); the inline "rejected" outcome with the summary "CR <id> rejected"; the CR leaves the pending set. | e2e-ui | GAP
- [cross.journeys.53] | Decide on an UNKNOWN CR id (stale UI state, id no longer exists in the registry). | `change-control.ts decide` fails with "no CR with id"; `classifyDecisionError` maps it to `ControlError("unknown_cr")` (`control.ts` line 1031); the UI surfaces the i18n'd error text via `errorText(tErrors, "control.unknown_cr", …)`. | integration | GAP
- [cross.journeys.54] | Decide on a CR that is NOT in a decidable status (already decided, or `docs_applied`). | `classifyDecisionError`'s regex matches "only idea|under_review can be decided" → `ControlError("cr_not_decidable")` (`control.ts` lines 1032-1034); surfaced as an honest 422-class error, never a silent no-op. | integration | GAP
- [cross.journeys.55] | Retiring a disproven spike as part of a CR apply that names it in `affected_verification_gates` (case for `cr-apply.ts`'s `retireAffectedSpikes`, lines 480-500). | ONLY a spike currently `status: failed` and named on this CR is flipped to `deferred`; a gate resolving to no spike, or to a non-failed spike, is a deliberate no-op — never auto-re-authored, never downgrading a verified spike. | unit | GAP (through the CR-approval UI flow specifically; presumably unit-tested at the `cr-apply.ts` level directly)

### 6. Per-stage retry buttons (every retryable stage) — `components/pipeline/pipeline-widget.tsx` + `app/api/control/retry-stage/route.ts`

- [cross.journeys.56] | Retry "extract" (S6): pipeline widget's S6 node shows a retry icon-button (only stages with a `retryStage` get one, `pipeline-stages.ts` line 39); clicking opens a confirm AlertDialog with stage-specific description text, confirming POSTs `{stage: "extract"}`. | `runExtract` re-runs; success toast with the summary; a blocked outcome returns 422 and the widget's toast reflects "retry_extract_blocked" honestly (not a silent green). | e2e-ui + integration | `app/api/control/retry-stage/route.test.ts` (route-level unit), but GAP for the widget's confirm-dialog → API round trip end to end
- [cross.journeys.57] | Retry "skills" (SK): confirm dialog, POST `{stage: "skills"}` → `startSkillsInstall(spawner)` in AUTO mode (detached). | Returns `{ok: true, stage, run: {pid, mode: "auto", ids: []}}` immediately (fire-and-forget — the actual result lands in the skills report, polled separately); toast "retry succeeded" is really just "retry STARTED" (the widget cannot know the terminal outcome synchronously). | e2e-ui + integration | GAP

- [cross.journeys.59] | Retry "dev" (S9): confirm dialog, POST `{stage: "dev"}` → `startSupervisor(spawner, "resume")`. | Relaunches the supervisor as a resume (picks up from `done/` + ledger, `dev-loop-supervised.ts`); returns `{ok: true, run}`; refused with 409 if a run is ALREADY active (`isRunActive`/`claimRunLock`, `control.ts` lines 313-336) — the single-run lock applies here exactly as it does to the main Run button. | e2e-ui + integration | GAP
- [cross.journeys.60] | Retry an UNSUPPORTED stage (a stray/forged `{stage: "map"}` — the route explicitly notes "map generation lives INSIDE extraction, so there is no standalone map stage"). | 400 response listing the actual `RETRYABLE_STAGES = ["extract","skills","dev"]` (`retry-stage/route.ts` lines 31-40); the UI never offers this (only 3 of the 14 pipeline stages carry a `retryStage`, `pipeline-stages.ts` line 39-43), so this is purely a route-level defense-in-depth case, not reachable via a legitimate click. | unit | `app/api/control/retry-stage/route.test.ts` (presumed, route-level)
- [cross.journeys.61] | Retry button disabled state while pending: `retryPending === stage.retryStage` disables the specific stage's button and its tooltip switches to "retry pending" copy (`pipeline-widget.tsx` lines 82, 222, 303). | Only the STAGE actually being retried is disabled; the other two retryable stages remain clickable (independent per-stage pending state, not a single global "any retry in flight" lock). | e2e-ui | GAP
- [cross.journeys.62] | Retry a stage whose confirm dialog description varies per stage (`t(\`widget.retryDialogDescription.${retryStage}\`)`, line 310) — three distinct i18n strings for extract/skills/dev. | Each retryable stage's AlertDialog shows its OWN honest description of what re-running means (not a generic "are you sure?"). | e2e-ui | GAP

### 7. Settings changes mid-run

- [cross.journeys.63] | Changing the implementer/reviewer CLI assignment, model, thinking level, fast mode, or max-parallel WHILE a run is active (Run button shows Stop, `resolveRunPhase` = running). | Settings PUT succeeds regardless of run state (`lib/settings.ts`/`settings-store.ts` — no run-state gate observed in the settings route); the CURRENTLY RUNNING supervisor process keeps using whatever env it was launched with (env is captured at `startSupervisor` spawn time, `control.ts` lines 403-407) — a mid-run settings change only takes effect on the NEXT `start`/`resume`, never retroactively re-configuring a live child process. | integration | GAP — no test asserts settings changes are inert on an in-flight run and only apply on the next (re)start
- [cross.journeys.64] | Changing `maxParallel` mid-run then Stop+Resume. | The resumed supervisor picks up the NEW `VIVICY_MAX_PARALLEL` value (since `startSupervisor`/`runLoop` reads `clampConcurrency(process.env.VIVICY_MAX_PARALLEL)` fresh at each invocation via `settingsToEnv`) — so a resume can switch from sequential to parallel (or vice versa) between runs of the SAME issue set. | integration | GAP
- [cross.journeys.65] | The distinct-CLI invariant enforced live in the Settings dialog: assigning the implementer to the SAME CLI as the reviewer auto-flips the reviewer to the other CLI (`resolveAgentLegs`, `dev-loop.ts` lines 444-475, mirrored client-side in `settings-dialog.tsx`). | Selecting "Codex" for Implementer while Reviewer is already "Codex" immediately flips Reviewer to "Claude Code" in the SAME interaction, before Save. | e2e-ui | `e2e/settings.spec.ts` ("swap the role -> CLI assignment…") — covered for the client-side swap; server-side `resolveAgentLegs`'s repair (used if a hand-edited env ever assigns the same CLI twice) is presumably unit-tested separately
- [cross.journeys.66] | Fast-mode compatibility gate live in Settings: switching to a non-fast-capable model (e.g. `claude-opus-4-5`) disables the Fast toggle immediately, and switching back re-enables it (reset to off, never silently re-enabling a stale "on"). | `FAST_CAPABLE_MODELS` gate (`dev-loop.ts` lines 396-399) mirrored client-side; `modelSupportsFast` is the single authoritative check on both ends — a settings payload requesting fast on an incompatible model is corrected server-side too (`fast: fastRequested && modelSupportsFast(...)`, line 468), so even a forged PUT can't smuggle an invalid fast flag into a live run. | e2e-ui + unit | `e2e/settings.spec.ts` ("pick a model + fast mode, with strict per-model compatibility") for the client; server-side correction is presumably unit-tested on `resolveAgentLegs` directly
- [cross.journeys.67] | The concurrency stepper's 1-12 clamp is enforced BOTH client-side (input `min`/`max` + disabled arrows at the bounds) and server-side (`clampConcurrency`, `dev-loop.ts` lines 433-437 — unparseable or <1 falls back to 1, >12 caps at 12). A forged PUT of `maxParallel: 50` or `maxParallel: "abc"`. | Server-side clamp still protects the actual loop invocation even if a client bypasses the UI's stepper bounds (defense in depth, explicit comment "defence in depth against a hand-edited or out-of-band VIVICY_MAX_PARALLEL"). | e2e-ui + unit | `e2e/settings.spec.ts` ("enforces the 1–12 range with up/down arrows") for the client; server clamp presumably unit-tested on `clampConcurrency` directly — GAP for a forged-payload integration test through the settings API route
- [cross.journeys.68] | Toggling "Allow risky skills" (`allowUnsafeSkills`) mid-run, then a LATER skills stage run (auto or retry) picks it up. | `VIVICY_ALLOW_UNSAFE_SKILLS` is read fresh from `settingsToEnv(readSettings())` at EVERY `startSkillsInstall` call (`control.ts` line 774) — so toggling it between runs changes the very next install's audit-waiver behavior, exactly like case 31 but reached via Settings directly rather than via Vivi's directive. | integration | GAP

### 8. Quota exhaustion display

- [cross.journeys.69] | An agent hits a real rate limit / quota exhaustion during a leg (`detectRateLimit` matches `DEFAULT_QUOTA_PATTERNS` against a FAILED leg's combined output — required to be BOTH non-zero exit AND pattern match, `dev-loop.ts` lines 586-609, specifically to avoid a successful leg that merely DESCRIBES rate-limiting in its own summary text from being misclassified). | The dev-loop enters a bounded backoff/retry (`runLegWithQuota`/`runLegWithQuotaAsync`, lines 2478+/2546+) computing a wait from `computeWaitMs`/`parseResetMs`, writes `AgentQuota{status: "throttled", reset_at, last_message}` to the quota-state file; the SSE status stream carries this `quota` block; `QuotaFooter` renders the agent's row with the destructive "Throttled" badge (`quota-footer.tsx` line 219) instead of "Available". | e2e-ui + unit | GAP — `quota-footer.test.tsx` presumably unit-tests the component's rendering of a given quota state, but no e2e/integration drives an ACTUAL simulated rate-limit through the fake spawner into the live SSE stream and observes the footer flip
- [cross.journeys.70] | Quota window with a real percentage vs. an HONEST unknown: Codex exposes 5h + weekly %; Claude's `-p` invocation exposes neither (only via the separate status-line probe, `captureClaudeStatusLine`). | `WindowBar` renders an actual `Progress` bar + `"NN%"` only when `used_pct` is a number; otherwise renders "—" (`pctText`, `quota-footer.tsx` lines 159-162) — NEVER a fabricated percentage. This is explicitly a P1 honesty invariant (comment "Honest by construction: no bar and no number when the provider exposes nothing"). | unit | `quota-footer.test.tsx` (presumed unit coverage; not directly read in this pass, inferred from file presence)
- [cross.journeys.71] | Reset countdown: `formatReset` renders "resets in Xh MM" / "resets in Nm" from a future ISO time, and renders NOTHING (null) once the reset time is in the past or unparseable — never a stale/negative countdown. | The ticking `ResetCountdown` sub-component re-renders once per minute (`setInterval(…, 60_000)`) purely from re-evaluating `formatReset(resetAt, nowMs)` — no separate "expired" state to fall out of sync. | unit | `quota-footer.test.tsx` (presumed, `formatReset` is exported specifically for unit testing per its comment)
- [cross.journeys.72] | The Claude quota-line PROBE itself is throttled to at most once per `claudeQuotaProbeMinIntervalMs` (30 min default) — a busy loop never spends more than one trivial turn per window on telemetry (`dev-loop.ts` lines 546-555). | Two legs completing within the same 30-minute window trigger at most ONE probe invocation; the second reuses the still-fresh `last_probe_at` stamp. | unit | GAP — no test found exercising the probe-throttling window specifically (only the config constant + its comment were read)
- [cross.journeys.73] | Quota exhaustion EXCEEDS `quotaMaxWaitMs` (8h default cumulative wait) without the window ever resetting. | The loop gives up waiting and the issue is blocked for a human rather than retrying forever — surfaced through the same `issue_blocked` mechanism as any other exhausted-retry case (S9 pipeline stage flips red, `applyDevStates`, `pipeline-stages.ts` line 214). | unit | GAP — no test found exercising the max-cumulative-wait give-up path specifically

### 9. Notification history across browser close/reopen (panel Notifications tab)

- [cross.journeys.74] | Notification persistence is SERVER-SIDE, never client-only: every dismiss/clear-all POSTs to `/api/control/notifications` and the panel feed re-fetches from there (`vivi-notifications.tsx`) — "single source of truth: the server log, never a client-only hide". Closing and reopening the BROWSER (fresh page load, no client state at all) re-fetches from the same server log. | The notification list and unread tab badge count are IDENTICAL before and after a browser close/reopen (same server-side dismissed/undismissed state); nothing resets to "unread" just because the client lost its in-memory state. | e2e-ui | GAP — no e2e closes and reopens the browser context to verify this specific persistence guarantee; vivi-panel.test.tsx covers the component in isolation with a mocked fetch
- [cross.journeys.75] | Dismissing one notification, then reloading the page (simulating close/reopen): the dismissed item must NOT reappear, and the unread badge count must reflect the remaining un-dismissed set. | `visibleNotifications` filters `!n.dismissed` (`vivi-notifications.tsx`) — purely a function of server-returned data, so a reload that re-fetches naturally excludes it; no localStorage/sessionStorage involved in dismissal state. | e2e-ui | GAP
- [cross.journeys.76] | The feed's poll (`POLL_INTERVAL_MS = 10_000`, active while the panel is open) is the fallback source of freshness for events the SSE status stream cannot reflect (upload verify/apply, CR decisions — explicitly named in the hook's comment). A notification fired by, say, a completed CR apply while the panel sits on the Chat tab. | Within 10s of the event (even with no SSE signature change, since a CR decision doesn't move `issues_done/total/gates.fail/run_active`), the next poll picks it up and the tab badge updates without any user interaction. | e2e-ui | GAP — no test asserts the 10s-poll fallback specifically (only the SSE-signature-change trigger is tested)
- [cross.journeys.77] | Malformed/legacy notification line (predates the `id` field, keyed only by `ts`, or neither). | `NotificationRow`'s key/dismiss-ref falls back `n.id ?? n.ts ?? String(i)` (`vivi-notifications.tsx`); a line with NEITHER `id` nor `ts` still renders (keyed by index) but its dismiss button is simply absent (`onDismiss` is `undefined` when `dismissRef` is falsy) — never a crash, never an unremovable-but-clickable ghost control. | unit | GAP

### 10. Cross-cutting / composite journeys (multiple areas touching one session)

- [cross.journeys.78] | Full brownfield adoption: point Vivicy at an existing large repo with NO `.vivicy/` at all, but the repo itself has substantial pre-existing source code (the "brownfield" case named in the task, distinct from a truly empty greenfield target). Card 1 "Open a project" acquires it; the app then shows the 4-card chooser is REPLACED by whichever state fits (`no_target` initially, since there's no `.vivicy/` yet) — user must still go through Scaffold OR Import OR Vivi to get a canonical spec, exactly as greenfield, EXCEPT the target directory already has files the scaffold/import must coexist with. | Scaffold (`components/project/scaffold-dialog.tsx`) presumably still succeeds (writes only under `.vivicy/`, doesn't touch existing source) — or Import's collision detection (case 18) becomes far MORE likely to fire in practice since a brownfield repo may already have README.md-like files at destinations import would target. | e2e-ui | GAP — no test specifically exercises acquiring a non-empty pre-existing repo (vs. the scaffold's own fresh empty target, or the demo target that's already fully Vivicy-managed)
- [cross.journeys.79] | Switching the target project (via `POST /api/project` or the now trigger-less picker) WHILE a run is active on the previous project. | The run-state lock is PER-PROJECT (`<runtime>/projects/<key>/run-state.json`) and `readRunState`/`clearRunState` resolve the CURRENTLY-selected target — so switching to a different project surfaces THAT project's own run state (idle, or its own live run), while the original project's supervised run keeps executing under its own lock; cross-project concurrent builds are legal and no lock is stranded. The residual question is purely UX: does the control bar re-resolve to the selected project's run state on the first poll after the switch? | integration | GAP — no UI test drives a project switch mid-run and asserts the control bar re-resolves to the newly-selected project's run state
- [cross.journeys.80] | Import a corpus into a target that ALREADY has a frozen baseline (post S1, mid-development) — i.e., "import" used as a mid-run docs top-up rather than initial intake. | `applyUpload` has no baseline-awareness at all (`lib/upload.ts` — it only checks `report.verdict === "green"` and destination collisions); placing a NEW canonical doc into an ALREADY-FROZEN project would silently drift the live canonical away from what was frozen/extracted, with no re-freeze/re-extract triggered automatically (unlike Vivi's post-freeze CR-only path, which structurally PREVENTS a canonical write once frozen, case 26). This is an asymmetry: Vivi enforces frozen-canonical immutability structurally; the raw Import path does not appear to. | integration | GAP — worth flagging as a potential real gap in the product's own invariant enforcement, not just a missing test

### 11. Governess loop + feature-cycle journeys

- [cross.journeys.81] | Governess loop happy path end-to-end: user message → round-1 `vivicy-action` fence (`crs.list`) → `executeViviActions` → an "action" transcript turn → continuation round reads the results and closes; `ViviReply.actions` carries the crs.list result. | Full arc; transcript user→vivi→action→vivi. | e2e-process | vivi.test.ts ("executes a batch, feeds the results back, and closes on the follow-up round")
- [cross.journeys.82] | The loop drives a REAL control-plane side effect through the injected spawner: a `pipeline.start` fence spawns dev-loop-supervised.ts detached, then closes on the follow-up round. | Detached spawn + honest close. | e2e-process | vivi.test.ts ("executes a real control side effect through the SAME spawner (pipeline.start)")
- [cross.journeys.83] | Round-limit journey: every round emits another fence → the loop cuts at `VIVICY_VIVI_MAX_ROUNDS` (=2), runs exactly 2 leg rounds, and returns the honest limit close. | 2 rounds, honest "round limit reached" close. | e2e-process | vivi.test.ts ("stops at the bounded round limit and reports the results honestly")
- [cross.journeys.84] | Rejection-mid-loop journey: a violation on the follow-up round rolls back the WHOLE turn's `.vivicy` writes (round-1 legit write reverted) yet the already-executed action remains in effect, with an honest note. | Rejected turn, executed actions kept. | e2e-process | vivi.test.ts ("a violation on the follow-up round rejects the WHOLE turn's writes but keeps executed actions honest")
