- [cross.chaos-process.44] | DEGRADATION — 5MB hash-only fallback: an owner-dirty file over `PRE_DIRTY_SNAPSHOT_CAP` is hash-guarded but not byte-captured; tampering is still detected, but restore is impossible → loud "could not restore your pre-turn bytes … — check them manually" instead of a silent lie about restoration. | Tamper detected+rejected; unrestorable warning present. | boundary | GAP |
- [cross.chaos-process.45] | DEGRADATION — git unavailable weakens the guarantee loudly: with no usable git (deleted `.git` / git missing from PATH) `gitDirtyPaths` returns null, whole-target no-code enforcement is off, and P3 requires the reply to SAY so ("⚠ … no usable git repository … was unavailable this turn") while `.vivicy` enforcement still holds. | Loud note on the accepted reply; `.vivicy` allowlist still enforced. | e2e-process | vivi.test.ts ("appends a LOUD note (and still enforces .vivicy) when the target has no git") |
- [cross.chaos-process.46] | TAMPER — owner WIP destroyed mid-turn: a leg DELETES an owner's pre-turn dirty file; the disappearance is treated as tampering and the file is restored from the captured bytes (owner's WIP, not HEAD). | Rejection names the file as tampered WIP; file restored to owner bytes. | e2e-process | GAP |

### Critical GAPs (top 5, ranked by blast radius)

1. **No lock at all guards `runExtract`/extraction** (`lib/control.ts:518-552`, scenarios 2 and 35). Every other long-running/stateful factory stage (`startSupervisor`, `startSkillsInstall`) has an O_EXCL pid-liveness lock; extraction has none, so two browser tabs, a UI+CLI pair, or a killed-and-retried request can spawn overlapping `extract-issues.ts` runs against the same target with no serialization, racing canonical/baseline/issue-corpus writes. This is the highest-blast-radius gap in the whole cross-cutting surface.

2. **A killed `applyUpload` leaves a permanently-wedged, partially-placed upload** (`lib/upload.ts:454-512`, scenario 20). The "check-all-then-place-all" comment only protects against a *discovered* collision, not a process kill mid-copy-loop; a partial placement makes every retry hit `would_overwrite` for the files that did land, with no resume or rollback path — the upload is stuck forever without manual `.vivicy/` surgery.

3. **`.integration.lock` and the progress-ledger lock can force-steal from a still-alive-but-slow holder** (`factory/dev-loop.ts:2311-2330`, `factory/progress-ledger.ts:279-309`, scenarios 30/32). Both reclaim on `tooOld OR dead`, not `tooOld AND dead`; a legitimately slow (not crashed) merge/gate/CAS-retry-storm under high parallelism (N up to 12) can have its lock stolen by a waiting sibling while still working, risking two holders believing they own the critical section simultaneously — the exact failure mode the lock exists to prevent.

4. **`runViviTurn` has zero mutual exclusion around its snapshot/spawn/diff window** (`lib/vivi.ts`, scenario 17). Unlike the ledger (O_EXCL+CAS) or the integration merge (dedicated lock), two concurrent Vivi turns — even on different sessions, since enforcement scope is the whole `.vivicy` tree — can have their snapshot-before / diff-after windows interleave, causing one turn's legitimate write to be misattributed to (or misrejected as a violation by) the other turn's diff.

5. **Deleting the runtime dir mid-run silently defeats every lock simultaneously** (scenario 14). `run-state.json`, `skills-install.lock`, and the notify-log location all live under `.vivicy-runtime/`; removing that directory makes every liveness check report "nothing running" even while the supervisor, the parallel dev-loop, and a skills install are all genuinely alive headless, opening the door to a second full set of processes being started on top of the orphaned ones with no detection.

---

Case count: 35 (`cross.chaos-process.1` .. `cross.chaos-process.35`)

---

## Maintenance (automatic updates)

**The rule: every code change that adds, changes, or removes behavior MUST update this matrix in the same working session.** No exceptions, no "later". Concretely:

- New behavior → add its cases (scenario | expected | kind | GAP or the test you wrote) under the right area, at the next free id.
- Changed behavior → update the affected cases' scenario/expected text; if the covering test changed, update the covered-today reference.
- Removed behavior → delete the case lines (ids are retired, never reused).
- New test written for an existing case → flip its `GAP` to the `covered-today: <file> ("<test name>")` reference.
- Update the **status table** counts for every touched area in the same edit.

This obligation is wired into `AGENTS.md`, so every agent session working on Vivicy is bound by it — and it is machine-enforced: `scripts/test-matrix.test.ts` fails `npm test` when behavior sources change without a reconciliation re-stamp (`npm run matrix:stamp`, which records the content fingerprint + the HEAD commit), listing via git exactly which files changed since the stamped commit. The matrix stays current because an unreconciled behavior change turns the suite red.

### Top-10 priority GAPs to burn down next (ranked by blast radius)

1. **No lock guards extraction at all** — [cross.chaos-process.2], [cross.chaos-process.35], [cross.chaos-process.1]. Two tabs, UI+CLI, or a killed-and-retried request can spawn overlapping `extract-issues.ts` runs against the same target with zero serialization, racing canonical/baseline/issue-corpus writes. Every other stateful stage (`startSupervisor`, `startSkillsInstall`) has an O_EXCL pid-liveness lock; `runExtract` has none. Product fix + tests.
2. **A killed `applyUpload` permanently wedges the upload** — [cross.chaos-process.20], [cross.journeys.19]. The check-all-then-place-all guard only covers discovered collisions, not a process kill mid-copy-loop; a partial placement makes every retry hit `would_overwrite` for the files that already landed, with no resume or rollback. Product fix + tests.
3. **Stale-lock reclaim can steal from a slow-but-alive holder** — [cross.chaos-process.30], [cross.chaos-process.32], [cross.chaos-parallel-merge.37], [cross.chaos-parallel-merge.30]. Both `.integration.lock` (120s) and the progress-ledger lock (30s) reclaim on `tooOld OR dead`, so a legitimately slow merge/gate/CAS-storm under N=12 can be double-entered — the exact failure the locks exist to prevent. Verify intent, then test (or fix to require dead-pid AND staleness).
4. **`runViviTurn` has zero mutual exclusion around its snapshot/spawn/diff window** — [cross.chaos-process.17], [cross.journeys.25]. Two concurrent Vivi turns (even different sessions — enforcement scope is the whole `.vivicy` tree) can misattribute each other's writes, corrupting allowlist enforcement/rollback. Also: no end-to-end test of a real allowlist violation and byte-for-byte rollback.
5. **Deleting the runtime dir mid-run silently defeats every lock simultaneously** — [cross.chaos-process.14]. `run-state.json`, `skills-install.lock`, and the notify log all live under `.vivicy-runtime/`; wiping it makes every liveness check report idle while the supervisor, dev-loop, and skills install keep running headless.
6. **CRITICAL error-code collision renders the wrong message to the user** — [upload-vivi-chat.248]. Four semantically distinct failures in `lib/vivi.ts` all throw `ControlError` code `"missing_target"`, so the UI shows the wrong diagnosis for three of them. Product fix + tests.
7. **No diff-scope enforcement on the CR-applier and upload-verify agent legs** — [baselines-change-requests.223], [baselines-change-requests.196], [upload-vivi-chat.101], [upload-vivi-chat.239]. Unlike Vivi's snapshot/diff/rollback, nothing detects a CR-applier leg editing outside `.vivicy/canonical/**` (or a verify-upload leg editing anything at all) — an asymmetric trust model with a fully undetected out-of-bounds-edit path.
8. **Ledger validation failure is unhandled in the `/api/map` read path** — map-ui-data-viewer cross-note (highest-severity GAP of that area). `readLedger` guards read/parse only; `applyLiveOverlay` → `deriveDevelopmentOverlay` can throw on a structurally invalid ledger, turning one bad file into a total map outage (500) instead of a degraded overlay.
9. **Import bypasses the frozen-canonical invariant** — [cross.journeys.80]. (The run-lock half is resolved: the run lock is per-project now, so switching projects mid-run surfaces the newly-selected project's own run state — see [cross.journeys.79].) The raw Import path can place canonical docs into an already-frozen project with no re-freeze/re-extract (Vivi structurally prevents this; Import does not).
10. **No e2e drives the import journey end-to-end** — [cross.journeys.4], [cross.journeys.10], [cross.journeys.13]–[cross.journeys.17], [cross.journeys.88]–[cross.journeys.89]. `import-docs-flow.test.tsx` now covers the component contract (gating, single accept-list picker, per-file rejected preview, governed-refusal toast, success + language toast, `onImported`), but no Playwright spec drives docs→folder→submit from the panel or the map empty-state dialog (real multipart upload + a fresh target folder). The largest remaining e2e GAP on this surface.
